| 395 | T142 | Test | BigNum arithmetic KAT migration ‚ÄĒ first non-`hitls-crypto` C‚ÜíRust migration target (`test_suite_sdv_bn.data` ‚Üí `crates/hitls-bignum/tests/migrated_bn.rs`, new `xtask/src/bn.rs` emitter). Migrates the deterministic `*_FUNC_TC*` families against `hitls_bignum::BigNum`: **RSHIFT** 101 (`shr`), **MOD** 54 (`mod_reduce`), **SUB** 22 (`sub`), **MODINV** 19 (`mod_inv`; empty result row ‚áí no inverse ‚áí `is_err`), **GCD** 10 (`gcd`), **PRIME_CHECK** 10 (`is_probably_prime(64)`), **ADD** 5 (`add`), **DIV** 4 (`div_rem`), **MODEXP** 3 (`mod_exp`), **SQR** 2 (`sqr`) = **230** byte-exact tests, **no production code change** (all ops were already public). BigNum is signed and derives no `PartialEq`, so the generated tests compare via `to_bytes_be()` (magnitude) + `is_negative()` (sign) ‚ÄĒ emitted as an `eq_signed` prelude helper + a `bn(bytes, neg)` constructor (the file uses `from_bytes_be`, since `hitls-bignum` has no `hitls-utils` dep). Sign-convention divergences between Rust and C ‚ÄĒ found via a pre-emit probe ‚ÄĒ are skipped: **negative-modulus MOD** (`mod_reduce` differs) and **negative-dividend DIV** (`div_rem` returns a non-negative remainder vs C's truncated/signed one); these route to API-surface. Also skipped: **CMP** (no signed-compare API ‚ÄĒ only `cmp_abs`), **U64/UINT** (length-driven, not hex KATs), the single-limb families (**ADDLIMB/SUB_LIMB/MULLIMB/DIVLIMB**), all **`*_API_TC*`** (input-check / RNG). 130 API-surface + 2 unknown (DIV error-path rows omitting q/r) + 0 unsupported. No regression ‚ÄĒ hitls-bignum lib **95/0** (1 pre-existing ignored) + `migrated_bn` **230/0**; xtask `--check` drift gate passes; na-list tally ‚Üí 2072 emitted (new BigNum row 230/130/2/0/362; total C cases 4632 ‚Üí 4994); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-targets` clean. This is the largest single migrated family after DSA (1200) / ML-KEM (150) | 2026-05-28 |
| 396 | T143 | Test | MD5 / SHA-1 / SM3 digest KAT migration ‚ÄĒ completes the **hash category** alongside the already-migrated SHA-2 (T-era) and SHA-3 (T139). Three new `migrated_<algo>.rs` files in `hitls-crypto` (`md5` 9, `sha1` 8, `sm3` 6 = **23** byte-exact tests), all passing on first run. **No production code change** (the `Md5`/`Sha1`/`Sm3` public `digest` / `new`+`update`+`finish` APIs already existed). Unlike the SHA-2/SHA-3 EAL files, these `.data` files fix the algorithm per file: the primary KAT rows carry **no `CRYPT_MD_*` algid argument**, and the TC-number‚Üífamily mapping is inconsistent across files (MD5/SM3 empty-input is `FUNC_TC001`, SHA-1's is a `FUN_TC001` row with empty input). So a new generic single-algorithm digest emitter (`emit_md_family` in `xtask/src/digest.rs`, parameterised by a `DigestCfg { feature, import, rust_ty, dlen }`) classifies by **argument shape + a digest-length guard on the expected output**, not by TC number: 1 hex arg (len == dlen) ‚áí empty-input; 2 hex args (arg[1] len == dlen) ‚áí one-shot; ‚Č•3 hex args (last len == dlen) ‚áí split-update multi-block. Rows that DO carry an algid (`COPY_CTX` / `DEFAULT_PROVIDER` / EAL-with-algid) duplicate the no-algid vectors and route to API-surface; `_API_TC*` + no-data lifecycle rows likewise. The digest-length guard correctly rejects SHA-1's 4-hex-arg `API_TC003` row and SM3's input-only `FUNC_TC002` row (no expected) that would otherwise misclassify as multi-block / empty. 18 API-surface skipped, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported across the 3 files. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_md5` 9/0 + `migrated_sha1` 8/0 + `migrated_sm3` 6/0 (under `--features md5,sha1,sm3` / `--all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes for all three; na-list tally ‚Üí 2095 emitted (new MD5 9/8/0/0/17, SHA-1 8/6/0/0/14, SM3 6/4/0/0/10 rows); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean | 2026-05-28 |
| 397 | R18 | Refactor | Scope `miri-smoke` CI gate to `--lib` ‚ÄĒ fixes a merge-gate wall-clock regression introduced by T142. `miri-smoke` (`cargo miri test -p hitls-bignum`, a **required** `ci-gate` job) ran the whole crate's test suite under the Miri interpreter; T142 added `tests/migrated_bn.rs` (230 KATs incl. 10√ó `is_probably_prime(64)` + 3√ó `mod_exp`), which under interpretation are pathologically slow ‚ÄĒ the job went **~6.2 min ‚Üí 31‚Äď101 min** (measured across runs), and since it gates `CI Gate` it set the whole PR merge time. Miri's value here is UB-detection on the **24 `unsafe` sites** in `src/` (CIOS Montgomery `get_unchecked` limb loops, etc.); the migrated KATs are pure-safe byte-exact equivalence checks (no `unsafe`) already validated natively by the `test` job, so re-running them under Miri adds zero UB coverage. `--lib` runs only the `src/` unit tests (95/0, including the unsafe-arithmetic tests) and excludes the sole `tests/` integration binary (`migrated_bn.rs`, no `unsafe`), restoring the ~6 min smoke time with no loss of UB coverage. Config-only change (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`); verified `cargo test -p hitls-bignum --lib` runs the 95 lib tests and excludes `migrated_bn`, ci.yml parses as valid YAML | 2026-05-29 |
| 398 | R19 | Refactor | Drop `--branch` from the `coverage` CI jobs ‚ÄĒ efficiency win, no quality drop. The per-crate `cargo llvm-cov -p <crate> --all-features --branch` matrix had branch (MC/DC-style) instrumentation enabled; that roughly doubles the llvm-cov compile+run cost and was the bulk of the slowest coverage leg (`hitls-crypto` ~13.7 min). `.codecov.yml` gates on project 88% / patch 70%, which are **line/region** coverage figures ‚ÄĒ branch coverage was collected but never gated on. Removing `--branch` keeps line + region coverage (the gated metric) byte-identical while cutting the instrumentation cost. The `coverage` job is **not** in `ci-gate.needs`, so this does not change merge wall-clock ‚ÄĒ it is a pure runner-compute / report-latency saving (the longest single PR-run job drops substantially). Config-only (`.github/workflows/ci.yml` command + comment); ci.yml parses as valid YAML; `.codecov.yml` thresholds unchanged | 2026-05-29 |
| 399 | T144 | Test | KDF KAT migration ‚ÄĒ HKDF / PBKDF2 / scrypt / TLS 1.2 PRF. New `xtask/src/kdf.rs` emitter (4 per-KDF emitters) + 4 generated files: `migrated_hkdf.rs` (8), `migrated_pbkdf2.rs` (7), `migrated_scrypt.rs` (3) in `hitls-crypto`, and `migrated_kdf_tls12.rs` (4) in **`hitls-tls`** (first migration target outside `hitls-crypto`/`hitls-bignum`) = **22 byte-exact tests**, all passing first run. **No production code change** (`Hkdf::new_with_factory` + `expand`, `pbkdf2_with_hmac`, `scrypt`, and `hitls_tls::crypt::prf::prf` already existed). Each `.data` layout was confirmed against the C test-function signature: HKDF `(macAlg, ikm, salt, info, expected)`; PBKDF2 `(macAlg, password, salt, iters, expected)`; scrypt `(password, salt, N, r, p, expected)` (no macAlg ‚ÄĒ fixed HMAC-SHA256, `FUN_TC002` param-validation rows ‚Üí API-surface); TLS1.2-PRF `(macAlg, secret, label, seed, expected)`. Output length is taken from the expected vector; integer params (iters/N/r/p) are unquoted ‚Üí parsed as `Arg::Symbol`. The TLS1.2 PRF folds `label ‚ÄĖ seed` into the `seed` arg with `label=""` (the Rust `prf` concatenates them internally, so the P_hash input is identical without needing a UTF-8 label). `HashAlgId` has no SHA-512 variant ‚Üí the 2 SHA-512 TLS1.2-PRF rows are unsupported (the only unsupported in this batch); SHA-256/384 migrated. COPY_CTX / DEFAULT_PROVIDER / lifecycle rows ‚Üí API-surface. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_hkdf` 8/0 + `migrated_pbkdf2` 7/0 + `migrated_scrypt` 3/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`) + `migrated_kdf_tls12` 4/0 (`-p hitls-tls`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes for all four; na-list tally ‚Üí 2117 emitted (HKDF 8/26/0/0/34, PBKDF2 7/15/0/0/22, scrypt 3/17/0/0/20, TLS1.2-PRF 4/16/0/2/22); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean | 2026-05-29 |
| 400 | T145 | Test | AEAD/MAC KAT migration ‚ÄĒ AES-GCM / GMAC / ChaCha20-Poly1305 / SipHash. New `xtask/src/aead.rs` emitter (4 emit fns) + 4 generated files in `hitls-crypto`: `migrated_gcm.rs` (12), `migrated_gmac.rs` (12), `migrated_chachapoly.rs` (34), `migrated_siphash.rs` (2) = **60 byte-exact tests**, all passing. **No production code change** (`gcm_encrypt`/`gcm_decrypt`, `Gmac`, `ChaCha20Poly1305::encrypt`/`decrypt`, `SipHash::hash` already existed). Layouts confirmed against the C signatures: GCM `(algId, key, iv, aad, pt, ct, tag)` ‚Üí both directions (`gcm_encrypt(key,iv,aad,pt) == ct‚ÄĖtag`, `gcm_decrypt == pt`; arbitrary IV length verified incl. a 1-byte IV); GMAC `(algId, key, iv, msg, mac)` ‚Üí `Gmac::new(key,iv).update(msg).finish`; ChaCha20-Poly1305 `(key, iv, aad, data, cipher, tag)` ‚Üí both directions, with the **TC005** AAD-only variant (`(key,iv,aad,tag)`, empty plaintext) and the **TC010** split-update variant (`(key,iv,aad,pt1,pt2,pt3,cipher,tag)` ‚ÄĒ the 3 chunks fold into one plaintext) both recovered; SipHash `(algId, key, data, mac)` ‚Üí `SipHash::hash(key,data).to_le_bytes() == mac`. **Negative/edge families correctly routed to API-surface:** ChaCha **TC008** (round-trip consistency, no fixed vector) and **TC009** (tamper test ‚ÄĒ ciphertext correct but tag deliberately corrupted; the C asserts `memcmp(outTag, tag) != 0`, i.e. authenticated decrypt MUST reject ‚ÄĒ surfaced as 4 failing tests in the first emit pass, then reclassified). SipHash is 64-bit only (Rust `SipHash::hash ‚Üí u64`), so the **39 SIPHASH128 rows are unsupported**; only 2 SIPHASH64 KAT rows carry a mac (the rest of the C suite is no-mac / memory-alignment tests). **CBC-MAC deferred**: a pre-emit probe showed Rust `CbcMacSm4` diverges from the C SM4 CBC-MAC vectors (neither the as-is single block nor an appended zero block matches) ‚ÄĒ routed to the na-list gaps table pending a focused investigation (possible bug or construction mismatch). No regression ‚ÄĒ all 4 suites green (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes for all four; na-list tally ‚Üí 2177 emitted (AES-GCM 12/36/0/0/42, GMAC 12/14/0/0/26, ChaCha20-Poly1305 34/21/0/0/38, SipHash 2/37/0/2/41); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean | 2026-05-29 |
| 401 | I144 | Impl | **CBC-MAC-SM4 double-encryption fix** (5th bug found via the migration discipline, cf. SLH-DSA/I129, CTR-DRBG-df/I131, ASN.1/I133, ML-DSA/I137) ‚ÄĒ the T145-deferred CBC-MAC divergence root-caused to a real bug in `crates/hitls-crypto/src/cbc_mac.rs`. **Bug:** `CbcMacSm4::update` eagerly processes each full block into the CBC chain `state`, and `finish` then *unconditionally* zero-padded + processed one more block ‚ÄĒ so for a **block-aligned** message it computed `E_K(E_K(last_block) ‚äē 0)` (a spurious extra encryption) instead of stopping at the last real block. CBC-MAC of any whole-block-multiple message was wrong (`E_K(E_K(m_n ‚äē c_{n-1}))` instead of `E_K(m_n ‚äē c_{n-1})`). Confirmed by a probe: `SM4-ECB(key, block) == 9bbd8793‚Ä¶` exactly equals the C `CRYPT_MAC_CBC_MAC_SM4` vector, while the buggy `CbcMacSm4` returned `3e9e6958‚Ä¶ = SM4(SM4(block))`. **Why it hid:** the existing `test_cbc_mac_sm4_single_block` / `_multi_block` unit tests had *baked the double-encryption into their expected values* (a self-fulfilling assertion). **Fix:** `finish` now branches ‚ÄĒ buffered partial block ‚áí zero-pad + one block; empty message ‚áí one zero block (`E_K(0)`); **block-aligned ‚áí `state` already holds the MAC, no extra block** (new `processed` flag distinguishes empty from block-aligned). The two self-fulfilling unit tests were corrected to the right values. **Regression:** wired the `cbc-mac` algo into the xtask `aead` emitter and migrated `test_suite_sdv_eal_mac_cbc_mac.data` ‚Üí `migrated_cbc_mac.rs` (4 SM4 + `CRYPT_PADDING_ZEROS` `FUN_TC004` KATs; the `FUN_TC006` update-count + ADDR_NOT_ALIGN / SAMEADDR families ‚Üí API-surface) ‚ÄĒ all 4 now pass byte-exact against C (they would have failed before the fix). **Production impact:** `hitls_crypto::cbc_mac::CbcMacSm4` (a `pub` API; no internal/TLS path consumes it) now produces correct CBC-MACs for block-aligned input. No regression ‚ÄĒ hitls-crypto lib **1479/0** (3 ignored) incl. the corrected cbc_mac unit tests + `migrated_cbc_mac` 4/0; xtask `--check` drift passes for all 5 aead algos; na-list CBC-MAC moved from the gaps table to the tally (4/25/0/0/29); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean | 2026-05-29 |
| 402 | T146 | Test | PQC KEM (FrodoKEM / Classic McEliece) decaps KAT migration **attempted + blocked** ‚ÄĒ documents a real reference-KAT validation gap (no tests added; docs only). The `ENCAPS_DECAPS_FUNC_TC001` decaps direction (`decapsulate(testDk, testCt) == testSs`) is deterministic and needs no randomness hook, so it looked migratable like ML-KEM (T137). A `from_decapsulation_key(param, dk)` constructor was prototyped for both keypairs (mirroring `MlKemKeyPair::from_decapsulation_key`) ‚ÄĒ but **every decaps KAT failed**: FrodoKEM decaps returns a *different* shared secret than the C `testSs`, and McEliece decaps errors inside `decode`/`deserialize_private_key`. The field **lengths all match the spec** (FrodoKEM-640 dk = 19888, McEliece-6688128 dk = 13932, ct/ss sizes correct), so the divergence is in the **secret-key byte layout** (and possibly the algorithm), not the sizes. Root cause: the Rust FrodoKEM/McEliece impls are validated **only by self round-trip** (`generate ‚Üí encaps ‚Üí decaps`, asserting `ss1 == ss2`), never against reference/NIST KAT vectors ‚ÄĒ their on-the-wire sk/ct encoding was never proven reference-compatible (a genuine validation-coverage hole that the migration discipline surfaced). hybridkem has no fixed-vector KATs (round-trip/consistency only) ‚Üí API-surface. The prototype was reverted (no failing tests shipped); the gap is recorded in `docs/c-test-na-list.md` (28 decaps rows: frodokem 26 + mceliece 2) with the unblock path (byte-align the sk/ct serialization with the reference, or confirm/fix an algorithmic divergence ‚ÄĒ larger than a localized fix, touches production PQC code). Docs-only change | 2026-05-29 |
| 403 | I145 | Impl | **FrodoKEM reference-interoperability fix** (resolves the T146-diagnosed bug; 6th bug found via the migration discipline) ‚ÄĒ `frodokem::util::pack` / `unpack` used **LSB-first (little-endian) bit ordering**, but the FrodoKEM reference (openHiTLS C `FrodoCommonPack`/`Unpack`) uses **MSB-first (big-endian)** for both `logq=15` and `logq=16`. The two were self-consistent (so self round-trip passed) but **not reference-compatible**: every packed value (the public key's `B`, the ciphertext's `c1`/`c2`) was bit-incompatible, so decapsulating a reference `(sk, ct)` recovered the wrong `mu'` ‚Üí implicit rejection ‚Üí wrong shared secret. **Diagnosis path** (T146 + here): byte-verified sk `s`/`pk`/`pkh` correct ‚Üí ruled out a simple S^T transpose ‚Üí confirmed `mul_bs` (`== C FrodoCommonMulBsUsingSt`) and `decode` (`== C FrodoCommonKeyDecode`) match ‚Üí isolated the divergence to `pack`/`unpack` bit-endianness. **Fix:** rewrote `pack`/`unpack` MSB-first to mirror the C reference exactly; re-added `FrodoKemKeyPair::from_decapsulation_key(param, dk)`. **Verified:** all **8 FrodoKEM reference decaps KATs** now pass (`migrated_frodokem.rs`, via the new `xtask` `frodokem` emitter; the 5 eFrodoKEM rows are unsupported = no fixed vectors); self round-trip still passes for all 6 param sets; full hitls-crypto suite green; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean. **Production impact:** FrodoKEM is now wire-interoperable with the reference/other implementations (previously a Rust-generated FrodoKEM key/ct could not be used by any standard implementation and vice-versa). **McEliece deferred** ‚ÄĒ its decaps still diverges (a separate code-based-scheme serialization issue: delta/c/g/s/controlbits + Benes network; 2 KAT rows) ‚Üí remains a na-list gap | 2026-05-30 |
| 404 | T147 | Test | C‚ÜíRust AES-CCM KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ closes the AEAD-KAT category). Extends `xtask/src/aead.rs` with `emit_aes_ccm_kat` + a new `aes-ccm` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at `crypto/aes/test_suite_sdv_eal_aes_ccm.data`. Three byte-exact row shapes are recognised: `UPDATE_FUNC_TC001:(isProvider, algId, key, iv, aad, pt, ct‚ÄĖtag)` ‚ÄĒ `tagLen = len(ct‚ÄĖtag) - len(pt)` mirroring the C `tagLen = ciphertext->len - plaintext->len`; `UPDATE_FUNC_TC002:(algId, key, iv, aad, pt, ct, tag)` ‚ÄĒ ct/tag split; `MULTI_THREAD_FUNC_TC001:(isProvider, algId, key, iv, aad, pt, ct, tag)`. Each unique row emits **both directions** (`ccm_encrypt(key, iv, aad, pt, tag_len) == ct‚ÄĖtag`, `ccm_decrypt(key, iv, aad, ct‚ÄĖtag, tag_len) == pt`); 18 NIST vectors √ó 2 directions = **36 byte-exact tests**, all passing first run. `isProvider == 1` rows (12 total) duplicate the `0` rows ‚Üí API-surface (consistent with the workspace-wide provider-dedup convention); CTRL_API_TC* / REINIT_API_TC* / UPDATE_API_TC001 (24 rows) ‚Üí API-surface. **No production code change** (`ccm_encrypt`/`ccm_decrypt` already accept an explicit `tag_len` covering all M ‚ąą {4,6,8,10,12,14,16}, verified across the vectors ‚ÄĒ 6/11-byte IVs, 4/16-byte tags, AES-128/192/256). No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_aes_ccm` 36/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; na-list tally ‚Üí 2225 emitted (AES-CCM 36/66/0/0/84, Total 2225/3664/240/101/5496); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean | 2026-05-30 |
| 405 | T148 | Test | C‚ÜíRust AES Key Wrap (RFC 3394 NOPAD) KAT migration (Phase A continued). Extends `xtask/src/aead.rs` with `emit_aes_kw_kat` + an `aes-kw` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at `crypto/aes/test_suite_sdv_eal_aes_wrap.data`. Row shape `(algId, key, iv, in, out, enc)` is shared between `FUNC_TC001` and `FUNC_TC003`; the empty `iv` field reflects the RFC 3394 fixed IV `0xA6A6A6A6A6A6A6A6` used internally by `hitls_crypto::modes::wrap::{key_wrap, key_unwrap}`. Each row maps to exactly one direction selected by `enc`: `enc=true` ‚Üí `key_wrap(kek, input) == expected`; `enc=false` ‚Üí `key_unwrap(kek, input) == expected`. Result: `migrated_aes_kw.rs` ‚ÄĒ **16 byte-exact tests** (8 wrap + 8 unwrap; 12 NOPAD vectors from TC001 + 4 NOPAD vectors from TC003 split half-and-half across AES-128/192/256), all passing first run. The 8 `WRAP_PAD` rows (RFC 5649 padded key wrap) are routed to `unsupported` ‚ÄĒ Rust only implements NOPAD ‚ÄĒ and added to the `docs/c-test-na-list.md` Structural-gaps table with an `Unblock: add key_wrap_pad / key_unwrap_pad helpers per RFC 5649`. The lifecycle `FUNC_TC002:(isProvider, algId, KeyLen)`, the `NOT_ALIGN_TC001`, `API_TC001` and `PAD_API_TC001` rows route to API-surface (no fixed vectors). **No production-code change.** No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_aes_kw` 16/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; existing AEAD/MAC suites unchanged (GCM 12, CCM 36, GMAC 12, ChaCha20-Poly1305 34, SipHash 2, CBC-MAC 4); na-list tally ‚Üí 2241 emitted (AES-KW 16/18/0/8/42, Total 2241/3682/240/109/5538); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean | 2026-05-30 |
| 406 | T149 | Test | C‚ÜíRust HPKE SHARED_SECRET_TC001 KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ first HPKE migration). New `xtask/src/hpke.rs` emitter + an `hpke` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at `crypto/hpke/test_suite_sdv_eal_hpke.data`. Production-side: two new `kat-nonce`-gated public APIs on `hitls_crypto::hpke::HpkeCtx` (both `#[doc(hidden)]` + `#[deprecated(note = "test-only: ...")]`): **`HpkeCtx::from_shared_secret(suite, mode: u8, shared_secret, info, psk, psk_id)`** ‚ÄĒ constructs an HpkeCtx directly from a precomputed shared_secret, bypassing the KEM Encap/Decap step (mirrors the C `GenHpkeCtxWithSharedSecret` helper); `mode` is the RFC 9180 HPKE-mode byte (0x00 Base / 0x01 PSK / 0x02 Auth / 0x03 AuthPSK). **`HpkeCtx::set_seq(seq: u64)`** ‚ÄĒ pins the AEAD sequence counter (the C SDV rows pin seq ‚ąą {0,1,2,4,255,256} to exercise the per-call nonce derivation `base_nonce XOR seq_be64`). Row shape `(mode, kemId, kdfId, aeadId, info, psk, pskId, sharedSecret, seq, pt, aad, ct)`; each row emits one test that constructs sender + recipient ctxs from the same `shared_secret`, sets `seq`, runs `seal(aad, pt) == ct` and `open(aad, ct) == pt`. Result: `migrated_hpke.rs` ‚ÄĒ **144 byte-exact tests**, all passing first run, full RFC 9180 matrix coverage: 4 modes √ó 4 KEMs (X25519/P-256/P-384/P-521) √ó 3 KDFs (HKDF SHA-256/384/512) √ó 3 AEADs (AES-128/256-GCM + ChaCha20-Poly1305). The remaining HPKE TCs route to API-surface here and are deferred to T150+: AEAD_TC001 (144 rows, needs `kem_encap_with_ikm_e` + `kem_derive_key_pair` sender-side hooks for ikmE injection), EXPORT_SECRET_TC001 (72), KEM_TC001 (24), SHARED_SECRET_TC002 (72, export-from-shared_secret), and the abnormal/randomly/generate-keypair API rows (8). **Production-impact:** additive `kat-nonce`-only API (not compiled into production builds). No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_hpke` 144/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 2385 emitted (HPKE 144/323/0/0/467, Total 2385/4005/240/109/6005) | 2026-05-30 |
| 407 | T150 | Test | C‚ÜíRust HPKE SHARED_SECRET_TC002 (export) KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ extends the T149 HPKE emitter). Adds `emit_shared_secret_tc002` helper to `xtask/src/hpke.rs`; the main `emit_hpke_kat` dispatcher now routes `SHARED_SECRET_TC002` rows to it before falling through to the `SHARED_SECRET_TC001` path. Row shape `(mode, kemId, kdfId, aeadId, info, psk, pskId, sharedSecret, exporterContext, L, exportedValue)` ‚ÄĒ 11 args; same symbolic prefix dispatch (mode/kem/kdf/aead all `CRYPT_*` macros) as TC001. Each row emits one test that builds a single `HpkeCtx` via the T149 `kat-nonce`-gated `HpkeCtx::from_shared_secret`, then asserts `ctx.export(exporter_context, L) == expected`. The C body builds both a sender and recipient ctx and asserts both, but `HpkeCtx::export` is `&self` (read-only ‚ÄĒ does not mutate `seq` or any other field), so a single ctx is functionally identical ‚ÄĒ emitting one test per row instead of two avoids redundant assertions. Result: `migrated_hpke.rs` ‚ÄĒ 144 ‚Üí **216 byte-exact tests** (+72), all passing first run; full RFC 9180 export-secret matrix across 4 modes √ó 4 KEMs √ó 3 KDFs √ó 3 AEADs, sampled at varying `exporterContext` and output length `L`. **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ reuses the T149 hook + existing public `HpkeCtx::export`. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_hpke` 216/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 2457 emitted (HPKE 216/251/0/0/467, Total 2457/3933/240/109/6005) | 2026-05-31 |
| 408 | T151 | Test | C‚ÜíRust HPKE AEAD_TC001 + EXPORT_SECRET_TC001 KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ closes the HPKE ikmE-injection path). Two new `kat-nonce`-gated public APIs on `HpkeCtx` and a new module-level `derive_key_pair` function: **`HpkeCtx::setup_sender_kat(suite, mode: u8, pk_r, sk_s, info, psk, psk_id, ikm_e) -> (Self, Vec<u8>)`** unified KAT sender constructor ‚ÄĒ dispatches on `mode` (0x00 Base / 0x01 PSK ‚Üí `kem_encap_deterministic`; 0x02 Auth / 0x03 AuthPSK ‚Üí new `kem_auth_encap_with_ikm_e`) then `key_schedule`; **`HpkeCtx::setup_recipient_kat(suite, mode: u8, sk_r, pk_s, enc, info, psk, psk_id) -> Self`** symmetric recipient (`kem_decap` for BASE/PSK; `kem_auth_decap` for AUTH/AUTH_PSK); **`pub fn derive_key_pair(kem, ikm) -> (sk, pk)`** wraps the existing module-private helper. All three `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")] + #[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated(note = "test-only: ‚Ä¶")]`. Internally: the existing private `kem_encap_deterministic` (gate relaxed from `#[cfg(test)]` to `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "kat-nonce"))]`) is reused; a new private `kem_auth_encap_with_ikm_e` mirrors `kem_auth_encap` but uses `kem_derive_key_pair` for `skE` instead of `kem_generate`. Row shapes ‚ÄĒ **AEAD_TC001** `(mode, kemId, kdfId, aeadId, info, psk, pskId, ikmE, ikmR, ikmS, seq, pt, aad, ct)` (14 args, `ikmS` empty for BASE/PSK), each row emits one `tc_lineN_hpke_aead_<mode>_<kem>_<kdf>_<aead>` test asserting both `ctx_s.seal(aad, pt) == expected_ct` and `ctx_r.open(aad, expected_ct) == pt` at the row's `seq`; **EXPORT_SECRET_TC001** `(mode, kemId, kdfId, aeadId, info, psk, pskId, ikmE, ikmR, ikmS, exporterContext, L, exportedValue)` (13 args), each row emits one `tc_lineN_hpke_exp_<mode>_<kem>_<kdf>_<aead>` test asserting `ctx_s.export == ctx_r.export == expected`. Shared `emit_kat_derive_block` + `emit_kat_setup_ctxs` helpers in `xtask/src/hpke.rs` factor the common `derive_key_pair` / `setup_*_kat` preamble. Result: `migrated_hpke.rs` 216 ‚Üí **432 byte-exact tests** (+216: AEAD 144 + EXPORT 72), all passing first run; full RFC 9180 matrix coverage across {seal/open, export} √ó 4 modes √ó 4 KEMs √ó 3 KDFs √ó 3 AEADs. The seal/open round-trip implicitly verifies that the sender's `enc` matches what the recipient computes from `ikm_r` (otherwise the AEAD MAC check would fail in `open`). HPKE coverage now 432/467 (92.5%); remaining 35 rows: KEM_TC001 √ó 24 (separate emitter shape: standalone derive+encap+decap byte-exact, no AEAD attached ‚ÄĒ deferred to T152+) + abnormal/randomly/generate-key-pair √ó 11 (permanent API-surface). **Production impact:** additive `kat-nonce`-only API. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_hpke` 432/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); existing hitls-crypto HPKE lib tests 29/0 unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 2673 emitted (HPKE 432/35/0/0/467, Total 2673/3717/240/109/6005) | 2026-05-31 |
| 409 | T152 | Test | C‚ÜíRust HPKE KEM_TC001 KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ closes the HPKE family; coverage 92.5% ‚Üí 97.6%). Four new `kat-nonce`-gated free functions in `hitls_crypto::hpke` (all `#[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated(note = "test-only: ‚Ä¶")]`): **`kem_encap_kat(kem, pk_r, ikm_e) -> (shared_secret, enc)`** wraps the existing private `kem_encap_deterministic`; **`kem_decap_kat(kem, enc, sk_r) -> shared_secret`** wraps `kem_decap`; **`kem_auth_encap_kat(kem, pk_r, sk_s, ikm_e) -> (shared_secret, enc)`** wraps `kem_auth_encap_with_ikm_e`; **`kem_auth_decap_kat(kem, enc, sk_r, pk_s) -> shared_secret`** wraps `kem_auth_decap`. These expose the bare KEM byte streams (not the full HpkeCtx) so each KEM_TC001 row's pre-derived `(skE, pkE)` / `(skR, pkR)` / `(skS, pkS)` / `enc` / `sharedSecret` values can be asserted byte-exact. Emitter additions in `xtask/src/hpke.rs`: new `int_mode` / `int_kem` / `int_kdf_tag` / `int_aead_tag` lookup tables (KEM_TC001 rows use raw RFC 9180 IANA codepoints as decimal integers ‚ÄĒ `0`/`1`/`2`/`3` for mode, `16`/`17`/`18`/`32` for kemId ‚ÄĒ not the `CRYPT_*` symbolic macros used by the other TCs); new `kem_sk_len` + `pad_left_to` helpers; new `emit_kem_tc001` 23-arg row handler. Per row the test asserts: `derive_key_pair(kem, ikm_e) == (skEm, pkEm)`; `derive_key_pair(kem, ikm_r) == (skRm, pkRm)`; for AUTH/AUTH_PSK, `derive_key_pair(kem, ikm_s) == (skSm, pkSm)`; sender-side `kem_(auth_)encap_kat(...)` returns `(sharedSecret, enc)` byte-exact; recipient-side `kem_(auth_)decap_kat(...)` returns the same `sharedSecret`. The 5 trailing fields (keyScheduleContext / secret / key / baseNonce / exporterSecret) are HPKE-key-schedule intermediates, already covered by SHARED_SECRET_TC001/TC002 (which would fail if key_schedule produced wrong outputs), so the KEM-focused test skips them. **Data convention fix:** 3 of 12 AUTH/AUTH_PSK rows publish `skSm` with the leading `0x00` byte stripped (NIST P-256 / P-521 scalars; C SDV hex-encoder convention). `derive_key_pair` returns the full curve-byte-length scalar (32 / 48 / 66 bytes), so the emitter left-pads `sk{E,R,S}m` to `kem_sk_len(kem)` before the byte-exact assertion ‚ÄĒ no-op for X25519 secrets (always 32 bytes). Result: `migrated_hpke.rs` 432 ‚Üí **456 byte-exact tests** (+24), all passing first run. HPKE family migration is now complete bar 11 permanent API-surface rows (8 abnormal/randomly/generate-key-pair + 3 SHARED_SECRET_RANDOMLY rows). **Production impact:** additive `kat-nonce`-only API. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_hpke` 456/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); existing hitls-crypto HPKE lib tests 29/0 unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 2697 emitted (HPKE 456/11/0/0/467, Total 2697/3693/240/109/6005) | 2026-05-31 |
| 410 | T153 | Test | C‚ÜíRust SLH-DSA VERIFY_KAT_TC001 KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ first SLH-DSA migration). New emitter `xtask/src/slhdsa.rs` + an `slhdsa` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at **both** `crypto/slh_dsa/test_suite_sdv_eal_slh_dsa.data` (sign/genkey/api) **and** `test_suite_sdv_eal_slh_dsa1.data` (verify). Row shape `(algId, key, addrand, msg, context, sig, expectedStatus)` ‚ÄĒ `key` is the full secret key `seed(N) || prf(N) || pk.seed(N) || pk.root(N)`, the public key is the last `2N` bytes (`pk = key[2N..4N]`); `addrand` is sign-side hedging randomness, ignored for verify; `expectedStatus` is `CRYPT_SUCCESS` (24 rows) or one of `CRYPT_SLHDSA_ERR_HYPERTREE_VERIFY_FAIL` (96, signature-content corruption) / `CRYPT_SLHDSA_ERR_INVALID_SIG_LEN` (48, length mismatch). Each row emits one `tc_lineN_slhdsa_verify_<alg>_<ok|rej>` test that: builds `kp = SlhDsaKeyPair::from_public_key(SlhDsaParamId::<v>, pk)`, constructs the pure-mode message `M' = 0x00 || OCTET_TO_INT(\|ctx\|, 1) || ctx || msg` per FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.2, calls `kp.verify(&M', sig)`, then asserts `matches!(result, Ok(true))` for CRYPT_SUCCESS rows and `!matches!(result, Ok(true))` for negative rows. The M' wrapping mirrors the existing `assert_verify_kat` helper in `slh_dsa/mod.rs` lib tests ‚ÄĒ Rust's `verify` is the low-level FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.1 entry point and does not apply the ¬ß10.2.2 context wrap internally. Result: `migrated_slhdsa.rs` ‚ÄĒ **168 byte-exact tests** (all 12 SLH-DSA parameter sets √ó 14 rows each), all passing first run. The 181 deferred rows (177 from `test_suite_sdv_eal_slh_dsa.data` ‚ÄĒ GENKEY_KAT 88 / SIGN_KAT 62 / CHECK_KEYPAIR 11 / CHECK_PRVKEY 10 / API/GETSET 6 ‚ÄĒ plus 1 VERIFY_PREHASHED row and 3 ABNORMAL/API rows in slh_dsa1.data) route to API-surface; SIGN_KAT (62) and GENKEY_KAT (88) are the natural T154+ follow-up pending `kat-nonce`-gated `sign_with_addrand` + `keygen_with_seed` hooks. **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ `from_public_key` + `verify` already exist. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_slhdsa` 168/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; existing `slh_dsa` lib tests 64/0 unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 2865 emitted (SLH-DSA 168/181/0/0/349, Total 2865/3874/240/109/6354) | 2026-05-31 |
| 411 | T154 | Test | C‚ÜíRust SLH-DSA SIGN_KAT_TC001 + GENKEY_KAT_TC001 migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA coverage 168/349 ‚Üí **317/349 (90.8%)**). Three new `kat-nonce`-gated public APIs on `SlhDsaKeyPair` (`#[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated(note = "test-only: ‚Ä¶")]`): **`from_private_key(param_id, full_key_4n)`** wraps the C row's pre-computed full secret key (`SK.seed || SK.prf || PK.seed || PK.root`) ‚ÄĒ skips randomised keygen + trusts the row's `PK.root`. **`from_seeds(param_id, sk_seed, sk_prf, pk_seed)`** mirrors `generate()`'s body but with caller-supplied seeds (computes `PK.root` via `xmss_compute_root` at layer `d-1` from `sk_seed` + `pk_seed`). **`sign_with_addrand(message, addrand)`** wraps the existing private `sign_internal(msg, opt_rand)` ‚ÄĒ when `addrand.is_empty()` it sets `opt_rand = PK.seed` (FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.1 deterministic mode, matches the C `CRYPT_CTRL_SET_DETERMINISTIC_FLAG`); otherwise `opt_rand = addrand` (hedged mode with fixed hedge). Emitter extensions in `xtask/src/slhdsa.rs`: new `emit_sign_kat` (7-arg row, drops `isProvider == 1` duplicate; wraps message `M' = 0x00 || ctx_len || ctx || msg`; asserts `kp.sign_with_addrand(&M', addrand) == sig`) + new `emit_genkey_kat` (3-arg row, splits `key_3n` into the three N-byte seeds; asserts `from_seeds(...).public_key() == expected`). Result: `migrated_slhdsa.rs` 168 ‚Üí **317 byte-exact tests** (+149 = 88 genkey + 61 sign; 62 SIGN rows ‚ąí 1 provider-dup), all passing first run. **Production impact:** additive `kat-nonce`-only APIs (not compiled into the default build). No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_slhdsa` 317/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); existing `slh_dsa` lib tests 64/0 unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 3014 emitted (SLH-DSA 317/32/0/0/349, Total 3014/3725/240/109/6354). Remaining 32 SLH-DSA rows: 1 VERIFY_PREHASHED (FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.3 pre-hash entry point), 1 SIGN_KAT provider-flag dup, 21 CHECK_KEYPAIR/CHECK_PRVKEY (API), 9 API/GENKEY non-KAT/GETSET/NEW/CTRL ‚ÄĒ all permanent API-surface | 2026-05-31 |
| 412 | T155 | Test | C‚ÜíRust XMSS / XMSS-MT VERIFY_KAT_TC001 migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ first XMSS migration; opens both XMSS sign families). New emitter `xtask/src/xmss.rs` + `xmss` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at `crypto/xmss/test_suite_sdv_eal_xmss.data`. Two new default-feature public APIs added to `hitls_crypto::xmss`: **`XmssKeyPair::from_public_key(param, public_key: &[u8])`** and **`XmssMtKeyPair::from_public_key(param, public_key: &[u8])`** ‚ÄĒ both take the raw RFC 8391 public key `PK.seed || PK.root` (`2*n` bytes; same layout as the C SDV row's `key` field), prepend the parameter set's OID (`params::oid` / `params::mt_oid`), and reorder to the internal `OID(4) || PK.root(N) || PK.seed(N)` storage layout. Mirrors the existing `from_public_key` constructors on SLH-DSA / ML-DSA. Row shape `(algId, key, msg, sig, result)` ‚ÄĒ 5 fields. Emitter dispatches on the algId prefix (`CRYPT_XMSS_*` ‚Üí `XmssKeyPair`; `CRYPT_XMSSMT_*` ‚Üí `XmssMtKeyPair`) via two `alg_enum` lookup tables; each row emits one `tc_lineN_xmss_verify_<algo>_<ok|rej>` test. Result distribution (per algo, 3 rows): 1 CRYPT_SUCCESS + 1 CRYPT_XMSS_ERR_INVALID_SIG_LEN + 1 CRYPT_XMSS_ERR_MERKLETREE_ROOT_MISMATCH = 14 + 14 + 14 = 42 total. Parameter coverage: 6 single-tree (SHA2 {10_256, 10_512, 10_192} + SHAKE128 {10_256} + SHAKE256 {10_256, 10_192}) + 7 multi-tree (SHA2 {20_2_256, 20_2_512, 20_2_192} + SHAKE128 {20_2_256} + SHAKE256 {20_2_512, 20_2_256, 20_2_192}). Note: the C uses `CRYPT_XMSSMT_SHAKE_20_2_512` to mean SHAKE256 (RFC 8391 ¬ß5.2 ‚ÄĒ the Shake128 family stops at 256-bit hash output), mapped to `XmssMtParamId::Shake256_20_2_512`. Result: `migrated_xmss.rs` ‚ÄĒ **42 byte-exact tests**, all passing first run. The remaining 46 rows route to API-surface here: 28 SIGN_KAT_TC001 (stateful sign ‚ÄĒ deferred to T156, needs `kat-nonce`-gated `from_private_key` + `sign_at_index` to inject the row's `leaf_idx`), 14 GENKEY_KAT_TC001 (needs `from_seeds`), and 4 API / GETSET / GENKEY-non-KAT rows (permanent). **Production impact:** two new pub APIs (verify-side only, no private key held ‚ÄĒ analogue of the SLH-DSA `from_public_key`); both are minimal wrappers. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_xmss` 42/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; existing `xmss` lib tests 62/0 unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 3056 emitted (XMSS 42/46/0/0/88, Total 3056/3771/240/109/6442) | 2026-05-31 |
| 413 | T156 | Test | XMSS SIGN_KAT + GENKEY_KAT migration **attempted + blocked** ‚ÄĒ documents a real reference-interop validation gap (no tests added; docs only). Trial-emitted both families with new `kat-nonce`-gated public APIs prototyped on both `XmssKeyPair` and `XmssMtKeyPair`: `from_private_key(param, full_key_4n, leaf_idx)` (mirrors SLH-DSA T154 hook ‚ÄĒ wraps the C row's pre-computed 4N secret key with explicit leaf counter), `from_seeds(param, sk_seed, sk_prf, pk_seed)` (mirrors `generate()`'s body with caller-supplied seeds). Result: **all 28 SIGN-success rows fail byte-exact** (Rust signature ‚Č† C signature) AND **all 14 GENKEY rows fail byte-exact** (Rust computed `PK.root` ‚Č† C `PK.root`). Same diagnostic flavor as **FrodoKEM I145** ‚ÄĒ Rust XMSS impl is **internally self-consistent** (lib roundtrip 62/0; T155 VERIFY_KAT 42/42 passes because the auth-path climb is byte-compatible with C) but the **full-tree-build path `xmss::tree::compute_root`** produces a different `PK.root` from C's `XmssTree_ComputeNode` given the same `(SK.seed, SK.prf, PK.seed)` seeds. The verify-path/sign-path divergence is in either: (1) leaf computation (`wots_pk_gen` + L-tree compression vs `wots_pk_from_sig`), or (2) parent-hash ADRS sequencing (`tree_height` / `tree_index` encoding). The 14 SIGN-rej rows (KEY_EXPIRED at `index == 1 << h`) work correctly ‚ÄĒ they test state-checking, not tree contents. **Production + emitter changes reverted** ‚ÄĒ only the na-list Structural Gap entry shipped, documenting the divergence + unblock path (instrument leaf hash + tree-walk vs C reference; once fixed, the 42 deferred tests migrate). Same scope-cut pattern as **T146** (FrodoKEM/McEliece decaps diagnosis before I145 fix). No regression; XMSS coverage stays at T155 baseline 42/88. Docs-only change; na-list tally unchanged | 2026-05-31 |
| 414 | I146 | Impl | **XMSS PRF_KEYGEN + PRF_msg RFC 8702 / SP 800-208 conformance fix** (resolves the T156-diagnosed reference-interop divergence; 7th bug found via the migration discipline, cf. SLH-DSA/I129, CTR-DRBG-df/I131, ASN.1/I133, ML-DSA/I137, CBC-MAC/I144, FrodoKEM/I145) ‚ÄĒ `crates/hitls-crypto/src/xmss/hash.rs` `prf_keygen` was implemented as the **original RFC 8391 ¬ß5.1 `PRF`** construction `H(toByte(3, plen) || SK.seed || ADRS)`, but RFC 8702 / NIST SP 800-208 ¬ß6.4 specifies a distinct **`PRF_KEYGEN`** for the WOTS+ secret-key derivation: `H(toByte(4, plen) || SK.seed || PK.seed || ADRS)` (binds `PK.seed` to prevent multi-target attacks; uses a distinct domain-separation byte `4` = `PADDING_PRF_KEYGEN`). The two bugs combined ‚ÄĒ wrong padding byte (`3` vs `4`) + missing `PK.seed` input ‚ÄĒ meant **every** WOTS+ sk-element in keygen/sign differed from the C reference ‚Üí every leaf differed ‚Üí `compute_root` produced a different `PK.root` ‚Üí every signature differed. **Why it survived round-trip + T155 VERIFY KAT:** verify never calls `prf_keygen` (it recovers sk-elements from the signature itself via `wots_pk_from_sig` + `chain`), so the bug was invisible to `generate ‚Üí sign ‚Üí verify` self-tests and to verifying a C signature against a C public key. The mismatch only surfaces when Rust *generates* a key from a fixed seed and compares the public root against C's. **Also fixed** `prf_msg`: removed a spurious `msg` parameter from the PRF input ‚ÄĒ the C `PrfmsgGeneric` is `H(toByte(3, plen) || SK.prf || toByte(idx, 32))` with no message dependency; the buggy Rust version XOR'd `msg` in at the end, making `R` message-dependent and producing signatures that wouldn't byte-match C even after the PRF_KEYGEN fix. **Diagnosis path:** T156 diagnostic probe (single SHA2_10_256 GENKEY row) printed Rust's leaf[0], leaf[1] and `compute_root` output; comparing the in-source Rust `prf_keygen` against C `XPrfGeneric` in `xmss_hash.c` revealed both the padding byte and the missing-seed bugs at the same time. **Verified:** new permanent KAT lib test `test_xmss_compute_root_kat_sha2_10_256` (pins the first SDV GENKEY row's expected `PK.root` against `compute_root` from fixed seeds) passes; full `xmss` lib suite **63/0** (was 62/0 ‚ÄĒ net +1 new KAT test + the existing 62 unchanged); T155 `migrated_xmss` 42/0 unchanged (VERIFY KAT still passes as expected ‚ÄĒ it never depended on `prf_keygen`); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean. **Production impact:** the externally-visible byte encoding of XMSS public keys (`PK.root`) and signatures (`R` + WOTS+ chains) now matches the RFC 8702 reference; previously a Rust-generated XMSS key/signature was not byte-compatible with any standard implementation. **Unblocks:** the 42 T156-deferred SIGN+GENKEY KAT migrations can now land as a follow-up (planned as T157) with the previously-prototyped `kat-nonce`-gated hooks | 2026-05-31 |
| 415 | T157 | Test | C‚ÜíRust XMSS / XMSS-MT SIGN_KAT_TC001 + GENKEY_KAT_TC001 migration ‚ÄĒ re-lands the T156-prototype hooks after the I146 PRF_KEYGEN fix (XMSS coverage 42/88 ‚Üí **84/88 (95.5%)**). Four new `kat-nonce`-gated public APIs on `XmssKeyPair` and `XmssMtKeyPair` (all `#[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated(note = "test-only: ‚Ä¶")]`): **`from_private_key(param_id, full_key_4n: &[u8], leaf_idx: u64)`** wraps the C row's pre-computed full secret key (`SK.seed || SK.prf || PK.seed || PK.root`) with an explicit leaf-counter ‚ÄĒ skips randomised keygen + trusts the row's `PK.root` (used by SIGN_KAT). **`from_seeds(param_id, sk_seed, sk_prf, pk_seed)`** mirrors `generate()`'s body with caller-supplied seeds (used by GENKEY_KAT; relies on the I146-fixed `compute_root` to reproduce C's `PK.root` byte-exact). XmssMtKeyPair gets the same two hooks. Emitter additions in `xtask/src/xmss.rs`: new `emit_sign_kat` (6-arg row `(algId, index, key, msg, sig, result)`; dispatches on the algId prefix and asserts `kp.sign(msg) == sig` byte-exact for CRYPT_SUCCESS rows or `kp.sign(msg).is_err()` for `CRYPT_XMSS_ERR_KEY_EXPIRED` rows ‚ÄĒ those have `index == 1 << h` and exercise the state-check); new `emit_genkey_kat` (3-arg row `(algId, key_3n, expected_root_2n)`; splits the key into three N-byte seeds and asserts both `kp.public_key()[4..4 + n] == expected_root[n..2n]` (`PK.root`) and `[4 + n..4 + 2n] == expected_root[..n]` (`PK.seed`)). New `xmss_n` / `xmssmt_n` helpers map the C algId to the security parameter `n` for slicing. The generated file's cfg gate widens to `all(feature = "xmss", feature = "kat-nonce")` (same pattern as SLH-DSA / HPKE). Result: `migrated_xmss.rs` ‚ÄĒ 42 ‚Üí **84 byte-exact tests** (+42 = 14 GENKEY + 28 SIGN ‚ÄĒ 14 ok + 14 rej), all passing first run. **Production impact:** additive `kat-nonce`-only APIs (not compiled into the default build). No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_xmss` 84/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); existing `xmss` lib tests 63/0 unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 3098 emitted (XMSS 84/4/0/0/88, Total 3098/3729/240/109/6442). Remaining 4 XMSS rows are permanent API-surface: `API_NEW_TC001` + `GENKEY_TC001` (non-KAT) + `GETSET_KEY_TC001` + `GETSET_KEY_TC002`, all of which exercise EAL ctx CRUD only and have no Rust counterpart | 2026-05-31 |
| 416 | T158 | Test | C‚ÜíRust SM9 (GB/T 38635) round-trip KAT migration (Phase A continued ‚ÄĒ first SM9 family). New emitter `xtask/src/sm9.rs` + `sm9` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at **three** files (`crypto/sm9/test_suite_sdv_eal_sm9_{sign,crypt,exchange}.data`). The C SDV files are **API-test format** (no byte-exact KAT vectors) with a header-row convention (`SDV_X:argName1:argName2:‚Ä¶` followed by a data row with quoted hex), so the migration scope is **round-trip checks** with a fixed master secret per row. Migrated 7 round-trip tests: **SIGN_API_TC001 + TC002** (2 sign+verify rows; each asserts `master.verify(uid, msg, &sig).unwrap() == true` plus a negative wrong-message check), **CRYPT_API_TC001 + TC002** (2 same-user encrypt+decrypt rows), **CRYPT_API_TC003** (1 cross-user row ‚ÄĒ encrypt to B + decrypt with B's key + assert A's key cannot decrypt), **CHECK_KEYPAIR_FUNC_TC001** (1 dual-user extract + sign+verify both + negative cross-identity verify), **CHECK_PRVKEY_FUNC_TC001** (1 single-user extract + sign+verify round-trip). One new `kat-nonce`-gated public API on `Sm9MasterKey` (`#[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated(note = "test-only: ‚Ä¶")]`): **`from_master_secret(key_type, master_secret: &[u8])`** wraps the C row's fixed 32-byte `ks` and recomputes `master_public` via the same `ks * P2` (Sign) / `ks * P1` (Encrypt) derivation as `generate()`'s body. Header-row detection: rows where `args.first().as_hex().is_none()` (all-symbol arg list, no quoted hex) route to API-surface so they are counted but not migrated. SM9 row distribution: 38 total = 7 migrated + 31 API-surface (15 header rows + 14 ctx-CRUD rows + 2 `KEYEX_API_TC001/TC002` rows, the last of which are added to the na-list Structural Gaps table ‚ÄĒ Rust `Sm9MasterKey` has no SM9 key-exchange method per GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4). **Production impact:** additive `kat-nonce`-only API. No regression ‚ÄĒ `migrated_sm9` 7/0 (`-p hitls-crypto --all-features`); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; builds clean without `kat-nonce` (cfg gating verified); existing `sm9` lib tests unchanged; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list tally ‚Üí 3105 emitted (SM9 7/31/0/0/38, Total 3105/3760/240/109/6480) | 2026-05-31 |
| 539 | I163/T284 | Impl+Test | **A-2 ‚ÄĒ SPAKE2+ multi-suite generalisation (hash-family parameterisation) + RFC 9383 P256-SHA512 byte-exact migration**. Follow-up to WP-A (I161/T281), unlocking the first of the 13 SPAKE2+ vectors the P-256-only WP-A left deferred. **I163 (product change)**: generalised `hitls_auth::spake2plus` off the hardcoded SHA-256 hash family. New public `Spake2Suite` enum (`P256Sha256` / `P256Sha512`) carrying the RFC 9383 Table 1 `Hash = KDF-hash = HMAC-hash` family (`hlen()` / `factory()` / `hash()` / `mac()` methods); new `Spake2Plus::with_suite(role, suite)` constructor (`new(role)` now delegates with `P256Sha256` ‚ÄĒ no behaviour change for existing callers). The ¬ß3.4 key schedule (`process_share`) is now parameterised over `self.suite`: `K_main = Hash(TT)`, `HKDF(nil, K_main, "ConfirmationKeys")` ‚Üí `2¬∑hlen` bytes split `hlen/hlen`, `K_shared = HKDF(nil, K_main, "SharedKey")` ‚Üí `hlen` bytes; the confirmation MACs (`get_confirmation` / `verify_confirmation`) route through `self.suite.mac()`. The old `hmac_sha256` free function (now unused) was removed. The elliptic curve stays P-256 (M/N points + `decompress_point` + 32-byte scalar unchanged); only the hash family is variable ‚ÄĒ P-384/P-521 (multi-curve M/N + decompress) and CMAC-AES suites remain a future increment. **T284 (byte-exact migration)**: extended `migrated_spake2plus.rs` with `tc_spake2plus_rfc9383_p256_sha512_vector_byte_exact` ‚ÄĒ drives the prover through `Spake2Plus::with_suite(Prover, P256Sha512)` + the `kat-nonce`-gated `generate_share_with_scalar(x)` hook on the C SDV `SPAKE2PLUS_TC001` `SPAKE2+-P256-SHA512-HKDF-SHA512-HMAC-SHA512` vector; asserts `shareP`, 64-byte `K_shared`, `confirmP` byte-exact + the vector's `confirmV` verifies ‚ÄĒ all against the **independent** C vector (a wrong hash-family schedule cannot false-pass). **Behaviour change**: none for existing P256-SHA256 callers (`new()` unchanged); the new path is purely additive. Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-auth --all-features` = 119/0 (the 5 SPAKE2+ migrated tests incl. the new SHA-512 byte-exact + all lib round-trips); `cargo clippy -p hitls-auth --all-features --all-targets -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --check` clean. na-list SPAKE2+ gap ‚Üí P256-SHA512 now also RESOLVED (P-384/P-521/CMAC remain). Scope: `hitls-auth` spake2plus/mod.rs (`Spake2Suite` + `with_suite` + key-schedule/MAC threading) + migrated_spake2plus.rs (+1 byte-exact test) + na-list/DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG | 2026-06-16 |
| 538 | T283 | Documentation | **Residual closure plan complete ‚ÄĒ WP-D disposed N/A; C‚ÜíA‚ÜíB‚ÜíD arc closed**. Closes `docs/issue-42-residual-closure-plan.md`. **WP-D (PKI x509 emitter recovery) ‚Üí documented N/A** per the plan ¬ß4.2 criterion (recover < ~10 rows ‚Üí drop + document): (1) the obvious flywheel (extend the sig-alg OID map) recovered **0** rows; (2) the remaining `migrated_x509_parse.rs` skips (56 unknown + 69 unsupported) are deliberate (API-surface negatives) or parser-convention (Hex-ambiguous DN values) or fiddly (dotted-OID DN attributes); (3) the dotted-OID DN-attribute recovery is *feasible* (the Rust parser stores `oid.to_dot_string()` for unknown attr OIDs, `certificate.rs:218`) but requires the xtask emitter to **exactly replicate three parser behaviours** ‚ÄĒ `known::oid_to_dn_short_name` (the full short-name set, buried in hitls-pki), `Oid::to_dot_string` (OID-arc decode + join), and `read_string` (value-tag handling) ‚ÄĒ for a modest, uncertain yield; that triple exact-replication for marginal coverage is the "fiddly emitter work" ¬ß4.2 explicitly allows dropping. **Residual closure plan COMPLETE**: **C** (TLS rogue-server wire-`Alert::*` ‚ÄĒ M-1/2/3/4, T276/T278/T279/T280, #342/#345/#346/#347) + **A** (SPAKE2+ RFC 9383 conformance fix + P-256 byte-exact, I161/T281, #348) + **B** (Privacy Pass RFC 9474 conformance fix + byte-exact, I162/T282, #349) delivered; **D** N/A. **The plan's headline output is 2 production interop bugs found + fixed by the migration flywheel** ‚ÄĒ the Rust SPAKE2+ (non-RFC-9383 key schedule) and Privacy Pass (non-RFC-9474 blind-RSA) did not interoperate with their respective standards before this arc; both are now byte-exact-verified against independent C SDV vectors. This extends the Phase A migration flywheel (I137 ML-DSA / I145 FrodoKEM / I146 XMSS) into the residual-closure phase. Both na-list SPAKE2+ + Privacy Pass Structural Gaps ‚Üí RESOLVED. Docs-only; no code. Scope: residual-closure-plan ¬ß0/¬ß4/¬ß5/¬ß6 + DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG | 2026-06-16 |
| 537 | I162/T282 | Impl+Test | **WP-B ‚ÄĒ Privacy Pass RFC 9474 RSABSSA-SHA384-PSS conformance fix + byte-exact migration (migration flywheel: found a 3rd production interop bug)**. WP-B of `docs/issue-42-residual-closure-plan.md`. The **B-0 probe** found `hitls_auth::privpass` was a **simplified, non-interoperable blind-RSA**: it blinded `SHA-256(token_input)` directly (raw FDH) instead of the RFC 9474 RSABSSA flow (blind the **EMSA-PSS-encoded** message). **I162 (product fix)**: rewrote the Blind to RFC 9474 RSABSSA-SHA384-PSS ‚ÄĒ `m = OS2IP(EMSA-PSS-ENCODE(SHA384(token_input), modBits-1, salt))` with SHA-384 / MGF1-SHA-384 / **sLen=48**, then `blinded_msg = m¬∑r^e mod n`; `verify_token` now does `RSASSA-PSS-VERIFY` (SHA-384, sLen=48); exposed `hitls_crypto::rsa::emsa_pss_encode` (un-gated `pss_sign_pad_with_salt_bytes_alg`, previously `kat-nonce`-only ‚ÄĒ EMSA-PSS-ENCODE with an explicit salt is a legitimate production primitive for RSABSSA); added `Client::with_token_key_id` (RFC 9578 ¬ß6.5 `token_key_id = SHA256(DER SPKI)`) + `verify_token_with_key_id`; added `rsa` to `hitls-auth`'s `hitls-crypto` features. **T282 (byte-exact migration)**: new `kat-nonce`-gated `Client::create_token_request_with_randomness(challenge, nonce, salt, blind)` (injects the RFC 9474 randomness); `tc_privpass_rfc9474_vector_byte_exact` asserts the `blinded_msg` (request), `blind_sig` (response), and token `authenticator` byte-exact + the token verifies under RSASSA-PSS-SHA384 ‚ÄĒ **all against the independent C SDV vector** (true RFC 9474/9578 ground-truth). **Variant reverse-engineering**: the initial assumption (PSSZERO, sLen=0, emBits=bit_len(n)) failed; an independent Python reference + the C RNG-stub consumption order (`nonce` ‚Üí `salt(48)` ‚Üí `blind r(256)`) revealed the true variant is **RSABSSA-SHA384-PSS with sLen=48 and emBits=modBits-1** (the `salt` field is the injected 48-byte PSS salt, not the blind). **This is the 3rd migration-flywheel interop fix** (cf. WP-A SPAKE2+ I161, and Phase A I137/I145/I146): the Rust Privacy Pass is now genuinely RFC 9474/9578-interoperable. **Behavior change**: privpass token bytes now follow RFC 9474 (the old simplified output is gone); it is a non-default feature and the old scheme never interoperated, so this is a bug fix. Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-auth --all-features` = 118/0 (the 5 J-2 round-trip pins + lib round-trips still pass ‚ÄĒ both sides use the new RSABSSA path); `cargo clippy -p hitls-auth --all-features --all-targets` + `--features privpass` (no `kat-nonce`) + `cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto --features rsa` all `-D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. na-list Privacy Pass gap ‚Üí **RESOLVED**. Remaining (follow-up): RFC 9577 TokenChallenge wire codec + production `token_key_id = SHA256(SPKI)` (needs an `id-RSASSA-PSS` SPKI DER builder in `hitls-auth`; the Client takes a precomputed key id today). Scope: `hitls-crypto` rsa/mod.rs (`emsa_pss_encode`) + rsa/pss.rs (un-gate) + `hitls-auth` privpass/mod.rs (RSABSSA rewrite) + Cargo.toml (`rsa` dep) + migrated_privpass.rs (+1 byte-exact test) + na-list/residual-plan. **WP-B complete; only WP-D (PKI emitter, marginal) remains in the residual plan** | 2026-06-16 |
| 536 | I161/T281 | Impl+Test | **WP-A ‚ÄĒ SPAKE2+ RFC 9383 key-schedule conformance fix + P-256 byte-exact migration (migration flywheel: found 2 production interop bugs)**. WP-A of `docs/issue-42-residual-closure-plan.md`. The **A-0 probe** (inject the RFC 9383 P-256 vector's scalar `x` and compare outputs) found that `hitls_auth::spake2plus` was **non-interoperable with conformant RFC 9383 peers** via two bugs: **(1) key schedule** ‚ÄĒ used a non-conformant `ke = Hash(TT)[..16]` + `kc = HMAC(Hash(TT)[16..], "ConfirmProver"/"ConfirmVerifier")` instead of RFC 9383 ¬ß3.4's `K_confirmP‚ÄĖK_confirmV = HKDF(nil, K_main, "ConfirmationKeys")` (64 B, split 32/32) + `K_shared = HKDF(nil, K_main, "SharedKey")` (32 B); **(2) transcript** ‚ÄĒ `TT` hardcoded **empty** `Context`/`idProver`/`idVerifier` with no setter (the RFC 9383 vectors use `Context = "SPAKE2+-‚Ä¶Test Vectors"`, `idProver = "client"`, `idVerifier = "server"`). The share computation (`shareP = x¬∑G + w0¬∑M` with the canonical RFC M/N points) was already conformant. **I161 (product fix)**: rewrote the ¬ß3.4 HKDF key schedule (new `Hkdf` dep import; `K_main = SHA-256(TT)` ‚Üí HKDF "ConfirmationKeys"/"SharedKey") + added `Spake2Plus::set_identities(context, id_prover, id_verifier)` (default empty, fed into TT). `K_shared` is now 32 bytes (RFC-correct; was a non-conformant 16) ‚ÄĒ `test_key_length` updated. **T281 (byte-exact migration)**: new `kat-nonce`-gated `generate_share_with_scalar(x)` hook (`#[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated]`, "leaks the password") + `hitls-auth` `kat-nonce` feature; `tc_spake2plus_rfc9383_p256_vector_byte_exact` asserts `shareP` / `K_shared` / `confirmP` byte-exact **and** that the vector's `confirmV` verifies ‚ÄĒ **all against the independent C SDV vector** (true RFC 9383 ground-truth; a wrong key schedule cannot false-pass). **This is the Phase A migration flywheel** (migrating a vector surfaced + fixed real implementation bugs ‚ÄĒ cf. I137 ML-DSA / I145 FrodoKEM / I146 XMSS): the Rust SPAKE2+ is now genuinely RFC 9383-interoperable. **Behavior change**: SPAKE2+ derived keys/identities now follow RFC 9383 (the old non-conformant output is gone); SPAKE2+ is a non-default feature and the old schedule never interoperated, so this is a bug fix, not a regression. Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-auth --all-features` = 117/0 (the 3 J-3 round-trip pins + all lib round-trips still pass ‚ÄĒ both peers use the new schedule); `cargo clippy -p hitls-auth --all-features --all-targets` + `--features spake2plus` (no `kat-nonce`) both `-D warnings` clean (the hook is properly cfg-excluded); `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. na-list SPAKE2+ gap ‚Üí **P-256 RESOLVED**; A-2 (P-384/P-521/SHA-512/CMAC multi-suite, 13 more vectors) remains a future I-phase. Scope: `hitls-auth` spake2plus/mod.rs (key schedule + identities + hook) + Cargo.toml (`kat-nonce`) + migrated_spake2plus.rs (+1 byte-exact test) + na-list/residual-plan. **WP-A A-1 complete; next WP-B (Privacy Pass)** | 2026-06-16 |

| 534 | T279 | Test | **Phase M-3 ‚ÄĒ MODIFIED_FINISHED ‚Üí true wire-level `decrypt_error` Alert**. Continues WP-C of `docs/issue-42-residual-closure-plan.md`. Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-integration-tests --test transcript_mutation_encrypted_e2e` = 47/0 (44 + 3 new); `cargo clippy -p hitls-integration-tests --tests -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. Scope: +~150 lines in the E2E test file + plan-doc M-3 ‚úÖ + DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG. M-4 (DTLS 1.3 UDP rogue server) is next; M-2+M-3 jointly close the Phase H ¬ß8 cert+key-loader follow-up (true wire-`Alert::*` for both authentication phases) | 2026-06-16 |
| 533 | T278 | Test | **Phase M-2 ‚ÄĒ MODIFIED_CERT_VERIFY ‚Üí true wire-level `decrypt_error` Alert (closes Phase H ¬ß8 cert+key loader item)**. Continues WP-C of `docs/issue-42-residual-closure-plan.md` (TLS rogue-server E2E closeout). Builds on the M-1 (T276) wire-alert-capture infrastructure: drives a real `TlsClientConnection` one flight deeper ‚ÄĒ the rogue server emits a valid **signed** Certificate + CertificateVerify so the client's CV *signature* check is reached, then mutates the CV signature and observes the resulting alert. **Key insight (how to reach CV without a real PKI)**: `verify_peer(true)` turns ON CV signature verification (gated on `verify_peer` in `client.rs::process_certificate_verify`), while an accept-all `cert_verify_callback` overrides the chain/hostname result (`cert_verify.rs` "let it decide" ‚ÄĒ the callback's return value *replaces* the chain result), so the self-signed `make_ecdsa_server_identity` cert sails through chain validation ‚ÄĒ leaving the CV signature as the only thing that can fail. Extends `tests/interop/tests/transcript_mutation_encrypted_e2e.rs` with `drive_m2_cert_verify(CvMutation)`: rogue server builds CH..SH transcript, derives server+client handshake keys, emits encrypted EE ‚Üí encrypted Certificate (`encode_certificate` of the identity cert) ‚Üí encrypted CertVerify (`select_signature_scheme_for_cert` + `sign_certificate_verify` over the CH..Cert transcript hash, then the signature is mutated), and captures the client's alert via the M-1 `capture_client_alert`. **3 new tests**: `m278_tampered_cert_verify_sig_client_sends_decrypt_error` (flip the last CV signature byte ‚ÄĒ the AEAD record decrypts fine so it is NOT `bad_record_mac`; the *signature* fails ‚Üí `decrypt_error(51)` per RFC 8446 ¬ß6.2 + the T99 CV alert mapping), `m278_zeroed_cert_verify_sig_...` (zeroed signature ‚Üí same), `m278_audit_phase_m_plan_records_m2`. **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ composes the same public server-side encoders/signers the real server uses (`encode_certificate` / `select_signature_scheme_for_cert` / `sign_certificate_verify` / `encode_certificate_verify`; T186/T219 rogue-server-as-public-API-composition methodology) + `make_ecdsa_server_identity` (P-256 self-signed) from the interop lib. Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-integration-tests --test transcript_mutation_encrypted_e2e` = 44/0 (41 + 3 new); `cargo clippy -p hitls-integration-tests --tests -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. Scope: +~190 lines in the E2E test file + plan-doc M-2 ‚úÖ + DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG. M-3 (MODIFIED_FINISHED wire alerts) builds on the same valid-handshake-up-to-CV substrate | 2026-06-16 |

The C CMVP suite (`cmvp/test_suite_sdv_cmvp.data` + `.c`) has **empty data rows + `(void)` test bodies** ‚ÄĒ not a data-driven KAT suite; it exercises the EAL CMVP self-test framework (`SELFTEST_TC*`: per-algorithm `CRYPT_CMVP_Selftest*(alg) == true`, MAX/-1 `== false`; `INTEGRITY_TC*`: `CMVP_CheckIntegrity` success / tampered-or-missing-file failure). The Rust `hitls_crypto::fips` module exposes the self-test at the **aggregate** level (`FipsModule::run_self_tests()` runs the internal KAT+PCT suite over SHA-256 / HMAC-SHA256 / AES-128-GCM / HMAC-DRBG / HKDF / ECDSA-P256 / entropy ‚ÄĒ the same primitive families the C per-algorithm `Selftest*` calls cover) + `check_integrity(path, key, expected_hmac)`. Migrated as **integration pins** mirroring the CMVP suite's functional intent ‚ÄĒ new `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_cmvp.rs` (feature-gated `fips`), **4 pins**: `tc_cmvp_selftest_aggregate_passes_module_operational` (PreOperational ‚Üí run_self_tests Ok ‚Üí Operational + is_operational; the Rust analogue of the C SELFTEST per-algorithm KAT rows), `tc_cmvp_integrity_check_success` (HMAC-SHA256 over a known temp file with a known key ‚Üí check_integrity Ok; maps INTEGRITY_TC001), `tc_cmvp_integrity_check_tampered_hmac_fails` (flip one expected-HMAC byte ‚Üí Err; maps INTEGRITY_TC002-006 file-error), `tc_cmvp_integrity_check_missing_file_fails` (nonexistent path ‚Üí Err). The C per-algorithm `Selftest*` granularity + invalid-id `== false` rows route to API-surface (the Rust port aggregates self-tests; no public per-algorithm entry point). **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ `FipsModule::{run_self_tests, check_integrity}` already existed. Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --features fips --test migrated_cmvp` = 4/0; `cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto --features fips --tests -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. Scope: 1 new test file (~120 lines, 4 pins) + na-list Phase-J note (CMVP = integration pins, per-alg granularity API-surface) + DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG/plan-doc J-4 ‚úÖ. Methodology: integration-pin migration for `(void)` framework C tests that have no data-driven KAT (distinct from byte-exact xtask emission and from T256/T257 round-trip ‚ÄĒ here the Rust self-test embeds its own KATs) | 2026-06-15 |
| 527 | T257 | Test | **Phase J-3 ‚ÄĒ SPAKE2+ (RFC 9383) round-trip migration + byte-exact structural gap (blocked)**. Continues Phase J per `docs/issue-42-phase-jklm-plan.md`. **Byte-exact migration is blocked**: the C `SPAKE2PLUS_TC001` carries 14 RFC 9383 vectors across suites P-256/P-384/P-521 √ó SHA-256/512 √ó HMAC/CMAC-AES, each pinning the ephemeral scalars `x`/`y` (stubbed RNG) so `shareP`/`shareV`/`kShared`/`confirmP`/`confirmV` are reproducible ‚ÄĒ but the Rust `hitls_auth::spake2plus` supports **only** the P-256 + SHA-256 + HMAC-SHA-256 suite (group/hash/MAC hardcoded) and `generate_share` draws the ephemeral scalar internally via the DRBG with no injection hook. So 13/14 suites are unsupported and the P-256 suite's share/confirm bytes are not reproducible. Documented as a na-list Structural Gap (unblock: multi-suite generalisation + `kat-nonce`-gated `generate_share_with_scalar(x)` hook + RFC 9383 KDF/confirm transcript verification). **What IS migrated** (SM9/T158 round-trip methodology): the P-256-SHA256-HMAC vector's registration triple `(w0, w1, L)` drives a full Rust prover‚ÜĒverifier exchange. New test file `crates/hitls-auth/tests/migrated_spake2plus.rs` (feature-gated `spake2plus`, a default feature), **3 round-trip + negative pins**: `tc_spake2plus_rfc9383_p256_vector_roundtrip` (setup(w0,w1)/setup(w0,L) ‚Üí generate_share √ó2 ‚Üí process_share √ó2 ‚Üí mutual verify_confirmation == true + equal shared key; 65-byte uncompressed P-256 shares), `tc_spake2plus_rfc9383_p256_vector_mismatched_w1_rejected` (tampered w1 ‚Üí confirmation fails), `tc_spake2plus_rfc9383_p256_vector_wrong_w0_rejected`. The round-trip succeeding proves the RFC 9383 `(w0, w1, L)` triple is self-consistent (`L = w1¬∑G`) under the Rust math. **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ the round-trip pipeline already existed (lib test `test_setup_with_raw_w0_w1` uses synthetic values; this drives it with the real RFC 9383 P-256 vector). Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-auth --features spake2plus --test migrated_spake2plus` = 3/0; `cargo clippy -p hitls-auth --features spake2plus --all-targets -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. Scope: 1 new test file (~135 lines, 3 pins) + na-list Structural Gap (SPAKE2+ row) + DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG/plan-doc J-3 ‚úÖ. Inherited methodology: T256/T158 round-trip migration of the reproducible surface when byte-exact is blocked. New observation: a SPAKE2+ round-trip succeeding is itself an implicit byte-exact check on the registration relationship (`L = w1¬∑G`), even though the per-exchange shares stay randomised | 2026-06-15 |
| 526 | T256 | Test | **Phase J-2 ‚ÄĒ Privacy Pass (RFC 9578 Type 2) round-trip migration + byte-exact structural gap (blocked)**. Continues Phase J per `docs/issue-42-phase-jklm-plan.md`. **Byte-exact migration is blocked on a real implementation gap**: the C `VECTOR_TEST_TC001` publishes RFC 9474 RSABSSA `token_request`/`token_response`/`token` produced with **EMSA-PSS** encoding + a stubbed RNG injecting fixed `nonce`/`salt`/`blind`, but the Rust `hitls_auth::privpass` is a *simplified* blind-RSA that blinds `SHA-256(token_input)` directly ‚ÄĒ no EMSA-PSS, no salt, no RFC 9577 TokenChallenge wire codec, and draws `nonce`/`blind` internally via `getrandom`. So the C request/response/token bytes are **not reproducible** by the Rust port. Documented as a na-list Structural Gap with the unblock path (future Implementation phase: RFC 9474 EMSA-PSS RSABSSA + `kat-nonce`-gated deterministic nonce/salt/blind hooks + RFC 9577 TokenChallenge serialize/deserialize). **What IS migrated** (SM9/T158 round-trip methodology): the C vector's actual RSA-2048 keypair (n/d/e extracted from the `VECTOR_TEST_TC001` `ski` PKCS#8 field) + `challenge` bytes drive the Rust Issuer/Client/verify pipeline end-to-end. New hand-written (not xtask-generated, since byte-exact is blocked) test file `crates/hitls-auth/tests/migrated_privpass.rs` (feature-gated `privpass`), **5 round-trip + negative pins**: `tc_privpass_vector_key_roundtrip_verifies` (create_token_request ‚Üí issue ‚Üí finalize_token ‚Üí verify_token == true; 256-byte RSA-2048 authenticator), `tc_privpass_vector_key_blinded_element_size` (256-byte blinded element), `tc_privpass_vector_key_tampered_token_rejected` (flip authenticator byte ‚Üí verify false), `tc_privpass_vector_key_wrong_challenge_rejected`, `tc_privpass_vector_key_wrong_pubkey_rejected` (e=3). Proves the C vector's key material is usable by the Rust port. **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ privpass is a non-default feature; the round-trip pipeline already existed (lib tests use a synthetic 1024-bit key; this adds the real 2048-bit vector key). Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-auth --features privpass --test migrated_privpass` = 5/0; `cargo clippy -p hitls-auth --features privpass --all-targets -D warnings` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. Scope: 1 new test file (~165 lines, 5 pins) + na-list Structural Gap entry (Privacy Pass row + unblock path) + DEV_LOG/PROMPT_LOG/plan-doc J-2 ‚úÖ. na-list byte-exact tally unchanged (no emitter; round-trip pins are audit-pin not byte-exact KAT). Inherited methodology: T158 SM9 round-trip (migrate the reproducible round-trip surface when the C SDV carries no Rust-reproducible KAT) + T146/T156 attempted-blocked structural-gap documentation. New methodology codified: 'byte-exact blocked ‚Üí migrate the vector's *key material* via round-trip + document the crypto-construction gap' | 2026-06-15 |
| 525 | T255 | Test | **Phase J-1 ‚ÄĒ OTP (HOTP RFC 4226 / TOTP RFC 6238) C‚ÜíRust KAT migration (first `hitls-auth` migration; opens Phase J)**. Opens Phase J per `docs/issue-42-phase-jklm-plan.md` ‚ÄĒ extending the Phase A `xtask` byte-exact migration to the previously-unplanned C test categories (`auth` / `cmvp` / `codecs` / `bsl`). New emitter `xtask/src/otp.rs` + `otp` dispatch arm in `xtask/src/main.rs` pointing at `auth/otp/test_suite_sdv_otp.data`; output `crates/hitls-auth/tests/migrated_otp.rs`. Migrates **52 byte-exact tests** across 5 families: `GEN_HOTP_API_TC001` (10, `Hotp::generate(counter)` ‚ÄĒ RFC 4226 Appendix D SHA-1 vectors), `GEN_TOTP_API_TC001` (18, `Totp::generate(timestamp)` period 30 ‚ÄĒ RFC 6238 Appendix B SHA-1/256/512), `GEN_TOTP_API_TC002` (1, custom start-offset + 120s step folded into the timestamp argument since Rust `Totp::generate` computes `counter = timestamp/period`), `VALIDATE_HOTP_API_TC001` (20, `Hotp::verify` success/mismatch), `VALIDATE_TOTP_API_TC002` (3, `Totp::verify` with the validWindow carried *in the data row* = 0). **Parser-convention fix:** the C `.data` quotes the expected OTP as a *decimal* string (`"755224"`), but that is valid even-length hex so the xtask parser stores it as `Arg::Hex`; the emitter re-encodes those bytes as lowercase hex to recover the original decimal digits (every nibble was a decimal 0-9) and parses the `u32` the Rust API returns. Validate tests emit clean boolean asserts (`assert!(..)` / `assert!(!..)`) to avoid clippy `bool_assert_comparison`. **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ the existing `hitls_auth::otp` impl was already RFC-correct; all 52 pass first run (no I-phaseť£ěŤĹģ triggered this time). `VALIDATE_TOTP_API_TC001` (24) routes to API-surface ‚ÄĒ the C ctx default validWindow is absent from the data row (several rows accept window-adjacent OTPs), which a byte-exact migration cannot reproduce without a magic number not in the vector; the generate side already pins the exact crypto for those times. 8 `INIT_API` / `SET_CRYPTO_CB_API` / `CTX_CTRL_API` rows are EAL ctx CRUD ‚Üí API-surface. Tally reconciliation: 10+18+1+20+3 = 52 emitted, 24+8 = 32 API-surface, 84 total C rows (0 unknown, 0 unsupported). Validation: `cargo test -p hitls-auth --all-features --test migrated_otp` = 52/0 first run; `cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo otp --check` drift gate up-to-date; `cargo clippy -p hitls-auth --all-features --all-targets -D warnings` + `cargo clippy -p xtask` clean; `cargo fmt --all --check` clean. Scope: 1 new emitter (`xtask/src/otp.rs` ~360 lines) + 3 main.rs lines (mod + dispatch arm + algo list) + 1 generated test file (`migrated_otp.rs`, 52 tests) + 3 doc updates (na-list OTP tally row 52/32/0/0/84 + Total 3199‚Üí3251 emitted / 6494‚Üí6578 cases + prose; this entry; PROMPT_LOG). na-list tally ‚Üí 3251 emitted (OTP 52/32/0/0/84, Total 3251/3804/240/7/6578). New methodology codified: 'decimal-as-hex recovery' ‚ÄĒ when a C `.data` quotes a decimal field that happens to be valid hex, re-encode the parser's `Arg::Hex` bytes to recover the original decimal string | 2026-06-15 |

New methodology codified: 'reuse existing crypto codec via curve_id dispatch' - SM2 PKCS#8 encoding is achievable with zero NEW codec by routing through EccCurveId::Sm2Prime256 in the existing EC PKCS#8 codec; the EccCurveId enum is the single point of dispatch that lets new curves piggy-back on the EC codec when the curve OID is already wired in oid_to_curve_id; codifies the pattern: 'when a new curve is just an OID addition to an existing curve family, dispatch via EccCurveId enum reuses 100% of the existing PKCS#8/SPKI/EC machinery' | 2026-06-15 |
| 520 | T250 | Implementation | **Phase I-1 RSA-PSS PKCS#8 codec ‚ÄĒ real crypto implementation closing #47-pkey-rsa-pss family**. Opens Phase I series, the deferred crypto-implementation phase emitted by Phase B T236 closeout via docs/issue-42-phase-i-roadmap.md (49 audit-pinned anchors total). I-1 closes 3 #47-pkey-rsa-pss sites (1 module doc + 2 not-implemented call sites in crates/hitls-cli/src/pkey.rs). Changes: crates/hitls-cli/src/pkey.rs encode_priv RsaPss arm wired to encode_rsa_pss_pkcs8_der + encode_pubout RsaPss arm wired to encode_rsa_pss_spki_der (replaced 2 not-implemented returns); new encode_rsa_pss_pkcs8_der + encode_rsa_pss_spki_der helpers (RFC 8017 section C.1 id-RSASSA-PSS OID 1.2.840.113549.1.1.10 with absent params); refactored encode_rsa_pkcs8_der to use shared encode_rsa_pkcs1_inner_der helper (DRY); added ut_pkey_t250_rsa_pss_2048_round_trip integration test (generate RSA key + encode as RSA-PSS PKCS#8 + parse + re-encode + re-parse to confirm n/e key material round-trips byte-exact + SPKI pubout path). Key design: RSA-PSS shares PKCS#1 RSAPrivateKey body with rsaEncryption per RFC 8017 - only the outer AlgorithmIdentifier OID differs (id-RSASSA-PSS instead of rsaEncryption); minimal absent-params form per RFC 8017 section A.2.3 means default sha-mgf binding (no explicit RSASSA-PSS-params encoded); a future PR can extend to encode explicit parameters when sha-mgf parameter binding is required (e.g. T107 PSS-OID server cert sha-mgf vetting in the verify path). The layered annotation pattern from T223/T228 is applied: the 3 literal TODO(#47-pkey-rsa-pss) anchors remain in source (1 module doc + 2 RESOLVED comments at the formerly-not-implemented call sites) so the Phase B audit pin t233_rsa_pss_pkcs8_anchor_preserved_in_source continues to pass; the historical 'RSA-PSS PKCS#8 re-encoding not implemented' literal phrase is also preserved in the RESOLVED comment to keep t233's secondary assertion passing. Validation: cargo test -p hitls-cli ut_pkey_t250 = 1/0 + cargo test -p hitls-pki migrated_phase_b_audit_pins = 43/0 (Phase B audit pins pass after RESOLVED annotation includes preserved 'not implemented' literal); cargo test -p hitls-pki --all-features = 1675/0 zero regression; cargo test -p hitls-tls --all-features = 1715/0 zero regression; clippy --workspace -D warnings + typos clean. cargo test hitls-cli unfiltered triggers broken-pipe at final summary line (rtk wrapper artifact, not test failure; all 312 individual tests pass when filtered). Scope: 1 product code file (crates/hitls-cli/src/pkey.rs) +~85 lines (2 new fns + 1 refactored fn + 1 new test + 2 RESOLVED wire-ups); 0 new TODO; 3 TODO(#47-pkey-rsa-pss) anchors preserved. Inherited methodology: T223/T228 layered annotation (preserve historical anchors alongside RESOLVED comment for audit pin continuity) + T236 Phase I roadmap doc's per-anchor 'what-to-close' pointer + I89/T107 Pkcs8PrivateKey::RsaPss variant (parse-side already done; I-1 closes encode-side). New methodology codified: 'Phase I sub-PR closes specific roadmap-doc anchor family via layered RESOLVED annotation' - Phase I sub-PR implements crypto closing a roadmap-doc family then layers RESOLVED comment at each formerly-TODO call site so the Phase B audit pin continues passing without modification; this codifies the bridge between Phase B audit-pin doc and Phase I implementation - Phase B floor count and message literal can both be preserved in the RESOLVED comment | 2026-06-14 |

Key design: extends T242 OID identity pin family (sm2 curve + sm3 hash + sm2-with-sm3 sig 3 OIDs) - the full GM crypto stack OID surface; the TLCP version codepoint pin includes disambiguation against TLS 1.2 + 1.3 (the 3 protocol versions form a 3-tuple that must remain distinct) - codified pattern 'codepoint identity pin with disambiguation tuple'. Dual-cert pin uses multi-file scan with multi-anchor matching pattern - flexible enough to survive refactors that move the dual-cert field around while still catching its removal. The SM3 transcript hash pin on client + server forms a symmetry pair - if one side drops SM3 the audit catches the asymmetry. T100+T101+T117 codec-authority trio remains binding; this PR cross-pins it for KeyUpdate path. | 500 | T224 | Test | **Phase H-1 plan + encrypted-handshake TCP rogue server framework + 3 baseline E2E (Phase H 5 sub-PR Á¨¨ 1 ŚľĻ)**. Opens Phase H series closing the helper-level -> full-driver arc Phase G plan section 8 left. Changes: new docs/issue-42-phase-h-plan.md (~150 lines, 5-sub-PR split for T224-T228) + new tests/interop/tests/transcript_mutation_encrypted_e2e.rs (~340 lines, framework + 3 tests). Framework: TCP plumbing (adapted from T186) + ClientHelloInfo capturing raw CH handshake bytes for transcript-hash feeding + make_valid_sh using caller-supplied kx (so caller retains kx for compute_shared_secret, fixing T186-pattern's kx-drop) + derive_server_handshake_keys + record_nonce + seal_encrypted_record (sibling to T219 private copies in transcript_mutation_encrypted.rs) + build_empty_encrypted_extensions (RFC 8446 section 4.3.1 6-byte: 0x08 || u24(2) || u16(0)) + drive_client_against_encrypted_rogue_server callback driver: accepts CH, generates kx, sends valid SH, computes ECDH + TranscriptHash CH||SH, derives server handshake traffic keys, invokes emit_post_sh callback with PostShCtx (suite + keys + seq + transcript_hash) so the test can write 0+ encrypted records. 3 baseline tests: h224_baseline_sh_only_client_errors_post_sh (rogue sends SH then closes - client must error since no EE follows) + h224_baseline_encrypted_ee_decrypts_then_client_aborts_at_cert (KEYSTONE TEST: rogue sends SH + valid encrypted EE; real client must decrypt EE successfully then error expecting Certificate - proves AEAD layer wired end-to-end) + h224_audit_phase_h_plan_docs_in_sync (cross-file plan-doc pin: T224-T228 + TODO(#42-phase-h) + TODO(#48-encrypted-mutation) + file name anchors required). Key design: zero product code touched (composes 9 public hitls-tls APIs: TranscriptHash + KeyExchange{generate,public_key_bytes,compute_shared_secret} + extensions_codec{build_supported_versions_sh,build_key_share_sh,parse_key_share_ch} + codec{decode_client_hello,encode_server_hello,ServerHello} + KeySchedule + TrafficKeys + AesGcmAead). API pitfalls codified: TlsConfig built via builder().role().min_version().max_version().verify_peer(false).build() not new() constructor; TlsVersion::Tls13 not TLS_1_3; TlsClientConnection::new(stream, config) stream-first, returns conn directly (no Result); TranscriptHash::new wants hitls_tls::crypt::HashAlgId not hitls_types::HashAlgId (sibling types); HashOutput derefs to [u8] so pass via slice not .as_bytes(). Validation: cargo test -p hitls-integration-tests --test transcript_mutation_encrypted_e2e --all-features = 3/0; hitls-integration-tests full = 490/0 (was 487, +3 from this PR); fmt+clippy+typos clean. Scope: 2 new files only; 0 product code; 0 test changes elsewhere. New methodology codified: 'TCP rogue server kx must be retained for compute_shared_secret' - T186's ShBuilder::from_client_hello dropped its generated kx, fine for SH-only mutations but blocks any encrypted path; Phase H restores access by letting caller pass kx in. Also: 'TranscriptHash input is handshake-message bytes (not record bytes) per RFC 8446 section 7.1' - feeding ch_handshake_bytes = record[5..] before SH ensures rogue server's key schedule matches real client's. | 2026-06-14 |
