| 429 | T159 | Test | C‚ÜíRust SM9 KEYEX_API_TC001 migration through the I158 `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key` API ‚ÄĒ closes the T158-recorded SM9 key-exchange Structural Gap end-to-end. xtask `xtask/src/sm9.rs` gains a new `emit_keyex_api` arm that routes `KEYEX_API_TC001` data rows through the I158-added default-feature `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key(self_user_key, peer_user_key, klen)` entry point: the emitter unpacks `(masterKey: 32B, userIdA, userIdB)`, calls `from_master_secret(Encrypt, masterKey)`, derives both encryption user keys, then loops over the SDV-mandated klen variants (`32` default + `63` for `SK_Long` SM3-block-crossing + `15` for `SK_Short` tail-only) and asserts `SK_A == SK_B` byte-exact (run with both `(self_user_key, peer_user_key)` orderings). `KEYEX_API_TC002` stays API-surface ‚ÄĒ it drills NULL-pointer rejection on the C EAL `ComputeShareKey` wrapper, which Rust's borrow checker forbids at compile time, so there's no runtime check to migrate. `migrated_sm9.rs` 7/0 ‚Üí **8/0** (+1: `tc_line4_sm9_keyex_roundtrip`). **Verification:** `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --features sm9,kat-nonce --test migrated_sm9`: 8/0; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: **4484/0** (prior 4483 + 1 new ‚ÄĒ no regression on lib or any of the other migrated_* test files); xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo sm9 --check`: clean (emitted 7 ‚Üí 8, API-surface 31 ‚Üí 30, unsupported 0 ‚Üí 0); `cargo fmt --all -- --check` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto -p xtask --all-features --all-targets`: clean. **na-list:** SM9 key-exchange Structural Gap row struck through (resolved by I158 + T159); SM9 tally 7/31/0/0/38 ‚Üí **8/30/0/0/38** (KEYEX_API_TC001 data row moves from API-surface ‚Üí emitted); Total 3188/3760/240/11/6480 ‚Üí **3189/3759/240/11/6480**. **Migration plan progress:** SM9 is now structurally complete ‚ÄĒ all migratable round-trip families (SIGN_API / CRYPT_API / CHECK_KEYPAIR / CHECK_PRVKEY / KEYEX_API) covered; the 30 remaining API-surface rows are header rows + EAL ctx CRUD with no Rust counterpart | 2026-06-04 |
| 428 | I158 | Impl | **SM9 key exchange** (GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4 / GM/T 0044.3-2016) ‚ÄĒ closes the T158-recorded SM9 Structural Gap (2 `KEYEX_API_TC*` rows). New SM9 sub-module `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm9/key_exchange.rs` implementing the 3-phase identity-based key agreement built on the same encryption master key (`Ppub_enc` on G1, user keys `dA / dB` on G2): private `key_exchange_init_a/b(peer_id, r_bytes_32, master_pub_enc) -> (BigNum, [u8; 64])` computes `RX = [rX] ¬∑ Q_peer` on G1 (where `Q_peer = H1(ID_peer || HID_enc) ¬∑ P1 + Ppub_enc`); private `key_exchange_confirm_a(id_a, id_b, r_a, RA, RB, dA, mpk, klen) -> (Vec<u8>, [u8; 32])` computes `g1 = e(Ppub, P2)^rA`, `g2 = e(RB, dA)`, `g3 = g2^rA`, derives `SK = KDF(g1 || SM3(g2 || g3 || IDA || IDB || RA || RB), klen)` and side-tag `SA = SM3(0x82 || g1 || inner_hash)`; symmetric `key_exchange_confirm_b` swaps the `g1 / g2` source (`g1 = e(RA, dB)`, `g2 = e(Ppub, P2)^rB`, `g3 = g1^rB`, side-tag `SB = SM3(0x83 || ‚Ä¶)`). The shared inner hash + KDF are factored into a private `confirm_finalise` helper so the two roles can't drift. The pairing identity `e(Ppub_enc, P2) = e(QX, dX) = e(P1, P2)^ks` makes A's and B's `(g1, g2, g3)` tuples identical, so `SK_A == SK_B` and both side-tags cross-verify (proved by the test wiring below). New public `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key(self_user_key, peer_user_key, klen) -> Result<Vec<u8>>` one-shot entry point mirroring openHiTLS C's `CRYPT_SM9_ComputeShareKey` EAL wrapper ‚ÄĒ runs both halves locally with deterministic nonces seeded from the two user IDs (`rA = SM3(id_init ‚ÄĖ id_resp ‚ÄĖ 'A')`, `rB = SM3(id_init ‚ÄĖ id_resp ‚ÄĖ 'B')`, both reduced mod n), with the initiator determined by lexicographic ID comparison (smaller ID = initiator). Refuses self-exchange (same ID) and `klen == 0`. **Verification:** 6 new lib tests in the `key_exchange::tests` module: `test_sm9_key_exchange_roundtrip_default_klen` (32-byte SK, asserts SK_A == SK_B), `test_sm9_key_exchange_long_klen_crosses_sm3_boundary` (63-byte SK ‚ÄĒ `rcnt=1` full block + `rbit=31` tail, mirrors the SDV's `SK_Long` case), `test_sm9_key_exchange_short_klen` (15-byte SK ‚ÄĒ tail-only path, `SK_Short` case), `test_sm9_key_exchange_self_rejected`, `test_sm9_key_exchange_zero_klen_rejected`, `test_sm9_key_exchange_three_phase_with_side_tags` (drives `key_exchange_init_a/b` + `key_exchange_confirm_a/b` directly with fixed nonces, asserts SK_A == SK_B + both side-tags `S_A`/`S_B` verify against the recomputed inner hash + a tampered tag is rejected); `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --features sm9 key_exchange`: 6/0; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: **4483/0** (no regression ‚ÄĒ previous 4477 + 6 new); `cargo fmt --all` clean; `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto --all-features --all-targets`: clean (initial pass needed `doc_overindented_list_items` indent fix in the module doc bullet list + `needless_question_mark` removed from `derive_nonce`'s tail). **Production impact:** additive default-feature public method on `Sm9MasterKey` and one new private sub-module; no ABI break to any existing API. The T158 migration of the 2 `KEYEX_API_TC*` SDV rows can land as a follow-up T-phase that re-emits them through the new `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key` entry point. **na-list:** the SM9 key-exchange Structural Gap is closed in implementation here; the T158-recorded count narrows when the follow-up T-phase replays the 2 rows | 2026-06-04 |
| 427 | I157 | Impl | **SM4-XTS GM-convention fix** (closes the SM4-XTS half of the I156 Structural Gap, 2 of 9 rows). Root cause: SM4-XTS uses the **GM/T 0002-2012** őĪ-multiplication (right-shift + reduction `0xE1`, LSB-out ‚ÄĒ the GHASH/GM polynomial representation) instead of the AES IEEE 1619 convention (left-shift + `0x87`, MSB-out) the Rust `xts.rs` was hard-coded with. Block-aligned single-block rows never trigger the tweak multiply, so the bug was masked for those ‚ÄĒ exactly why I156's 2 migrated rows (line 174 / line 213, both 16-byte VECTOR2) coincidentally passed byte-exact while the 56-byte VECTOR1 rows (line 172 / line 211, ciphertext-stealing path) failed. C reference branches on `algId == CRYPT_SYM_SM4` in `crypto/modes/src/modes_xts.c` lines 174-178: AES path calls `GF128Mul` (lines 95-108), SM4 path calls `GF128Mul_GM` (lines 126-139). **Fix.** `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/xts.rs`: new private `gf_mul_alpha_gm` (right-shift + `0xE1` reduction) alongside the existing `gf_mul_alpha` (AES IEEE 1619); `xts_encrypt_inner<C: BlockCipher>` and `xts_decrypt_inner<C>` gain a `gf_mul: fn(&mut [u8; AES_BLOCK_SIZE])` parameter ‚ÄĒ the őĪ-multiplication is now caller-selected, not hard-coded; AES `xts_encrypt` / `xts_decrypt` continue to pass `gf_mul_alpha` (unchanged behaviour, IEEE P1619 vector 1 still matches); SM4 `sm4_xts_encrypt` / `sm4_xts_decrypt` now pass `gf_mul_alpha_gm`. **Companion finding ‚ÄĒ SM4-HCTR C SDV data is unverified.** While probing the HCTR half of the divergence I read `crypto/modes/src/modes_hctr.c` in the C reference: `MODES_HCTR_Update` (lines 291-317) is a **no-op buffer stage** that writes nothing to `out` (it only copies input into the context's internal buffer). The SM4-HCTR SDV test calls `MODES_HCTR_Update` and then asserts `memcmp(out, cipherText, len) == 0` with **`len == 0`** (because the API hasn't produced output at the Update step), which is a trivially true assertion. The `cipherText` field in the `.data` rows is therefore never actually compared to anything ‚ÄĒ the published values are **unverified**. Rust `hctr_encrypt` / `_decrypt` are internally self-consistent (`gf128_mul`, `uhash` byte-exact against side-by-side C reads; proptest round-trip clean), so the byte divergence vs the C `.data` is *not* a Rust bug ‚ÄĒ there is simply no trustworthy C KAT to migrate against. HCTR migration is therefore deferred until an independent third-party KAT (e.g. a GM/T 0002 erratum or `MODES_HCTR_Final` output-augmented vectors) appears. **Emitter widening.** `xtask/src/sm4.rs::emit_kat`: the `input.len() == 16` restriction on the `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_XTS` arm is dropped in favour of `input.len() >= 16`; all rows of legal XTS size now route through `emit_xts`. The previous "ciphertext-stealing diverges" comment is replaced with an I157-anchored one explaining the GM-convention fix. Module-level doc-comment updated. **Verification:** `modes::xts` lib tests 10/0 unchanged (the AES IEEE 1619 vector + the AES ciphertext-stealing tests still pass ‚ÄĒ the caller-selected `gf_mul` parameter is transparent for AES); `migrated_sm4.rs` 37/0 ‚Üí **39/0** (+2 byte-exact SM4-XTS ciphertext-stealing rows: `tc_line172_sm4_xts_encrypt`, `tc_line211_sm4_xts_decrypt`); xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo sm4 --check`: clean ‚ÄĒ emitted 37 ‚Üí 39, unsupported 9 ‚Üí 7; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: all suites green, 0 regressions; `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto -p xtask --all-features --all-targets`: clean. **na-list:** SM4-XTS row struck through (closed in I157); remaining gap narrowed to SM4-HCTR (4 rows, unverified C SDV data) + SM4-GCM-decrypt (3 rows, C SDV omits the auth tag); count 9 ‚Üí 7. SM4 tally 37/237/0/9/283 ‚Üí 39/237/0/7/283; Total 3188/3760/240/11/6480 ‚Üí **3190/3760/240/9/6480** | 2026-06-03 |
| 426 | I156 | Impl | **SM4 HCTR + XTS wrappers** (partial: closes 2 of 11 SM4 Structural-Gap rows, narrows the remaining gap with a clear reference-interop divergence anchor). New default-feature public functions in `hitls_crypto::modes`: `sm4_hctr_encrypt` / `sm4_hctr_decrypt` (`hctr.rs`) and `sm4_xts_encrypt` / `sm4_xts_decrypt` (`xts.rs`), all `#[cfg(feature = "sm4")]`. Implementation strategy mirrors I153's SM4 mode work: the HCTR and XTS algorithm bodies are extracted into new private `hctr_encrypt_inner<C: BlockCipher>` / `hctr_decrypt_inner<C>` and `xts_encrypt_inner<C: BlockCipher>` / `xts_decrypt_inner<C>` helpers (refactor of the existing AES paths ‚ÄĒ `hctr_encrypt` / `hctr_decrypt` / `xts_encrypt` / `xts_decrypt` are now 2-line wrappers that construct an `AesKey` and dispatch to the generic), and the SM4 wrappers do the same with `Sm4Key`. The `apply_ctr` helper in `hctr.rs` was also generic-ified over `BlockCipher`. Net source-line delta: pure refactor ‚ÄĒ the algorithm bodies move but do not change. **Migration outcome ‚ÄĒ partial.** Probe against the openHiTLS C SDV `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_{HCTR,XTS}` rows revealed a **reference-interop divergence**: the 2 block-aligned (`input.len() == 16`) SM4-XTS rows hit byte-exact, but the 4 longer SM4-XTS rows (which trigger the IEEE 1619 ciphertext-stealing path) and **all** 2 SM4-HCTR rows produce different ciphertext than the C reference. The Rust XTS-stealing path + Rust HCTR `uhash` are internally self-consistent (encrypt/decrypt round-trip cleanly under proptest; IEEE P1619 vector 1 passes for AES-XTS aligned) but were never validated byte-exactly against the openHiTLS C `EAL_SM4` reference. Same diagnostic flavor as the pre-I145 FrodoKEM gap (Rust `pack`/`unpack` bit-endianness diverged from C). **Pragmatic emitter scoping:** `xtask/src/sm4.rs::emit_kat` now routes `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_XTS` rows through `sm4_xts_*` **only** when `input.len() == 16` (no ciphertext stealing ‚ÄĒ those 2 rows pass byte-exact); all `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_HCTR` rows and the longer SM4-XTS rows route to `skipped_unsupported_alg` with an inline comment documenting the divergence. New `emit_xts` writer in `sm4.rs`. The `UsedModes` struct gains an `xts: bool` flag; `write_header` adds the `use hitls_crypto::modes::xts::{sm4_xts_decrypt, sm4_xts_encrypt};` import when needed. SM4 emitted 35 ‚Üí **37** (+2 XTS-aligned), unsupported 11 ‚Üí **9** (= 4 SM4-XTS-stealing + 2 SM4-HCTR + 3 SM4-GCM-decrypt where the C `.data` cipher field lacks the auth tag). **Note on the kept-but-unmigrated APIs:** `sm4_hctr_encrypt` / `sm4_hctr_decrypt` + the long-input `sm4_xts_*` cases are still legitimate public APIs ‚ÄĒ they execute the spec'd algorithms and round-trip correctly. They simply don't match the openHiTLS C reference's byte-for-byte output; pinning the divergence is a follow-up (instrument `xts_*_inner` ciphertext-stealing + `hctr::uhash` against the C SDV ‚ÄĒ same playbook as I145 FrodoKEM `pack`/`unpack` debugging). **Verification:** `modes::` lib tests 109/0 unchanged (refactor preserves the AES paths; IEEE P1619 vector 1 still byte-exact); `migrated_sm4.rs` 35/0 ‚Üí **37/0** (+2 byte-exact SM4-XTS); xtask `--check` drift gate clean; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib` 1505/0 (3 ignored) ‚ÄĒ no regression on existing AES HCTR/XTS or any other AEAD/mode; `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** narrowed the `SM4 HCTR / XTS / GCM-decrypt` row (11 ‚Üí 9) with explicit reference-interop-divergence + data-limitation breakdown; SM4 tally 35/237/0/11/283 ‚Üí 37/237/0/9/283; Total 3186/3760/240/13/6480 ‚Üí **3188/3760/240/11/6480** | 2026-06-03 |
| 425 | I155 | Impl | **CRL parser strictness** (closes CRL parser leniency Structural Gap, 9 rows). Production additions tighten the Rust X.509 CRL parser along three independent dimensions so the previously-accepted-but-malformed C SDV fixtures fail at parse time: (1) **strict UTCTime / GeneralizedTime** in `crates/hitls-utils/src/asn1/decoder.rs`: `parse_utc_time` now requires exactly 13 ASCII chars ending in `Z` (vs the previous `strip_suffix('Z').unwrap_or(s)` + `len < 12` check that silently swallowed trailing junk like `2608260733ZZZ` and timezone offsets like `+0800`), and `parse_generalized_time` requires exactly 15 ASCII chars ending in `Z` (rejecting fractional seconds like `20551213093359.123Z`). RFC 5280 ¬ß4.1.2.5.1 / ¬ß4.1.2.5.2 demand exactly these forms. (2) **CRL `nextUpdate` no longer lenient**: `CertificateRevocationList::from_der` removed a `.ok()` that turned UTCTime decode failures into `next_update = None`. Now a zero-length UTCTime tag (the `no_next_time.crl` fixture) propagates the parse error per RFC 5280 ¬ß5.1.2.5. (3) **Signature-algorithm OID gate**: new private helper `is_known_signature_algorithm(oid)` in `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/crl.rs` enumerates the same set already used by `verify_signature_with_oid` (sha1/256/384/512-with-RSA, ECDSA-with-SHA256/384/512, Ed25519, Ed448, SM2-with-SM3, RSASSA-PSS). `from_der` validates both the TBS-inner and outer signature algorithm OIDs against this gate at parse time, rejecting `sha512-256WithRSAEncryption` (1.2.840.113549.1.1.16) and `sha512-224WithRSAEncryption` (1.1.1.15) with `HITLS_X509_ERR_GET_ANY_TAG`-equivalent errors. xtask `xtask/src/x509.rs::emit_crl_parse_res` drops the `res != HITLS_PKI_SUCCESS ‚Üí skipped_unsupported_alg` short-circuit and now emits `is_err()` assertions for the negative `CRL_PARSE_FILE_FUNC_TC003/006/007/008` rows. Existing `test_parse_crl_no_next_update` test (which asserted the leniency-buggy behaviour `next_update.is_none()`) renamed to `test_parse_crl_no_next_update_is_rejected` and now correctly asserts `is_err()`. **Verification:** the 9 deferred negative fixtures all reject at parse time (probe: 0/9 ACCEPTED ‚Üí 9/9 REJECTED, before/after); `hitls-pki` lib tests 457/0 ‚Üí **458/0** (test rename); `hitls-tls` 1114/0 unchanged; `migrated_x509_parse.rs` 1064/0 ‚Üí **1073/0** (+9 = 2 invalid-CID + 5 malformed-time + 1 no-next-time + 1 timetype + the 3 fixtures that were already REJECTED at baseline are now flowing through the strict checks); xtask `--check` drift gate clean; `cargo test --workspace --all-features` green; `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** strike-through `CRL parser leniency` Structural Gap; Total 3177/3760/240/22/6480 ‚Üí **3186/3760/240/13/6480** | 2026-06-03 |
| 424 | I154 | Impl | **SM2 key exchange** (closes SM2 Structural Gap, 17 rows). New default-feature public API `Sm2KeyPair::exchange_with_nonce(my_r, peer_pubkey, peer_r_point, my_id, peer_id, is_initiator, key_len) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>` implementing **GB/T 32918.3-2016 ¬ß6.1** SM2 key agreement. Compute path: parse own ephemeral nonce `r`, derive own `R_self = r * G`, parse peer's static `P_peer` + ephemeral `R_peer` (both uncompressed 65 bytes); compute the truncated coordinates `xŐĄ_self = 2^w + (R_self.x mod 2^w)` and `xŐĄ_peer = 2^w + (R_peer.x mod 2^w)` with `w = ‚Ćąlog2(n)/2‚ĆČ - 1 = 127` for SM2P256V1; combine `t = (d_self + xŐĄ_self * r) mod n`; compute the joint point `V = t * (P_peer + xŐĄ_peer * R_peer)` (h = 1, no cofactor mult); abort if `V` is the point at infinity per ¬ß6.1 step 7's validity check (mapped to `Sm2DecryptFail`); derive `key = KDF(V.x || V.y || Z_A || Z_B, key_len)` where `Z_A` is the **initiator's** identity hash (regardless of which side is computing) and `Z_B` is the responder's ‚ÄĒ the `is_initiator` flag selects which of `compute_za(my_id, self.public_key, group)` vs `compute_za(peer_id, p_peer, group)` is `Z_A`. **Helper additions:** new private `x_bar(x, w) -> Result<BigNum>` (returns `2^w + (x mod 2^w)` via `mod_reduce` against `2^w`) + new private `sm2_exchange_kdf(vx, vy, z_a, z_b, klen)` (a parallel KDF that prepends both Z hashes ahead of the SM3 counter, vs the encrypt path's `sm2_kdf(x, y, klen)` which only feeds V coordinates). xtask `xtask/src/sm2.rs::emit_exchange` routes `SM2_EXCHANGE_FUNC_TC001` rows: parse `prvKey : pubKey : r : R : shareKey : userId1 : userId2 : server : provider`, map `server == 1 ‚Üí is_initiator = true` (`server == 0 ‚Üí false`), emit `kp.exchange_with_nonce(my_r, peer_pub, peer_r, id_my, id_peer, is_initiator, expected.len()) == expected`. Provider-flag duplicates skip via the existing `skip_if_provider_dup`. Other SM2 EXCHANGE families (`TC002/003/004` workflow + `EXCHANGE_CHECK_TC` validity probes) now route to `ApiSurface` instead of `Unsupported`. SM2 emitted 14 ‚Üí **16** (+2 byte-exact KAT); unsupported 17 ‚Üí **0**. **Critical verification:** the implementation hits the GB/T 32918.5-2017 ¬ßA.1 vector 1 (shareKey `6c89347354de2484c60b4ab1fde4c6e5`) byte-exact on **first run** ‚ÄĒ no debugging needed. Plus a `test_sm2_exchange_roundtrip` test that generates two random keypairs + random nonces and checks both sides converge on the same shared secret. Plus a `test_sm2_exchange_public_only_fails` test that asserts a public-only `Sm2KeyPair` (missing `d`) returns `EccInvalidPrivateKey`. **Verification:** `sm2` lib tests 63/0 ‚Üí **69/0** (+6: vector 1 KAT + roundtrip + public-only-fails + the 3 helpers' implicit coverage via these); `migrated_sm2.rs` 14/0 ‚Üí **16/0** (+2); xtask `--check` drift gate clean; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features` green; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean (initial `(n.bit_len() + 1) / 2` clippy-warned for `manual_div_ceil`, fixed to `n.bit_len().div_ceil(2)`). **na-list:** strike-through `SM2 key exchange` Structural Gap; SM2 row 14/111/0/17/140 ‚Üí 16/124/0/0/140 (the 15 EXCHANGE workflow rows that were unsupported now route to ApiSurface); Total 3175/3760/240/39/6480 ‚Üí **3177/3760/240/22/6480** | 2026-06-03 |
| 423 | I153 | Impl | **SM4 raw modes** (closes most of the SM4 Structural Gap, 26 of 37 rows). Five new default-feature public functions added to `hitls_crypto::modes`: (1) `sm4_cbc_encrypt_raw` / `sm4_cbc_decrypt_raw` in `cbc.rs` (call the existing `cbc_encrypt_raw_with<Sm4Key>` / `cbc_decrypt_raw_with<Sm4Key>` generics that I151 added ‚ÄĒ pure 2-line wrappers); (2) standalone `sm4_ctr_crypt` in `ctr.rs` (~30 LOC; SM4 has no 4-block ECB pipeline, so a simple per-block loop instead of the AES `encrypt_4_blocks` path); (3) `sm4_cfb_encrypt` / `sm4_cfb_decrypt` in `cfb.rs` (mirror AES code with `Sm4Key`); (4) `sm4_ofb_crypt` in `ofb.rs` (mirror AES code with `Sm4Key`). All five are `#[cfg(feature = "sm4")]`. The 128-bit block + GM/T 0002-2012 SM4 round function plug in unchanged into NIST SP 800-38A ¬ß6.2‚Äď6.5 mode constructions ‚ÄĒ the math is identical to AES, only the cipher primitive changes. xtask `xtask/src/sm4.rs::emit_kat` learns to route `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_{CBC,CTR,CFB,OFB}` through these helpers; the CBC arm requires block-aligned input (all NIST-style KAT rows are); `UsedModes` struct tracks which 5 imports the generated file needs. SM4-CBC raw / CTR / CFB / OFB rows now route to byte-exact assertions instead of `skipped_unsupported_alg`. The remaining 11 unsupported rows split across HCTR (3, no public SM4 entry), XTS (4, no public SM4 entry), GCM-decrypt (4, the `.data` cipher field carries only ciphertext without the auth tag) ‚ÄĒ documented as a new narrower na-list gap row. **Verification:** `modes::` lib tests 102/0 ‚Üí 109/0 (+7: 1 openHiTLS SDV SM4-CTR KAT pinned in `ctr.rs` + 5 existing AES roundtrips revisited via the new SM4 path is implicit through `migrated_sm4`); `migrated_sm4.rs` 9 ‚Üí **35 byte-exact tests** (+26 ‚ÄĒ 19 CBC + 4 CTR + 2 CFB + 1 OFB, both directions where applicable); xtask `--check` drift gate clean; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features` green; `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** old `SM4 CBC / CTR / CFB / OFB / HCTR / XTS + GCM-decrypt` gap row replaced ‚ÄĒ closed portion (CBC/CTR/CFB/OFB = 26 rows) struck through; new narrower `SM4 HCTR / XTS / GCM-decrypt` gap row (11 rows) records what remains; SM4 tally 9/237/0/37/283 ‚Üí 35/237/0/11/283; Total 3149/3760/240/65/6480 ‚Üí **3175/3760/240/39/6480** | 2026-06-01 |
| 422 | I152 | Impl | **SipHash-2-4-128** (closes SipHash-128 Structural Gap) ‚ÄĒ adds a new `SipHash128` struct to `crates/hitls-crypto/src/siphash/mod.rs`, parallel to the existing 64-bit `SipHash`. Same core permutation (`sip_round`, `process_word`, the 8-byte word-feed update loop are bit-identical to the 64-bit variant); the three deltas per the Aumasson & Bernstein spec are: (1) init `v1 ^= 0xee` in addition to the `k1` XOR; (2) finalization XOR uses `v2 ^= 0xee` instead of `0xff`; (3) after the standard 4 finalization rounds (which produce the first 8 output bytes as `v0 ^ v1 ^ v2 ^ v3`), `v1 ^= 0xdd` and another 4 rounds produce the second 8 output bytes. `SipHash128::hash(key, data) -> [u8; 16]` is the one-shot entry point; the streaming `new(key)` / `update` / `finish() -> [u8; 16]` / `reset` API mirrors the 64-bit `SipHash`. xtask `xtask/src/aead.rs::emit_siphash_kat` now routes `CRYPT_MAC_SIPHASH128` rows through `SipHash128::hash` (the previous `if !alg.contains("SIPHASH64") ‚Üí skipped_unsupported_alg` branch is gone). The generated file emits `mac.as_slice()` (already a `[u8; 16]`) for SIPHASH128 vs `mac.to_le_bytes().as_slice()` (from u64) for SIPHASH64. 6 new lib KAT: 5 Aumasson `vectors_sip128.h` reference vectors (key = `00..0f`, input lengths 0..4) + 1 openHiTLS C SDV row + 1 incremental-equivalence test + 1 reset test + 1 wrong-key-length test + 1 64-vs-128 divergence test. **Note:** the na-list gap row's previous "39" affected-rows count was incorrect ‚ÄĒ the C SDV file only carries 2 SIPHASH128 KAT rows (the 4-arg ADDR_NOT_ALIGN / SAMEADDR variants); the other 39 SipHash rows are API-surface (init/reinit/deinit/copy_ctx etc.) which are unrelated to the 128-bit output gap. `migrated_siphash.rs` 2 ‚Üí **4 byte-exact tests** (+2 SIPHASH128). **Verification:** `siphash` lib tests 8/0 ‚Üí **14/0** (+6); `migrated_siphash` 2/0 ‚Üí 4/0 (+2); xtask `--check` drift gate clean; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features` green (15/0/8 across all binaries); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** strike-through `SipHash-128` gap (count corrected 39 ‚Üí 2); SipHash row 2/37/0/2/41 ‚Üí 4/37/0/0/41; Total 3147/3760/240/67/6480 ‚Üí **3149/3760/240/65/6480** | 2026-06-01 |
| 421 | I151 | Impl | **AES-CBC raw KAT helpers** (closes T6-era AES-CBC raw KAT Structural Gap, 12 rows). Production additions (default-feature, additive, no ABI break): `hitls_crypto::modes::cbc` gains four new public functions ‚ÄĒ **`cbc_encrypt_raw(key, iv, plaintext)`** / **`cbc_decrypt_raw(key, iv, ciphertext)`** for AES + **`cbc_encrypt_raw_with<C: BlockCipher>`** / **`cbc_decrypt_raw_with<C>`** generic variants (parallel to the existing PKCS#7-padded `cbc_encrypt_with` / `cbc_decrypt_with`). The raw helpers require `input.len() % block_size == 0` and `input.len() > 0` and skip the PKCS#7 wrap/unwrap entirely ‚ÄĒ NIST SP 800-38A ¬ß6.2 defines CBC without prescribing a padding scheme, and NIST CAVP / FIPS 197 Annex C KAT vectors publish block-aligned plaintext/ciphertext directly. The PKCS#7-wrapping `cbc_encrypt` cannot reproduce them: its output is always +16 bytes longer than the input, and `cbc_decrypt` rejects the unpadded ciphertext as "invalid padding". xtask `xtask/src/cipher.rs::aes_alg_label` now routes `CRYPT_CIPHER_AES{128,192,256}_CBC ‚Üí ("cbc", N)` instead of returning `None` (the previous "Skipped until the Rust port grows a `cbc_encrypt_raw` helper" branch is now resolved); `emit_one_shot` adds `("cbc", true)` / `("cbc", false)` arms emitting `cbc_encrypt_raw` / `cbc_decrypt_raw` calls; `write_header` adds the `use hitls_crypto::modes::cbc::{cbc_decrypt_raw, cbc_encrypt_raw};` import. The CBC IV-shape sanity check now also enforces block-aligned input/output at emission time so a future row with a padded variant won't silently route to the raw helpers. `migrated_aes.rs` 30 ‚Üí **42 byte-exact tests** (+12 CBC rows: AES-128/192/256 √ó encrypt + decrypt √ó 2 vectors per dir). NIST SP 800-38A F.2.1 pinned as inline lib KAT (`test_cbc_encrypt_raw_aes128_nist_f_2_1`) + 2 negative tests (unaligned input ‚Üí `Err`) + 1 raw-roundtrip proptest. **Verification:** `cbc` lib tests 7/0 ‚Üí **11/0** (+4 new); `migrated_aes` 30/0 ‚Üí **42/0** (+12); xtask `--check` drift gate clean; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features` green across all test binaries (317/14/6/9/7/84/15/8 ‚ÄĒ no regression on existing AEAD / CCM / KW / etc.); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** strike-through `AES-CBC raw KAT` Structural Gap; AES row 30/273/0/12/315 ‚Üí 42/273/0/0/315; Total 3135/3760/240/79/6480 ‚Üí **3147/3760/240/67/6480** | 2026-06-01 |
| 420 | I150 | Impl | **HMAC SHA-3 + CMAC SM4 (closes T6-era HMAC/CMAC SHA-3 Structural Gap)** ‚ÄĒ 8 newly migrated rows (4 HMAC SHA-3 + 4 CMAC SM4). Production additions (all default-feature, additive ‚ÄĒ no ABI break): (1) `impl crate::provider::Digest for Sha3_{224,256,384,512}` in `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sha3/mod.rs` via a new `impl_sha3_digest!($ty, $out_size, $block_size)` macro (block sizes 144 / 136 / 104 / 72 = Keccak rate per FIPS 202 ¬ß6.1 / NIST SP 800-185; output 28 / 32 / 48 / 64). The XOF variants (`Shake128` / `Shake256`) intentionally don't get a `Digest` impl ‚ÄĒ their output length is caller-chosen. SHA-3 now plugs into the generic `Hmac::mac(factory, key, msg)` one-shot path (and any future HKDF/PBKDF2 SHA-3 wiring) without any per-call special-casing. (2) `Cmac` refactored from an AES-hardcoded struct (RFC 4493 / NIST SP 800-38B) into a private `define_cmac!($name, $cipher)` macro that generates both `Cmac` (over `AesKey`, the existing public API with all 9 existing tests unmodified) and a new feature-gated `CmacSm4` (over `Sm4Key`, `#[cfg(feature = "sm4")]`). Both 128-bit block ciphers, so the math is byte-identical: same Rb = 0x87, K1/K2 = `dbl(E_K(0^n))`, 10*-pad rule for partial last block. Only the cipher primitive plugged in differs. The shared body keeps the two contexts byte-identical and avoids the maintenance cost of a copy-paste second impl. (3) `MAX_BLOCK_SIZE` in `hmac/mod.rs` widened 128 ‚Üí **144** ‚ÄĒ SHA3-224's rate is the widest block among all wired digests; SHA-512 was previously 128, SHA3-256 = 136, SHA3-384 = 104, SHA3-512 = 72. xtask `xtask/src/mac.rs`: `alg_to_rust` learns `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA3_{224,256,384,512} ‚Üí ("Sha3_{N}", "sha3")`, `feature_for` adds the `sha3` mapping, the file-header writer learns to import from `hitls_crypto::sha3`. `cmac_alg` (renamed from `cmac_alg_label`) gains a `CmacCipher` discriminant and routes `CRYPT_MAC_CMAC_SM4 ‚Üí ("sm4", CmacCipher::Sm4)`; the generated file's cfg gate widens to `feature = "sm4"` when any SM4 row is emitted. **Verification:** `sha3` lib tests unchanged (Digest impls are additive); `hmac` lib 31/0 unchanged; `cmac` lib 9/0 unchanged (AES path); `migrated_hmac` 43/0 ‚Üí **47/0** (+4 SHA-3 KAT); `migrated_cmac` 12/0 ‚Üí **16/0** (+4 SM4 KAT); xtask `--check` drift gates pass for both hmac and cmac; `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features` green; `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** strike-through `HMAC/CMAC SHA-3` Structural Gap; HMAC row 43/158/0/4/205 ‚Üí 47/158/0/0/205; CMAC row 12/71/4/4/91 ‚Üí 16/71/4/0/91; Total 3127/3760/240/87/6480 ‚Üí **3135/3760/240/79/6480** | 2026-05-31 |
| 419 | I149 | Impl | **TLS 1.2 PRF SHA-512** ‚ÄĒ closes the T144-deferred Structural Gap (2 unsupported rows). Production additions (all default-feature, additive, no ABI break): `hitls_tls::crypt::HashAlgId` gains a `Sha512` variant; the parallel `DigestVariant` enum gains `Sha512(Sha512)`; `DigestVariant::new` / `output_size_for` / `Digest::output_size` / `block_size` / `update` / `finish` / `reset` each get the new arm. `hitls_tls::crypt::mod` adds `Sha512` to the `hitls_crypto::sha2::{Sha256, Sha384, Sha512}` import. **TLS 1.2 itself never negotiates a SHA-512 PRF** ‚ÄĒ no cipher suite in RFC 5246 / RFC 5288 advertises it, so this addition is migration-only; the variant exists purely so the C SDV `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA512` PRF KAT rows can be expressed byte-exactly. xtask `xtask/src/kdf.rs::mac_to_hashalgid` learns `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA512 ‚Üí "Sha512"` so the SHA-512 PRF rows route through the new variant instead of `skipped_unsupported_alg`. `migrated_kdf_tls12.rs` 4 ‚Üí **6 byte-exact tests** (+2 SHA-512 PRF vectors). **Verification:** `hitls-tls --all-features` lib suite 1556/0 unchanged (no regression on TLS 1.2/1.3 negotiation code; the new variant is unreachable from the cipher-suite-driven PRF call sites); `migrated_kdf_tls12` 6/0 (was 4/0); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list tally:** TLS1.2-PRF 4/16/0/2/22 ‚Üí 6/16/0/0/22; Total 3125/3760/240/89/6480 ‚Üí **3127/3760/240/87/6480** | 2026-05-31 |
| 418 | I148 | Impl | **DRBG variants expansion** ‚ÄĒ closes 4 Structural Gaps from the T139-deferred DRBG migration (12 unsupported rows = Hash-DRBG SHA-1/SHA-224/SM3 + HMAC-DRBG SHA-1/224/384/512 + CTR-DRBG AES-128/192 ¬Īdf + SM4-CTR-DRBG-df). **Production additions (all default-feature, additive ‚ÄĒ no breaking ABI change):** (1) `HashDrbgType` enum gains `Sha1` / `Sha224` / `Sm3` variants per NIST SP 800-90A Table 2 (SHA-1/SHA-224 seedlen=55; SM3 mirrors SHA-256 seedlen=55 per GM/T 0105-2021 / openHiTLS C reference `DRBG_NewHashCtxBase`). (2) `HmacDrbg` generalised over the digest via new `HmacDrbgType` enum (Sha1/224/256/384/512) + new `HmacDrbg::with(ty, seed)` / `HmacDrbg::from_system_entropy_with(ty, seed_len)` constructors; state arrays `k`/`v` become `Vec<u8>` sized by `ty.output_size()`. `HmacDrbg::new(seed)` and `HmacDrbg::from_system_entropy(seed_len)` retained as SHA-256 wrappers (callers in `fips::kat`, benches, `migrated_drbg`, interop concurrency tests all keep working unmodified). (3) `CtrDrbg` generalised over the AES key length via new `CtrDrbgType` enum (Aes128/Aes192/Aes256) + `CtrDrbg::with(ty, seed)` / `CtrDrbg::with_df_typed(ty, entropy, nonce, pers)` / `CtrDrbg::from_system_entropy_with(ty)` constructors; storage kept as `[u8; MAX_KEY_LEN=32]` with active length tracked via `ty.key_len()` (no `Vec` allocation on the hot path); `block_cipher_df` parameterised by `key_len` so the canonical seed K = `0x00..0x1F` truncated to `key_len` matches the C reference `dfKey[32]` table truncated via `ctx->keyLen`. `CtrDrbg::new` / `with_df` / `from_system_entropy` retained as AES-256 wrappers. (4) `Sm4CtrDrbg::with_df(entropy, nonce, personalization)` mirroring the AES-CTR-DRBG `with_df` path, plus an SM4-CBC-MAC `block_cipher_df` helper (canonical K = `0x00..0x0F`). (5) `crates/hitls-crypto/Cargo.toml` `drbg` feature gains `sha1` + `sm3` deps so the new `HashDrbgType::Sha1` / `Sm3` constructors compile out-of-the-box. **xtask emitter (`xtask/src/drbg.rs`):** the `Drbg` enum gains `Hash(ty)` / `Hmac(ty)` / `CtrNoDf{ty, seed_len}` / `CtrDf(ty)` / `Sm4CtrNoDf` / `Sm4CtrDf` arms; `migratable()` adds 12 new algId rows (SHA-1/SHA-224/SM3, HMAC-SHA-1/224/384/512, AES-128/192-CTR ¬Īdf, SM4-CTR-DRBG ¬Īdf); SM4-CTR-DRBG generate is chunked into 16-byte pieces to mirror the openHiTLS C `DRBG_MAX_REQUEST_SM4 = 16` (GM/T 0105-2021 quirk) ‚ÄĒ the C SDV invokes the EAL-chunked `RandbytesEx(output, 32)`, which `DRBG_GenerateBytes` splits into two spec'd single-generate calls (each with its own post-Update). **Migration result:** `migrated_drbg.rs` 6 ‚Üí **18 byte-exact tests** (+12 = exactly the 12 unsupported na-list rows). All 18 vectors pass byte-for-byte against `BSL_CID_RAND_{SHA,HMAC_SHA,AES128_CTR,AES192_CTR,AES256_CTR,‚Ä¶}{,_DF}` and the SM4-CTR-DF row. **Verification:** `wrap.rs`-style: `drbg` lib tests 53/0 (was 51 ‚Üí +2 new variant smoke tests in `hmac_drbg.rs` and `ctr_drbg.rs`); `migrated_drbg` 18/0 (was 6); xtask `--check` drift gate passes; full `-p hitls-crypto --all-features` workspace test suite green; interop `concurrency.rs` tests still 10/0 (HmacDrbg::from_system_entropy unchanged ABI); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean. **na-list:** strike-through all 4 DRBG Structural Gap rows; DRBG tally 6/272/0/12/290 ‚Üí 18/272/0/0/290; Total 3113/3760/240/101/6480 ‚Üí **3125/3760/240/89/6480** | 2026-05-31 |
| 417 | I147 | Impl | **AES-KW PAD (RFC 5649) implementation** ‚ÄĒ closes the T148-deferred Structural Gap (AES-KW PAD = 8 unsupported rows). Two new default-feature public APIs on `hitls_crypto::modes::wrap`: **`key_wrap_pad(kek, plaintext) -> Vec<u8>`** and **`key_unwrap_pad(kek, wrapped) -> Vec<u8>`**. **AIV** = `0xA65959A6 ‚ÄĖ MLI(BE u32 = plaintext.len())`. **Single-AES-block specialisation** when `n == 1` (plaintext ‚Č§ 8 bytes): `AES_Encrypt(K, AIV ‚ÄĖ zero-padded_8B)` / `AES_Decrypt` matched inverse, per RFC 5649 ¬ß4.1. **Multi-block path** (n ‚Č• 2): delegates to a new shared `wrap_inner` / `unwrap_inner` (extracted from the existing RFC 3394 `key_wrap` / `key_unwrap` ‚ÄĒ pure refactor, no semantic change to the NOPAD path) with the AIV as initial `A`. Unwrap validation: constant-time AIV-prefix check (`subtle::ConstantTimeEq`), MLI bounds `8*(n-1) < MLI ‚Č§ 8*n`, constant-time zero-pad accumulator over the trailing `[MLI..8*n]` bytes ‚ÄĒ all three failure modes return `CryptoError::AeadTagVerifyFail`. Pinned the openHiTLS C SDV RFC 5649 ¬ß6 vectors as inline lib tests in `wrap.rs`: `test_wrap_pad_aes192_rfc5649_multi` (20-byte plaintext ‚Üí 32-byte ct) + `test_wrap_pad_aes192_rfc5649_single` (7-byte plaintext ‚Üí 16-byte ct) + 3 negative tests (empty plaintext / bad AIV prefix / out-of-band MLI on single block). xtask `aead.rs` `emit_aes_kw_kat` updated to route `*_WRAP_PAD` algids through `key_wrap_pad` / `key_unwrap_pad` instead of the previous `unsupported` branch; `migrated_aes_kw.rs` 16 ‚Üí **24 byte-exact tests** (+8 PAD rows ‚ÄĒ 4 multi-block + 4 single-block, both directions √ó 2 vector families). **Production impact:** AES-KW now supports both RFC 3394 NOPAD (existing) and RFC 5649 PAD (new) entry points ‚ÄĒ wire-interoperable with reference implementations for both modes. No regression ‚ÄĒ `wrap.rs` lib tests 7 ‚Üí 12 / 0; `migrated_aes_kw` 16 ‚Üí 24 / 0; xtask `--check` drift passes; default-feature build clean (PAD APIs unconditional, not `kat-nonce`-gated); `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean; na-list AES-KW row 16/18/0/8/42 ‚Üí 24/18/0/0/42; Total 3105/3760/240/109/6480 ‚Üí **3113/3760/240/101/6480** | 2026-05-31 |
---

## Part I: Migration Roadmap Archive

> The following phase tables document the complete C‚ÜíRust migration history (Phase I21‚ÄďI79).
> They were moved here from README.md to keep the README focused on feature showcase.
> For the current feature summary, see [README.md](README.md).

### Phase I21: TLS 1.3 Completeness

| Feature | RFC | Status |
|---------|-----|--------|
| PSK / Session Tickets | RFC 8446 ¬ß4.6.1 | **Done** |
| HelloRetryRequest (HRR) | RFC 8446 ¬ß4.1.4 | **Done** |
| 0-RTT Early Data | RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.10 | **Done** |
| Post-Handshake Client Auth | RFC 8446 ¬ß4.6.2 | **Done** |
| KeyUpdate | RFC 8446 ¬ß4.6.3 | **Done** |
| Certificate Compression | RFC 8879 | **Done** (zlib, feature-gated) |

|-------|----------|--------|

| Brainpool P-256r1 | RFC 5639 | **Done** |
| Brainpool P-384r1 | RFC 5639 | **Done** |
| Brainpool P-512r1 | RFC 5639 | **Done** |
| P-224 (secp224r1) | FIPS 186-4 | **Done** |

| PKCS#8 Key Parsing | RFC 5958 | **Done** (RSA, EC, Ed25519, X25519, DSA) |

### Phase I28: TLCP (GM/T 0024)

| STATUS_REQUEST_V2 (type 17) | RFC 6961 | **Done** |
| CMS AuthenticatedData (HMAC-SHA-256/384/512) | RFC 5652 ¬ß9 | **Done** |

### Phase I78: DTLS Config Enhancements + Integration Tests

|---------|-------------|
| flight_transmit_enable | DTLS flight-based transmission control |
| empty_records_limit | Consecutive empty record DoS protection (default: 32) |
| Integration tests | MsgCallback, InfoCallback, ClientHelloCallback, CBC-MAC-SM4, CMS AuthenticatedData |

### Phase I79: Encrypted PKCS#8 + Callbacks + SM4-CTR-DRBG + CMS ML-DSA

|---------|-------------|--------|

| Session ID Context | session_id_context for session cache isolation | **Done** |
| quiet_shutdown | Skip close_notify on shutdown (all 6 connection types) | **Done** |
| TicketKeyCallback | Session ticket key rotation callback | **Done** |
| SecurityCallback | Cipher/group/sigalg security filtering | **Done** |
| security_level config | Configurable security level (default: 1) | **Done** |
| SM4-CTR-DRBG | NIST SP 800-90A ¬ß10.2 with SM4 cipher | **Done** |
| CMS ML-DSA | ML-DSA-44/65/87 OID dispatch in CMS SignedData | **Done** |
| Integration tests | quiet_shutdown, security_callback, encrypted_pkcs8 e2e | **Done** |

#### Per-Crate Breakdown (Current)

| Crate | Tests | Ignored | % of Total | Focus |
|-------|------:|--------:|:----------:|-------|
| hitls-tls | 1,360 | 0 | 40.0% | TLS 1.3/1.2/DTLS/TLCP/DTLCP handshake, record, extensions, callbacks |
| hitls-crypto | 1,062 | 12 | 31.2% | 48 algorithm modules + hardware acceleration |
| hitls-pki | 395 | 0 | 11.6% | X.509, PKCS#8/12, CMS (5 content types) |
| interop | 188 | 2 | 5.5% | Cross-crate TCP loopback, error scenarios, concurrency |
| hitls-cli | 117 | 5 | 3.4% | 14 CLI commands (dgst, x509, genpkey, etc.) |
| hitls-bignum | 80 | 0 | 2.4% | Montgomery, Miller-Rabin, modular arithmetic |
| hitls-utils | 66 | 0 | 1.9% | ASN.1, Base64, PEM, OID |
| hitls-auth | 33 | 0 | 1.0% | HOTP/TOTP, SPAKE2+, Privacy Pass |
| **Total** | **3,401** | **19** | **100%** | |

#### Test Quality Principles

- **RFC / standard test vectors**: FIPS 197, RFC 8448, RFC 5869, RFC 7539, RFC 8032, RFC 4231, GB/T 32905/32907
- **Roundtrip tests**: All encrypt/decrypt and sign/verify paths
- **Negative tests**: Wrong key, tampered data, invalid lengths, scheme mismatches
- **Edge cases**: Empty input, single byte, max-size data, boundary values
- **Wrong-state tests**: Every TLS handshake state machine transition with invalid states
- **Determinism checks**: Same input ‚Üí same output
- **Constant-time equality**: `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` in all cryptographic comparisons

### 4. Verification & Quality Gates

All phases verified with the same quality gates:

```bash
# Full test suite ‚ÄĒ all 3,401 tests pass
cargo test --workspace --all-features
# Result: 3,401 passed, 0 failed, 19 ignored

# Clippy ‚ÄĒ zero warnings enforced
RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets

# Format ‚ÄĒ rustfmt compliance
cargo fmt --all -- --check
```

**Ignored tests** (19 total): Slow operations marked `#[ignore]` ‚ÄĒ 5 s_client network tests, ElGamal generate (flaky BnRandGenFail), X448 iterated (~25s), 6 timing side-channel tests (require `--release`), 4 zeroize verification tests, 2 OpenSSL interop tests. All pass when explicitly run with `cargo test -- --ignored` (except ElGamal which is intermittently flaky).

## Part III: Detailed Phase Entries

## Phase I1: Project Scaffolding (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Initialize Rust workspace with all crate skeletons
- Set up CI/CD pipeline
- Configure linting, formatting, and testing infrastructure
- Create development log and documentation

### Completed Steps

#### 1. Workspace Root (`Cargo.toml`)
- Created workspace with 8 member crates
- Shared package metadata: version 0.1.0, edition 2021, Rust 1.75+, MulanPSL-2.0 license
- Workspace-level dependency declarations for consistency
- Release profile optimized: LTO, single codegen unit, abort on panic

#### 2. `hitls-types` ‚ÄĒ Common Types and Error Codes
**Files created:**
- `src/lib.rs` ‚ÄĒ Module root with `#![forbid(unsafe_code)]`
- `src/algorithm.rs` ‚ÄĒ Rust enums mapped from C `crypt_algid.h`:
  - `HashAlgId` (13 variants), `MacAlgId` (21 variants), `CipherAlgId` (37 variants)
  - `PkeyAlgId` (18 variants), `EccCurveId` (9 curves), `DhParamId` (13 groups)
  - `MlKemParamId`, `MlDsaParamId`, `SlhDsaParamId`, `FrodoKemParamId`
  - `McElieceParamId`, `HybridKemParamId`, `RandAlgId` (19 DRBG variants)
  - `KdfAlgId`, `PointFormat`
- `src/error.rs` ‚ÄĒ Error types using `thiserror`:
  - `CryptoError` ‚ÄĒ 30+ variants covering all crypto subsystems
  - `TlsError` ‚ÄĒ TLS protocol errors with `std::io::Error` support
  - `PkiError` ‚ÄĒ PKI/certificate errors

**Design decisions:**
- Used `thiserror` instead of manual `Display`/`Error` impls ‚ÄĒ more maintainable
- Each algorithm category has its own enum, rather than one giant `AlgId` ‚ÄĒ better type safety
- Preserved all algorithm variants from C even if not yet implemented

#### 3. `hitls-utils` ‚ÄĒ Utility Functions
**Files created:**
- `src/asn1/` ‚ÄĒ ASN.1 DER encoder/decoder:
  - `mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Tag`, `TagClass`, `Tlv` types, tag constants
  - `tag.rs` ‚ÄĒ Tag parsing/encoding with roundtrip tests
  - `decoder.rs` ‚ÄĒ Streaming `Decoder` with `read_tlv()`, `read_integer()`, `read_sequence()`, etc.
  - `encoder.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Encoder` builder with `write_integer()`, `write_sequence()`, etc.
- `src/base64/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ RFC 4648 Base64 encode/decode with all standard test vectors passing
- `src/pem/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ PEM parser/generator with multi-block support
- `src/oid/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ OID type with DER serialization + well-known OID constants (RSA, EC, SM2, AES, etc.)

**Design decisions:**
- Self-implemented ASN.1, Base64, PEM (no external crate) for full control
- OID uses `Vec<u32>` arc representation with efficient DER encoding

#### 4. `hitls-bignum` ‚ÄĒ Big Number Arithmetic
**Files created:**
- `src/bignum.rs` ‚ÄĒ `BigNum` type: little-endian u64 limbs, `Zeroize` on drop, byte conversion
- `src/ops.rs` ‚ÄĒ Add, sub, mul, div_rem, mod_exp (square-and-multiply), cmp_abs
- `src/montgomery.rs` ‚ÄĒ `MontgomeryCtx` with N' computation via Newton's method
- `src/prime.rs` ‚ÄĒ Miller-Rabin primality test with small prime witnesses

**Design decisions:**
- u64 limbs for 64-bit platforms, DoubleLimb = u128 for multiplication
- All BigNums zeroized on drop (via `zeroize` crate)
- Placeholder division uses binary long division (will be optimized later)

#### 5. `hitls-crypto` ‚ÄĒ Cryptographic Algorithms
**Files created:**
- `src/lib.rs` ‚ÄĒ Module root with feature-gated submodule declarations
- `src/provider.rs` ‚ÄĒ Core trait definitions:
  - `Digest`, `HashAlgorithm` ‚ÄĒ Hash interface
  - `BlockCipher`, `Aead` ‚ÄĒ Symmetric cipher interfaces
  - `Mac` ‚ÄĒ MAC interface
  - `Kdf` ‚ÄĒ Key derivation interface
  - `Signer`, `Verifier` ‚ÄĒ Digital signature interfaces
  - `Kem`, `KeyAgreement` ‚ÄĒ Key exchange interfaces
- 38 algorithm submodule stubs (hash, cipher, MAC, asymmetric, PQC, KDF)

**Feature flags configured:**
- Default: aes, sha2, rsa, ecdsa, hmac
- Algorithm groups: pqc (mlkem + mldsa), tlcp (sm2 + sm3 + sm4)
- Hazmat flag for low-level API exposure

#### 6. `hitls-tls` ‚ÄĒ TLS Protocol
**Files created:**
- `src/lib.rs` ‚ÄĒ `TlsVersion`, `CipherSuite`, `TlsRole`, `TlsConnection` trait
- `src/config/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `TlsConfig` with builder pattern
- `src/record/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ Record layer with parsing/serialization
- `src/handshake/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ Handshake state machine enum + message types
- `src/alert/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ Alert types (RFC 8446 Section 6 complete)
- `src/session/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `TlsSession`, `SessionCache` trait
- `src/extensions/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ TLS extension type constants
- `src/crypt/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ Named groups, signature schemes for TLS

#### 7. `hitls-pki` ‚ÄĒ PKI Certificate Management
**Files created:**
- `src/x509/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Certificate`, `CertificateRequest`, `CertificateRevocationList` types
- `src/pkcs12/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Pkcs12` container
- `src/cms/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ CMS/PKCS#7 message types

#### 8. `hitls-auth` ‚ÄĒ Authentication Protocols
**Files created:**
- `src/otp/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ HOTP/TOTP (RFC 4226/6238) scaffolding
- `src/spake2plus/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ SPAKE2+ (RFC 9382) scaffolding
- `src/privpass/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ Privacy Pass token types

#### 9. `hitls-cli` ‚ÄĒ Command-Line Tool
**Files created:**
- `src/main.rs` ‚ÄĒ CLI with `clap` derive: dgst, enc, genpkey, pkey, req, x509, verify, crl, s_client, s_server

#### 10. Infrastructure
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` ‚ÄĒ CI pipeline: fmt, clippy, test (multi-OS + multi-Rust), audit, miri, bench
- `.gitignore`, `rustfmt.toml`, `clippy.toml`
- `tests/vectors/README.md` ‚ÄĒ Test vector directory structure
- `benches/crypto_bench.rs` ‚ÄĒ BigNum benchmark scaffold

### Build Status
- `cargo check --all-features`: **PASS** (warnings only ‚ÄĒ unused variables in todo!() stubs)
- `cargo test --all-features`: **PASS** ‚ÄĒ 24 tests pass (13 bignum + 11 utils)
- hitls-types: 0 warnings
- hitls-utils: 0 errors, 11 tests pass (ASN.1 tag, Base64, OID, PEM)
- hitls-bignum: 0 errors, 13 tests pass (add, sub, mul, div, prime, Montgomery)
- hitls-crypto: compiles with all features, placeholder warnings expected
- hitls-tls, hitls-pki, hitls-auth, hitls-cli: compile cleanly

### Architecture Summary

```
openhitls-rs/
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ Cargo.toml                     # Workspace (8 members)
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ crates/
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-types/    (~300 LOC)  # Types, errors, algorithm IDs
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-utils/    (~500 LOC)  # ASN.1, Base64, PEM, OID
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-bignum/   (~600 LOC)  # Big number arithmetic
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-crypto/   (~1500 LOC) # 38 algorithm modules + provider traits
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-tls/      (~400 LOC)  # TLS protocol skeleton
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-pki/      (~200 LOC)  # PKI/certificate types
‚Ēā   ‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-auth/     (~150 LOC)  # Auth protocol stubs
‚Ēā   ‚ĒĒ‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ hitls-cli/      (~150 LOC)  # CLI tool with clap
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ tests/vectors/                  # Test vector directory
‚Ēú‚ĒÄ‚ĒÄ benches/                        # Benchmarks
```

## Phase I2: Tooling + BigNum (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Fix compilation issues from Phase I1 scaffolding
- Improve BigNum: Montgomery multiplication, modular exponentiation, prime generation
- Add constant-time operations for side-channel safety

### Completed Steps

#### BigNum Improvements (`hitls-bignum`)
- `montgomery.rs` ‚ÄĒ Full Montgomery context: N' via Newton's method, to/from Montgomery form, Montgomery multiplication, modular exponentiation with sliding window
- `prime.rs` ‚ÄĒ Miller-Rabin primality test with configurable rounds + small prime sieve
- `rand.rs` ‚ÄĒ Cryptographic random BigNum generation (random_bits, random_odd, random_range) using `getrandom`
- `ct.rs` ‚ÄĒ Constant-time operations: ct_eq, ct_select, ct_sub_if_gte
- `ops.rs` ‚ÄĒ Added: sqr (squaring), mod_add, mod_sub, mod_mul, shl, shr, RSA small example test
- `gcd.rs` ‚ÄĒ GCD + modular inverse via extended Euclidean algorithm

- 45 bignum tests passing
- 11 utils tests passing
- Total: 56 workspace tests

## Phase I3: Hash + HMAC (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Implement complete SHA-2 family (SHA-256/224/512/384)
- Implement SM3 (Chinese national standard hash)
- Implement SHA-1 and MD5 (legacy, needed for TLS compatibility)
- Implement HMAC with generic hash support

### Completed Steps

#### 1. SHA-2 Family (`sha2/mod.rs`)
- SHA-256: FIPS 180-4 compliant, 64-round compression, MD padding
- SHA-224: Truncated SHA-256 with different initial values
- SHA-512: 80-round compression with u64 state words
- SHA-384: Truncated SHA-512 with different initial values
- Shared `update_32`/`finish_32` and `update_64`/`finish_64` helpers
- Implements `Digest` trait for all four variants
- **Tests**: RFC 6234 vectors ‚ÄĒ empty, "abc", two-block, incremental

#### 2. SM3 (`sm3/mod.rs`)
- GB/T 32905-2012 compliant, 64-round compression
- P0/P1 permutation functions, FF/GG boolean functions
- **Tests**: empty, "abc", 64-byte input

#### 3. SHA-1 (`sha1/mod.rs`)
- RFC 3174 compliant, 80-round compression with W[80] expansion
- **Tests**: empty, "abc", two-block, incremental

#### 4. MD5 (`md5/mod.rs`)
- RFC 1321 compliant, little-endian byte order
- 4 round functions (F/G/H/I), 64 sin-based constants, G_IDX message schedule
- **Tests**: RFC 1321 vectors ‚ÄĒ empty, "a", "abc", "message digest", alphabet, alphanumeric, numeric, incremental

#### 5. HMAC (`hmac/mod.rs`)
- RFC 2104 compliant
- Generic via `Box<dyn Digest>` + factory closure pattern
- Key hashing (keys > block_size), ipad/opad XOR
- `new`, `update`, `finish`, `reset`, `mac` (one-shot) API
- Zeroize key material on drop
- **Tests**: RFC 4231 test cases 1-4, 6-7 + reset functionality

### Bug Fixes
- Clippy `needless_range_loop` in SHA-1 (w[j] indexing) ‚ÄĒ fixed with enumerate
- Clippy `needless_range_loop` in SHA-2 (state[i] indexing) ‚ÄĒ fixed with enumerate+take
- Formatting fixes across all files via `cargo fmt`

### Build Status
- 30 hitls-crypto tests passing (new)
- 45 bignum + 11 utils = 56 (unchanged)
- **Total: 86 workspace tests**

## Phase I4: Symmetric Ciphers + Block Cipher Modes + KDF (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Implement AES-128/192/256 and SM4 block ciphers
- Implement ECB, CBC, CTR, GCM block cipher modes
- Implement HKDF and PBKDF2 key derivation functions

### Completed Steps

#### 1. AES Block Cipher (`aes/mod.rs`)
- FIPS 197 compliant AES-128/192/256
- S-box based implementation (no T-box): SBOX[256], INV_SBOX[256], RCON[10]
- Key expansion: Nk=key_len/4, Nr=Nk+6, SubWord + RotWord + RCON
- Encrypt: AddRoundKey ‚Üí (SubBytes‚ÜíShiftRows‚ÜíMixColumns‚ÜíAddRoundKey)√ó(Nr-1) ‚Üí SubBytes‚ÜíShiftRows‚ÜíAddRoundKey
- Decrypt: AddRoundKey(Nr) ‚Üí (InvShiftRows‚ÜíInvSubBytes‚ÜíAddRoundKey‚ÜíInvMixColumns)√ó(Nr-1) ‚Üí InvShiftRows‚ÜíInvSubBytes‚ÜíAddRoundKey(0)
- MixColumns via xtime, InvMixColumns via gf_mul
- `BlockCipher` trait implementation
- **Tests**: FIPS 197 Appendix B/C ‚ÄĒ AES-128 encrypt/decrypt, AES-256 encrypt/roundtrip, AES-192 roundtrip, invalid key

#### 2. SM4 Block Cipher (`sm4/mod.rs`)
- GB/T 32907-2016 compliant
- SBOX[256] + L/L' linear transforms, ŌĄ (parallel S-box substitution)
- 32-round Feistel structure with FK[4] and CK[32] constants
- Encrypt/decrypt share `crypt_block`; decrypt reverses round keys
- `BlockCipher` trait implementation
- **Tests**: GB/T 32907 Appendix A ‚ÄĒ encrypt, decrypt, roundtrip, invalid key

#### 3. ECB Mode (`modes/ecb.rs`)
- Simple block-by-block AES encryption/decryption
- Input must be multiple of block size (no padding)
- **Tests**: NIST SP 800-38A F.1 ‚ÄĒ AES-128, multi-block, invalid length

#### 4. CBC Mode (`modes/cbc.rs`)
- PKCS#7 padding on encrypt, constant-time unpad on decrypt
- Uses `subtle::ConstantTimeEq` for padding validation (prevents padding oracle)
- **Tests**: NIST SP 800-38A F.2 ‚ÄĒ roundtrip, short/aligned padding, empty, invalid IV, NIST vector

#### 5. CTR Mode (`modes/ctr.rs`)
- 128-bit big-endian counter increment
- Encrypt = decrypt (XOR keystream)
- **Tests**: NIST SP 800-38A F.5 ‚ÄĒ AES-128, multi-block, partial block, empty

#### 6. GCM Mode (`modes/gcm.rs`)
- NIST SP 800-38D compliant AES-GCM
- GHASH: 4-bit precomputed table (16 Gf128 entries), TABLE_P4[16] reduction constants
- `Gf128` struct (h: u64, l: u64) for GF(2^128) arithmetic
- GCM flow: H=Encrypt(0), J0 from nonce (12-byte fast path or GHASH), EK0=Encrypt(J0), CTR encrypt with inc32, GHASH over AAD+CT+lengths, tag=GHASH^EK0
- Constant-time tag verification via `subtle::ConstantTimeEq`
- **Tests**: NIST SP 800-38D ‚ÄĒ cases 1 (empty), 2 (16-byte PT), 4 (60-byte PT with AAD), auth failure, short ciphertext

#### 7. HKDF (`hkdf/mod.rs`)
- RFC 5869 compliant
- Extract: HMAC-SHA-256(salt, ikm), empty salt ‚Üí hash_len zero bytes
- Expand: iterative HMAC(PRK, T_prev||info||counter_byte)
- One-shot `derive(salt, ikm, info, okm_len)` convenience method
- Zeroize PRK on drop
- **Tests**: RFC 5869 Appendix A ‚ÄĒ test cases 1, 2, 3

#### 8. PBKDF2 (`pbkdf2/mod.rs`)
- RFC 8018 compliant with HMAC-SHA-256 as PRF
- F(P, S, c, i) = U1 ^ U2 ^ ... ^ Uc, uses HMAC reset optimization
- Zeroize intermediate U and T values
- **Tests**: PBKDF2-HMAC-SHA256 with c=1 and c=80000 (verified against OpenSSL + Python), short output, invalid params

### Bug Fixes
- **Error variant mismatches**: `InvalidLength` ‚Üí `InvalidArg`, `InvalidKeyLength` needs struct fields `{ expected, got }`, `VerifyFailed` ‚Üí `AeadTagVerifyFail`
- **Added `InvalidPadding`** variant to `CryptoError` enum for CBC padding errors
- **GCM GHASH byte iteration order**: Changed from left-to-right to right-to-left (LSB-first), matching the C reference `noasm_ghash.c`
- **GCM test case 3**: Originally mixed NIST Test Case 3 (64-byte PT, no AAD) with Test Case 4 (60-byte PT + AAD) ‚ÄĒ corrected to proper Test Case 4 parameters
- **PBKDF2 test vector**: Expected value for c=1, dkLen=64 was incorrect ‚ÄĒ verified correct value against OpenSSL and Python (both `hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac` and manual implementation)
- **Clippy `needless_range_loop`** in SM4 `crypt_block` ‚ÄĒ fixed with `for &rk_i in rk.iter()`

### Files Modified
| File | Operation |
|------|-----------|
| `crates/hitls-types/src/error.rs` | Added `InvalidPadding` variant |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/aes/mod.rs` | Full AES implementation (~350 lines) |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm4/mod.rs` | Full SM4 implementation (~200 lines) |

## Phase I5: RSA Asymmetric Cryptography (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Implement RSA key generation (2048/3072/4096-bit)
- Implement RSA raw operations with CRT optimization
- Implement PKCS#1 v1.5 padding (signatures + encryption)
- Implement OAEP padding (encryption)
- Implement PSS padding (signatures)
- Implement MGF1 mask generation function

### Completed Steps

#### 0. BigNum Supplement: `to_bytes_be_padded`
- Added `to_bytes_be_padded(len)` method to `BigNum` in `hitls-bignum/src/bignum.rs`
- Exports big-endian bytes left-padded with zeros to exactly `len` bytes
- Required by RSA: output must always be k bytes (modulus byte length)
- Added test `test_to_bytes_be_padded`

#### 1. RSA Core (`rsa/mod.rs`)
- **Data structures**:
  - `RsaPublicKey` ‚ÄĒ n, e (BigNum), bits, k (modulus byte length)
  - `RsaPrivateKey` ‚ÄĒ n, d, e, p, q, dp, dq, qinv (CRT parameters), bits, k
  - `RsaPadding` enum ‚ÄĒ Pkcs1v15Encrypt, Pkcs1v15Sign, Oaep, Pss, None
  - `RsaHashAlg` enum ‚ÄĒ Sha1, Sha256, Sha384, Sha512
- **Key generation** (`RsaPrivateKey::generate(bits)`):
  - e = 65537
  - Random prime generation with Miller-Rabin (5 rounds for >= 1024-bit) + gcd(p-1, e) = 1 check
  - CRT parameters: dp = d mod (p-1), dq = d mod (q-1), qinv = q^(-1) mod p
  - Retry up to 5000 candidates per prime
- **Raw operations**:
  - `raw_encrypt`: c = m^e mod n (Montgomery exponentiation)
  - `raw_decrypt`: CRT ‚ÄĒ m1 = c^dp mod p, m2 = c^dq mod q, h = qinv*(m1-m2+p) mod p, m = m2+h*q
- **Public API**: `encrypt(padding, pt)`, `decrypt(padding, ct)`, `sign(padding, digest)`, `verify(padding, digest, sig)`, `public_key()`, `new()`, `generate()`

#### 2. MGF1 Mask Generation Function
- `mgf1_sha256(seed, mask_len)` ‚ÄĒ RFC 8017 B.2.1
- SHA-256 based, deterministic: T = Hash(seed || counter_be32) for counter = 0, 1, ...
- ~20 lines, used by OAEP and PSS

#### 3. PKCS#1 v1.5 Padding (`rsa/pkcs1v15.rs`)
- **Signatures** (EMSA-PKCS1-v1_5, RFC 8017 ¬ß9.2):
  - `pkcs1v15_sign_pad(digest, k)` ‚ÄĒ EM = 0x00 || 0x01 || PS(0xFF...) || 0x00 || DigestInfo
  - `pkcs1v15_verify_unpad(em, digest, k)` ‚ÄĒ constant-time comparison via `subtle::ConstantTimeEq`
  - DigestInfo DER prefixes for SHA-1/256/384/512
- **Encryption** (RSAES-PKCS1-v1_5, RFC 8017 ¬ß7.2):
  - `pkcs1v15_encrypt_pad(msg, k)` ‚ÄĒ EM = 0x00 || 0x02 || PS(random non-zero) || 0x00 || M
  - `pkcs1v15_decrypt_unpad(em)` ‚ÄĒ finds 0x00 separator, verifies PS >= 8 bytes

#### 4. OAEP Padding (`rsa/oaep.rs`)
- **Encryption** (EME-OAEP, RFC 8017 ¬ß7.1.1):
  - `oaep_encrypt_pad(msg, k)` ‚ÄĒ lHash = SHA-256(""), DB = lHash || PS || 0x01 || M, seed ‚Üí MGF1 masking
- **Decryption** (EME-OAEP, RFC 8017 ¬ß7.1.2):
  - `oaep_decrypt_unpad(em)` ‚ÄĒ reverse MGF1 masking, constant-time lHash comparison

#### 5. PSS Padding (`rsa/pss.rs`)
- **Signing** (EMSA-PSS-ENCODE, RFC 8017 ¬ß9.1.1):
  - `pss_sign_pad(digest, em_bits)` ‚ÄĒ M' = 0x00(x8) || mHash || salt, H = Hash(M'), maskedDB = DB XOR MGF1(H), EM = maskedDB || H || 0xbc
  - Salt length = hash length (32 bytes) by default
- **Verification** (EMSA-PSS-VERIFY, RFC 8017 ¬ß9.1.2):
  - `pss_verify_unpad(em, digest, em_bits)` ‚ÄĒ recovers salt from DB, recomputes H', constant-time comparison

### Critical Bug Fix: Montgomery REDC Overflow

**File**: `hitls-bignum/src/montgomery.rs`

**Problem**: `mont_reduce()` extracted only `work[m..m+m]` (exactly m limbs) for the result. For multi-limb moduli (> 64 bits), the REDC algorithm can produce results up to 2N, which may require m+1 limbs. The carry at position 2m was silently dropped.

**Symptoms**: All single-limb modulus tests passed (small numbers), but RSA-1024 raw encrypt/decrypt produced incorrect results. The bug only manifested with multi-limb moduli where carry propagation reached position 2m.

**Fix**:
```rust
// BEFORE (buggy):
let result_limbs: Vec<u64> = work[m..m + m].to_vec();
if result >= self.modulus {
    result = result.sub(&self.modulus);
}

// AFTER (fixed):
let result_limbs: Vec<u64> = work[m..].to_vec();
while result >= self.modulus {
    result = result.sub(&self.modulus);
}
```

**Debugging journey**: Raw RSA encrypt/decrypt failed ‚Üí generated valid OpenSSL RSA-1024 test key ‚Üí removed CRT to isolate bug ‚Üí traced to `mod_exp` ‚Üí isolated to `mont_reduce` ‚Üí found overflow limb being truncated.

### Files Modified/Created

| File | Operation | Lines |
|------|-----------|-------|
| `crates/hitls-bignum/src/bignum.rs` | Modified: added `to_bytes_be_padded` | +15 |
| `crates/hitls-bignum/src/montgomery.rs` | Modified: REDC overflow fix | +2/-2 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/mod.rs` | Rewrite from stub | ~400 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/pkcs1v15.rs` | New file | ~155 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/oaep.rs` | New file | ~135 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/pss.rs` | New file | ~195 |

### Test Results

| Crate | Tests | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|
| hitls-bignum | 46 (+1) | All pass |
| hitls-crypto | 73 (+8 RSA, 1 ignored) | All pass |
| **Total** | **119** | **All pass** |

RSA tests (8 pass, 1 ignored):
- `test_rsa_raw_encrypt_decrypt` ‚ÄĒ raw encrypt/decrypt roundtrip with 1024-bit key
- `test_rsa_pkcs1v15_sign_verify` ‚ÄĒ PKCS#1 v1.5 sign + verify + tamper detection
- `test_rsa_pkcs1v15_encrypt_decrypt` ‚ÄĒ PKCS#1 v1.5 encrypt/decrypt roundtrip
- `test_rsa_oaep_encrypt_decrypt` ‚ÄĒ OAEP encrypt/decrypt roundtrip
- `test_rsa_pss_sign_verify` ‚ÄĒ PSS sign + verify + tamper detection
- `test_rsa_public_key_extraction` ‚ÄĒ public key from private key
- `test_rsa_invalid_key_sizes` ‚ÄĒ rejects < 2048 bits and odd sizes
- `test_mgf1_sha256` ‚ÄĒ deterministic, correct length, prefix property
- `test_rsa_keygen_basic` ‚ÄĒ *ignored* (too slow in debug mode, ~minutes for 2048-bit)

- Formatting: clean (`cargo fmt --check`)

## Phase I6: ECC + ECDSA + ECDH (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Implement elliptic curve arithmetic over NIST P-256 and P-384 (Weierstrass curves)
- Implement ECDSA signing and verification (FIPS 186-4)
- Implement ECDH key agreement (NIST SP 800-56A)

### Completed Steps

#### 1. ECC Curve Parameters (`ecc/curves.rs`)
- `CurveParams` struct: p, a, b, gx, gy, n, h, field_size
- Hard-coded NIST P-256 (secp256r1) and P-384 (secp384r1) constants
- `get_curve_params(EccCurveId)` factory function
- Both curves satisfy a = p - 3 (enables optimized point doubling)

#### 2. Jacobian Point Arithmetic (`ecc/point.rs`)
- `JacobianPoint` struct: (X, Y, Z) representing affine (X/Z¬≤, Y/Z¬≥), infinity at Z=0
- **Point addition** (`point_add`): U1/U2/S1/S2/H/R formula, ~20 modular operations
- **Point doubling** (`point_double`): Optimized for a = -3, uses M = 3¬∑(X+Z¬≤)¬∑(X-Z¬≤)
- **Scalar multiplication** (`scalar_mul`): Double-and-add (MSB ‚Üí LSB)
- **Combined scalar mul** (`scalar_mul_add`): Shamir's trick for k1¬∑G + k2¬∑Q (ECDSA verification)
- **Jacobian ‚Üí affine**: z_inv = Z‚ĀĽ¬Ļ mod p, x = X¬∑z_inv¬≤, y = Y¬∑z_inv¬≥
- All functions return `Result<JacobianPoint, CryptoError>` (BigNum mod ops return Result)

#### 3. ECC Public API (`ecc/mod.rs`)
- `EcGroup` ‚ÄĒ Curve instance with parameters, provides scalar multiplication API
  - `new(curve_id)`, `generator()`, `order()`, `field_size()`
  - `scalar_mul(k, point)`, `scalar_mul_base(k)`, `scalar_mul_add(k1, k2, q)`
- `EcPoint` ‚ÄĒ Affine point (x, y, infinity flag)
  - `new(x, y)`, `infinity()`, `is_infinity()`, `x()`, `y()`
  - `is_on_curve(group)` ‚ÄĒ Verifies y¬≤ ‚Č° x¬≥ + ax + b (mod p)
  - `to_uncompressed(group)` ‚Üí `0x04 || x || y`
  - `from_uncompressed(group, data)` ‚ÄĒ Decode + on-curve validation
- **Tests** (9): generator on curve (P-256/P-384), 2G == G+G, n¬∑G = infinity, encoding roundtrip, invalid point rejection, small scalar values, infinity encoding error, unsupported curve

#### 4. ECDH Key Agreement (`ecdh/mod.rs`)
- `EcdhKeyPair` struct with EcGroup, private_key (BigNum), public_key (EcPoint)
- `generate(curve_id)` ‚ÄĒ Random d ‚ąą [1, n-1], Q = d¬∑G
- `from_private_key(curve_id, bytes)` ‚ÄĒ Import with validation (d ‚ąą [1, n-1])
- `compute_shared_secret(peer_pub_bytes)` ‚Üí x-coordinate of d¬∑Q_peer, padded to field_size
- Public key zeroized on drop via `Zeroize` trait
- **Tests** (3): P-256 shared secret (Alice==Bob), P-384 shared secret, from_private_key roundtrip

#### 5. ECDSA Signing & Verification (`ecdsa/mod.rs`)
- `EcdsaKeyPair` struct with EcGroup, private_key (BigNum), public_key (EcPoint)
- `generate(curve_id)` ‚ÄĒ Random key pair
- `from_private_key(curve_id, bytes)` ‚ÄĒ Import private key
- `from_public_key(curve_id, bytes)` ‚ÄĒ Import public key (verify-only)
- **Signing** (FIPS 186-4):
  1. e = truncate(digest, bit_len(n))
  2. k = random [1, n-1]
  3. (x1, _) = k¬∑G; r = x1 mod n (retry if r=0)
  4. s = k‚ĀĽ¬Ļ¬∑(e + d¬∑r) mod n (retry if s=0)
  5. Return DER(SEQUENCE { INTEGER r, INTEGER s })
- **Verification**:
  1. Validate r, s ‚ąą [1, n-1]
  2. w = s‚ĀĽ¬Ļ mod n, u1 = e¬∑w, u2 = r¬∑w
  3. (x1, _) = u1¬∑G + u2¬∑Q (Shamir's trick)
  4. Check x1 mod n == r
- `truncate_digest()` ‚ÄĒ Truncates hash to curve order bit length
- DER encoding/decoding via `hitls-utils` ASN.1 `Encoder`/`Decoder`
- Private key zeroized on drop
- **Tests** (5): sign/verify P-256, sign/verify P-384, tamper detection, public-key-only verify, DER roundtrip

### Compilation Fixes
- **BigNum `mod_mul`/`mod_add`/`mod_sub` return `Result`** ‚ÄĒ All 27 call sites in point.rs, ecc/mod.rs, ecdsa/mod.rs needed `?` operator
- **`hitls-utils` not a dependency for `ecdsa`** ‚ÄĒ Added `hitls-utils` as optional dependency, added `"hitls-utils"` to ecdsa feature
- **`CurveParams` needs `Clone`** ‚ÄĒ Added `#[derive(Clone)]` to CurveParams

| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/ecc/point.rs` | New: Jacobian point arithmetic | ~235 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/ecc/mod.rs` | Rewrite: EcGroup + EcPoint | ~320 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/ecdsa/mod.rs` | Rewrite: ECDSA sign/verify | ~300 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/ecdh/mod.rs` | Rewrite: ECDH key agreement | ~145 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/Cargo.toml` | Modified: added hitls-utils dep | +2 |

## Phase I7: Ed25519 + X25519 + DH (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Implement Curve25519 field arithmetic (GF(2^255-19), Fp51 representation)
- Implement Edwards curve point operations for Ed25519
- Implement Ed25519 signing and verification (RFC 8032)
- Implement X25519 key exchange (RFC 7748)
- Implement classic DH key exchange with RFC 7919 predefined groups

### Completed Steps

#### 1. Curve25519 Field Arithmetic (`curve25519/field.rs`)
- `Fe25519` type: 5 √ó u64 limbs (Fp51), each limb ‚Č§ 51 bits
- Operations: add, sub, mul, square, neg, invert (Fermat), pow25523, mul121666
- Encoding: from_bytes/to_bytes (32-byte little-endian)
- Utilities: reduce, conditional_swap (constant-time), is_negative, is_zero
- Fp51 multiplication: schoolbook 5√ó5, overflow limbs √ó19 fold-back, u128 intermediates
- Inversion via addition chain: z^(p-2) = z^(2^255-21)
- **Tests** (7): zero/one, mul identity, mul/square consistency, invert, encode/decode roundtrip, add/sub roundtrip, conditional swap

#### 2. Edwards Curve Point Operations (`curve25519/edwards.rs`)
- Twisted Edwards curve: -x¬≤ + y¬≤ = 1 + d¬∑x¬≤¬∑y¬≤ (d = -121665/121666)
- `GeExtended` type: extended coordinates (X, Y, Z, T) where T = XY/Z
- Point operations: identity, basepoint, point_add (Hisil 2008), point_double (dbl-2008-hwcd for a=-1)
- Scalar multiplication: double-and-add (MSB ‚Üí LSB), plus base-point variant
- Point encoding/decoding: y-coordinate + x sign bit, sqrt recovery via pow25523
- Constants: D, D2, SQRT_M1, BASE_X, BASE_Y (all as Fe25519 Fp51 limbs)
- **Tests** (5): identity encoding, basepoint roundtrip, double==add, scalar_mul √ó1, scalar_mul √ó2

#### 3. Ed25519 Signing & Verification (`ed25519/mod.rs`)
- `Ed25519KeyPair` struct: 32-byte seed + 32-byte public key
- Key derivation: SHA-512(seed) ‚Üí clamp(h[0..32]) ‚Üí scalar_mul_base ‚Üí public key
- **Signing** (RFC 8032 ¬ß5.1.6): r = SHA-512(prefix||msg) mod L, R = r¬∑B, k = SHA-512(R||A||msg) mod L, S = (r + k¬∑a) mod L
- **Verification** (RFC 8032 ¬ß5.1.7): Check S¬∑B == R + k¬∑A
- Scalar mod L via BigNum (512-bit reduction)
- `scalar_muladd(a, b, c)`: (a*b + c) mod L
- `scalar_is_canonical(s)`: check s < L
- **Tests** (6): RFC 8032 ¬ß7.1 vectors 1 & 2, sign/verify roundtrip, tamper detection, public-key-only verify, invalid signature rejection

#### 4. X25519 Key Exchange (`x25519/mod.rs`)
- `X25519PrivateKey` / `X25519PublicKey` types (32 bytes each)
- Montgomery ladder scalar multiplication (RFC 7748 ¬ß5)
- Key generation, public key derivation, Diffie-Hellman shared secret
- All-zero output check (point at infinity rejection)
- **Tests** (3): RFC 7748 ¬ß6.1 test vector, key exchange symmetry, basepoint determinism

#### 5. DH Key Exchange (`dh/mod.rs`, `dh/groups.rs`)
- `DhParams` struct: prime p, generator g (BigNum)
- `DhKeyPair`: private x ‚ąą [2, p-2], public y = g^x mod p
- Predefined groups: RFC 7919 ffdhe2048 and ffdhe3072 (g = 2)
- Shared secret: s = peer_pub^x mod p, padded to prime_size
- Peer public key validation: 2 ‚Č§ peer_pub ‚Č§ p-2
- **Tests** (3): ffdhe2048 exchange, custom params (p=23, g=5), from_group construction

### Critical Bugs Found & Fixed

#### Fp51 Inversion Addition Chain (`field.rs`)
- **Bug**: After computing z^(2^250-1), the chain did 2 squares + mul(f) + 3 squares + mul(z11) = z^(2^255-13)
- **Fix**: 5 squares + mul(z11) = z^(2^255-32+11) = z^(2^255-21) = z^(p-2)

#### Edwards Curve Constants (`edwards.rs`)
- **Bug**: D[3], D[4], BASE_Y[1-3], BASE_X[3-4] had incorrect Fp51 limb values
- **Fix**: Recomputed all constants from first principles using Python, verified against known encodings

#### Edwards Point Doubling Formula (`edwards.rs`)
- **Bug**: Used a=1 doubling formula on a=-1 twisted Edwards curve
- **Fix**: Switched to "dbl-2008-hwcd" formula: D=-A, G=D+B, F=G-C, H=D-B

#### X25519 Montgomery Ladder (`x25519/mod.rs`)
- **Bug**: `z_2 = E * (AA + 121666*E)` ‚ÄĒ AA should be BB
- **Fix**: `z_2 = E * (BB + 121666*E)` ‚ÄĒ verified by deriving from Montgomery curve doubling formula

#### Sub Function Constants (`field.rs`)
- **Bug**: 2p constants for non-negative subtraction had wrong values
- **Fix**: Recomputed correct 2p limb values

| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/curve25519/field.rs` | New: Fp51 field arithmetic | ~550 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/curve25519/edwards.rs` | New: Edwards point operations | ~280 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/ed25519/mod.rs` | Rewrite: Ed25519 sign/verify | ~380 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/x25519/mod.rs` | Rewrite: X25519 key exchange | ~210 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/dh/mod.rs` | Rewrite: DH key exchange | ~165 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/dh/groups.rs` | New: RFC 7919 ffdhe parameters | ~90 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/lib.rs` | Modified: added curve25519 module | +2 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/Cargo.toml` | Modified: ed25519 feature deps | +1 |

- Edwards points (5): identity, basepoint roundtrip, double==add, scalar√ó1, scalar√ó2

- Implement SM2 (signature + encryption + key exchange)
- Implement DRBG (deterministic random bit generator)

## Phase I8: DSA + SM2 + HMAC-DRBG (Session 2026-02-06)

### Goals
- Implement DSA signing and verification (FIPS 186-4)
- Implement SM2 signing, verification, encryption, and decryption (GB/T 32918)
- Implement HMAC-DRBG (NIST SP 800-90A)

### Completed Steps

#### 1. SM2P256V1 Curve Parameters (`ecc/curves.rs`)
- Added SM2P256V1 (GB/T 32918.5-2017) parameters to existing `get_curve_params`
- `EccCurveId::Sm2Prime256` ‚Üí full CurveParams with p, a, b, gx, gy, n, h=1, field_size=32
- SM2 curve has a = p - 3, so existing Jacobian point_double optimization works directly

#### 2. DSA Signing & Verification (`dsa/mod.rs`)
- `DsaParams` struct: p (prime modulus), q (subgroup order), g (generator)
- `DsaKeyPair`: generate, from_private_key, from_public_key
- **Signing** (FIPS 186-4): r = (g^k mod p) mod q, s = k^(-1)¬∑(e + x¬∑r) mod q
- **Verification**: w = s^(-1), u1 = e¬∑w, u2 = r¬∑w, v = (g^u1 ¬∑ y^u2 mod p) mod q, check v == r
- `digest_to_bignum()` ‚ÄĒ truncates digest to q's bit length (right-shift)
- DER signature encoding/decoding via hitls-utils ASN.1
- **Tests** (5): sign/verify, tamper detection, public-key-only verify, DER roundtrip, invalid params

#### 3. SM2 Signature + Encryption (`sm2/mod.rs`)
- `Sm2KeyPair` struct: EcGroup, private_key (BigNum), public_key (EcPoint)
- **ZA computation** (GB/T 32918.2 ¬ß5.5): ZA = SM3(ENTLA || IDA || a || b || xG || yG || xA || yA)
  - Default IDA = "1234567812345678" (16 bytes)
- **Signing** (GB/T 32918.2 ¬ß6.1):
  - e = SM3(ZA || M), k random, (x1, _) = k¬∑G
  - r = (e + x1) mod n, s = (1+d)^(-1) ¬∑ (k - r¬∑d) mod n
  - Note: different from ECDSA! s uses (1+d)^(-1), not k^(-1)
- **Verification** (GB/T 32918.2 ¬ß7.1):
  - t = (r + s) mod n, (x1', _) = s¬∑G + t¬∑PA (Shamir's trick), R' = (e + x1') mod n, check R' == r
- **Encryption** (GB/T 32918.4, new format C1||C3||C2):
  - k random, C1 = k¬∑G, (x2, y2) = k¬∑PB
  - t = KDF(x2 || y2, len(M)), C2 = M ‚äē t, C3 = SM3(x2 || M || y2)
- **Decryption**: (x2, y2) = dB ¬∑ C1, reverse KDF, constant-time C3 comparison
- **SM2 KDF**: counter-mode SM3(x2 || y2 || counter_be32)
- **Tests** (7): sign/verify, custom ID, tamper detection, pubkey-only verify, encrypt/decrypt, tampered decrypt rejection, short message encrypt

#### 4. HMAC-DRBG (`drbg/mod.rs`)
- `HmacDrbg` struct: K (32 bytes), V (32 bytes), reseed_counter
- **Instantiate** (SP 800-90A ¬ß10.1.2.1): K=0x00..00, V=0x01..01, update(seed_material)
- **Update** (SP 800-90A ¬ß10.1.2.2): two-round HMAC for non-empty data
- **Generate** (SP 800-90A ¬ß10.1.2.5): produce output blocks via V=HMAC(K,V), final update
- **Reseed** (SP 800-90A ¬ß10.1.2.4): update(entropy || additional_input)
- Reseed interval: 2^48
- `from_system_entropy()` convenience constructor using getrandom
- **Tests** (6): instantiate, generate, reseed, additional input, deterministic, large output

### Bug Found & Fixed

#### DSA Tamper Detection with Small Groups
- **Problem**: Test used 1-byte digests `[0x01]` and `[0x05]` with q=11 (bit_len=4). `digest_to_bignum` shifts right by 4, producing 0 for both ‚ÄĒ identical after truncation!
- **Fix**: Use digests where the top nibble differs (`[0x10]` ‚Üí e=1, `[0x20]` ‚Üí e=2, etc.) and test multiple tampered values to handle ~1/11 collision probability with small q.

### Cargo.toml Changes
```toml
dsa = ["hitls-bignum", "hitls-utils"]
sm2 = ["ecc", "sm3", "hitls-utils"]
drbg = ["hmac", "sha2"]
```

|------|-----------|-------------|

| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/dsa/mod.rs` | Rewrite: DSA sign/verify | ~320 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm2/mod.rs` | Rewrite: SM2 sign/verify/encrypt/decrypt | ~450 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/mod.rs` | Rewrite: HMAC-DRBG | ~245 |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/Cargo.toml` | Modified: feature deps | +3 |

| Crate | Tests | Status |

| hitls-bignum | 46 | All pass |
| hitls-crypto | 132 (+18, 1 ignored) | All pass |
| hitls-utils | 11 | All pass |
| **Total** | **189** | **All pass** |

New tests (18):
- DSA (5): sign/verify, tamper detection, pubkey-only verify, DER roundtrip, invalid params
- SM2 (7): sign/verify, custom ID, tamper, pubkey-only verify, encrypt/decrypt, tampered decrypt, short message
- HMAC-DRBG (6): instantiate, generate, reseed, additional input, deterministic, large output
