## Phase I97 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.3 Post-Handshake-Auth CertificateVerify Transcript Fix (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I97 fixes a real RFC 8446 ¬ß4.4.1 violation in TLS 1.3 post-handshake
client authentication, surfaced by the T122 PHA probe: both the server
(`request_client_auth`) and the client (post-handshake
CertificateRequest handler) computed the post-handshake
CertificateVerify signature ‚ÄĒ and the post-handshake Finished MAC ‚ÄĒ
over `Transcript-Hash(CertificateRequest ‚ÄĖ Certificate [‚ÄĖ CV])`
**alone**, instead of continuing the completed main-handshake
transcript.

RFC 8446 ¬ß4.4.1: "the transcript hash is always taken from the
following sequence of handshake messages, starting at the first
ClientHello ‚Ä¶ Note, however, that subsequent post-handshake
authentications do not include each other, just the messages through
the end of the main handshake." So the correct post-handshake CV
transcript is `Hash(ClientHello ‚Ä¶ client Finished ‚ÄĖ CertificateRequest
‚ÄĖ Certificate)`.

The bug was **symmetric** ‚ÄĒ the same mistake on both the request and
response sides ‚ÄĒ so our server and our client interoperated with each
other (the pre-existing `test_async_post_hs_auth_roundtrip` Rust test
passed), but neither interoperated with a conformant peer. tlsfuzzer
(`test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py`, driven via a temporary local
`--post-handshake-auth` s-server probe) failed every PHA conversation
with `decrypt_error` until the fix.

### Code changes (all `hitls-tls`)

- **`crypt/transcript.rs`** ‚ÄĒ `TranscriptHash` derives `Clone`
  (it is a message-buffer + replay design, so a clone is a cheap
  snapshot). New unit test `test_transcript_clone_independence`.
- **`handshake/server.rs`** ‚ÄĒ `ServerHandshake::transcript_clone()`
  getter. `process_client_finished` already feeds the client Finished
  into the transcript, so a clone taken after it is the
  CH‚Ä¶client-Finished baseline.
- **`handshake/client.rs`** ‚ÄĒ `ClientHandshake::transcript_clone()`
  getter (`process_finished` feeds the client Finished in at line
  ~1313).
- **`connection/server.rs`** + **`connection_async.rs`** ‚ÄĒ new
  `post_handshake_transcript: Option<TranscriptHash>` field on both
  the sync and async server connections.
- **`macros.rs`** ‚ÄĒ `tls13_server_do_handshake_body!` stores
  `hs.transcript_clone()` into the connection at the end of the
  handshake (covers sync + async ‚ÄĒ one macro).
- **`connection/server.rs`** + **`connection_async.rs`** ‚ÄĒ
  `request_client_auth` (sync + async) rewritten: clone the retained
  baseline transcript, append CertificateRequest + Certificate for the
  CV hash, append CertificateVerify for the Finished hash. Replaces the
  four fresh `DigestVariant` hashers that omitted the base transcript.
  Also added `ECDSA_SECP521R1_SHA512` to the advertised post-HS
  CertificateRequest sig-algs (I96 made P-521 signable).
- **`macros.rs`** ‚ÄĒ `tls13_client_handle_post_hs_cert_request_body!`
  rewritten the same way: clone `client_hs`'s retained transcript as
  the baseline instead of starting fresh from the CR message bytes
  (covers the sync + async client ‚ÄĒ one macro).
- Cloning the baseline (rather than mutating it) means repeated
  post-handshake authentications each restart from the main handshake
  and do not include one another, per ¬ß4.4.1.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-tls --all-features --lib`: **1539 PASS / 0 FAIL**
  ‚ÄĒ including `test_async_post_hs_auth_roundtrip` /
  `test_async_post_hs_auth_no_cert`, which now exercise the full
  client‚ÜĒserver PHA roundtrip on the *correct* transcript (+1 new
  `test_transcript_clone_independence`).
- `cargo clippy -p hitls-tls --all-features` `-D warnings`: 0;
  `cargo fmt`: clean.
- End-to-end against tlsfuzzer (server probed with a temporary,
  uncommitted local `--post-handshake-auth` s-server flag):
  `test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py` **2/6 ‚Üí 4/6 PASS**. The fix
  closes `post-handshake authentication` and `post-handshake
  authentication with no client cert`. The residual 2 FAIL are
  unrelated to the transcript: `malformed signature in PHA` needs the
  server to send a fatal `decrypt_error` alert (rather than close
  abruptly) on a bad post-HS CV, and `post-handshake authentication
  with KeyUpdate` needs `request_client_auth` to tolerate a KeyUpdate
  interleaved into the post-HS read loop ‚ÄĒ each a separate robustness
  gap, queued for the follow-up.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/crypt/transcript.rs` | Modified | `TranscriptHash: Clone` + clone-independence test. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server.rs` | Modified | `ServerHandshake::transcript_clone()`. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/client.rs` | Modified | `ClientHandshake::transcript_clone()`. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection/server.rs` | Modified | `post_handshake_transcript` field; `request_client_auth` transcript rewrite; import cleanup. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection_async.rs` | Modified | Same as `connection/server.rs` for the async server; import cleanup for the async client. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection/client.rs` | Modified | Import cleanup (post-HS CR macro no longer uses `DigestVariant`). |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/macros.rs` | Modified | Store transcript at end of server handshake; rewrite client post-HS CR handler transcript. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 315 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI96 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI97. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I97 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I97)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1539/0 (+1 from
I97). Follow-up: a Testing phase wires `test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py`
into CI behind a `--post-handshake-auth` s-server flag; the 2 residual
FAILs (alert-on-failure, KeyUpdate-interleave) are separate fixes.

## Phase T125 ‚ÄĒ PHA tlsfuzzer Wiring: `--post-handshake-auth` s-server Flag + CI Coverage (2026-05-17)

### Summary

T125 is the Testing-phase wrap-up of the PHA work begun in T122 and
fixed in I97. It commits the `--post-handshake-auth` `s-server` flag
(used as a *temporary, uncommitted* probe during I97's verification)
and wires tlsfuzzer's `test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py` into the
curated suite against a dedicated instance.

Mirrors T122's `--key-update` structure exactly: the flag makes the
TLS 1.3 echo loop watch for a request-path marker ‚ÄĒ `/secret` here ‚ÄĒ
and on a match call `request_client_auth()` (the I97-fixed
post-handshake CertificateRequest path). A plain `GET /` matches
nothing, so sanity steps are unaffected; the discriminator is the
request path, not the presence of application data. TLS 1.3 only
(rejected up front for a `--tls 1.2` listener).

### Code changes

**`crates/hitls-cli/src/main.rs`** ‚ÄĒ new `--post-handshake-auth` bool
flag on `SServer`, threaded into `s_server::run(...)`.

**`crates/hitls-cli/src/s_server.rs`** ‚ÄĒ `run()` gains a
`post_handshake_auth` parameter; the TLS-1.2-rejection check covers
both `--key-update` and `--post-handshake-auth`; `handle_connection_tls13`
gains the `/secret` ‚Üí `conn.request_client_auth()` branch. 3 existing
`run()` unit tests updated for the new arity.

**`.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml`** ‚ÄĒ new `HITLS_PORT_PHA: 4455` +
a `--post-handshake-auth` s-server instance; new `scripts_pha` array +
run loop passing the client identity (`-k`/`-c`, reusing the existing
mTLS client cert); wait/stop/upload lists extended.

**`tests/tlsfuzzer/args/test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.txt`** (added) ‚ÄĒ
`--pha-as-reply`, so tlsfuzzer expects the CertificateRequest as a
reply to its `GET /secret` query (matching the server's path-trigger
model) rather than unsolicited after the handshake.

**`tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.txt`** (added) ‚ÄĒ
2 XFAILs with per-entry rationale + next-step:

- `malformed signature in PHA` ‚ÄĒ `request_client_auth` correctly
  rejects a bad CertificateVerify signature but returns `Err` and the
  s-server drops the connection; tlsfuzzer expects a fatal
  `decrypt_error` alert (RFC 8446 ¬ß6.2). Fix: extend the T89
  alert-before-close discipline to the post-handshake path.
- `post-handshake authentication with KeyUpdate` ‚ÄĒ the conversation
  interleaves a KeyUpdate into the post-handshake exchange;
  `request_client_auth`'s read loop expects exactly
  Certificate/CertificateVerify/Finished and errors on it. Fix: the
  post-handshake read loop must process an interleaved KeyUpdate.

Both are distinct robustness gaps (not transcript bugs), queued for a
follow-up Implementation phase.

### Verification

- `cargo build --release -p hitls-cli`: clean;
  `cargo test -p hitls-cli --all-features --bins`: 167 PASS / 0 FAIL.
- `cargo clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets` with
  `-D warnings`: 0; `cargo fmt`: clean; `actionlint`: no errors (only
  the pre-existing SC2129 style nits in the server-startup step).
- End-to-end: `test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py` driven through
  `tests/tlsfuzzer/run.sh` against a `--post-handshake-auth` s-server
  ‚ÄĒ **4 PASS / 2 XFAIL / 0 FAIL / 0 XPASS**, `run.sh` exit 0.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-cli/src/main.rs` | Modified | `--post-handshake-auth` flag. |
| `crates/hitls-cli/src/s_server.rs` | Modified | `run()` param + 1.2 reject; `/secret` branch in `handle_connection_tls13`; 3 tests re-arity'd. |
| `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml` | Modified | PHA instance (port 4455); `scripts_pha` array + run loop; wait/stop/upload lists. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/args/test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.txt` | Added | `--pha-as-reply`. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.txt` | Added | 2 XFAILs (alert-on-failure, KeyUpdate-interleave). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 316 + Testing summary. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T125 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | Suite count 48 ‚Üí 49; T125 phase reference. |

### Build Status (Post T125)

`hitls-cli` builds clean; `cargo test -p hitls-cli` 167/0. Curated
tlsfuzzer suite: 48 ‚Üí 49 script-runs. The PHA work (T122 probe ‚Üí I97
fix ‚Üí T125 CI wiring) is complete bar the 2 XFAIL'd robustness gaps.

## Phase I98 ‚ÄĒ Post-Handshake-Auth Robustness: Alert-on-Failure + Interleaved-KeyUpdate Tolerance (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I98 closes the two `test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py` XFAILs left by
T125 ‚ÄĒ the last gaps in the post-handshake client authentication
(PHA) work line (T122 probe ‚Üí I96/I97 fixes ‚Üí T125 CI wiring ‚Üí I98
robustness). Both are `request_client_auth` defects, distinct from the
I97 transcript bug:

1. **No alert on failure.** When `request_client_auth` rejected a
   malformed post-handshake CertificateVerify, it returned `Err` and
   the `s-server` echo loop dropped the connection ‚ÄĒ the peer saw a
   bare TCP close. RFC 8446 ¬ß6.2 requires a fatal `decrypt_error`
   alert.

2. **Interleaved KeyUpdate not tolerated.** RFC 8446 ¬ß4.6.3 allows a
   KeyUpdate at any point post-handshake. `request_client_auth`'s read
   loop expected exactly Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished
   and errored on a KeyUpdate slipped into the exchange.

### Code changes (sync `connection/server.rs` + async `connection_async.rs`)

- **Alert-on-failure**: `request_client_auth` is now a thin wrapper
  around `request_client_auth_inner`; on any `Err` it runs the T89
  `send_fatal_alert_for_error_body!` macro (the same
  `tls_error_to_alert` mapping the main read/handshake paths use)
  before returning the error. A malformed CertificateVerify ‚Üí
  `verify_certificate_verify` returns an error string containing
  `"decrypt_error"` ‚Üí the wrapper emits a fatal `decrypt_error` (51).
  Best-effort: a seal/write failure is swallowed (the caller closes
  anyway).
- **Interleaved KeyUpdate**: new `read_post_hs_skipping_key_update`
  helper ‚ÄĒ reads a record, and if it is a `KeyUpdate` handshake
  message, routes it to `handle_key_update` (which rekeys the read
  side and, for `update_requested`, sends the matching response) and
  reads again. `request_client_auth_inner`'s four post-handshake
  record reads (Certificate / empty-cert Finished / CertificateVerify
  / Finished) all go through it, so a KeyUpdate may interleave
  anywhere in the exchange. The KeyUpdate is consumed transparently
  and never fed to the PHA transcript (RFC 8446 ¬ß4.4.1 ‚ÄĒ KeyUpdate is
  not a transcript message).

No new connection state; `read_post_hs_skipping_key_update` composes
the existing `read_record` + `handle_key_update`.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-tls --all-features --lib`: **1539 PASS / 0 FAIL**
  (unchanged ‚ÄĒ the PHA roundtrip tests still pass). `cargo clippy
  -p hitls-tls --all-features` `-D warnings`: 0; `cargo fmt`: clean.
- End-to-end: `test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py` through
  `tests/tlsfuzzer/run.sh` against a `--post-handshake-auth` s-server
  ‚ÄĒ **4/6 ‚Üí 6/6 PASS** (0 XFAIL / 0 FAIL), `run.sh` exit 0.
  `malformed signature in PHA` now sees the fatal `decrypt_error`
  alert it expects; `post-handshake authentication with KeyUpdate`
  completes through the interleaved KeyUpdate.
- `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.txt` deleted ‚ÄĒ
  with both conversations PASSing, a stale XFAIL list would XPASS and
  fail the gate.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection/server.rs` | Modified | `request_client_auth` alert wrapper + `_inner`; `read_post_hs_skipping_key_update`; 4 reads routed through it. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection_async.rs` | Modified | Same for the async server connection. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.txt` | Deleted | Both XFAILs closed ‚ÄĒ script now 6/6. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 317 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI97 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI98. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I98 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | PHA phase reference updated to 6/6. |

### Build Status (Post I98)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1539/0. The PHA
work line (T122 ‚Üí I96 ‚Üí I97 ‚Üí T125 ‚Üí I98) is now complete:
`test-tls13-post-handshake-auth.py` is 6/6 in CI with no XFAILs.

## Phase T113 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust Test Migration Phase C: PKI Fixture Corpus + X.509 Parse KAT (2026-05-17)

### Summary

Opens `docs/c-test-migration-plan.md` **Phase C** ‚ÄĒ migrating the openHiTLS PKI SDV suite (X.509 / CSR / CRL parse tests), which load fixture *files* rather than carrying inline hex. Two parts so far:

- **¬ß4.1 fixture corpus** (committed in PR #88) ‚ÄĒ `rsync -a` of `openhitls/testcode/testdata/{cert,certificate}/` into `tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/` (1298 files, ~8.7 MB) + `MANIFEST.sha256` + a provenance/licence `README.md`. Every PKI SDV `.data` file references fixtures under `cert/`, so this corpus is the shared base for Phase B's CSR/CRL sub-tasks and all of Phase C.
- **¬ß4.2 cert + CRL parse** ‚ÄĒ `xtask/src/x509.rs` migrates the positive parse families `X509_CERT_PARSE_FUNC_TC001` (certificates) and `X509_CRL_PARSE_FILE_FUNC_TC001/002` + the success rows of `TC003/006/007/008` (CRLs). 116 parse tests in `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` (111 cert + 5 CRL).
- **¬ß4.2 CSR field-check** ‚ÄĒ `X509_CSR_PARSE_FUNC_TC001/002/003` (40 rows): parse a PKCS#10 CSR and assert public `CertificateRequest` fields ‚ÄĒ `TC001` `version` / encode-length / signature, `TC002` subject DN, `TC003` attribute count. **17 rows migrate**; the rest route to `skipped_unsupported_alg` ‚ÄĒ `TC002` rows with DN attribute types outside the parser's short-name set (or hex-ambiguous values), and `TC003` rows with ‚Č•1 attribute (Rust's `attributes` field is the flattened *extension* list, so its count diverges from C's *attribute* count). `CSR_PARSE_FUNC_TC004` (negative) ‚Üí `ApiSurface`.
- **¬ß4.2 cert field-extraction** ‚ÄĒ nine field-check families migrated: `X509_CERT_PARSE_VERSION_FUNC` (91), `SERIALNUM_FUNC` (91), `SIGNATURE_FUNC` (136), `START_TIME_FUNC` (91), `END_TIME_FUNC` (91), `SIGNALG_FUNC` (93), `ISSUERNAME_FUNC` (91), `SUBJECTNAME_FUNC` (91), `PUBKEY_FUNC_TC001` (97). Each parses the fixture and asserts a public `Certificate` field (or, for `PUBKEY`, a `verify_signature` call). `migrated_x509_parse.rs` grows 136 ‚Üí **1008 tests** (+872).

- **¬ß4.2 CRL revocation chain-verify** ‚ÄĒ `X509_CRL_FILE_VERIFY_FUNC_TC001`‚Äď`TC005` (63 rows): the C test verifies every CRL's signature against its CA, then verifies a cert's chain with CRL revocation checking. The migrated test mirrors it via `CertificateRevocationList::verify_signature` + `CertificateVerifier`; **10 rows migrate** (the `SUCCESS` / `CERT_REVOKED` outcomes Rust's verifier faithfully reproduces), the rest route to `skipped_unsupported_alg` (SM2 fixtures, multi-level `CRL_DEV`, and the stricter-than-Rust C result codes ‚ÄĒ all verifier/API gaps).
- **¬ß4.2 CRL field-check** ‚ÄĒ `X509_CRL_PARSE_FILE_FUNC_TC005` / `TC009`‚Äď`TC013` (29 rows): parse a CRL, assert one parsed field ‚ÄĒ thisUpdate/nextUpdate year (`TC005`), CRL-number extension (`TC009`), AKI-extension criticality (`TC010`), revoked-entry reason code (`TC011`), invalidity date (`TC012`), certificate-issuer extension presence (`TC013`). **20 rows migrate**; the rest route to `skipped_unsupported_alg` (reason code 7 ‚ÄĒ unassigned, absent from Rust's `RevocationReason` enum; `TC013` negatives ‚ÄĒ Rust's parser populates `certificate_issuer` where C's getter fails; non-`SUCCESS`-`res` rows). `TC004` (issuer-DN string) ‚Üí `ApiSurface` (C/Rust DN-string format differ).
- **¬ß4.2 cert-chain build + verify** ‚ÄĒ `X509_BUILD_CERT_CHAIN_FUNC` (`pki/verify/test_suite_sdv_x509_vfy.data`): `rootPath : caPath : certPath : crlPath`. The C test builds a cert chain for the end-entity and verifies it with the CRL flag cleared (revocation off). The migrated test loads the root (+ optional intermediate CA) into a `CertificateVerifier` and asserts `verify_cert` succeeds ‚ÄĒ **12 rows migrate**; ECDSA NIST P-192 chains skip (legacy curve unsupported by Rust's signature verifier).
- **¬ß4.2 verify-by-pubkey** ‚ÄĒ `X509_CERT_VERIFY_BY_PUBKEY_FUNC` (`certPath : issuerPath : otherPath`): the C test verifies the cert with the issuer's public key (success) and with an unrelated cert's key (failure); the migrated test mirrors both via `Certificate::verify_signature`. **5 rows migrate.**
- **¬ß4.2 charset chain-verify** ‚ÄĒ `X509_CERT_VERIFY_WITH_VARIOUS_CHARSET_FUNC_TC001` (single-level), `_TC002` (multi-level), and `X509_CRL_VERIFY_WITH_VARIOUS_CHARSET_FUNC` (single-level with CRL revocation): chain-verify with charset-varied DN fixtures and a result-code outcome. The migrated test runs each through a `CertificateVerifier` and asserts the outcome (`SUCCESS` ‚Üí `is_ok`; `ISSUE_CERT_NOT_FOUND` / `CERT_REVOKED` ‚Üí `Err` Display match). **12 rows migrate.** Rows under `charset/string_canon/` (Rust does byte-exact DN matching, no canonicalisation) or `user_err_*` (Rust falls back to DN-only on AKI mismatch / rejects some malformed fixtures at parse time) are skipped. `X509_CA_PATH_WITH_VARIOUS_CHARSET_FUNC` (uses `STORECTX_ADD_CA_PATH` ‚ÄĒ directory-based CA loading with no Rust analogue) routes to `ApiSurface`. `migrated_x509_parse.rs` reaches **1064 tests**.

Phase C is **not closed** ‚ÄĒ `x509_cert.data` has the malformed-DER negatives plus two deliberate API gaps (`TBS_SIGNALG` ‚ÄĒ the TBS inner AlgorithmIdentifier is not exposed; `PUBKEY_FUNC_TC002` ‚ÄĒ XMSS public-key structure extraction has no Rust analogue); the CSR field-check / expected-return families remain. Those land in subsequent T113 commits under the no-sub-phase rule.

#### Finding ‚ÄĒ Rust CRL parser is more lenient than C

`CRL_PARSE_FILE_FUNC_TC003/006/007/008` rows are `path : res` where `res` is the expected C return code. The `HITLS_PKI_SUCCESS` rows migrate as positive parse tests; the **9 negative rows** (`res` is `HITLS_X509_ERR_GET_ANY_TAG` / `‚Ä¶_PARSE_NO_ELEMENT`) are **not** migrated ‚ÄĒ `CertificateRevocationList::from_pem` *accepts* those structurally-malformed CRLs that the C parser rejects, so an `is_err()` assertion would not hold. They route to `skipped_unsupported_alg`. This is a genuine Rust CRL-parser strictness gap and a candidate hardening Implementation phase ‚ÄĒ recorded here and in `docs/c-test-na-list.md`.

### X.509 cert + CSR parse pilot

`X509_CERT_PARSE_FUNC_TC001` / `X509_CSR_PARSE_FUNC_TC001-003` rows are `format : path : ‚Ä¶` ‚ÄĒ the C test parses the file (and, for some, checks fields). The migrated test ports the parse-succeeds half: it loads the mirrored fixture via `std::fs::read(concat!(env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR"), "/../../tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/<rel>"))` and asserts `Certificate` / `CertificateRequest` `::from_der` (`BSL_FORMAT_ASN1`) / `::from_pem` (`BSL_FORMAT_PEM`) returns `Ok`. `BSL_FORMAT_UNKNOWN` (C auto-detect) has no Rust analogue ‚Üí `skipped_unknown`. A single generic `emit_parse` covers both subjects (a `Subject` enum picks the Rust type / fn-name suffix). `CSR_PARSE_FUNC_TC004` carries an expected-return code (negative-capable) and is deferred to a later increment. From 1270 TC rows across the two `.data` files: 131 emitted, 1131 API-surface, 8 unknown.

### Cert field-extraction families

The cert field-check families are `path : expected‚Ä¶` rows ‚ÄĒ the C test parses the cert and checks one field via `HITLS_X509_CertCtrl`. The migrated test loads the fixture through a generated `load_cert_fixture` helper (auto-detects PEM vs DER by scanning the bytes for `-----BEGIN`) and asserts the matching public `Certificate` field:

- **version** ‚ÄĒ `cert.version`. C `GET_VERSION` returns the raw DER integer (`v1=0/v2=1/v3=2`); Rust's `Certificate::version` is **1-indexed** (`from_der` does `der_int.saturating_add(1)`, default `v1 ‚Üí 1`), so the emitted literal is the C value `+ 1` with a `// C version field = N` inline note.
- **serial_number** / **signature_value** ‚ÄĒ compared as raw DER bytes via `format_byte_slice`; `read_integer` / `read_bit_string` return the TLV value verbatim (no leading-zero stripping), so the C-`.data` hex matches byte-for-byte.
- **not_before** / **not_after** ‚ÄĒ the `START_TIME` / `END_TIME` rows carry a `year:month:day:hour:min:sec` UTC tuple; `Certificate::not_before` / `not_after` are `i64` Unix timestamps. The expected literal is computed at generation time by `civil_to_unix`, a copy of the ASN.1 decoder's own `datetime_to_unix` civil-date ‚Üí epoch formula, with a `// YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ` inline note.
- **signature_algorithm** ‚ÄĒ the `SIGNALG` row's `BSL_CID_*` token is mapped to OID arcs (`cid_to_oid_arcs`, 5 entries) and DER-encoded to the raw OID value bytes (`oid_der_value`) at generation time, then compared against `cert.signature_algorithm`. The trailing PSS hash/MGF/salt args are not ported (they live in `signature_params` as raw DER). The companion `TBS_SIGNALG` family is **not** migrated: the TBS inner AlgorithmIdentifier is parsed by `from_der` but discarded, so `Certificate` exposes no field for it (RFC 5280 ¬ß4.1.1.2 mandates it equal the outer one) ‚ÄĒ routed to `ApiSurface`.
- **issuer** / **subject** ‚ÄĒ the `ISSUERNAME` / `SUBJECTNAME` rows are `2N : (oid_hex, value_tag, value_hex) √ó N`; the migrated test asserts `cert.issuer.entries` / `cert.subject.entries` (a `Vec<(short-name, value)>`). Each RDN triple's attribute OID is mapped via `dn_oid_short_name` (8 entries ‚ÄĒ CN/C/L/ST/O/OU/serialNumber/emailAddress, matching the parser's `oid_to_dn_short_name`), and the value hex is decoded as UTF-8 (all DN values in the corpus are PrintableString/UTF8String).
- **pubkey** ‚ÄĒ `PUBKEY_FUNC_TC001` is `path1 : path2`; the C test verifies `path1`'s certificate signature with `path2`'s parsed public key (`HITLS_X509_CheckSignature(cert2->pubkey, cert->tbsRawData, ‚Ä¶)`). The migrated test loads both fixtures and asserts `cert.verify_signature(&issuer)` is `Ok(true)` ‚ÄĒ exercising RSA-PKCS#1, ECDSA (P-224 through P-521 + brainpool), RSA-PSS and SM2 verify paths. `PUBKEY_FUNC_TC002` (one XMSS row) is **not** migrated: it extracts the XMSS `root`/`seed`/`xdr` sub-fields via the C EAL pkey API, which `SubjectPublicKeyInfo` (raw key bytes only) cannot reproduce ‚ÄĒ routed to `ApiSurface`.

All 872 field-check tests pass on first generation ‚ÄĒ no leniency or off-by-one gaps in these fields (contrast the CRL finding above).

### CRL revocation chain-verify

`X509_CRL_FILE_VERIFY_FUNC_TC001`‚Äď`TC005` build a `StoreCtx`, verify every CRL's signature against its CA (`HITLS_X509_VerifyCrl` ‚Üí `crlVerResult`), then verify a cert's chain with CRL revocation checking (`HITLS_X509_CertVerify` ‚Üí `certVerResult`). The five TCs have distinct arg layouts ‚ÄĒ single-level (TC001/004) vs root+intermediate (TC002/003/005), result-code order, and an optional `isUseSm2UserId` / hardcoded root in TC003 ‚ÄĒ which `plan_crl_verify` normalises into `{trusted certs, CRLs, end-entity cert, flags, crlVer, certVer}`. The migrated test mirrors the C flow with `CertificateRevocationList::verify_signature` + a `CertificateVerifier` (`add_trusted_cert` + `add_crl` + `set_check_revocation`).

A row migrates only when its outcomes are ones Rust's verifier faithfully reproduces ‚ÄĒ all CRL signatures verify (`crlVerResult == SUCCESS`) and `certVerResult` is `SUCCESS` (`verify_cert` is `Ok`) or `CERT_REVOKED` (`Err`, Display `"certificate revoked"`). Everything else routes to `skipped_unsupported_alg`, each a **Rust-verifier / API gap**:

- **SM2 fixtures** (`test_for_crl/sm2/‚Ä¶`) ‚ÄĒ SM2 CRL signature verification needs the GM/T 0009 user-id, which `verify_signature` does not expose; the SM2 CRL signatures fail to verify under Rust's default. TC004/005 are almost entirely SM2.
- **multi-level `CRL_DEV`** ‚ÄĒ `CRL_DEV` checks revocation of the end-entity only; Rust's verifier has no device-only mode and checks every non-root cert, so on a root+intermediate chain it over-checks the intermediate CA.
- **stricter-than-Rust C codes** ‚ÄĒ `CRL_NOT_FOUND` (Rust soft-fails on a missing CRL), `PROCESS_CRITICALEXT` (no unhandled-critical-extension rejection), `KU_NO_CRLSIGN` (no CRL-issuer keyUsage check).

Of the 63 `FILE_VERIFY` rows, 10 migrate. Each gap is a candidate verifier-hardening Implementation phase (the SM2 one needs a user-id parameter on the CRL/cert verify API).

### CRL field-check

`X509_CRL_PARSE_FILE_FUNC_TC005` / `TC009`‚Äď`TC013` parse a CRL and assert one parsed field. `emit_crl_field` handles six per-TC shapes against `CertificateRevocationList`:

- **TC005** (thisUpdate/nextUpdate year) ‚ÄĒ `this_update` / `next_update` are `i64` Unix timestamps; the C test compares only `.year`, so the migrated test asserts the timestamp falls in `[civil_to_unix(year,1,1‚Ä¶), civil_to_unix(year+1,1,1‚Ä¶))`.
- **TC009** (CRL number) ‚ÄĒ `crl.crl_number()` for the value + an `extensions` lookup (OID `55 1d 14`) for the criticality flag.
- **TC010** (AKI criticality) ‚ÄĒ an `extensions` lookup (OID `55 1d 23`) for `.critical`.
- **TC011** (reason code) ‚ÄĒ `crl.revoked_certs[0].reason` cast to `i32`; the C `reasonCode` arg is a `HITLS_X509_REVOKED_REASON_*` token mapped via `reason_name_to_code`. Reason code 7 (RFC 5280 ¬ß5.3.1 leaves it unassigned) is absent from Rust's `RevocationReason` enum ‚Üí skipped.
- **TC012** (invalidity date) ‚ÄĒ `revoked_certs[0].invalidity_date` year-range, same trick as TC005.
- **TC013** (certificate-issuer extension) ‚ÄĒ `revoked_certs[0].certificate_issuer.is_some()`. Only the both-`SUCCESS` rows migrate: where C's `GET_REVOKED_CERTISSUER` getter fails, Rust's parser still populates `certificate_issuer` (a parser-leniency gap).

`TC004` (issuer-DN string) is routed to `ApiSurface`: Rust's `DistinguishedName` `Display` joins RDNs with `", "` where the C `GET_ISSUER_DN_STR` uses `","` ‚ÄĒ a serialization-format mismatch, not a coverage gap (the DN is already covered field-by-field by the cert `ISSUERNAME` family). Of the 29 field-check rows, 20 migrate.

### Parser enhancement ‚ÄĒ `Arg::Str`

PKI `.data` rows quote *file paths* (`"../testdata/cert/foo.der"`), not hex. The parser previously assumed every quoted field was hex and hard-failed otherwise. `parse_tc_line` now decodes a quoted field as hex when it can and falls back to a new `Arg::Str(String)` variant (with an `as_str()` accessor) otherwise ‚ÄĒ backward-compatible, since every existing algorithm's quoted fields are valid hex (verified: all 9 crypto `--check` still up-to-date).

### Changes

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `xtask/src/parser.rs` | Modified | New `Arg::Str` variant + `as_str()`; quoted non-hex fields parse as string literals instead of failing. |
| `xtask/src/x509.rs` | Added | X.509 cert + CSR + CRL + verify KAT emitter; `emit_cert_parse` + `emit_crl_parse` / `emit_crl_parse_res` + `emit_cert_field` (9 cert field families) + `emit_csr_field` (3 CSR field-check families) + `emit_crl_file_verify` (CRL-revocation chain verify) + `emit_crl_field` (6 CRL field-check families) + `emit_build_cert_chain` (cert-chain build+verify) + `emit_cert_verify_by_pubkey` + `emit_charset_verify` (cert/CRL `*_WITH_VARIOUS_CHARSET`) + `civil_to_unix` / `cid_to_oid_arcs` / `oid_der_value` / `dn_oid_short_name` / `reason_name_to_code` / `bool_assert` helpers + generated `load_{cert,crl,csr}_fixture` PEM/DER-autodetect helpers; C fixture-path ‚Üí `c-asn1-fixtures/` rewrite. |
| `xtask/src/main.rs` | Modified | `x509-parse` branch in the `--algo` dispatch ‚ÄĒ multi-input (cert + csr + crl + verify `.data`), output to `crates/hitls-pki/tests/`. |
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Added | 1064 generated X.509 KAT tests (111 cert-parse + 5 CRL-parse + 872 cert field-check + 17 CSR field-check + 10 CRL revocation chain-verify + 20 CRL field-check + 12 cert-chain build-verify + 5 verify-by-pubkey + 12 charset chain-verify). |
| `tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/` | Added (PR #88) | Mirrored openHiTLS PKI fixture corpus + `MANIFEST.sha256` + `README.md`. |

### Build Status

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse --all-features`: 1064/1064 PASS (111 cert-parse + 5 CRL-parse + 872 cert field-check + 17 CSR field-check + 10 CRL revocation chain-verify + 20 CRL field-check + 12 cert-chain build-verify + 5 verify-by-pubkey + 12 charset chain-verify).
- `cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo x509-parse --check`: up-to-date.
- `cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo {sha2,hmac,cmac,aes,curve25519,dsa,dh,sm4,sm2} --check`: all up-to-date (parser change is a no-op for the crypto algorithms).
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p xtask -p hitls-pki --all-features --tests`: 0.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`: clean.

---

## Phase T120 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.3 `psk_ke`: PSK Resumption Without (EC)DHE (2026-05-17)

### Summary

T120 implements server-side `psk_ke` (RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.9 mode 0 ‚ÄĒ PSK key
establishment **without** an (EC)DHE exchange), the last long-standing
PSK item: the DEV_LOG has carried "queued for T120" since T119.

Pre-T120 the server's PSK path required the client to advertise
`psk_dhe_ke` (mode 1) ‚ÄĒ a client offering `psk_ke` alone fell through
to a full (non-PSK) handshake, so tlsfuzzer's `session resumption -
PSK_ONLY` conversation (which resumes with `psk_key_exchange_modes:
[psk_ke]`) was XFAIL'd.

### Code changes (`crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server.rs`)

`ServerHandshake::process_client_hello` + `build_server_flight` ‚ÄĒ
shared by the sync and async server connections, so one change covers
both:

- **Negotiation**: the PSK block's guard widened from
  `modes.contains(&0x01)` to `modes.contains(&0x01) || modes.contains(&0x00)`
  so a `psk_ke`-only ClientHello reaches the binder-verification logic.
  After a PSK verifies, `psk_ke` is set true when the client did *not*
  also offer `psk_dhe_ke` (RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.9 ‚ÄĒ `psk_dhe_ke` is preferred
  for forward secrecy; `psk_ke` is taken only when offered alone).
- **`ServerFlightParams`** gains a `psk_ke: bool`, threaded from
  `process_client_hello` (the retried-CH path passes `false` ‚ÄĒ HRR
  retry carries no PSK).
- **`build_server_flight`**: when `psk_ke` is set, (1) the key-exchange
  step is skipped ‚ÄĒ `shared_secret` is a `Hash.length` zero string,
  `server_key_share_bytes` is empty; (2) the ServerHello carries no
  `key_share` extension. `ks.derive_handshake_secret(&shared_secret)`
  therefore extracts the Handshake Secret over zeros (RFC 8446 ¬ß7.1).
  The PSK-mode cert skip (SH / EE / Finished, no Certificate /
  CertificateVerify) is unchanged.

The client still sends a `key_share` (for full-handshake fallback);
the server simply ignores it under `psk_ke`. No client-side change ‚ÄĒ
our own client offers `psk_dhe_ke`, so the existing `psk_dhe_ke` path
is untouched.

### Verification

- `cargo build -p hitls-tls --all-features` (`-D warnings`): clean;
  `cargo test -p hitls-tls --all-features --lib`: **1539 PASS / 0 FAIL**
  (the `psk_dhe_ke` resumption tests are unaffected ‚ÄĒ `psk_ke` is a new
  branch); `cargo clippy` + `cargo fmt`: clean.
- End-to-end: `test-tls13-session-resumption.py` through
  `tests/tlsfuzzer/run.sh` against a `--ticket-key` s-server ‚ÄĒ
  `session resumption - PSK_ONLY` **FAIL ‚Üí PASS**; script now
  **5 PASS / 2 XFAIL / 0 FAIL** (`run.sh` exit 0). The 2 residual
  XFAILs are `sanity - TLS 1.2` / `use TLS 1.2 ticket in TLS 1.3` ‚ÄĒ
  the TLS-1.2 cross-version gap that awaits the `--tls auto` phase,
  unrelated to `psk_ke`.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server.rs` | Modified | `psk_ke` negotiation + `ServerFlightParams.psk_ke` + `build_server_flight` zero-DHE / no-key_share path. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-session-resumption.txt` | Modified | `session resumption - PSK_ONLY` entry removed (now PASS); 2 TLS-1.2 XFAILs retained. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 319 + Testing summary (T120 no longer reserved). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T120 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | T120 phase reference. |

### Build Status (Post T120)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1539/0.
`test-tls13-session-resumption.py` is 5/2 in CI. The PSK story
(resumption, external PSK, `psk_dhe_ke`, `psk_ke`) is now complete.

## Phase T126 ‚ÄĒ mass-fail tlsfuzzer Triage, Batch 1 (2026-05-17)

### Summary

T126 is the first batch of the mass-fail-script triage ‚ÄĒ probing the
~10 `test-tls13-*` scripts that T92 deferred as "mass-fail, need real
protocol fixes". Batch 1 took 4 scripts; each was probed to a
definitive root cause. One produced a real fix; the rest are
documented dispositions.

### Triage results (4 scripts)

| Script | Probe | Root cause | Disposition |
|--------|-------|-----------|-------------|
| `test-tls13-zero-content-type` | 2/8 | **Real bug** ‚ÄĒ a TLS 1.3 record whose decrypted inner plaintext has no non-zero type octet ("zero content type", RFC 8446 ¬ß5.2) is rejected, but with `internal_error`; RFC 8446 ¬ß5.1/¬ß5.4 require `unexpected_message`. | **Fixed** + added to CI. |
| `test-tls13-legacy-version` | 2/10 | Our server is RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.1-correct: with `supported_versions` present it MUST ignore `ClientHello.legacy_version`. tlsfuzzer expects the server to *reject* non-`0x0303` legacy_version values. | **Won't-fix** ‚ÄĒ not added to CI. |
| `test-tls13-non-support` | 0/53 | A TLS-1.2-fallback test (`ExpectServerHello` v3.3 + ServerKeyExchange/ServerHelloDone). Misnamed `test-tls13-*`; still ~2/53 against the TLS 1.2 listener ‚ÄĒ a deeper version-fallback gap. | Deferred to batch 2. |
| `test-tls13-unencrypted-alert` | 2/4 | The server emits an extra `unexpected_message` alert where the script expects a clean close ‚ÄĒ an alert-handling edge. | Deferred to batch 2. |

### Code change

**`crates/hitls-tls/src/alert/mod.rs`** ‚ÄĒ the `tls_error_to_alert`
`RecordError` arm matched only `"unexpected content type"` for
`UnexpectedMessage`; the record layer's actual zero-content-type
error string is `"inner plaintext has no content type"` (and the
unknown-type paths use `"unknown content type"` /
`"unknown inner content type"`). All three are now in the
`UnexpectedMessage` condition, so a zero-content-type record
terminates with `unexpected_message` (RFC 8446 ¬ß5.1) rather than the
`internal_error` fall-through.

The fix closes the 4 handshake-phase conversations
(`test-tls13-zero-content-type.py` 2/8 ‚Üí 6/8). The 2 residual
app-data-phase conversations (`zero content type [and padding]
during application data`) are XFAIL'd with rationale ‚ÄĒ there the
record reaches the `s-server` echo loop rather than the handshake
read path and the server emits ApplicationData where the script
expects the alert; a separate echo-loop-vs-record-reject ordering
investigation, deferred.

### Verification

- `cargo clippy -p hitls-tls --all-features` `-D warnings`: 0;
  `cargo fmt`: clean; `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib -- alert::`:
  15 PASS. `actionlint`: no new issues.
- End-to-end: `test-tls13-zero-content-type.py` through
  `tests/tlsfuzzer/run.sh` ‚ÄĒ **6 PASS / 2 XFAIL / 0 FAIL**,
  `run.sh` exit 0.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/alert/mod.rs` | Modified | `tls_error_to_alert` `RecordError` arm: zero/unknown content-type ‚Üí `unexpected_message`. |
| `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml` | Modified | `test-tls13-zero-content-type.py` added to the curated TLS 1.3 `scripts` array. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-zero-content-type.txt` | Added | 2 app-data-phase XFAILs with rationale. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 320 + Testing summary. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T126 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | Suite count 49 ‚Üí 50; T126 phase reference. |

### Build Status (Post T126)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); alert lib tests 15/0.
Curated tlsfuzzer suite 49 ‚Üí 50 script-runs. Batch 2 of the mass-fail
triage (`non-support`, `unencrypted-alert`, `shuffled-extentions`,
`large-number-of-extensions`, `serverhello-random`, `ecdhe-curves`,
`crfg-curves`, `dhe-shared-secret-padding`) follows.

## Phase I99 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.3 ECDHE for secp384r1 / secp521r1 (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I99 closes a real bug surfaced by the batch-2 mass-fail triage
(`dhe-shared-secret-padding` / `ecdhe-curves` probing): the TLS 1.3
ephemeral key exchange could not perform a secp384r1 or secp521r1
ECDHE, even though the `s-server` advertises those groups in
`supported_groups` and `hitls-crypto::ecdh` has supported P-384/P-521
ECDH since project start.

`crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/key_exchange.rs`'s
`KeyExchange::generate` matched X25519 / X448 / SECP256R1 / SM2P256 /
X25519MLKEM768 and fell through everything else to
`"unsupported named group"`. A client offering only secp384r1 ‚Üí
the server matched it (it *is* in `supported_groups`) ‚Üí `generate`
errored ‚Üí `handshake failed: unsupported named group: NamedGroup(24)`.

This is the same shape as I96 (P-521 ECDSA *signing* worked in
`hitls-crypto` but was never wired into the TLS layer) ‚ÄĒ here it is
P-384/P-521 *ECDH*.

### Code changes (`handshake/key_exchange.rs`)

- `KeyExchangeInner` gains `EcdhP384(Box<EcdhKeyPair>)` and
  `EcdhP521(Box<EcdhKeyPair>)` (mirrors the existing `EcdhP256`).
- `KeyExchange::generate` gains `NamedGroup::SECP384R1` /
  `SECP521R1` arms ‚ÄĒ `EcdhKeyPair::generate(EccCurveId::NistP384 /
  NistP521)`, same shape as the SECP256R1 arm.
- `compute_shared_secret` gains the matching `EcdhP384` / `EcdhP521`
  arms.
- 2 new unit tests: `test_key_exchange_secp384r1` /
  `secp521r1` (generate two keypairs, exchange, assert the shared
  secrets agree ‚ÄĒ 48 / 66 bytes; public keys 97 / 133-byte
  uncompressed points).

`KeyExchange` is shared by the sync + async, client + server
connections, so the fix covers all of them.

### Verification

- `cargo build -p hitls-tls --all-features` (`-D warnings`): clean;
  `cargo test -p hitls-tls --all-features --lib`: **1541 PASS / 0 FAIL**
  (+2 I99 tests); clippy + fmt clean.
- End-to-end (pinned tlsfuzzer, local `s-server`):
  - `test-tls13-dhe-shared-secret-padding.py`: **559/5 ‚Üí 703/3 PASS**
    (the secp384r1 + secp521r1 conversations now pass; the residual 3
    are `ffdhe2048` / `ffdhe3072` ‚ÄĒ pending the FFDHE phase ‚ÄĒ and
    `x448`, not in the default `supported_groups`).
  - `test-tls13-ecdhe-curves.py`: 4/33 ‚Üí **6/33** (secp384r1 +
    secp521r1; the remaining 27 are brainpool curves, not supported).

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/key_exchange.rs` | Modified | `EcdhP384`/`EcdhP521` variants + `generate` / `compute_shared_secret` arms; +2 roundtrip tests. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 321 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI98 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI99. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I99 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | I99 phase reference. |

### Build Status (Post I99)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1541/0. The
batch-2 mass-fail CI wiring (adding `dhe-shared-secret-padding` etc.
to the curated suite) follows as the next Testing phase.

## Phase T127 ‚ÄĒ mass-fail tlsfuzzer Triage, Batch 2 CI Wiring (2026-05-17)

### Summary

T127 closes the ‚Ď† mass-fail-triage task. It wires the batch-2 win
into CI and records the per-script disposition for the remainder.

`test-tls13-dhe-shared-secret-padding.py` joins the curated TLS 1.3
suite. Post-I99 it is **513 PASS / 3 FAIL**; the 3 are XFAIL'd, each a
structural "named group not advertised" gap (not a padding bug):

- `TLS 1.3 with ffdhe2048` / `TLS 1.3 with ffdhe3072` ‚ÄĒ RFC 7919
  FFDHE groups; the TLS layer does not negotiate FFDHE named groups
  (the planned FFDHE phase).
- `TLS 1.3 with x448` ‚ÄĒ X448 ECDHE works, but X448 is not in the
  `s-server` default `supported_groups`.

### mass-fail triage ‚ÄĒ final disposition (all ~10 T92-deferred scripts)

| Script | Outcome |
|--------|---------|
| `zero-content-type` | **Fixed** (T126 ‚ÄĒ alert mapping) + in CI, 6/8. |
| `dhe-shared-secret-padding` | **Fixed** (I99 ‚ÄĒ secp384/521 ECDHE) + in CI (T127), 513/3. |
| `legacy-version` | Won't-fix ‚ÄĒ server is RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.1-correct. |
| `non-support` | Deferred ‚ÄĒ a TLS-1.2-fallback test; revisit with the `--tls auto` phase. |
| `ecdhe-curves` | I99 took it 4/33 ‚Üí 6/33; the residual 27 are brainpool curves (not supported ‚ÄĒ separate "won't-fix without brainpool" item). |
| `crfg-curves` | Deferred ‚ÄĒ CFRG-curve (X25519/X448) edge cases; deeper investigation. |
| `shuffled-extentions` | Deferred ‚ÄĒ ClientHello extension-order handling; deeper investigation. |
| `serverhello-random` | Deferred ‚ÄĒ ServerHello.random validation, largely OpenSSL-specific expectations. |
| `large-number-of-extensions` | Deferred ‚ÄĒ 81/1; the residual is a non-deterministic huge-extension-list parse-limit case, not a clean stable CI add. |
| `unencrypted-alert` | Deferred ‚ÄĒ server emits an extra `unexpected_message` where a clean close is expected; alert-handling edge. |

Two real protocol bugs were found and fixed by this triage (T126
zero-content-type alert mapping, I99 secp384r1/secp521r1 ECDHE); two
scripts (`zero-content-type`, `dhe-shared-secret-padding`) joined CI.
The deferred scripts each need real protocol work (brainpool support,
extension-order handling, OpenSSL-ism analysis) ‚ÄĒ left as documented
follow-ups rather than bulk-XFAIL'd into the gate.

## Phase I100 ‚ÄĒ `s-server --tls auto` Version-Range Listener (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I100 is task ‚Ď° of the server-side tlsfuzzer plan. `s-server` could
only listen as a single pinned protocol ‚ÄĒ `--tls 1.3` *or* `--tls
1.2` ‚ÄĒ because the connection type is chosen up front
(`TlsServerConnection` is TLS 1.3-only, `Tls12ServerConnection` is
TLS 1.2-only). A test client speaking the other version got a
handshake failure. That blocks any tlsfuzzer script that wants to
exercise *both* versions on one endpoint (cross-version session
resumption, downgrade-protection).

`--tls auto` adds a third mode: one listener that decides the
protocol **per connection** by peeking the pending ClientHello.

### How it works

The TLS version a client actually wants is carried in the
`supported_versions` extension (RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.1), not the legacy
record/`ClientHello.legacy_version` field. So for each accepted TCP
connection in `auto` mode the server:

1. `TcpStream::peek`s the socket ‚ÄĒ a **non-consuming** read, so the
   bytes stay in the kernel buffer for the real connection to read
   normally. Retries a few times (8 √ó 20 ms) in case the first peek
   catches a partial write.
2. Walks the raw record + ClientHello far enough to find the
   `supported_versions` extension and checks whether it lists
   `0x0304` (TLS 1.3).
3. Constructs `TlsServerConnection` (1.3) or `Tls12ServerConnection`
   (1.2) accordingly ‚ÄĒ the connection then reads the un-consumed
   ClientHello from byte 0 as usual.

The parser (`client_hello_offers_tls13`) is pure (`&[u8] -> bool`)
and every step is bounds-checked: a truncated / malformed / absent
buffer returns `false`, so a wrong guess can only ever fall back to
the TLS 1.2 handler ‚ÄĒ whose own record parser then rejects a genuine
malformed handshake with a proper alert. No `panic!`, no silent
state corruption.

### Code changes (`crates/hitls-cli`)

- `s_server.rs`:
  - `--tls` value validation extended to `1.2` / `1.3` / `auto`
    (with a clear error for anything else); the
    `--key-update`/`--post-handshake-auth` 1.2-rejection check is
    unchanged (still legal under `auto` ‚ÄĒ they only fire when a 1.3
    client connects).
  - `run()` refactored: the mTLS-CA bundle, ticket key, and PSK are
    resolved once into locals up front (so a bad path still fails at
    startup); per-version cipher-list + version selection is pulled
    into a `make_config(want_tls13: bool) -> TlsConfig` closure.
    `--tls auto` builds one config per version (`cfg_tls13` +
    `cfg_tls12`); the pinned modes build only the one they need.
  - New `default_tls13_suites()` / `default_tls12_suites()` helpers
    (the T96/T105 default cipher lists, previously inline).
  - New `peek_client_wants_tls13(&TcpStream)` (the peek loop) +
    `client_hello_offers_tls13(&[u8])` (the pure parser).
  - The accept loop computes `want_tls13` (constant for pinned
    modes, peek for `auto`) and dispatches.
- `main.rs`: `s-server --tls` doc comment now documents `auto`.

### Tests

7 new unit tests in `s_server.rs` driving `client_hello_offers_tls13`
via a synthetic-ClientHello builder: TLS 1.3 listed ‚Üí true; TLS 1.2
only ‚Üí false; no `supported_versions` extension ‚Üí false;
`supported_versions` found after an unrelated extension ‚Üí true;
truncated buffers at 5 offsets ‚Üí false; non-Handshake record + empty
buffer ‚Üí false. Plus `test_s_server_auto_version_accepted` (the
`auto` string passes version validation).

### Verification

- `cargo build -p hitls-cli --all-features` clean; `cargo test -p
  hitls-cli` s_server module **23 PASS / 0 FAIL** (+7 I100 tests).
- End-to-end smoke test against a live `s-server --tls auto`
  listener with an RSA cert:
  - Rust `s-client --tls 1.3` ‚Üí negotiated **TLS 1.3** (0x1302).
  - Rust `s-client --tls 1.2` ‚Üí negotiated **TLS 1.2** (0xC02F).
  - `openssl s_client -tls1_3` ‚Üí `TLSv1.3, TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384`.
  - `openssl s_client -tls1_2` ‚Üí `TLSv1.2, ECDHE-RSA-AES128-GCM-SHA256`.
  Both implementations, both versions, one port.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-cli/src/s_server.rs` | Modified | `--tls auto` mode: `make_config` closure, `peek_client_wants_tls13` + `client_hello_offers_tls13`, per-version config dispatch; +7 unit tests. |
| `crates/hitls-cli/src/main.rs` | Modified | `s-server --tls` doc comment documents `auto`. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 323 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI99 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI100. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I100 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I100)

`hitls-cli` builds clean (`-D warnings`); s_server tests 23/0. The
`--tls auto` listener is ready for cross-version tlsfuzzer coverage ‚ÄĒ
task ‚ĎĘ (TLS 1.2 tlsfuzzer script breadth) can point scripts at it.

---

## Phase I101 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 Server-Conformance: `signature_algorithms`-Absent Default + `ec_point_formats` Echo (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I101 is the foundational fix of task ‚ĎĘ (TLS 1.2 tlsfuzzer script
breadth). Probing the curated tlsfuzzer corpus against a local
`s-server --tls 1.2` measured **453 of 889 connections** failing with
`handshake failed: no common signature scheme`, plus a second gap
behind it. Two TLS 1.2 ServerHello / ServerKeyExchange conformance
bugs, fixed here as Part A + Part B.

### Part A ‚ÄĒ `signature_algorithms`-absent default (RFC 5246 ¬ß7.4.1.4.1)

The `signature_algorithms` extension is **OPTIONAL** in a TLS 1.2
ClientHello. `select_signature_scheme_tls12` looped the server's key
candidates against the client's advertised list and, on an empty
list, fell through to `Err("no common signature scheme")` ‚ÄĒ so the
server rejected every TLS 1.2 client that omitted the extension (a
common tlsfuzzer pattern; the `sanity` conversation of most TLS 1.2
scripts builds a minimal ClientHello without it).

RFC 5246 ¬ß7.4.1.4.1 requires the server to "behave as if the client
had sent the value `{sha1,rsa}`" (or `{sha1,ecdsa}` / `{sha1,dsa}`).
This is **strictly enforced by peers**: tlsfuzzer's
`ExpectServerKeyExchange` (`expect.py`) sets
`valid_sig_algs = [(sha1, rsa)]` when the ClientHello carried no
`signature_algorithms`, and tlslite-ng rejects anything else with
`TLSIllegalParameterException: Server selected invalid signature
algorithm`. A pragmatic SHA-256 fallback was tried first and
empirically rejected by tlslite-ng ‚ÄĒ so the literal `{sha1,*}`
default is the only interoperable option.

- `select_signature_scheme_tls12`: an empty `client_schemes` now
  returns `RSA_PKCS1_SHA1` (RSA) / `ECDSA_SHA1` (ECDSA); other key
  types keep their single scheme (degenerate with no-`sigalgs`).
- `sign_ske_data`: added the `RSA_PKCS1_SHA1` and `ECDSA_SHA1` arms
  (+ a `compute_sha1` helper). SHA-1 in the SKE signature is confined
  to this one legacy-compatibility path ‚ÄĒ reached only when a client
  explicitly signals it supports nothing beyond the TLS 1.2
  mandatory-to-implement floor ‚ÄĒ and signs ephemeral, handshake-fresh
  data (`client_random || server_random || params`), never a
  long-lived artifact. This is exactly what OpenSSL does.

### Part B ‚ÄĒ `ec_point_formats` echo (RFC 8422 ¬ß5.1.2)

When the client offers the `ec_point_formats` extension and the
server selects an ECC (ECDHE) cipher suite, the ServerHello SHOULD
carry `ec_point_formats` listing `uncompressed`. The TLS 1.2
ServerHello builder never emitted it, so tlsfuzzer's ECC scripts
(`ExpectServerHello` with a strict `ec_point_formats` entry) aborted
the handshake at the client.

- New `client_offered_ec_point_formats` flag (parsed from the
  ClientHello, mirroring `client_offered_etm`).
- The ServerHello echoes `build_ec_point_formats()` when the client
  offered it and `params.kx_alg` is `Ecdhe` / `EcdhePsk` /
  `EcdheAnon`. (`build_ec_point_formats` / `parse_ec_point_formats`
  already existed in `extensions_codec`.)

### Verification

- `cargo build`/`clippy -p hitls-tls --all-features` (`-D warnings`):
  clean; `fmt` clean; `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib`:
  **1543 PASS / 0 FAIL** (+2 I101 tests ‚ÄĒ
  `select_signature_scheme_tls12_empty_{rsa,ecdsa}`).
- End-to-end (pinned tlsfuzzer, local `s-server --tls 1.2`):
  - `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange.py`: 0/3 ‚Üí **2/3 PASS**.
  - `test-ecdhe-padded-shared-secret.py`: 0/3 ‚Üí **2/3 PASS**.
  - `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange-with-bad-messages.py` (curated):
    was 0/8 all-XFAIL (every conversation blocked on the missing
    `ec_point_formats` echo) ‚Üí now **3/8 PASS** (both `sanity` runs +
    `truncated ecdh_Yc value`); the xfail list trimmed 7 ‚Üí 5 entries.
- Regression: all 9 curated TLS 1.2 scripts still `run.sh` exit 0;
  the 4 mTLS-1.2 scripts use a lenient `ExpectServerHello(version=
  (3,3))` (no extension-list check), so the new `ec_point_formats`
  echo cannot break them.

### Residual (next phase)

With the handshake unblocked, `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange-with-bad-
messages.py` now reaches its actual checks and surfaces two further
TLS 1.2 bad-`ClientKeyExchange` gaps (kept XFAIL): invalid ECDH
points alert `internal_error` instead of `illegal_parameter`
(RFC 4492 ¬ß5.4), and a padding-extended ClientKeyExchange is accepted
rather than rejected. Each is its own follow-up phase.

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1543/0. Task ‚ĎĘ

## Phase T128 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 tlsfuzzer Curated-Suite Breadth (2026-05-17)

### Summary

T128 is the curate-and-bank step of task ‚ĎĘ. With I101 unblocking the
TLS 1.2 sanity handshake, the tlsfuzzer corpus was re-probed against
a local `s-server --tls 1.2` and the scripts that now pass cleanly
(or near-cleanly with a small, stable XFAIL set) were curated into
the CI `scripts_12` array ‚ÄĒ **9 ‚Üí 14 scripts**.

This is a deliberately *bounded* phase: it banks the concrete,
verified breadth gain rather than chasing every TLS 1.2 conformance
bug. Scripts that need deeper fixes (large XFAIL sets) were left for
documented follow-ups, mirroring the ‚Ď† mass-fail triage discipline of
not bulk-XFAIL'ing into the gate.

### Scripts curated (5 new)

| Script | Result | args | xfail |
|--------|--------|------|-------|
| `test-aes-gcm-nonces` | 6/6 | `-d` | ‚ÄĒ |
| `test-encrypt-then-mac` | 3/3 | `-d` | ‚ÄĒ |
| `test-version-numbers` | 8/9 | `-d` | 1 |
| `test-zero-length-data` | 2/3 | `-d` | 1 |
| `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange` | 2/3 | ‚ÄĒ | 1 |

All five were verified on the **full conversation set** (`-n 9999`),
not just the default CI sample, so the monthly `SWEEP_N` run stays
green. The `-d` args file makes a script negotiate ECDHE (rather than
its default static-RSA key exchange, which `s-server` does not
offer); `run.sh` auto-applies it.

### XFAIL entries (3, one each ‚ÄĒ all stable, documented)

- `test-version-numbers` ‚Üí `very low version (0, 0)`: the server
  does not reject a ClientHello whose `legacy_version` is below
  SSLv3 (no version floor check).
- `test-zero-length-data` ‚Üí `zero-length app data`: the TLS 1.2 read
  path answers a zero-length ApplicationData record with
  `close_notify` instead of transparently skipping it (the TLS 1.3
  path already passes it through ‚ÄĒ Phase T103).
- `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange` ‚Üí `ECDHE w/o extension`: a
  ClientHello offering ECDHE with no `supported_groups` gets
  `handshake_failure` (no default-curve fallback).

### Not curated

`test-ecdhe-padded-shared-secret` ‚ÄĒ the full set has **16 failures**,
almost all `Protocol (3, 0/1/2)` (SSLv3 / TLS 1.0 / TLS 1.1, which
the server intentionally does not support ‚ÄĒ it is TLS 1.2+) or
SSLv2-compatible ClientHellos / the un-advertised x448 group.
XFAIL'ing 16 conversations would be bulk-suppression, not a clean
win, so the script is left out.

### Verification

- All 5 curated scripts: `run.sh` exit 0 against a local
  `s-server --tls 1.2` (0 FAIL / 0 XPASS).
- Regression: the 9 pre-existing `scripts_12` entries unaffected.
- `tlsfuzzer.yml` is workflow-only; CI runs no `actionlint`, and the
  change adds only array elements + a comment.

## Phase I102 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.3 FFDHE Key Exchange (RFC 7919) (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I102 is task ‚Ď£ of the server-side tlsfuzzer plan. The TLS 1.3
ephemeral `KeyExchange` (`handshake/key_exchange.rs`) supported the EC
groups (X25519, X448, P-256/384/521) and the X25519MLKEM768 hybrid,
but had **no finite-field-DHE variant** ‚ÄĒ so a client offering only
an `ffdhe*` group (RFC 7919) hit `unsupported named group`. This is
the same "crypto-has-it / TLS-layer-missing-it" shape as I99: the
`hitls-crypto::dh` primitive (RFC 7919 params + DH keypair) has
existed since project start; only the `KeyExchange` wiring was absent.

### Code changes (`handshake/key_exchange.rs`)

- New `FfdheState { kp: DhKeyPair, params: DhParams }`, boxed in a new
  `KeyExchangeInner::Ffdhe(Box<FfdheState>)` variant (boxed because
  `DhParams` carries the multi-kilobit group prime ‚ÄĒ keeps the enum's
  other variants small).
- `ffdhe_param_id(NamedGroup) -> Option<DhParamId>` maps the 5 RFC
  7919 groups (ffdhe2048/3072/4096/6144/8192) to their `DhParamId`.
- `KeyExchange::generate` handles any FFDHE group up front:
  `DhParams::from_group` ‚Üí `DhKeyPair::generate` ‚Üí `public_key_bytes`
  (the DH public value Y, big-endian, left-padded to the prime
  length ‚ÄĒ RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.8.1).
- `compute_shared_secret` gains the `Ffdhe` arm: `Z = peer_Y^x mod p`,
  left-padded to the prime length (RFC 8446 ¬ß7.4.1) with peer-key
  range validation ‚ÄĒ both handled inside
  `DhKeyPair::compute_shared_secret`.
- FFDHE is **not** a KEM, so it flows through the same
  generate / `compute_shared_secret` path as ECDHE ‚ÄĒ no server
  handshake change beyond `KeyExchange` accepting the group.
- 3 unit tests: ffdhe2048 + ffdhe3072 roundtrip (256 / 384-byte
  key_share + shared secret), and an out-of-range peer-key rejection.

### `s-server` change

The default `supported_groups` gains the 5 FFDHE groups **after** the
EC groups (lowest preference ‚ÄĒ an ECDHE-capable client still gets the
faster EC exchange) plus **X448** (already supported by `KeyExchange`
but previously not advertised).

### Verification

- `cargo build` / `clippy -p hitls-tls -p hitls-cli` (`-D warnings`):
  clean; `fmt` clean; `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib`
  **1546 PASS / 0 FAIL** (+3 I102 tests); `hitls-cli` 174/0.
- End-to-end (pinned tlsfuzzer, local `s-server`):
  - `test-tls13-dhe-shared-secret-padding.py` (curated): was
    513 PASS / 3 XFAIL (ffdhe2048, ffdhe3072, x448) ‚Üí **2203 PASS /
    0 FAIL** on the full `-n 9999` set. XFAIL file removed.
  - `test-tls13-psk_dhe_ke.py` (curated): was 3 PASS / 1 XFAIL
    (`ffdhe2048`) ‚Üí **4/4 PASS**. XFAIL file removed.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/key_exchange.rs` | Modified | `Ffdhe` variant + `FfdheState` + `ffdhe_param_id` + `generate` / `compute_shared_secret` FFDHE arms; +3 unit tests. |
| `crates/hitls-cli/src/s_server.rs` | Modified | Default `supported_groups` += 5 FFDHE groups + X448. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-dhe-shared-secret-padding.txt` | Removed | Script now fully clean (2203/0). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-psk_dhe_ke.txt` | Removed | Script now fully clean (4/4). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 326 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI101 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI102. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I102 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | I102 phase reference. |

### Build Status (Post I102)

`hitls-tls` + `hitls-cli` build clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests
1546/0. Task ‚Ď£ (FFDHE) complete for TLS 1.3. Curating the TLS 1.2
FFDHE scripts (`test-ffdhe-negotiation` / `test-ffdhe-expected-params`)
is a separate follow-up ‚ÄĒ they exercise the `DHE_RSA` *cipher
suites*, which the `s-server` default TLS 1.2 cipher list does not
yet offer (it is ECDHE-only). Next: task ‚Ď§ (`s-server` DTLS mode).

## Phase I103 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 ClientKeyExchange Hardening (2026-05-17)

### Summary

I103 is the first of the post-‚Ď£ TLS 1.2 conformance-fix batch ‚ÄĒ the
small, well-scoped gaps documented as follow-ups in I101 / T128. It
closes both residual XFAILs of the curated
`test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange-with-bad-messages.py`, two distinct
TLS 1.2 ClientKeyExchange-handling bugs.

### Part A ‚ÄĒ invalid ECDHE point ‚Üí `illegal_parameter`

When a client's ClientKeyExchange carried an invalid ECDHE public
point (not on the curve, the identity, or out of range),
`process_client_key_exchange` propagated the `compute_shared_secret`
failure as a bare `TlsError::CryptoError`, which `tls_error_to_alert`
mapped to `internal_error`. RFC 4492 ¬ß5.4 / RFC 8422 require
`illegal_parameter` ‚ÄĒ the client controls the point, so a bad one is
a parameter error, not a server fault.

The `Ecdhe` and `EcdheAnon` arms of `process_client_key_exchange` now
`map_err` the `compute_shared_secret` failure to a `HandshakeFailed`
message containing `illegal_parameter` (routed by `tls_error_to_alert`
to `AlertDescription::IllegalParameter`).

### Part B ‚ÄĒ reject padding-extended ClientKeyExchange

`decode_client_key_exchange` checked `body.len() < 1 + point_len`
(rejecting a *truncated* body) but not the over-long case, so a
ClientKeyExchange with trailing bytes after the length-prefixed ECDH
point was silently accepted and the handshake continued. RFC 4492
¬ß5.7: the body is exactly `{ecdh_Yc opaque<1..2^8-1>}`. The check is
now `body.len() != 1 + point_len` ‚Üí `decode_error`.

### Verification

- `cargo build` / `clippy -p hitls-tls` (`-D warnings`) clean; `fmt`
  clean; `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib` **1548 PASS / 0 FAIL** (+2
  ‚ÄĒ `decode_client_key_exchange` trailing-bytes + truncated tests).
- End-to-end (pinned tlsfuzzer, local `s-server --tls 1.2`):
  `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange-with-bad-messages.py` was
  3 PASS / 5 XFAIL ‚Üí **8 PASS / 0 FAIL** on the full `-n 9999` set
  (the 4 `invalid point (...)` conversations now get
  `illegal_parameter`; `padded Client Key Exchange` now gets
  `decode_error`). XFAIL file removed ‚ÄĒ the script is fully clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/codec12.rs` | Modified | Part B: `decode_client_key_exchange` strict `len == 1 + point_len`; +2 unit tests. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server12.rs` | Modified | Part A: `Ecdhe` / `EcdheAnon` CKE arms map an invalid-point failure to `illegal_parameter`. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange-with-bad-messages.txt` | Removed | Script now fully clean (8/8). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 327 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI102 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI103. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I103 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I103)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1548/0. First of
the TLS 1.2 conformance-fix batch done. Next (I104): ClientHello
version-floor check + zero-length-AppData pass-through +
no-`supported_groups` ECDHE default-curve fallback.

## Phase I104 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 ClientHello / Record-Layer Conformance (2026-05-18)

### Summary

I104 is the second (and final) phase of the post-‚Ď£ TLS 1.2
conformance-fix batch. It closes the last 3 XFAILs of the
T128-curated TLS 1.2 scripts ‚ÄĒ three independent small gaps.

### Part A ‚ÄĒ ClientHello `legacy_version` floor (`test-version-numbers`)

`process_client_hello` never validated the ClientHello's
`legacy_version`: a `(0,0)` version was accepted and a normal
ServerHello sent. RFC 5246 ¬ßE.1 ‚ÄĒ when the client sends no
`supported_versions` extension, `legacy_version` is the highest
version it supports; a value below the server minimum (TLS 1.2 =
`0x0303`) cannot be satisfied. The handler now reads the first 2
body bytes and, when `supported_versions` is absent (RFC 8446
¬ß4.2.1 ‚ÄĒ it MUST be ignored when the extension is present) and
`legacy_version < 0x0303`, aborts with `protocol_version`. A
*too-high* version is unaffected ‚ÄĒ still clamped down to TLS 1.2.

### Part B ‚ÄĒ zero-length ApplicationData record (`test-zero-length-data`)

A zero-length ApplicationData record made `read()` compute
`n = min(buf.len(), 0) = 0` and `return Ok(0)` ‚ÄĒ which the caller
reads as end-of-stream. RFC 5246 ¬ß6.2.1 permits empty records; they
carry no data and must be skipped, not treated as close. All **4**
TLS 1.2 read paths ‚ÄĒ sync/async √ó server/client
(`connection12/{server,client}.rs`, `connection12_async.rs`) ‚ÄĒ now
`continue` on an empty `ApplicationData` plaintext. Mirrors the
TLS 1.3 fix (Phase T103).

### Part C ‚ÄĒ ECDHE without `supported_groups` (`test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange`)

`negotiate_group` aborted with `handshake_failure` when the client
offered ECDHE cipher suites but **no** `supported_groups`
(elliptic_curves) extension. RFC 4492 ¬ß5.1 ‚ÄĒ a client MAY omit it,
and the server is then free to choose any curve it supports. The
function now, on an empty client list, picks the universally
interoperable `secp256r1` (else the highest-preference non-FFDHE
server group).

### Verification

- `cargo build` / `clippy -p hitls-tls` (`-D warnings`) clean; `fmt`
  clean; `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib` **1549 PASS / 0 FAIL** (+1
  ‚ÄĒ `negotiate_group` no-client-extension test).
- End-to-end (pinned tlsfuzzer, local `s-server --tls 1.2`), full
  `-n 9999` conversation set:
  - `test-version-numbers.py` 8/9 ‚Üí **9/9** (`very low version
    (0, 0)` now gets `protocol_version`).
  - `test-zero-length-data.py` 2/3 ‚Üí **3/3** (`zero-length app
    data` now skipped, not read as EOF).
  - `test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange.py` 2/3 ‚Üí **3/3** (`ECDHE w/o
    extension` now negotiates secp256r1).
- The 3 corresponding `xfail/*.txt` files removed ‚ÄĒ all 3 scripts
  fully clean.
- Regression: all **14** curated `scripts_12` still `run.sh` exit 0.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server12.rs` | Modified | Part A: `legacy_version` floor check in `process_client_hello`. Part C: `negotiate_group` empty-client-list fallback; +1 unit test. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection12/server.rs` | Modified | Part B: skip zero-length ApplicationData (sync server). |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection12/client.rs` | Modified | Part B: skip zero-length ApplicationData (sync client). |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/connection12_async.rs` | Modified | Part B: skip zero-length ApplicationData (async server + client). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-version-numbers.txt` | Removed | Script now fully clean (9/9). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-zero-length-data.txt` | Removed | Script now fully clean (3/3). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange.txt` | Removed | Script now fully clean (3/3). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 328 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI103 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI104. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I104 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I104)

`hitls-tls` builds clean (`-D warnings`); lib tests 1549/0. The
post-‚Ď£ TLS 1.2 conformance-fix batch (I103 + I104) is complete ‚ÄĒ all
5 documented follow-up gaps closed; the 5 TLS 1.2 scripts curated in

## Phase T129 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 DHE / FFDHE tlsfuzzer Curation (2026-05-18)

### Summary

T129 is the closing phase of the server-side tlsfuzzer effort. It
curates the two `test-ffdhe-*` scripts ‚ÄĒ the last on-mission,
bounded coverage gain ‚ÄĒ completing the FFDHE story symmetrically
with Phase I102 (which wired TLS 1.3 FFDHE).

### Enabling change

`test-ffdhe-negotiation` / `test-ffdhe-expected-params` hard-code
`TLS_DHE_RSA_*` cipher suites (they verify finite-field DHE_RSA key
exchange). The `s-server` TLS 1.2 default cipher list
(`default_tls12_suites`) was ECDHE-only, so these scripts got
`handshake_failure` at the sanity step.

6 DHE_RSA suites were added ‚ÄĒ `TLS_DHE_RSA_WITH_AES_{128,256}_GCM_*`
and `..._CBC_SHA` / `..._CBC_SHA256` ‚ÄĒ **listed last**, so an
ECDHE-capable client still negotiates the faster EC exchange (server
preference order). No handshake-code change was needed: the TLS 1.2
server already implements `KeyExchangeAlg::Dhe` with RFC 7919 FFDHE
params (`negotiate_ffdhe_group`, exercised by the existing
`test_tls12_dhe_*_handshake` unit tests).

### Scripts curated (2; `scripts_12` 14 ‚Üí 16)

- `test-ffdhe-expected-params` ‚ÄĒ **3/3**, no XFAIL.
- `test-ffdhe-negotiation` ‚ÄĒ **38/41**. The 3 XFAILs are one
  coherent gap: TLS 1.2 cipher-suite / named-group *co-negotiation*.
  The server picks a cipher first, then a group; having picked a
  DHE_RSA suite with no usable FFDHE group it forces FFDHE2048
  instead of stepping back to a non-DHE suite (`fallback to
  non-ffdhe` √ó2) or aborting (`no overlap between groups`). Closing
  it needs real co-negotiation logic (and a static-RSA fallback
  suite) ‚ÄĒ a documented follow-up, not a curation item.

Both verified on the full conversation set (`-n 9999`); neither
needs an `args` file (the scripts default to `TLS_DHE_RSA_*`).

### Verification

- `cargo build` / `clippy --workspace --all-features --all-targets`
  (`-D warnings`) clean; `fmt` clean.
- `run.sh`: `test-ffdhe-expected-params` 3/3/0, `test-ffdhe-
  negotiation` 38 PASS / 3 XFAIL / 0 FAIL ‚ÄĒ both exit 0.
- Regression: all **16** curated `scripts_12` still `run.sh` exit 0
  ‚ÄĒ the new DHE_RSA suites (lowest preference) do not change what
  the existing ECDHE-oriented scripts negotiate.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-cli/src/s_server.rs` | Modified | `default_tls12_suites()` += 6 DHE_RSA suites (last). |
| `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml` | Modified | 2 scripts added to `scripts_12` (14 ‚Üí 16). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-ffdhe-negotiation.txt` | Added | 3 XFAILs (cipher/group co-negotiation gap) + rationale. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 329 + Testing summary T1‚ÄďT128 ‚Üí T1‚ÄďT129. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T129 prompt + result entry. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | Curated-script count + T129 reference. |

### Build Status (Post T129)

No handshake code changed ‚ÄĒ a `s-server` cipher-list addition +
workflow + xfail + docs. Curated TLS 1.2 suite 14 ‚Üí 16; full curated
suite 56 ‚Üí 58 script-runs. **The server-side tlsfuzzer effort is at
its closing milestone**: plan tasks ‚Ď†‚Äď‚Ď£ delivered, the post-‚Ď£ TLS 1.2
conformance batch (I103/I104) done, FFDHE curation (T129) complete.
Remaining items are documented follow-ups: TLS 1.2 cipher/group
co-negotiation (the 3 `ffdhe-negotiation` XFAILs) and task ‚Ď§ (DTLS
s-server ‚ÄĒ `docs/dtls-s-server-plan.md`; tlsfuzzer has no DTLS
scripts, so it is an independent CLI-feature item).
