## Phase I117 ‚ÄĒ PKCS#12 SHA-2 MAC Support (RFC 7292 ¬ß4) (2026-05-24)

### Summary

The `hitls-pki` PKCS#12 (PFX) MAC integrity path was hardcoded to
SHA-1. `pkcs12_kdf` ran the RFC 7292 Appendix B KDF with SHA-1, and
`verify_mac` **discarded** the MacData `DigestInfo` algorithm OID
(`let _alg = ‚Ä¶`), always deriving a 20-byte SHA-1 HMAC key. As a
result, any PFX carrying a **SHA-2 MAC** ‚ÄĒ which the openHiTLS C
implementation emits by default ‚ÄĒ failed integrity verification with
`MAC verification failed (wrong password?)` *even with the correct
password*. This surfaced during the T113 `pki/pkcs12` test migration:
every `SDV_PKCS12_PARSE_P12` fixture failed to parse.

This phase generalises the MAC path over the hash while leaving the
PBES2/PBKDF2 *encryption* path (already hash-agnostic) untouched.

### What changed (`crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs`)

- New `P12MacHash` enum (SHA-1 / SHA-224 / SHA-256 / SHA-384 /
  SHA-512) with `from_oid` (id-sha1 `1.3.14.3.2.26` + id-sha224
  `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.2.4` matched by dotted form ‚ÄĒ no `known::`
  helper; sha256/384/512 via `known::`), and `digest()` / `hash()` /
  `hash_concat()` / `hmac()` helpers built on the `Digest` trait
  (`output_size()` / `block_size()` drive the KDF lengths).
- `pkcs12_kdf` takes a `hash: P12MacHash` parameter; internal
  `hash_len` / `block_size` come from the hash instead of the
  hardcoded `20` / `64`.
- `verify_mac` now **reads** the MacData `DigestInfo` algorithm OID,
  maps it to a `P12MacHash`, and runs the KDF + HMAC under that hash.
- Encode side (`to_der`) continues to emit a **SHA-1** MAC ‚ÄĒ the RFC
  7292 baseline, widely interoperable; round-trip unaffected.
- Removed the now-redundant standalone `sha1_hash` / `sha1_hash_concat`
  / `hmac_sha1` helpers (folded into `P12MacHash`).

Security properties preserved: the MAC tag comparison stays
constant-time (`subtle::ConstantTimeEq` with a length pre-check), and
the existing zeroize discipline is unchanged.

### Verification

- New unit test `test_pkcs12_kdf_sha256_lengths` (SHA-256 KDF yields
  32-byte output and differs from SHA-1; SHA-384/512 output lengths).
- De-risk against the openHiTLS C `pki/pkcs12` `.p12` fixtures
  (`SDV_PKCS12_PARSE_P12_TC001`, password `123456`): all 5 rows now
  parse ‚ÄĒ 3 SHA-256-MAC + 2 SHA-224-MAC ‚ÄĒ with the entity certificate
  matching the expected DER (previously **0/5**, all MAC-verify
  failures).
- No regression: `hitls-pki` **455 lib** (incl. the new test) **+
  1123 migrated + 1 doc** PASS; `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean;
  `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features
  --all-targets` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs` | Modified | `P12MacHash` enum + hash-parameterised `pkcs12_kdf` + OID-aware `verify_mac`; SHA-1/224/256/384/512 MAC verification; SHA-256 KDF unit test. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 353 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI116 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI117. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I117 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I117)

Production-code change in `hitls-pki` only (PKCS#12 MAC path).
Unblocks the T113 `pki/pkcs12` SDV migration (`PARSE_P12` /
`PARSE_MACDATA` / `PARSE_AUTHSAFE` families that were failing MAC
verification). Follow-up (test-enhanced slot): migrate the now-passing
`pki/pkcs12` parse families.

## Phase I118 ‚ÄĒ CMS ML-DSA SignerInfo Verification (FIPS 204) (2026-05-25)

### Summary

`pki/cms` SignerInfo verification could not verify any ML-DSA signature
‚ÄĒ the dispatch fell through to "unsupported sig alg", and once that was
fixed the signature still failed to verify. This blocked the 6 T113
`pki/cms` ML-DSA migration cases (`#[ignore]`d in the SignedData-verify
phase). Three defects, fixed together:

1. **Wrong OID.** `known::ml_dsa_44/65/87()` carried the obsolete IETF
   draft Dilithium arc `1.3.6.1.4.1.2.267.12.*` despite the doc-comment
   claiming FIPS 204. openHiTLS C (and the ecosystem) emit the NIST CSOR
   ids `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.3.{17,18,19}` (id-ml-dsa-44/65/87).
   Retargeted the three constants. They are consumed only by
   `cms/mod.rs` (verify dispatch + one OID-distinctness test), so the
   change is self-contained.

2. **Wrong signed bytes.** `verify_signature_with_cert` fed ML-DSA the
   pre-computed *digest* ‚ÄĒ correct for RSA/ECDSA (hash-then-sign), wrong
   for ML-DSA, which hashes internally. `verify_signer_info` now also
   threads the raw `signed_message` (the DER `SET` re-encoding of
   signedAttrs, or the content when absent); the ML-DSA branch verifies
   over the message, RSA/ECDSA/EdDSA still use the digest.

3. **Pure-mode domain separator.** CMS uses *pure* ML-DSA (FIPS 204
   ¬ß5.2) with an empty context. `hitls_crypto::mldsa::mldsa_verify` is
   the *internal* variant (őľ = H(tr ‚ÄĖ M)), so the message handed to it
   is prefixed with `0x00 ‚ÄĖ len(ctx)=0x00` to form the pure-mode input.

### Investigation (de-risk)

Against the openHiTLS C `cms/signeddata/mldsa/mldsa44` fixture: the
entity ML-DSA-44 pubkey parsed to the expected 1312 bytes and the
signature to 2420 bytes, so key/sig extraction was correct. Trying the
message candidates pinned the convention: bare `enc_set(attrs)` ‚Üí
`false`, but `0x00 ‚ÄĖ 0x00 ‚ÄĖ enc_set(attrs)` ‚Üí `true`, confirming pure
ML-DSA with empty context over the SET-re-encoded signedAttrs.

### Verification

- C-fixture interop (`feature = mldsa`): `mldsa44/65/87` attached +
  detached SignedData all verify `Ok(true)`; a mutated detached message
  is rejected.
- No regression: hitls-pki **457 lib + 1142 migrated + 1 doc** PASS;
  hitls-utils **78** PASS; hitls-tls **1108 lib** PASS.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `clippy -D warnings` clean under
  both `--no-default-features --features x509,cms` (the no-mldsa path
  guards `signed_message` against unused-var) and `--all-features
  --all-targets`.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-utils/src/oid/mod.rs` | Modified | `ml_dsa_44/65/87()` retargeted to NIST FIPS 204 CSOR OIDs. |
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/cms/mod.rs` | Modified | `verify_signer_info` threads `signed_message`; `verify_signature_with_cert` gains the message param + ML-DSA pure-mode (`00 00` domain prefix) verify over the message; no-mldsa unused-var guard; 3 EdDSA unit-test call sites updated to the new arity. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 354 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI117 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI118. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I118 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I118)

Production-code change in `hitls-pki` + `hitls-utils` (CMS ML-DSA verify
path + ML-DSA OIDs). Unblocks the 6 T113 `pki/cms` ML-DSA SignedData
migration cases. Follow-up (test-enhanced slot): un-`#[ignore]` those 6
`tc_cms_sd_verify_mldsa*` tests.

---

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/verify` chain-structure family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Extends T113 Phase C with **5 more TCs** from
`pki/verify/test_suite_sdv_x509_vfy.c` ‚ÄĒ the chain-structure
family covering RFC 5280 ¬ß6.1 path-building bounds: depth limit
(`VFY_DEPTH_CHAINLEN_*`), issuer-DN continuity
(`VFY_CHAIN_SUBJECT_ISSUER_MISMATCH`), trust-anchor presence
(`VFY_TRUST_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND`), and loop detection
(`VFY_CHAIN_LOOP_DEPTH`). Anchored on the `cert/chain/bcExt/depth_suite/`
and `cert/chain/certVer/` fixture corpora (already mirrored in ¬ß4.1).

Coverage delta: 1085 ‚Üí **1090 emitted**. With I115/I116 having
closed the previous 3 AKI/SKI + EXT ignores, the migrated suite is
now **1090 PASS / 0 ignored** ‚ÄĒ no remaining `#[ignore]` in the
PKI migration.

### What landed

5 new tests appended to `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs`:

| Rust test | C source | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| `tc_line3729_x509_vfy_depth_chainlen_pass` | `VFY_DEPTH_CHAINLEN_PASS_TC001` | PASS ‚ÄĒ 3-cert chain within depth limit |
| `tc_line3866_x509_vfy_depth_chainlen_fail` | `VFY_DEPTH_CHAINLEN_FAIL_TC002` | `MaxDepthExceeded` ‚ÄĒ 4-cert chain over limit |
| `tc_line4116_x509_vfy_chain_subject_issuer_mismatch_fail` | `VFY_CHAIN_SUBJECT_ISSUER_MISMATCH_FAIL_TC001` | `IssuerNotFound` ‚ÄĒ no candidate's subject matches the leaf's issuer DN |
| `tc_line4163_x509_vfy_trust_anchor_not_found_fail` | `VFY_TRUST_ANCHOR_NOT_FOUND_FAIL_TC002` | error ‚ÄĒ only a fake root is trusted; the chain's real self-signed root is not in the trust store |
| `tc_line4215_x509_vfy_chain_loop_depth_fail` | `VFY_CHAIN_LOOP_DEPTH_FAIL_TC001` | `MaxDepthExceeded` ‚ÄĒ `certVer_cycle_a`/`_b` sign each other, the build never reaches a trusted root and trips the depth cap |

### Finding ‚ÄĒ `max_depth` convention difference (handled in-test, not a gap)

The C `maxDepth` counts the **total certificates** allowed in the
path; Rust's `CertificateVerifier::set_max_depth` counts the
**intermediate CA links** followed during building (it errors when
`chain.len()` reaches the cap mid-build, before pushing the final
root). So the C `maxDepth = 3` that admits a 3-cert chain and
rejects a 4-cert one maps to Rust `set_max_depth(2)` ‚ÄĒ the same
PASS/FAIL split over the identical `depth_suite` fixtures, expressed
in each side's units. This is a documented convention difference,
not a verifier bug: both are valid RFC 5280 ¬ß6.1 strategies (the
spec leaves the depth-counting base to the implementation). The
in-test comment records the mapping so the `set_max_depth(2)` (vs
the C `maxDepth=3`) is not mistaken for an off-by-one error.

The loop case uses `set_max_depth(4)` directly (matching the C
value) because a cyclic chain grows without bound and trips any
finite cap regardless of the counting base.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1090 PASS / 0 FAIL / 0 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/verify` chain signature-binding family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Extends T113 Phase C with **4 more TCs** from
`pki/verify/test_suite_sdv_x509_vfy.c` ‚ÄĒ the chain signature-binding
family covering RFC 5280 ¬ß6.1.3 (a)(3): every certificate's
signature must verify under its issuer's public key. Anchored on the
`cert/chain/certVer/` fixtures (mirrored in ¬ß4.1), including the
`_tampered` variants whose signature bytes are mutated.

Coverage delta: 1090 ‚Üí **1094 emitted** ‚ÄĒ migrated suite is

## Phase T132 ‚ÄĒ tlsfuzzer Coverage-Expansion Batch: 3 clean TLS 1.3 scripts (2026-05-24)

### Summary

With the tlsfuzzer XFAIL-reduction track wound down (I114 note:
remaining curated XFAILs are documented won't-fix / deferred
complex state-machine cases), this phase pivots from *reduction* to
*coverage expansion*: the pinned tlsfuzzer repo carries ~170 scripts;
the curated CI suite covered 52 pre-T132. A local triage sweep ran a
slate of uncurated candidates against the release `s-server` to find
clean-PASS additions and to map what the rest need.

**3 clean-PASS TLS 1.3 scripts added** to the curated suite (main
`scripts` array in `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml`, against the
shared RSA TLS 1.3 listener, no extra args, **0 XFAIL**):

| Script | Result | What it pins |
|---|---|---|
| `test-tls13-unrecognised-groups.py` | 32/32 | unknown `supported_groups` entries ignored (RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.7) |
| `test-tls13-serverhello-random.py` | 256/0 | no TLS 1.2/1.1 downgrade sentinel in `ServerHello.random`; well-distributed across repeated handshakes |
| `test-tls13-invalid-ciphers.py` | 52/52 | malformed / unknown cipher-suite list entries handled per spec |

(Re-verified at I-phase-completion time against the
`bf7f579`/`02d1506`-pinned tlsfuzzer + release `s-server` with
`-n 9999`: 32/32, 256/0, and 607/607 conversations respectively ‚ÄĒ
all 0 XFAIL / 0 FAIL, stable across repeated + back-to-back runs.)

### Triage map ‚ÄĒ what the remaining uncurated scripts need

Recorded so a future coverage phase has a starting point:

- `test-tls13-non-support.py` ‚ÄĒ **n/a**: targets a TLS-1.3-*disabled*
  server; we support 1.3, so all 51 non-sanity conversations fail on
  the close_notify-echo quirk of the 1.2 echo listener.
- `test-tls13-unencrypted-alert.py` ‚ÄĒ **real gap, I-phase candidate**:
  both conversations fail because the TLS 1.3 server replies to a
  client abort-alert with `unexpected_message` instead of closing
  silently (RFC 8446 ¬ß6.2: a received fatal alert ‚áí close without
  responding). Read-path hardening candidate.
- `test-tls13-large-number-of-extensions.py` ‚ÄĒ **robustness probe**:
  22/22 standalone but 20/2 under back-to-back contention (2 large
  multi-record ClientHellos, ext ids ‚Č§ 4119, intermittently draw
  `handshake_failure` / `decode_error`). Large-CH-reassembly
  robustness follow-up.
- `ecdhe-curves` 7/26, `obsolete-curves` 8/163, `shuffled-extentions`
  2/17 ‚ÄĒ heavy XFAIL / brainpool-curve gaps, deferred.

### Provenance note

The T132 Phase Index row (350-block) + PROMPT_LOG entry landed
slightly ahead of this detailed section: the DEV_LOG row was picked
up as a staged change by a concurrent T113 chain-structure commit in
the `test-enhanced` worktree (PR #135) while the workflow change was
stashed; the workflow change (3 scripts in `tlsfuzzer.yml`) then
landed from the `feature` slot as PR #137. This entry backfills the
detailed section + the Testing-summary line (T1‚ÄďT130 ‚Üí T1‚ÄďT132,
T131 skipped) so the phase is documented to the post-task standard.

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/verify` EKU / purpose family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Extends T113 Phase C with **6 more TCs** from
`pki/verify/test_suite_sdv_x509_vfy.c` ‚ÄĒ the ExtendedKeyUsage /
purpose family (RFC 5280 ¬ß4.2.1.12). Anchored on the
`cert/chain/eku_suite/` fixtures (+ the `anyEKU/` sub-suite).

Coverage delta: 1094 ‚Üí **1100 emitted** = **1097 PASS + 3 `#[ignore]`**.

### What landed

| Rust test | C source | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| `tc_line1965_..._tls_client_ku_eku_both_match_pass` | `VFY_TLS_CLIENT_KU_EKU_BOTH_MATCH_PASS_TC01` | PASS ‚ÄĒ `set_required_eku(kp_client_auth)`, `client_good` verifies |
| `tc_line2096_..._tls_server_ku_eku_both_match_pass` | `VFY_TLS_SERVER_KU_EKU_BOTH_MATCH_PASS_TC03` | PASS ‚ÄĒ `set_required_eku(kp_server_auth)`, `server_good` verifies |
| `tc_line2228_..._anyeku_eku_allow_ku_match_pass` | `VFY_ANYEKU_EKU_ALLOW_KU_MATCH_PASS_TC05` | PASS ‚ÄĒ no EKU restriction (PURPOSE_ANY), `anyeku_good` verifies |
| `tc_line2031_..._tls_client_eku_only_ku_missing_fail` | `VFY_TLS_CLIENT_EKU_ONLY_KU_MISSING_FAIL_TC02` | `#[ignore]` ‚ÄĒ purpose-KU gap (see below) |
| `tc_line2162_..._tls_server_eku_only_ku_missing_fail` | `VFY_TLS_SERVER_EKU_ONLY_KU_MISSING_FAIL_TC04` | `#[ignore]` ‚ÄĒ purpose-KU gap |
| `tc_line2294_..._anyeku_ku_missing_fail` | `VFY_ANYEKU_KU_MISSING_FAIL_TC06` | `#[ignore]` ‚ÄĒ purpose-KU gap |

### Finding ‚ÄĒ verifier-hardening gap: purpose-based end-entity KeyUsage

The C side uses `STORECTX_SET_PURPOSE` (TLS_CLIENT / TLS_SERVER /
ANY), which is a **joint EKU + end-entity KeyUsage** check: a leaf
must carry both the matching EKU purpose AND the matching KU bit
(digitalSignature for TLS client/server auth). hitls's
`CertificateVerifier` only exposes `set_required_eku(oid)` ‚ÄĒ it
checks the end-entity EKU (or accepts `anyExtendedKeyUsage`) but
**does not inspect the end-entity's KeyUsage**.

Consequently:

- The 3 BOTH-MATCH / ANY **PASS** cases migrate cleanly: the
  `*_good` fixtures carry both EKU + KU, and the EKU check passes.
- The 3 **EKU-ONLY-KU-MISSING FAIL** cases cannot be reproduced:
  the `*_badku` fixtures have the right EKU but no/wrong KU, so C
  returns `PURPOSE_UNMATCH` while hitls accepts them (EKU matches,
  KU is never inspected). These 3 are `#[ignore]`d as a
  verifier-hardening candidate ‚ÄĒ same pattern as the AKI/SKI gaps
  T113 surfaced and I115/I116 later closed. When a future I-phase
  adds purpose-based end-entity KU enforcement, the 3 tests
  unignore and assert the rejection.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1097 PASS / 0 FAIL / 3 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features
  --tests` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | +6 `#[test]` for the EKU/purpose family (3 active + 3 `#[ignore]`); Generation summary 1094 ‚Üí 1100. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 (T113) 1094 ‚Üí 1100 emitted / 1097 PASS + 3 ignored + family list updated. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

### Build Status (Post T113 ‚ÄĒ EKU/purpose continued)

Test-only ‚ÄĒ no production-code change. 3 new `#[ignore]`s record a
real verifier gap (purpose-based end-entity KU), the first PKI
migration ignores since I115/I116 cleared the AKI/SKI + EXT ones.
Phase C still **not closed**: remaining `pki/verify` families
(`VFY_SIGALG_*` / `VFY_PATHLEN_*` (API) / `STORE_*` /
`BUILD_MLDSA/MLKEM/SLHDSA_CERT_CHAIN_*` / `VFY_MLKEM_KEYUSAGE`) plus

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/verify` SIGALG / signature-param family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Extends T113 Phase C with the `VFY_SIGALG_*` (signature-algorithm /
sigParam) family from `pki/verify/test_suite_sdv_x509_vfy.c`. Of the
4 C TCs, **1 is cleanly migratable**; the other 3 are API-surface
skips (documented, not emitted ‚ÄĒ same disposition as `VFY_PATHLEN_*`).

Coverage delta: 1100 ‚Üí **1101 emitted** = 1098 PASS + 3 `#[ignore]`.

### What landed

| C TC | Disposition |
|---|---|
| `VFY_SIGALG_RSA_ROOT_PASS_TC001` | **migrated** ‚ÄĒ `tc_line3920_x509_vfy_sigalg_rsa_root_pass`: 2-cert chain on `sigParam/` fixtures (`rsa_leaf` signed by RSA trust anchor `rsa_root`), `verify_cert` succeeds |
| `VFY_SIGALG_TRUST_ANCHOR_ALG_MISMATCH_FAIL_TC002` | **skip** ‚ÄĒ mutates parsed `root->signAlgId.algId` ‚Üí `ECDSAWITHSHA256` in memory to force `ISSUE_CERT_NOT_FOUND`; Rust `Certificate` is immutable post-parse (no signAlgId setter) |
| `VFY_SIGALG_RSA_PSS_PARAM_MISSING_FAIL_TC003` | **skip** ‚ÄĒ mutates parsed `root->signAlgId.rsaPssParam` (saltLen/mdId/mgfId) in memory; same immutability gap |
| `VFY_SIGALG_SM2_USERID_MISMATCH_FAIL_TC004` | **skip** ‚ÄĒ needs `STORECTX_SET_VFY_SM2_USERID` to inject a mismatched SM2 user-id into the verify path; `CertificateVerifier` exposes no SM2 verify-userid setter (same gap recorded in the T113 SM2-CRL note) |

### Finding ‚ÄĒ two recurring API-surface gaps

1. **No post-parse cert mutation.** TC002/TC003 are negative tests
   built by tampering a *parsed* trust anchor's signature-algorithm
   fields. The C `HITLS_X509_Cert` struct is openly mutable; the Rust
   `Certificate` is parse-only with no field setters, so any test
   that depends on in-memory mutation of a parsed cert is
   unmigratable. (Same reason `VFY_PATHLEN_*` was skipped under T113.)
2. **No SM2 verify-userid setter.** TC004 relies on
   `STORECTX_SET_VFY_SM2_USERID`. hitls's verifier never exposes an
   SM2 user-id for the verification path ‚ÄĒ recording it again here as
   a verifier API-surface candidate (would also unblock the deferred
   SM2-CRL verify rows).

Neither is a verifier *correctness* gap, so unlike the EKU/KU purpose
cases these are **not** `#[ignore]`d regression anchors ‚ÄĒ they are
plain skips, documented in `docs/c-test-na-list.md`'s spirit.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1098 PASS / 0 FAIL / 3 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features
  --tests` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | +1 `#[test]` (`tc_line3920` RSA trust-anchor PASS) + doc-comment recording the 3 SIGALG skips; Generation summary 1100 ‚Üí 1101 emitted / 390 ‚Üí 393 API-surface skipped. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 (T113) 1100 ‚Üí 1101 emitted / 1098 PASS + family list updated. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

Test-only ‚ÄĒ no production-code change. Phase C still **not closed**:
remaining `pki/verify` work is PQC chains
(`BUILD_{MLDSA,MLKEM,SLHDSA}_CERT_CHAIN_*` + `VFY_MLKEM_KEYUSAGE`,
each needs PQC chain fixtures mirrored first) + `PARTIAL_CERT_VFY_*`

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/cms` SignedData-verify family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Opens the **`pki/cms`** SDV suite under T113 Phase C with the
`SDV_CMS_PARSE_SIGNEDDATA_VERIFY_TEST_TC001` family
(`pki/cms/test_suite_sdv_cms_sign.c` line 95) ‚ÄĒ parse a CMS
SignedData blob and verify its signer infos against the supplied CA
cert, in both attached (embedded content) and detached (external
`msg.txt`) modes.

Of the 29 enumerated `.data` cases, **23 migrate as active PASS** and
**6 (ML-DSA) are `#[ignore]`d** on a CMS production-code gap. Fixtures
were already mirrored (`tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/cert/asn1/cms/signeddata/`).

Coverage delta: 1101 ‚Üí **1130 emitted** = 1121 PASS + 9 `#[ignore]`.

### What landed

All in a new `#[cfg(feature = "cms")] mod cms_signeddata_verify` in
`crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs`.

| Algorithm | attached | detached | Result |
|---|---|---|---|
| RSA-PKCS1 (v1.5) | ‚úď | ‚úď | PASS |
| RSA-PSS | ‚úď | ‚úď | PASS |
| ECDSA P-256 / P-384 / P-521 | ‚úď | ‚úď | PASS |
| multi-signer (ECC) | ‚úď | ‚ÄĒ | PASS |
| `noattr/` (no signed attrs) P-256/384/521 + RSA-PKCS1/RSA-PSS | ‚úď | ‚úď | PASS |
| version3 SignerInfo | ‚úď | ‚úď | PASS |
| **ML-DSA 44 / 65 / 87** | ‚úď | ‚úď | **`#[ignore]`** |

Mapping to the Rust API (`CmsMessage::verify_signatures(detached, &[ca])`):
- **attached** ‚Üí `verify_signatures(None, &[ca])` is `Ok(true)` (content embedded).
- **detached** ‚Üí `verify_signatures(Some(&msg), &[ca])` is `Ok(true)`;
  a mutated message (`&msg[1..]`) and a missing one (`None`) both return `Err`.

### Finding ‚ÄĒ CMS `verify_signer_info` lacks ML-DSA OID dispatch

The 6 ML-DSA cases fail with `unsupported sig alg:
2.16.840.1.101.3.4.3.{17,18,19}` (id-ml-dsa-44/65/87). The crypto
primitive exists (`hitls_crypto::mldsa::mldsa_verify`, already used
elsewhere in `cms/mod.rs`), but the per-signer-info verification
dispatch in `crates/hitls-pki/src/cms/mod.rs` does not map these
signature-algorithm OIDs to it. This is a **production-code gap**, so
per the worktree-slot discipline the fix belongs in a future
Implementation phase (bug-fix / feature slot), not this test-migration
PR. The 6 tests are `#[ignore]`d asserting the *correct* (verify-OK)
behaviour, so wiring the OID dispatch unignores them ‚ÄĒ same regression-
anchor pattern as the EKU/KU purpose ignores.

The in-memory `encapContentInfo.contentType` mutation negatives from
the C case (ENCAPCONT_TYPE / VERSION_INVALID) are not reproduced ‚ÄĒ
`CmsMessage` is immutable after parse.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1121 PASS / 0 FAIL / 9 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features
  --tests` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | +`mod cms_signeddata_verify` (29 `#[test]`: 23 active + 6 ML-DSA `#[ignore]`) + helpers (`cms_raw` / `cms_ca`); Generation summary 1101 ‚Üí 1130 emitted. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 (T113) 1101 ‚Üí 1130 emitted / 1121 PASS + 9 ignored + family list updated (pki/cms opened). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

Test-only ‚ÄĒ no production-code change. `pki/cms` suite now open.

`test_suite_sdv_cms` file (DigestInfo ‚ÄĒ no Rust public API;
EncryptedData ‚ÄĒ C's PBES2/password model vs Rust pre-shared-key ‚ÄĒ
both API-surface skips). The ML-DSA CMS verify dispatch is recorded
as an Implementation-phase candidate. `pki/pkcs12` SDV suite still
unopened.

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/cms` SignedData sign-side family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Extends the `pki/cms` migration to the **sign side** with
`SDV_CMS_GEN_ATTACH_SIGNEDDATA_TC001`
(`pki/cms/test_suite_sdv_cms_sign.c` line 928) ‚ÄĒ generate a CMS
SignedData (`sign`), re-parse the produced DER, and verify it against
the CA cert.

Only the **RSA-PKCS#1** signer maps faithfully (2 active). The Rust
`CmsMessage::sign` API is minimal and cannot reproduce the rest of the
C GEN matrix, so the other 6 GEN_ATTACH rows + the
multi-signer / invalid-hash GEN families are documented skips.

Coverage delta: 1130 ‚Üí **1132 emitted** = 1123 PASS + 9 `#[ignore]`.

### What landed

New `#[cfg(feature = "cms")] mod cms_signeddata_gen` in
`crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs`:

| Test | Maps to |
|---|---|
| `tc_cms_gen_attach_rsa_pkcs1_signed_attrs` | GEN_ATTACH RSA-PKCS#1 `hasSignedAttrs=1` ‚ÄĒ `sign(msg, rsa_cert, rsa_key, Sha256)` ‚Üí `from_der(raw)` ‚Üí `verify_signatures(None, &[ca])` |
| `tc_cms_gen_detached_rsa_pkcs1_signed_attrs` | adapted single-signer detached (the detached-signing path the C `GEN_DETACHED_*_MULTI_SIGNER` family exercises) ‚ÄĒ `sign_detached` ‚Üí re-parse ‚Üí verify `Some(&msg)` ok, mutated/`None` err |

### Finding ‚ÄĒ `CmsMessage::sign` is a minimal API

Confirmed by de-risk (RSA-PKCS#1 `Ok(true)`; ECDSA + others fail or
misbehave). The Rust sign API cannot express most of the C GEN matrix:

- **no-signed-attrs** (`hasSignedAttrs=0`): `sign` always emits signedAttrs.
- **ECDSA** (ecc-p256): the fixture key is SEC1 (`EC PRIVATE KEY`); the
  ECDSA sign path's `parse_ec_private_key` expects a PKCS#8 wrapper ‚Üí
  `PKCS8 alg: decode` error.
- **RSA-PSS**: `sign` derives the scheme from the signer cert's
  `signature_algorithm` (PKCS#1 here) and has no PSS sign path ‚ÄĒ it would
  silently produce a PKCS#1 signature, so migrating it would not actually
  test PSS. Skipped as misleading.
- **ML-DSA**: no ML-DSA sign path (and no ML-DSA CMS verify dispatch ‚ÄĒ see
  the 6 `#[ignore]`d ML-DSA verify cases from the prior phase).
- **multi-signer / signerinfo version 3**: `sign` is single-signer, v1.
- **GEN_*_INVALID_HASH** negatives: `sign` computes the digest internally,
  no hook to inject a mismatched hash.

These are API-minimalism gaps in the *test-facing* sign API, not verifier
correctness bugs; recorded as future API-surface candidates if sign-side
coverage is to be deepened.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1123 PASS / 0 FAIL / 9 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/pkcs12` PARSE_P12 family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Opens the **`pki/pkcs12`** SDV suite under T113 Phase C with the
`SDV_PKCS12_PARSE_P12` family (`pki/pkcs12/test_suite_sdv_pkcs12.c`
lines 404 / 442 / 481 / 513) ‚ÄĒ parse a PKCS#12 (PFX) container with a
password and recover the private key + entity certificate.

This was previously **blocked**: the openHiTLS C fixtures carry a
SHA-256 / SHA-224 MacData that the SHA-1-only Rust PKCS#12 KDF rejected
with "MAC verification failed". **I117** (PKCS#12 SHA-2 MAC support)
landed first to unblock it; this phase migrates the now-passing tests
and locks the I117 implementation into CI as a cross-implementation
correctness oracle (parsing C-generated SHA-2-MAC `.p12` and matching
the entity cert).

Coverage delta: 1132 ‚Üí **1144 emitted** = 1134 PASS + 10 `#[ignore]`.

### What landed

New `#[cfg(feature = "pkcs12")] mod pkcs12_parse_p12` (11 active + 1
ignore), all via `Pkcs12::from_der(p12, pwd)`:

| C case | Rust | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `PARSE_P12_TC001` √ó5 | `tc001_0..4` | parse (SHA-256/224 MAC) + private key + **entity-cert DER match** |
| `PARSE_P12_TC002` | `tc002` | positive parse (the C MAC-verify toggle is N/A ‚ÄĒ Rust `from_der` always verifies a present MacData) |
| `PARSE_P12_WRONG_CONDITIONS_TC001` | `wrong_password` | wrong password ‚Üí MAC failure (`is_err`); correct password ‚Üí key recovered |
| `PARSE_P12_TC003` p12_2/3/4/5 | `tc003_p12_2..5` | parse from mirrored `.p12` files with 74-/149-char, single-char, and special-char passwords |
| `PARSE_P12_TC003` p12_1 (empty pwd) | `tc003_p12_1_empty_pwd` | **`#[ignore]`** ‚ÄĒ see below |

The TC001/TC002/WRONG `.p12` + cert blobs were inline hex in the C
`.data`; materialised into
`tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/cert/asn1/pkcs12/parse_p12/`. TC003 uses
the already-mirrored `cert/asn1/pkcs12/p12_{1..5}.p12`.

### Finding ‚ÄĒ empty-password BMPString KDF convention

`p12_1` uses an **empty** password. The C parser accepts it, but the
Rust PKCS#12 KDF treats an empty BMPString (`bmp.len() <= 2`, i.e. just
the 2-byte null terminator) as an *empty* diversifier, deriving a
different MAC key ‚Üí MAC verification fails. This is the classic
PKCS#12 empty-password ambiguity (RFC 7292 Appendix B); the `#[ignore]`d
test asserts the C-correct (parse-OK) behaviour, so a future KDF fix
unignores it. Independent of I117 (which fixed the *hash* generality,
not the empty-password diversifier).

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1134 PASS / 0 FAIL / 10 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features
  --tests` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | +`mod pkcs12_parse_p12` (12 `#[test]`: 11 active + 1 `#[ignore]`); Generation summary 1132 ‚Üí 1144 emitted. |
| `tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/cert/asn1/pkcs12/parse_p12/*` | Added | 13 materialised fixtures (TC001 5√ó`.p12`+5√ó`.der`, TC002 `.p12`+`.der`, WRONG `.p12`) from the C `.data` inline hex. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 (T113) 1132 ‚Üí 1144 emitted / 1134 PASS + family list updated (pki/pkcs12 opened). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

### Build Status (Post T113 ‚ÄĒ pki/pkcs12 opened)

Test-only ‚ÄĒ no production-code change (rides on the I117 SHA-2 MAC
support). `pki/pkcs12` suite now open. Remaining pkcs12 families to
migrate under T113: `PARSE_AUTHSAFE` / `PARSE_MACDATA` /
`PARSE_SAFEBAGS_*` (bag-level parsing ‚ÄĒ need a Rust bag API check),
`ENCODE_*` / `GEN_*` (encode/gen side), `CAL_KDF` / `CAL_MACDATA`
(KDF/MAC KAT), and the `pkcs12_util` families (bag enc/dec ‚ÄĒ likely
API-surface skips). The empty-password KDF convention is recorded as a
small Implementation-phase candidate.

---

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/pkcs12` CAL_KDF / CAL_MACDATA KAT (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Migrates the `pki/pkcs12` `SDV_PKCS12_CAL_KDF_TC001` +
`SDV_PKCS12_CAL_MACDATA_TC001` families (KDF / MAC known-answer tests)
from `pki/pkcs12/test_suite_sdv_pkcs12.c` (lines 377 / 348). These are
pure value KATs for the PKCS#12 KDF and MAC computation.

Because they exercise the **crate-private** `pkcs12_kdf` /
`P12MacHash`, they are migrated as **`hitls-pki` module unit tests**
(`crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs` `#[cfg(test)] mod tests`) rather
than the integration-test file. They double as the SHA-2 reference
vectors the I117 review noted were missing ‚ÄĒ directly closing that
gap by cross-validating I117 against openHiTLS C.

The integration-file `migrated_x509_parse.rs` emitted count is
unchanged (1144); these add **2 module unit tests** (pkcs12 lib tests
22 ‚Üí 24).

### What landed

| Rust unit test | C source | Coverage |
|---|---|---|
| `test_pkcs12_cal_kdf_kat_sha256` | `CAL_KDF_TC001` | SHA-256 PKCS#12 KDF (RFC 7292 App. B): pwd "123456" / salt `ed47‚Ä¶5984` / id=3 / 2048 ‚Üí `b08f3dad‚Ä¶2934f4b` |
| `test_pkcs12_cal_macdata_kat` | `CAL_MACDATA_TC001` | full MAC (KDF id=3 + HMAC over AuthSafe content), **SHA-512 / SHA-224 / SHA-384** vectors |

Mapping: C passes the BMP-encoded password to `HITLS_PKCS12_KDF`; the
Rust `pkcs12_kdf` takes the `&str` "123456" and BMP-encodes internally
(‚Üí `00 31 00 32 ‚Ä¶ 00 36 00 00`, matching the C `pwd` input). MAC =
`P12MacHash::hmac(pkcs12_kdf(pwd, salt, 3, iter, len, hash), init)`.

The 3 SHA-256 `CAL_MACDATA` rows are not re-encoded here ‚ÄĒ SHA-256 MAC
is already exercised end-to-end by the `PARSE_P12` SHA-256-MAC
fixtures + the SHA-256 KDF KAT above; the module KAT focuses on the
SHA-512 / 224 / 384 hashes not otherwise covered.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --lib pkcs12`: **24 PASS** (22 prior + 2).
  All KAT match the openHiTLS C reference vectors ‚Üí I117 SHA-2 KDF/MAC
  confirmed correct against an independent implementation.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo
  clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs` | Modified | +2 KAT unit tests (`test_pkcs12_cal_kdf_kat_sha256`, `test_pkcs12_cal_macdata_kat`) in `mod tests`. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 family-list note. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

### Build Status (Post T113 ‚ÄĒ pkcs12 CAL KAT)

Test-only (KAT unit tests). Retroactively supplies the SHA-2 KAT
coverage flagged in the I117 (#144) review. Remaining pkcs12 families:
`ENCODE_*` / `GEN_*` (encode/gen side), `PARSE_AUTHSAFE` /
`PARSE_MACDATA` / `PARSE_SAFEBAGS_*` + the `pkcs12_util` bag families
(need a Rust bag-level API check ‚ÄĒ likely API-surface skips).

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/pkcs12` ENCODE_P12 family (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Migrates the `pki/pkcs12` `SDV_PKCS12_ENCODE_P12_TC001..TC004` family
(`pki/pkcs12/test_suite_sdv_pkcs12.c` lines 973 / 1066 / 1150 / 1274)
‚ÄĒ the PKCS#12 encode (gen) path. **8 active** (no ignores).

The C cases parse a PFX, re-encode it (`HITLS_PKCS12_GenBuff`) and
re-parse, asserting the entity cert survives the round-trip; TC004
builds a PFX from a key + cert file. The Rust `Pkcs12::create`
regenerates from the *extracted* (key, certs), so the migration is a
**parse ‚Üí `create` ‚Üí re-parse** round-trip asserting the private key is
present and the entity cert is preserved.

Coverage delta: 1144 ‚Üí **1152 emitted** = 1142 PASS + 10 `#[ignore]`.

### What landed

New `#[cfg(feature = "pkcs12")] mod pkcs12_encode_p12` (8 active):

| C case | Rust | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `ENCODE_P12_TC001` √ó5 | `tc001_0..4` | round-trip + entity-cert preserved; **reuses the `parse_p12/tc001_*` fixtures** (byte-identical to the ENCODE inputs ‚ÄĒ verified by `cmp`) |
| `ENCODE_P12_TC002` | `tc002` | no-MAC encode variant ‚ÄĒ round-trip + entity-cert (no-MAC toggle N/A) |
| `ENCODE_P12_TC003` | `tc003` | round-trip (no entity-cert comparison in the C case) |
| `ENCODE_P12_TC004` | `tc004` | build a PFX from a PKCS#8 key file + cert file via `Pkcs12::create`, re-parse, confirm key + cert |

TC002/TC003 inline-hex inputs were materialised into
`cert/asn1/pkcs12/encode_p12/`; TC004 uses the mirrored
`cert/asn1/rsa2048key_pkcs8.der` + `cert/asn1/rsa_cert/end.der`.

### Finding ‚ÄĒ `Pkcs12::create` re-encodes rather than re-serialises

The C `output.dataLen == buff->len` exact-length check and the "no-MAC"
encode toggle are **not** reproduced: `Pkcs12::create` emits its own
encoding (SHA-1 MAC, PBES2/PBKDF2) from the (key, certs) rather than
re-serialising the parsed structure byte-for-byte. The migration
therefore asserts the *semantic* round-trip (valid PFX that re-parses
with the same private key + entity cert), which is the portable
invariant. Not a defect ‚ÄĒ an API-shape difference (`create` is a
component-level constructor, not a structure re-serialiser).

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1142 PASS / 0 FAIL / 10 ignored**.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` clean; `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo
  clippy -p hitls-pki --all-features --tests` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | +`mod pkcs12_encode_p12` (8 `#[test]`); Generation summary 1144 ‚Üí 1152 emitted. |
| `tests/vectors/c-asn1-fixtures/cert/asn1/pkcs12/encode_p12/*` | Added | 3 materialised fixtures (TC002 `.p12`+`.der`, TC003 `.p12`). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 (T113) 1144 ‚Üí 1152 emitted / 1142 PASS + family list updated. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

### Build Status (Post T113 ‚ÄĒ pki/pkcs12 ENCODE_P12)

Test-only ‚ÄĒ no production-code change. Remaining pkcs12 families:
`PARSE_AUTHSAFE` / `PARSE_MACDATA` / `PARSE_SAFEBAGS_*` + the
`GEN_*` / `BAG_*` / `pkcs12_util` bag families ‚ÄĒ these operate at the
SafeBag / AuthenticatedSafe / MacData granularity, which the Rust
`Pkcs12` API (`from_der` / `create`, key + cert vec) does not expose,
so they are expected API-surface skips. `pki/pkcs12` clean migration is
substantially complete.

## Phase T133 ‚ÄĒ tlsfuzzer Full Uncurated-Corpus Scan + Coverage Batch 2 (2026-05-24)

### Summary

Phase 1 of the post-T132 tlsfuzzer plan: stop guessing which
scripts to add and **systematically scan the entire uncurated
corpus**, then curate the clean-PASS results and write the rest
up as a durable backlog.

Ran all **99 server-testable uncurated scripts** (170 in repo ‚ąí
55 curated ‚ąí client-side/generator/SSLv2) against a fresh release
`s-server`: each tried against TLS 1.3 `:4444` first, falling back
to TLS 1.2 `:4445` when sanity aborted, with a 90 s per-run
watchdog. Bucketed results recorded in `docs/tlsfuzzer.md`
(¬ß"Uncurated-corpus scan backlog").

### Curated (4 new clean-PASS scripts, 0 XFAIL)

Each re-verified stable on a **fresh** server (the scan's
single-run results were re-checked 2√ó because some scripts proved
load-sensitive ‚ÄĒ see findings):

| Script | Result | Listener | Pins |
|---|---|---|---|
| `test-tls13-ffdhe-sanity.py` | 7/7 | 1.3 `:4444` | FFDHE (ffdhe2048/3072) key-exchange sanity in TLS 1.3 |
| `test-tls13-pkcs-signature.py` | 8/8 | 1.3 `:4444` | rsa_pkcs1_* CV refusal (RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.3) + legacy-compat spots |
| `test-cve-2004-0079.py` | 4/4 | 1.2 `:4445` | CVE-2004-0079 (SSL/TLS NULL-deref) regression |
| `test-no-mlkem-in-old-tls.py` | 12/12 | 1.2 `:4445` | ML-KEM groups rejected/ignored in TLS 1.2 (1.3-only) |

Curated suite **55 ‚Üí 59 scripts** (2 added to the main TLS 1.3
`scripts` array, 2 to `scripts_12`).

### Backlog (recorded in docs/tlsfuzzer.md)

- **Non-determinism, NOT a server leak (do not curate)**:
  `test-ecdhe-padded-shared-secret` varies run-to-run (2/1 ‚ÜĒ 77/0 ‚ÜĒ
  238/0) and `test-tls13-large-number-of-extensions` is occasionally
  20/2. A dedicated load probe (600 sequential openssl handshakes
  against a fresh `s-server`) **disproved** the server-degradation
  hypothesis: server fd count stayed flat at 8 across all 600
  connections, client TIME_WAIT stayed at 2, no accept errors, and
  `ecdhe-padded` returned the *same* 2/1 both before and after the
  load ‚ÄĒ load made zero difference. The variance is test-side random
  sampling of padding-length conversations (one intermittently
  fails). Not curated, to avoid flaky CI. The intermittent
  1-conversation fail is a separate script-level item (which padding
  case; value/timing dependent), not a robustness/resource bug.
  (`ecdhe-padded` was also T128-excluded for TLS 1.0/1.1/SSLv2-compat
  fails.)
- **Small-XFAIL curation candidates**: `signature-algorithms`
  (275/1), `invalid-cipher-suites` (25/2), `bleichenbacher-
  workaround` (50/2), `x25519` (20/4), `sig-algs` (13/5),
  `point-extension` (7/2).
- **Real gap / big-XFAIL (curve + extension coverage)**:
  `obsolete-curves` (8/163), `ffdhe-groups` (7/55), `ecdhe-curves`
  (7/26), `crfg-curves`, `certificate-compression`, `extensions`
  (215/77), `export-ciphers-rejected` (76/78), `alpn-negotiation`,
  `invalid-server-name-extension`.
- **¬ß6.2 read-path I-phase**: `unencrypted-alert` (2/2 fail ‚ÄĒ
  server replies `unexpected_message` to a peer abort-alert instead
  of closing silently). Phase 2 of the plan.
- **Needs cipher-args plumbing**: `chacha20`, `aesccm`,
  `extended-master-secret-extension`, `downgrade-protection`,
  `dhe-rsa-key-exchange`, `record-size-limit`, `fuzzed-*`.

### Verification

- 4 curated scripts via `tests/tlsfuzzer/run.sh` (the CI wrapper):
  exit 0, `7/0`, `8/0`, `4/0`, `12/0`; re-checked on fresh servers.
- Local env: tlsfuzzer @ `bf7f579` + tlslite-ng @ `02d1506` vs
  `cargo build --release -p hitls-cli`.
- Workflow + docs only; no Rust code touched.

## Phase I119 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.3 RFC 8446 ¬ß6.2 Close-on-Received-Alert (2026-05-25)

### Summary

Phase 2 of the tlsfuzzer plan: closes the read-path conformance gap
that the T133 corpus scan flagged. When the TLS 1.3 **server** is in
the handshake awaiting a client message (Certificate / CertificateVerify
/ Finished) and the client instead **aborts with an Alert record**, the
server replied with its own `unexpected_message` alert. RFC 8446 ¬ß6.2:

> Upon receipt of [a closure or error] alert, an implementation MUST
> [close]; ‚Ä¶ an endpoint that receives a fatal alert ‚Ä¶ MUST NOT send
> any further data.

i.e. the correct behaviour is to close the connection **without** a
responding alert.

### Root cause + fix

In `tls13_server_do_handshake_body!` (`crates/hitls-tls/src/macros.rs`)
the three in-handshake read loops gated on `ct != ContentType::Handshake`
and produced `TlsError::HandshakeFailed("‚Ä¶unexpected_message")`, which
`tls_error_to_alert` maps to `UnexpectedMessage` ‚Üí a sent alert. The
machinery for the correct behaviour already existed but was never
reached: `TlsError::AlertReceived` maps to `CloseNotify`, and
`send_fatal_alert_for_error_body!` **suppresses** the send for
`CloseNotify`. Each loop now checks `ct == ContentType::Alert` first
and returns `TlsError::AlertReceived` ‚Üí silent close. The record layer
(`open_record`) already passes plaintext Alert records through during
the encrypted phase, so both the **encrypted** and **plaintext**
abort-alert variants are handled by the same change.

### Verification

- `test-tls13-unencrypted-alert.py`: **2/2-fail ‚Üí 4/4 PASS** (both
  `encrypted Alert` + `unencrypted Alert`); curated into the CI
  workflow (suite 59 ‚Üí 60).
- No regression: `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib` **1108/0**; adjacent
  tlsfuzzer scripts via `run.sh` all rc=0 ‚ÄĒ `conversation` 3/0,
  `finished` 42/0, `ccs` 5/0, `connection-abort` 140/10-XFAIL,
  `empty-alert` 10/0, `zero-content-type` 6/2-XFAIL, `keyupdate` 62/0.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  -p hitls-tls --all-features` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/macros.rs` | Modified | 3 in-handshake read loops (client Certificate / CertificateVerify / Finished) return `AlertReceived` on `ContentType::Alert` ‚Üí silent close per ¬ß6.2. |
| `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml` | Modified | Curate `test-tls13-unencrypted-alert.py` (4/4, 0 XFAIL). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 355 (I119). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I119 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I119)

`hitls-tls` lib 1108/0; curated tlsfuzzer suite **60 scripts**. The
`unencrypted-alert` read-path gap from the T133 backlog is closed;
remaining T133 backlog items (small-XFAIL candidates, curve/extension
coverage, cipher-args plumbing) are unaffected.

---

## Phase T134 ‚ÄĒ tlsfuzzer small-XFAIL Curation Batch (2026-05-25)

### Summary

Worked the T133 backlog's "small-XFAIL candidates" bucket: 6 scripts
that the scan showed as mostly-PASS with a few failures. Triaged each
failing conversation (real bug vs won't-fix vs deferred-strictness)
before writing any XFAIL ‚ÄĒ the point of XFAIL is a *documented*
divergence, never a hidden bug.

### Curated (3 scripts, per-entry XFAIL lists, sanity OK)

All TLS 1.2 (`scripts_12`, `:4445`), each `run.sh` rc=0:

| Script | Result | XFAIL rationale |
|---|---|---|
| `test-signature-algorithms.py` | 275 PASS / 1 XFAIL | `explicit SHA-1+RSA/ECDSA` ‚ÄĒ SHA-1-only `signature_algorithms`; we refuse (deprecated per RFC 9155 / SP 800-131A), tlsfuzzer expects a legacy SHA-1-accepting server. Won't fix. |
| `test-x25519.py` | 20 PASS / 4 XFAIL | 2√ó ECDHE-offered‚ÜíDHE cross-key-exchange fallback (the I105 co-negotiation gap, same as the `ffdhe-negotiation` XFAILs) + 2√ó malformed (empty) ECDHE keyshare where tlsfuzzer wants `decode_error` (we're lenient). |
| `test-point-extension.py` | 7 PASS / 2 XFAIL | malformed/absent `ec_point_formats` (missing uncompressed ‚Üí `illegal_parameter`; empty ‚Üí `decode_error`); we only ever use uncompressed and tolerate the malformation. Strictness follow-up. |

+302 PASS conversations, 7 XFAIL, 0 FAIL. Curated suite **60 ‚Üí 63**.

### Triaged OUT (deliberately not curated ‚ÄĒ recorded in docs/tlsfuzzer.md)

- `test-invalid-cipher-suites` ‚ÄĒ sanity fails on both ports without a
  forced cipher ‚Üí cipher-args-plumbing bucket, not small-XFAIL.
- `test-bleichenbacher-workaround` ‚ÄĒ sanity needs static-RSA (`kRSA`)
  key exchange, which we intentionally do not offer (Bleichenbacher/
  ROBOT-safe). N/A: a script whose *sanity* fails cannot be curated.
- `test-sig-algs` ‚ÄĒ **real gap, not XFAIL material**. The 3
  `rsa_pss_pss_*-only` fails are a cert-type mismatch (legit), BUT
  `rsa_pss_rsae_sha384`-only ‚Üí `internal_error` and `sha512`-only ‚Üí
  `handshake_failure` show the TLS 1.2 server cannot sign
  CertificateVerify/SKE under RSA-PSS-rsae SHA-384/512 ‚ÄĒ the TLS-1.2
  analogue of the TLS 1.3 RSA-PSS-SHA-384/512 fix. Flagged as an
  **I-phase candidate**; masking it behind an XFAIL would hide a real
  conformance/interop gap.

### Verification

- `run.sh` (XFAIL lists attached) rc=0 for all 3: `signature-algorithms`
  275/1, `x25519` 20/4, `point-extension` 7/2 ‚ÄĒ 0 FAIL / 0 XPASS.
- Local env: tlsfuzzer @ `bf7f579` + tlslite-ng @ `02d1506` vs a fresh
  release `s-server` (TLS 1.2 `:4445`).
- Test/CI-config + docs only; no production code touched.

## Phase I120 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 Server RSA-PSS-rsae SHA-384/512 SKE Signing (2026-05-25)

### Summary

Closes the real gap T134 surfaced: the TLS 1.2 server could not sign
its ServerKeyExchange under `rsa_pss_rsae_sha384` (‚Üí `internal_error`)
or `rsa_pss_rsae_sha512` (‚Üí `handshake_failure`), even though its
RSA cert can produce those signatures. The TLS-1.2 analogue of the
TLS-1.3 fix that generalised RSA-PSS to SHA-384/512.

### Root cause + fix (`crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server12.rs`)

Two defects in the SKE signing path:

1. **`select_signature_scheme_tls12`** RSA candidate list omitted
   `RSA_PSS_RSAE_SHA512` / `RSA_PKCS1_SHA512`, so an `sha512`-only
   client got `handshake_failure` ("no common signature scheme").
2. **`sign_ske_data`** computed the digest for SHA-256/384 only and,
   for PSS, called the SHA-256-only `RsaPrivateKey::sign(RsaPadding::
   Pss, ‚Ä¶)` (which asserts a 32-byte digest) ‚ÄĒ so an `sha384`-only
   client selected the scheme but the 48-byte digest aborted signing
   with `CryptoError` ‚Üí `internal_error`.

Fix mirrors the TLS 1.3 `sign_certificate_verify` path: added the two
SHA-512 schemes to the candidate list and rewrote the RSA branch to be
hash-aware ‚ÄĒ PKCS#1 v1.5 via `sign(Pkcs1v15Sign, digest)` (hash-
agnostic), RSA-PSS via the hash-aware `sign_pss(digest,
RsaHashAlg::{Sha256,Sha384,Sha512})`. The crypto primitive already
supported 384/512 (`sign_pss` / `pss_sign_pad_alg`, added in T95), so
this is purely a TLS 1.2 caller fix ‚ÄĒ no crypto change.

### Verification

- `test-sig-algs.py`: **13/5 ‚Üí 15/3 PASS** (the two `rsa_pss_rsae_
  sha384/512`-only conversations now pass); the 3 residual XFAILs are
  `rsa_pss_pss_*-only` cert-type mismatches (need the `id-RSASSA-PSS`
  cert on :4449). Curated into CI (suite 63 ‚Üí 64).
- No regression: `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib` **1108/0**; adjacent
  TLS 1.2 signing scripts unchanged ‚ÄĒ `signature-algorithms` 275/1,
  `x25519` 20/4, `ecdhe-rsa-key-exchange` 3/0, `ffdhe-negotiation`
  38/3.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  -p hitls-tls --all-features` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server12.rs` | Modified | RSA candidate list +SHA-512; `sign_ske_data` RSA branch rewritten hash-aware (PSS ‚Üí `sign_pss`). |
| `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml` | Modified | Curate `test-sig-algs.py` (15/3). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-sig-algs.txt` | Added | 3-entry XFAIL (`rsa_pss_pss_*` cert mismatch). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 358 (I120). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I120 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I120)

`hitls-tls` lib 1108/0; curated tlsfuzzer suite **64 scripts**. TLS 1.2
RSA-PSS-rsae signing now covers SHA-256/384/512 (parity with TLS 1.3);
the `test-sig-algs` real gap from the T134 backlog is closed.

## Phase I121 ‚ÄĒ X.509 ML-DSA Certificate Signature Verification (FIPS 204) (2026-05-26)

### Summary

`Certificate::verify_signature` could not verify ML-DSA-signed
certificates ‚ÄĒ the OID fell through to "unsupported signature
algorithm" (`certificate.rs` else branch). This blocked the T113 PQC
cert-chain migration (`BUILD_MLDSA_CERT_CHAIN`, `BUILD_MLKEM_CERT_CHAIN`).
First half of the "PQC X.509 verify" task; reuses the I118 CMS ML-DSA
convention end-to-end.

### What changed (`crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/certificate.rs`)

- New branch (gated `#[cfg(feature = "mldsa")]`) in `verify_signature`
  dispatching on the NIST FIPS 204 OIDs `known::ml_dsa_44/65/87()`
  (fixed in I118) ‚Üí `verify_mldsa_cert`.
- `verify_mldsa_cert(sig_oid, tbs, signature, issuer_pubkey)`: picks the
  param set from the OID, then `hitls_crypto::mldsa::mldsa_verify` over
  the issuer's raw SPKI ML-DSA public key and the TBSCertificate. Since
  `mldsa_verify` is the internal variant (őľ = H(tr‚ÄĖM)) and X.509 uses
  *pure* ML-DSA (FIPS 204 ¬ß5.2) with an empty context, the message is
  prefixed with `0x00 ‚ÄĖ len(ctx)=0x00`.
- The no-mldsa arm keeps the "unsupported" error so non-mldsa builds are
  unaffected.

This is verify-only (there is no ML-DSA cert *builder*); it feeds the
chain verifier, since `validate_chain` calls `verify_signature` per link.

### Verification

- New gated unit test `test_mldsa_cert_chain_verify` against the
  openHiTLS C `cert/chain/mldsa-v3` chain (ML-DSA-65): root self-signed,
  inter-by-root, end-by-inter all `Ok(true)`; wrong issuer (end-by-root)
  `Ok(false)`.
- No regression: hitls-pki **458 lib** PASS (default); no-mldsa combos
  `x509,pkcs8` and `x509,pkcs8,cms,pkcs12` **454** PASS (ML-DSA branch
  cfg-excluded ‚Üí the OID is still "unsupported" without the feature, as
  before).
- `fmt` clean; `clippy -D warnings` clean under `--all-features
  --all-targets` and the no-mldsa `x509,pkcs8` combo.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/certificate.rs` | Modified | ML-DSA branch in `verify_signature` + `verify_mldsa_cert` helper (gated) + `test_mldsa_cert_chain_verify`. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 359 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI119 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI121 (I120 already merged via #155). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I121 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I122)

Production-code change in `hitls-pki` (X.509 ML-DSA verify). Unblocks
the ML-DSA + ML-KEM cert-chain migration (ML-KEM leaves are
ML-DSA-CA-signed). **SLH-DSA cert verify** is the planned follow-up
(needs a hitls-crypto public-key-only SLH-DSA verify entry + SLH-DSA
OIDs); then the test-enhanced migration of `BUILD_MLDSA/MLKEM/SLHDSA_CERT_CHAIN`
+ `VFY_MLKEM_KEYUSAGE`.

## Phase T135 ‚ÄĒ tlsfuzzer Cipher-Args Plumbing Batch (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Worked the T133 backlog's "needs cipher-args plumbing" bucket
(`chacha20` / `aesccm` / `extended-master-secret-extension` /
`downgrade-protection`). Hypothesis was that these only fail sanity
for lack of a forced cipher/`-d`. Triage showed only **1** was an
args fix ‚ÄĒ and surfaced a real bug.

(Aside ‚ÄĒ re-confirmed the local-env fragility: `/tmp/hitls-certs/`
had again been purged by the macOS `/tmp` cleaner; the first run
produced all-spurious `ConnectionRefused` "failures" until the certs
were regenerated. Reinforces the T132 note to add a `make
tlsfuzzer-setup` that regenerates certs into a gitignored in-repo dir.)

### Curated (1)

`test-extended-master-secret-extension.py` (TLS 1.2, `scripts_12`)
with `args/` = `-d` (negotiate ECDHE) + a 9-entry XFAIL list:
**9 PASS / 9 XFAIL / 0 FAIL**. The 9 PASS exercise the RFC 7627 EMS
three-state core (offered / not-offered / present-in-1.2) ‚ÄĒ real
regression coverage for our EMS policy. The 9 XFAILs are all
unsupported features / deliberate divergences (verified, none a real
EMS bug):
- TLS 1.1 √ó2 (we are TLS 1.2-only ‚Üí `protocol_version`);
- renegotiation √ó3 (unsupported ‚Üí `unexpected_message`);
- TLS 1.2 session resumption √ó3 (no abbreviated handshake in
  `s-server` ‚Üí full handshake instead of resumed ServerHello);
- malformed-EMS-ext √ó1 (RFC 7627: body MUST be empty; we are lenient
  where tlsfuzzer wants `decode_error` ‚ÄĒ strictness follow-up).

### Triaged OUT

- **`test-chacha20` ‚ÄĒ real bug, NOT args.** Sanity fails with
  `Alert(fatal, bad_record_mac)` on the client's *first encrypted
  record*. Our TLS 1.2 ChaCha20-Poly1305 record layer interoperates
  with itself (`test_tls12_dhe_chacha20_handshake` passes) but not
  with tlslite-ng ‚Üí an RFC 7905 nonce-construction (or key-block)
  deviation that is self-consistent but wrong on the wire.
  **High-value I-phase candidate** ‚ÄĒ flagged, not masked.
- **`test-aesccm` ‚ÄĒ N/A.** `default_tls12_suites()` offers no TLS 1.2
  AES-CCM suite (CCM is TLS 1.3-only here); needs new cipher suites
  (a feature), not args.
- **`test-downgrade-protection` ‚ÄĒ N/A / won't-fix.** Sanity fails even
  with `-d`; its substantive conversations check the TLS 1.3 downgrade
  sentinel for `(3,1)`/`(3,2)`, which a TLS 1.2-only server correctly
  rejects with `protocol_version`.

### Verification

- `run.sh` (args + xfail) rc=0: EMS **9/9** (0 FAIL / 0 XPASS).
- Local env: tlsfuzzer @ `bf7f579` + tlslite-ng @ `02d1506` vs a
  fresh release `s-server` (TLS 1.2 `:4445`, regenerated certs).
- Test/CI-config + docs only; no production code touched.

| `tests/tlsfuzzer/args/test-extended-master-secret-extension.txt` | Added | `-d`. |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-extended-master-secret-extension.txt` | Added | 9-entry XFAIL. |

## Phase I122 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 ChaCha20-Poly1305 RFC 7905 Record Nonce (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Fixes the high-value bug T135 surfaced: the TLS 1.2 server's
ChaCha20-Poly1305 handshake interoperated with itself but failed
against any RFC-7905-correct peer (tlslite-ng / tlsfuzzer) with
`bad_record_mac` on the first encrypted record.

### Root cause

`record/encryption12.rs` framed **every** TLS 1.2 AEAD suite like
AES-GCM (RFC 5288): a 4-byte salt `fixed_iv` plus an 8-byte explicit
per-record nonce prepended to the wire, with nonce = `salt ‚ÄĖ
explicit`. RFC 7905 instead requires ChaCha20-Poly1305 to use a
**12-byte** `write_iv` and an **implicit** nonce ‚ÄĒ `write_iv XOR
left_pad(seq, 12)`, exactly like TLS 1.3 ‚ÄĒ with **no** explicit nonce
on the wire. Because both our endpoints made the same wrong choice,
the self-interop test passed; a conformant peer computed a different
nonce and read a malformed record ‚Üí `bad_record_mac`.

### Fix (3 parts; record-layer only ‚ÄĒ the crypto primitive was already RFC 8439-correct)

1. `crypt/mod.rs`: all **7** TLS 1.2 ChaCha20-Poly1305 suite params
   (ECDHE_RSA/ECDSA, DHE_RSA, PSK/DHE_PSK/RSA_PSK/ECDHE_PSK) changed
   `fixed_iv_len 4‚Üí12`, `record_iv_len 8‚Üí0`. `derive_key_block`
   already sizes the write_iv from `fixed_iv_len`, so a 12-byte IV
   now flows through.
2. `encryption12.rs`: `RecordEncryptor12`/`RecordDecryptor12` now hold
   a `Vec<u8>` IV (4 = GCM salt, 12 = ChaCha20 write_iv) + an
   `implicit_nonce` flag (set when the mapped AEAD suite is
   `TLS_CHACHA20_POLY1305_SHA256`). `encrypt_record`/`decrypt_record`
   branch: GCM keeps the explicit-nonce framing; ChaCha20 builds the
   implicit nonce via the new `build_nonce_chacha20_tls12` (XOR) and
   carries no explicit-nonce prefix. Constructors validate the IV
   length (4 or 12) up front.
3. A record too short to contain the AEAD tag now reports
   `bad_record_mac` (RFC 5246 ¬ß6.2.3.3) instead of the previous
   `internal_error` ‚ÄĒ correct for any AEAD and removes a
   length-distinguishing oracle.

### Verification

- `test-chacha20.py` (tlsfuzzer, vs tlslite-ng): **0/52 (sanity fail)
  ‚Üí ~51/52** interop.
- No regression: `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib` **1108/0**, including
  a new RFC 7905 nonce KAT (`test_chacha20_tls12_rfc7905_nonce`) + the
  ChaCha20 self-interop handshake tests, and the updated param /
  roundtrip tests (12-byte IV, no explicit nonce). GCM-path tlsfuzzer
  via `run.sh` unchanged: `fuzzed-ciphertext` 338/0 (heavy
  malformed-record coverage ‚ÄĒ confirms the too-short‚Üíbad_record_mac
  change is safe), `aes-gcm-nonces` 6/0, `conversation` 2/0, `ccs`
  3/0, `encrypt-then-mac` 3/0.
- `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings` clean.

### Not curated

`test-chacha20.py` is **not** added to CI: two conversations
(`Chacha20 in TLS1.1`, `1/n-1 record splitting`) are intermittently
flaky (0‚Äď2 fails run-to-run) ‚ÄĒ a record-splitting / TLS-1.1-rejection
read-path timing non-determinism (same signature as
`ecdhe-padded-shared-secret` / `large-number-of-extensions`),
independent of the ChaCha20 nonce. xfail can't cover a flaky-*pass*
conversation (XPASS). The fix is locked in by the lib KAT + self-
interop tests instead; the flakiness is a separate read-path
follow-up recorded in `docs/tlsfuzzer.md`.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/crypt/mod.rs` | Modified | 7 ChaCha20 TLS 1.2 suite params ‚Üí `fixed_iv_len 12`, `record_iv_len 0`; updated param test. |
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/record/encryption12.rs` | Modified | RFC 7905 implicit-nonce branch (`build_nonce_chacha20_tls12`, `Vec<u8>` IV + `implicit_nonce` flag); too-short ‚Üí `bad_record_mac`; new nonce KAT + updated roundtrip test. |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | chacha20 backlog entry ‚Üí FIXED + not-curated (flaky) rationale. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 361 (I122). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I122 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I122)

`hitls-tls` lib 1108/0. TLS 1.2 ChaCha20-Poly1305 is now RFC 7905
wire-correct (interops with tlslite-ng); TLS 1.2 AEAD too-short
records report `bad_record_mac`. Curated tlsfuzzer suite unchanged
at 65 (chacha20 deliberately not added ‚ÄĒ flaky).

## Phase I123 ‚ÄĒ X.509 End-Entity KeyUsage Hardening (RFC 5280 ¬ß4.2.1.3) (2026-05-26)

### Summary

`CertificateVerifier::validate_chain` enforced KeyUsage only on CA
certs (keyCertSign) ‚ÄĒ it accepted **any** end-entity KeyUsage. The
T113 PQC + EKU/KU migration therefore had to `#[ignore]` 6 negative
cases (the verifier accepted bad-KU leaves C rejects). This phase adds
end-entity KeyUsage validation, the "(a)" of the post-PQC plan.

### What changed (`crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/verify.rs`)

A leaf-KeyUsage block after the EKU enforcement, three rules:

1. **ML-DSA leaf** (pubkey OID `ml_dsa_44/65/87`) ‚ÄĒ signature-only:
   must NOT assert key-establishment bits (keyEncipherment /
   dataEncipherment / keyAgreement).
2. **ML-KEM leaf** (pubkey OID `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.4.{1,2,3}`, matched
   by dotted form ‚ÄĒ no `known::` helper) ‚ÄĒ key-establishment-only:
   must carry keyEncipherment or keyAgreement **and** must NOT assert
   any signing bit (digitalSignature / keyCertSign / crlSign); a
   missing KeyUsage is rejected.
3. **TLS client/server auth** (`set_required_eku(kp_client_auth |
   kp_server_auth)`): the leaf must carry digitalSignature.

Rules 1‚Äď2 only trigger for PQC pubkey OIDs and rule 3 only when an
auth EKU is required, so non-PQC / no-purpose verification is
unchanged (no regression to the existing corpus).

### Verification

Against the openHiTLS C fixtures (KU bits confirmed by inspection):

| Leaf | KU | Result |
|---|---|---|
| mldsa-v3/end (valid) | 0x00c0 (digitalSig+nonRep) | verifies |
| mlkem/end (valid) | 0x0020 (keyEnc) | verifies |
| client/server/anyeku_good | digitalSig present | verify |
| mldsa end_invalid_ku | 0x00b0 (+keyEnc+dataEnc) | **rejected** |
| mlkem end_invalid_ku | 0x0080 (digitalSig) | **rejected** |
| mlkem end_missing_ku | none | **rejected** |
| client/server/anyeku_badku | no digitalSig | **rejected** |

No regression: hitls-pki **458 lib + 1150 migrated + 1 doc** PASS;
`clippy -D warnings` clean (`--all-features --all-targets` + no-mldsa
`x509,pkcs8`); `fmt` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/verify.rs` | Modified | End-entity KeyUsage block in `validate_chain` (ML-DSA / ML-KEM keytype rules + TLS-auth digitalSignature). |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 362 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI121 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI123 (I122 merged via #158). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I123 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I123)

Production-code change in `hitls-pki` (verifier). Unblocks the 6 T113
`#[ignore]`s ‚ÄĒ 3 PQC KU (mldsa/mlkem) + 3 EKU/KU purpose ‚ÄĒ un-ignored
in the follow-up test-enhanced PR (which provides the CI coverage
against the exact C fixtures).

## Phase T136 ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA FIPS 205 VERIFY KAT Regression Anchor (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Diagnosis + regression anchor for the **SLH-DSA primitive C-interop
gap** surfaced during the PQC X.509 work (the `BUILD_SLHDSA_CERT_CHAIN`
`#[ignore]`). `hitls-crypto`'s SLH-DSA round-trips its own signatures
but does **not** interop with openHiTLS C / NIST FIPS 205.

### Diagnosis (decisive)

Fed the openHiTLS C SDV `SDV_CRYPTO_SLH_DSA_VERIFY_KAT_TC001` SHA2-128F
`CRYPT_SUCCESS` vector ‚ÄĒ public key extracted from the KAT secret key,
pure SLH-DSA message `0x00 ‚ÄĖ len(ctx) ‚ÄĖ ctx ‚ÄĖ msg` ‚ÄĒ to `SlhDsaKeyPair::
verify`: it returns **`Ok(false)`** (and bare-message likewise). The
C marks this vector as verifying. So the primitive is **self-consistent
but not FIPS-205-compliant** ‚ÄĒ some component (h_msg / PRF / FORS /
WOTS+ / hypertree / ADRS) uses a non-standard convention applied
symmetrically in sign+verify (hence the passing round-trips). This is
why `BUILD_SLHDSA_CERT_CHAIN` and the SLH-DSA cert verify could not be
made to interop.

### What landed (anchor, not fix)

- `SlhDsaKeyPair::from_public_key(param_id, pk)` ‚ÄĒ a verify-only
  constructor from a bare public key (`2*n` bytes), needed to verify
  without a private key (X.509 / CMS / KAT). Covered by a self-roundtrip
  unit test + a wrong-length rejection.
- **Characterization** KAT test `test_slhdsa_verify_kat_sha2_128f_known_fips205_gap`
  ‚ÄĒ loads the C vector via `include_bytes!` (`slh_dsa/test_vectors/`)
  and pins the *current* non-compliant result (`verify` ‚Üí `Ok(false)`).
  It is CI-green and **not** `#[ignore]`d (the CI `--ignored` job runs
  ignored tests, so a designed-to-fail `#[ignore]`d test would redden
  it). When the primitive is corrected the result flips to `Ok(true)`,
  the assertion FLIPS and FAILS, forcing the fixer to update it.

### Not done (recommended dedicated effort)

The actual FIPS-205 compliance fix is a focused, high-risk crypto task:
bisect each SLH-DSA component against FIPS 205 + the C reference using
the C `VERIFY_KAT` / `SIGN_KAT` vectors. Deliberately **not** attempted
inline ‚ÄĒ a rushed primitive rewrite would risk subtle errors.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --features slh-dsa --lib slh_dsa`:
  **60 PASS / 1 ignored** (the anchor). The characterization
  anchor passes (pins the current `Ok(false)`); it flips red when fixed.
- `fmt` clean; `clippy -D warnings` clean.

## Phase T113 (continued) ‚ÄĒ Phase C: `pki/verify` PQC cert-chains (I121 follow-up) (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Migrates the `pki/verify` PQC certificate-chain families
(`BUILD_MLDSA_CERT_CHAIN`, `BUILD_MLKEM_CERT_CHAIN`,
`VFY_MLKEM_KEYUSAGE`, `BUILD_SLHDSA_CERT_CHAIN`) ‚ÄĒ verifying a PQC chain
root‚Üíinter‚Üíend through the full `CertificateVerifier`. The ML-DSA /
ML-KEM positives are unblocked by **I121** (X.509 ML-DSA verify); the
ML-KEM leaf is signed by an ML-DSA CA, so the chain signatures are
ML-DSA. Closes the implement‚Üímigrate loop for the achievable half of
the PQC X.509 work.

Coverage: 1152 ‚Üí **1158 emitted** = 1150 PASS + 8 `#[ignore]`.

### What landed

**2 active** (gated `#[cfg(feature = "mldsa")]` ‚ÄĒ the I121 dispatch is
mldsa-gated, per the #154 CI lesson):

| Test | C source | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| `tc_build_mldsa_cert_chain` | `BUILD_MLDSA_CERT_CHAIN_FUNC_TC001` | ML-DSA-65 root‚Üíinter‚Üíend verifies via `CertificateVerifier` |
| `tc_build_mlkem_cert_chain` | `BUILD_MLKEM_CERT_CHAIN` + `VFY_MLKEM_KEYUSAGE` (valid) | ML-KEM leaf under ML-DSA CA chain verifies |

**4 `#[ignore]`** (two distinct gaps):

| Test | Gap |
|---|---|
| `tc_build_mldsa_cert_chain_invalid_ku`, `tc_vfy_mlkem_keyusage_invalid_ku`, `tc_vfy_mlkem_keyusage_missing_ku` | **end-entity KeyUsage-vs-keytype** ‚ÄĒ the verifier has no purpose-based leaf-KU check (same family as the EKU/KU `#[ignore]`s), so it accepts the bad-KU leaves C rejects. Verifier-hardening candidate. |
| `tc_build_slhdsa_cert_chain_sha2_128s` | **SLH-DSA primitive C-interop** ‚ÄĒ `hitls-crypto`'s SLH-DSA verifies its own signatures but not openHiTLS C's (FIPS 205 interop gap; de-risk: self-roundtrip OK, C-sig `Ok(false)`), and X.509 has no SLH-DSA verify dispatch. Deeper than the X.509 layer. |

### Finding ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA blocked at the primitive level

Unlike ML-DSA (`mldsa_verify` verifies C's signatures, used by I118/I121),
`hitls-crypto`'s SLH-DSA does **not** interop with openHiTLS C: a
verify-only `SlhDsaKeyPair::from_public_key` round-trips against
self-generated signatures but returns `Ok(false)` for the C
`cert/chain/slhdsa/sha2_128s` chain (sig OID `2.16.840.1.101.3.4.3.20`,
pk 32 B, sig 7856 B ‚ÄĒ all lengths correct; neither bare-TBS nor the
pure-mode `0x00‚ÄĖ0x00` prefix verifies). This is a FIPS 205 primitive
divergence (simple/robust, ADRS, or message-randomizer convention),
a separate deep crypto investigation ‚ÄĒ recorded, not pursued here.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-pki --test migrated_x509_parse`:
  **1150 PASS / 0 FAIL / 8 ignored** (the 2 PQC actives pass).
- no-mldsa combo `x509,pkcs8,cms,pkcs12`: 1142 PASS / 5 ignored
  (mldsa-gated tests excluded; only the SLH-DSA `#[ignore]` remains of
  the new ones).
- `fmt` clean; `clippy -D warnings` clean (`--all-features` +
  no-mldsa combo).

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | +6 PQC cert-chain tests (2 active + 4 `#[ignore]`); Generation summary 1152 ‚Üí 1158. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 318 (T113) 1152 ‚Üí 1158 emitted / 1150 PASS + 8 ignored + family note. |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | T113 śé•ÁĽ≠ entry. |

### Build Status (Post T113 ‚ÄĒ PQC cert-chains)

Test-only ‚ÄĒ rides on the merged I121 X.509 ML-DSA verify. The ML-DSA /

remain as Implementation candidates: end-entity KeyUsage-vs-keytype
enforcement (3 ignores) and the SLH-DSA primitive C-interop +

## Phase I124 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.3 Malformed Peer key_share ‚Üí illegal_parameter (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Fixes the high-value bug the T133 curve-family tlsfuzzer triage
surfaced: a TLS 1.3 client offering a **malformed key_share for a
supported group** drew `internal_error` instead of the RFC 8446
¬ß4.2.8.2-required `illegal_parameter`.

### Root cause + fix (`crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server.rs`)

`build_server_flight` propagated the error from
`compute_shared_secret(client_pub_key)` (and `KeyExchange::encapsulate`
for the KEM hybrid) straight up; `tls_error_to_alert` mapped it to
`internal_error`. Those calls consume **attacker-controlled** peer
input ‚ÄĒ a malformed public value (EC point at infinity / off-curve /
wrong length, all-zero or low-order X25519/X448 producing an all-zero
shared secret, a bad FFDHE Y) is a peer-input error, which RFC 8446
¬ß4.2.8.2 says MUST be `illegal_parameter`, never `internal_error`.
Fix: wrap exactly those two peer-key_share-consuming calls (NOT the
server-side `KeyExchange::generate`) so the error becomes
`HandshakeFailed("invalid key_share: ‚Ä¶")`, which the alert mapper
already routes to `IllegalParameter`. Same "internal_error is never
correct for peer input" class as I122's too-short-record ‚Üí
`bad_record_mac` fix.

### Verification

Flipped **77 conversations** across 3 tlsfuzzer scripts:
- `test-tls13-crfg-curves` 8/10 ‚Üí **18/0** (malformed X25519/X448) ‚ÄĒ curated clean.
- `test-tls13-ecdhe-curves` 7/26 ‚Üí **33/0** (malformed P-256/384/521) ‚ÄĒ curated clean.
- `test-tls13-ffdhe-groups` 7/55 ‚Üí **48/14** ‚ÄĒ curated; the 14 XFAILs
  are a *separate* FFDHE key-share framing-validation gap (truncated /
  wrong-group / duplicated entries accepted ‚Üí ServerHello instead of
  `illegal_parameter`; would fail later at Finished on a mismatched
  secret). Follow-up: validate FFDHE key-share length/group/duplicate
  at parse time.

No regression: `cargo test -p hitls-tls --lib` **1109/0**; key-share-
adjacent curated scripts unchanged via `run.sh` ‚ÄĒ `conversation` 3/0,
`keyshare-omitted` 5/0, `dhe-shared-secret-padding` 342/0,
`no-unknown-groups` 259/0, `hrr` 3/0. `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings`
clean.

### Deferred (documented in docs/tlsfuzzer.md, not curated)

`test-tls13-obsolete-curves` (8/163 ‚ÄĒ obsolete sect/secp curves we
don't implement) and `test-tls13-certificate-compression` (4/25 ‚ÄĒ
RFC 8879 unimplemented) are genuine feature gaps, not triage items.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-tls/src/handshake/server.rs` | Modified | malformed peer key_share (`compute_shared_secret` / `encapsulate`) ‚Üí `illegal_parameter`. |
| `.github/workflows/tlsfuzzer.yml` | Modified | curate crfg-curves / ecdhe-curves / ffdhe-groups (TLS 1.3 `scripts`). |
| `tests/tlsfuzzer/xfail/test-tls13-ffdhe-groups.txt` | Added | 14-entry XFAIL (FFDHE key-share framing-validation gap). |
| `docs/tlsfuzzer.md` | Modified | curve-family triage outcomes + obsolete-curves / cert-compression feature gaps. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 363 (I124). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I124 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I124)

`hitls-tls` lib 1109/0. TLS 1.3 malformed key_share now reports
`illegal_parameter` per RFC 8446 ¬ß4.2.8.2 (EC + X25519/X448 + FFDHE
key-share *values*). Curated tlsfuzzer suite **68 scripts**.
