## Phase I127 ‚ÄĒ PKCS#12 Empty-Password KDF (RFC 7292 Appendix B.2) (2026-05-26)

### Summary

`pkcs12_kdf` built the password diversifier `P` by repeating the
BMPString password to fill whole blocks, but short-circuited `P` to
**empty** whenever the BMPString was `<= 2` bytes ‚ÄĒ which is exactly
the **empty** password (`password_to_bmp("") == [0x00, 0x00]`). That
matches a *NULL*-password convention; RFC 7292 App. B.2 (and openHiTLS
C / OpenSSL) instead treat an empty password as the 2-byte BMP null and
build `P` from `[0x00, 0x00]` (a block of zeros). So KDF/MAC derivation
for empty-password PFX files diverged from C ‚ÄĒ the verifier-hardening
"(b)"-adjacent gap that left the T113 `PARSE_P12` `p12_1` `#[ignore]`d.

### Fix (`crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs`)

Dropped the `bmp.len() <= 2 ‚Üí Vec::new()` short-circuit; `P` is now
always built from the BMPString (which is `>= [0,0]`). For an empty
password, `P` is `block_size` zeros. **Non-empty passwords are
byte-identical** (they already took the build-from-bmp branch, since
`bmp.len() > 2`), so the change is scoped to the empty-password case
only ‚ÄĒ zero regression for non-empty passwords.

### Verification

- De-risk: the openHiTLS C `cert/asn1/pkcs12/p12_1.p12` (empty
  password) now parses (private key + entity cert) ‚ÄĒ previously
  `MAC verification failed`.
- Un-ignored the migration test `tc_pkcs12_parse_p12_tc003_p12_1_empty_pwd`
  (was `#[ignore]`d on this gap). Migrated suite **1156 ‚Üí 1157 PASS,
  2 ‚Üí 1 ignored** ‚ÄĒ the only remaining `#[ignore]` is the SLH-DSA
  primitive anchor (T136).
- No regression: hitls-pki **24 pkcs12 lib** PASS (incl. the
  empty-password create/parse round-trip + SHA-2 KDF tests); `fmt` +
  `clippy -D warnings` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs` | Modified | empty-password `P` built from `[0,0]` (drop the `<= 2` short-circuit). |
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | un-`#[ignore]` `tc_pkcs12_parse_p12_tc003_p12_1_empty_pwd`; generation summary note. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 367 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI123 ‚Üí I1‚ÄďI127 (I124/I125/I126 merged via #164/#165/#166). |
| `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | I127 prompt + result entry. |

### Build Status (Post I127)

Production-code change in `hitls-pki` (PKCS#12 KDF empty-password
handling). The migrated PKI suite is down to a single `#[ignore]` (the
SLH-DSA FIPS-205 primitive anchor); all other T113 PKI migration
families are active.

## Phase I129 ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA FIPS-205 Compliance Fix + X.509 SLH-DSA Cert-Verify (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Closed the last `#[ignore]` in the T113 migrated PKI suite ‚ÄĒ the SLH-DSA
cert-chain test ‚ÄĒ by fixing the underlying **FIPS-205 primitive
non-compliance** (anchored by T136) and wiring **X.509 SLH-DSA signature
verification**. The SLH-DSA primitive round-tripped its own signatures
but rejected openHiTLS C / NIST FIPS-205 vectors, i.e. it was internally
self-consistent but wrong against the standard ‚ÄĒ the classic signature of
a bug that sits in code shared by *both* sign and verify.

### Root-cause (component bisection vs the C reference)

Bisecting the C SDV `VERIFY_KAT`/`SIGN_KAT` vectors against the C
reference (`/Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/crypto/slh_dsa/src/slh_dsa_hash.c`)
located **two** divergences in `crates/hitls-crypto/src/slh_dsa/hash.rs`,
both in the SHA-2 hash family (the SHAKE path was already correct):

1. **`H_msg` MGF1 seed prefix.** FIPS 205 ¬ß11.2.{1,2} defines
   `H_msg(R, PK.seed, PK.root, M) = MGF1-SHA-x(R ‚ÄĖ PK.seed ‚ÄĖ
   SHA-x(R ‚ÄĖ PK.seed ‚ÄĖ PK.root ‚ÄĖ M), m)`. The code used
   `SHA-x(R ‚ÄĖ PK.seed)` (an *extra* hash) as the MGF1-seed prefix
   instead of the raw `R ‚ÄĖ PK.seed`. Confirmed against C `HmsgSha`
   (`tmpSeed = R ‚ÄĖ seed`, then the digest appended, then `Mgf1`).
2. **SHA-2 cat-3/5 padding block size.** `padded_prefix` keyed the
   zero-pad length on the security *category* (64 bytes for cat 1, else
   128). But per FIPS 205 the pad length is `block_size ‚ąí n` where
   `block_size` is the **hash function's** block size ‚ÄĒ and for cat 3/5
   `F`/`PRF` use **SHA-256** (64-byte block), only `H`/`T_l`/`H_msg` use
   SHA-512 (128). So cat-3/5 `F`/`PRF` were padded to 128 instead of 64.
   Confirmed against C `g_slhDsaSha512Funcs` (`f = FSha256` ‚Üí
   `SHA256_PADDING_LEN`, `h = HSha512` ‚Üí `SHA512_PADDING_LEN`).

Both bugs are exercised identically by sign and verify, which is why
self-round-trip passed while C-interop failed (cat 1 hit only bug 1;
cat 3/5 hit both; SHAKE hit neither).

### Fix

- `hash.rs`: `H_msg` now uses the raw `R ‚ÄĖ PK.seed` MGF1-seed prefix;
  `padded_prefix(adrs, block_size)` takes an explicit block size,
  `sha256_fh`/`prf` pass 64 and `sha512_fh` passes 128.
- `mod.rs`: factored signing into a private deterministic
  `sign_internal(message, opt_rand)` (public `sign` generates a random
  `opt_rand` and delegates) so a fixed-randomizer SIGN KAT is testable.

### Cross-implementation KAT anchors (`hitls-crypto`)

Extracted from C SDV `test_suite_sdv_eal_slh_dsa*.data` (`CRYPT_SUCCESS`
vectors; pure-mode `0x00 ‚ÄĖ len(ctx) ‚ÄĖ ctx ‚ÄĖ msg`; public key =
`key[2n..4n]`):

- VERIFY **SHA2-128F** (cat 1, SHA-256 `H_msg`) ‚ÄĒ flips the T136 anchor.
- VERIFY **SHAKE-128F** ‚ÄĒ full SHAKE hash chain end-to-end.
- VERIFY **SHA2-192F** (cat 3) ‚ÄĒ the SHA-512 `H_msg` + SHA-512 `H`/`T_l`
  + 128-byte padded-prefix branch.
- SIGN **SHA2-128F** deterministic (`opt_rand = PK.seed`) ‚ÄĒ byte-exact
  signature match, proving the sign path is FIPS-correct, not merely
  self-consistent with our verify.

T136's characterization test (`assert!(!verified)`) is now a positive KAT
(`assert!(verified)`), renamed `test_slhdsa_verify_kat_sha2_128f`.

### X.509 SLH-DSA verify dispatch

- `hitls-utils`: 12 SLH-DSA signature OIDs (`known::slh_dsa_*`, NIST CSOR
  `id-slh-dsa-*` = 2.16.840.1.101.3.4.3.{20..31}).
- `hitls-pki` `certificate.rs`: `slhdsa_param_for_oid` (OID ‚Üí param set,
  defined unconditionally so the dispatch guard can reference it) +
  `verify_slhdsa_cert` (feature-gated) ‚ÄĒ pure-mode `0x00 ‚ÄĖ 0x00 ‚ÄĖ tbs`,
  issuer SPKI raw key = `PK.seed ‚ÄĖ PK.root`. Mirrors the I121
  `verify_mldsa_cert` convention.
- New `hitls-pki` `slhdsa` feature ‚Üí `hitls-crypto/slh-dsa` (added to
  `default`).
- `verify.rs`: end-entity-KeyUsage hardening now treats SLH-DSA leaves as
  signature-only (same class as ML-DSA ‚ÄĒ must not assert
  key-establishment usages).

### Verification

- `tc_build_slhdsa_cert_chain_sha2_128s` un-ignored ‚Üí migrated PKI suite
  **1158 PASS / 0 ignored** (the suite's final `#[ignore]` is gone).
- No regression: hitls-crypto **64 slh_dsa lib** (incl. 4 KATs) +
  hitls-pki **458 lib** + hitls-utils **8 oid** PASS; feature combos
  (`¬Īmldsa` / `¬Īslhdsa`) compile clean; workspace `fmt` +
  `clippy -D warnings --all-targets` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/slh_dsa/hash.rs` | Modified | `H_msg` MGF1-seed prefix fix + `padded_prefix` block-size-by-hash fix. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/slh_dsa/mod.rs` | Modified | `sign`‚Üí`sign_internal(opt_rand)`; T136 anchor ‚Üí positive KAT; +3 VERIFY KATs + 1 SIGN KAT. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/slh_dsa/test_vectors/*` | Added | C SDV fixtures: verify SHAKE-128F + SHA2-192F (.pk/.msg/.ctx/.sig) + sign SHA2-128F (.key/.msg/.ctx/.sig). |
| `crates/hitls-utils/src/oid/mod.rs` | Modified | 12 SLH-DSA CSOR OIDs (`slh_dsa_{sha2,shake}_{128,192,256}{s,f}`). |
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/certificate.rs` | Modified | `slhdsa_param_for_oid` + `verify_slhdsa_cert` + dispatch branch. |
| `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/verify.rs` | Modified | SLH-DSA leaves are signature-only in end-entity-KU hardening. |
| `crates/hitls-pki/Cargo.toml` | Modified | new `slhdsa` feature ‚Üí `hitls-crypto/slh-dsa` (in `default`). |
| `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` | Modified | un-`#[ignore]` SLH-DSA chain test (feature-gated); generation summary ‚Üí 0 ignored. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 369 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI129. |

### Build Status (Post I129)

100% of the T113 migrated PKI suite is now active (0 `#[ignore]`). The
SLH-DSA primitive is FIPS-205-compliant for verify (SHA-256 / SHA-512 /
SHAKE) and sign (deterministic byte-exact), anchored by C cross-impl
KATs.

## Phase T137 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust PQC KAT Migration: ML-DSA + ML-KEM (Phase A continued) (2026-05-26)

### Summary

Extended the `xtask migrate-c-tests` generator to the two remaining
mainline post-quantum families, taking the mechanically-migrated crypto
algorithm count from 9 (Phase A / T111) to **11**. Both families are
deterministic KATs and a direct analogue of the SLH-DSA cross-impl KAT
that found two real FIPS bugs in T136/I129 ‚ÄĒ but **neither ML-DSA verify
nor ML-KEM decaps revealed any divergence**; all 195 vectors passed on
the first run, confirming the two primitives interop with openHiTLS C /
NIST out of the box.

### ML-DSA (`crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_mldsa.rs`, 45 tests)

Migrates `MLDSA_FUNC_VERIFYDATA_TC001` (`type : pubKey : msg : sign :
res`; 15 vectors each for ML-DSA-44/65/87). The C case sets
`CRYPT_CTRL_SET_MLDSA_ENCODE_FLAG = 0` and no context, then
`CRYPT_EAL_PkeyVerify(ctx, CRYPT_MD_MAX, msg, ‚Ä¶)` ‚ÄĒ i.e. it verifies the
*raw* message under the FIPS 204 **internal** interface (őľ = H(tr ‚ÄĖ M)).
Rust `mldsa::mldsa_verify(pk, message, sig, params)` is exactly that
internal variant, so the message is passed through unmodified (the ¬ß5.2
`0x00‚ÄĖlen(ctx)‚ÄĖctx‚ÄĖM` *pure*-mode prefix is **not** applied here ‚ÄĒ that
is the X.509/CMS interface, not this KAT). `res == 1` ‚Üí assert
`Ok(true)`; otherwise assert the verify did not return `Ok(true)` (`Err`
or `Ok(false)` both satisfy the C `ASSERT_NE`).

### ML-KEM (`crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_mlkem.rs`, 150 tests)

Migrates the **decapsulation** side of `MLKEM_ENCAPS_DECAPS_FUNC_TC001`
(`bits : m : EK : DK : CT : SK`; 50 each for ML-KEM-512/768/1024).
Decapsulation is deterministic (DK + CT ‚Üí SK), so it maps onto
`MlKemKeyPair::from_decapsulation_key(bits, DK).decapsulate(CT)` checked
byte-for-byte against SK. This required a new **public** constructor
`MlKemKeyPair::from_decapsulation_key` (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/mlkem/mod.rs`)
‚ÄĒ it mirrors the existing `from_encapsulation_key`, length-checks against
`dk_len`, recovers the embedded `ek` (FIPS 203 `dk = dk_pke ‚ÄĖ ek ‚ÄĖ H(ek)
‚ÄĖ z`), and inherits the struct's zeroize-on-drop. A focused unit test
covers the generate‚Üíreload‚Üídecapsulate round-trip + wrong-length
rejection.

### What is *not* migrated (and why)

The sign / encaps / keygen halves are routed to *API-surface*, not
emitted: they consume injected randomness (ML-DSA sign nonce; ML-KEM `m`
for encaps and `(z,d)` for keygen) that the C test pins via a stubbed
RNG. The Rust port draws these from the system RNG and the deterministic
hooks (`generate_from_seed`, `encapsulate_deterministic`) are
crate-private ‚ÄĒ deliberately, to avoid a public misuse-of-randomness
footgun ‚ÄĒ so those vectors are not reproducible from an external
integration test. Same reproducibility limit as DSA/SM2 (documented in
`docs/c-test-na-list.md`).

### Verification

- `migrated_mldsa` **45/0**, `migrated_mlkem` **150/0**; mlkem lib
  **46/0** (incl. the new constructor test).
- `xtask migrate-c-tests --algo {mldsa,mlkem} --check` drift gate passes
  (committed files match generator output).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` tally updated: 995 emitted / 3427 total C
  cases across 11 algorithms.
- Workspace `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets`
  clean.

| `xtask/src/mldsa.rs` | Added | ML-DSA verify-KAT emitter. |
| `xtask/src/mlkem.rs` | Added | ML-KEM decaps-KAT emitter. |
| `xtask/src/main.rs` | Modified | `mldsa` + `mlkem` dispatch arms + module decls + help string. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/mlkem/mod.rs` | Modified | `pub fn from_decapsulation_key` + unit test. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_mldsa.rs` | Added | 45 generated ML-DSA verify KATs. |

## Phase T139 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust SHA-3/SHAKE + DRBG KAT Migration (Phase A continued) (2026-05-27)

### Summary

Extended the `xtask migrate-c-tests` generator to SHA-3/SHAKE and the
DRBG NIST vectors, taking the mechanically-migrated crypto algorithm
count from 11 (T137) to **13**. SHA-3 is a clean hash/XOF KAT; DRBG
surfaced a behavioural divergence in CTR-DRBG-df.

### SHA-3 (`crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sha3.rs`, 46 tests)

- `SDV_CRYPT_EAL_SHA3_FUNC_TC003` (`algId : in : digest`) ‚ÄĒ fixed-length
  SHA3-224/256/384/512 ‚Üí `Sha3_*::digest`.
- `SDV_CRYPT_EAL_SHA3_FUNC_TC005` (`‚Ä¶ : digest : isProvider`) ‚ÄĒ
  SHAKE128/256 squeezed to the digest's own length.
- `SDV_CRYPT_EAL_SHA3_FUNC_TC006` (`‚Ä¶ : outLen : digest : isProvider`) ‚ÄĒ
  SHAKE128/256 variable-length XOF squeeze of `outLen` bytes.
- `SDV_CRYPTO_SHA3_COPY_CTX_FUNC_TC001` (`algId : msg : hash`) ‚ÄĒ hash KAT.
- 2 unsupported = SHAKE ctx-copy mid-stream rows (no one-shot mapping).

### DRBG (`crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_drbg.rs`, 5 tests)

`SDV_PRIMARY_DRBG_VECTOR_FUN_TC001` (`algId : entropyLen : result`). The C
harness fixes the seed inputs in code: entropy = `entropyLen` bytes of
`0xff`, nonce = 20 bytes of `0xff`, personalization = `{00,01,02,03,04,
05}`; it instantiates and generates 32 bytes vs `result`. Reading the C
`getEntropy` callback (returns the *full* configured buffer when within
the length range) confirmed `seed_material = entropy ‚ÄĖ nonce ‚ÄĖ pers` ‚ÄĒ
exactly what `HashDrbg::new` (`Hash_df`) and `HmacDrbg::new` (HMAC
`Update`) consume; CTR-no-df uses `seed_material = entropy XOR pers`.

Migrated the variants with a matching deterministic Rust constructor,
each reproducing the NIST vector byte-for-byte:
- Hash-DRBG SHA-256 / SHA-384 / SHA-512.
- HMAC-DRBG SHA-256.
- CTR-DRBG AES-256 (no df).

### Finding: CTR-DRBG-AES-256-df divergence

`BSL_CID_RAND_AES256_CTR_DF` is **not** reproduced by
`CtrDrbg::with_df(entropy ‚ÄĖ nonce ‚ÄĖ pers)`. Since the Hash-DRBG,
HMAC-DRBG and CTR-DRBG-**no-df** vectors from the same C source all
match ‚ÄĒ and CTR-no-df shares the AES core and `update` with CTR-df ‚ÄĒ the
divergence is isolated to `block_cipher_df` (SP 800-90A ¬ß10.3.2). A
9-way input-construction sweep (entropy length 1000/48/32 √ó {e‚ÄĖn‚ÄĖp, e‚ÄĖp,
e‚ÄĖn}) reproduced none. Pinned by a CI-green divergence anchor
`test_ctr_drbg_aes256_df_nist_vector_divergence_anchor` in
`crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/ctr_drbg.rs` (asserts the current
divergence; flips to fail when `block_cipher_df` is corrected, forcing
the fixer to convert it to `assert_eq!` and migrate the vector). Fixing
`block_cipher_df` is a dedicated future Implementation effort.

### Gaps (Rust-API, documented in `c-test-na-list.md`)

13 DRBG variants have no matching Rust constructor: Hash-DRBG
SHA-1/SHA-224/SM3 (`HashDrbgType` is 256/384/512), HMAC-DRBG
SHA-1/224/384/512 (`HmacDrbg` is SHA-256-only), CTR-DRBG AES-128/192
(¬Īdf) (`CtrDrbg` is AES-256-only), SM4-CTR-df (`Sm4CtrDrbg` is
no-df-only), plus the CTR-AES-256-df divergence above.

### Verification

- `migrated_sha3` **46/0**, `migrated_drbg` **5/0**; CTR-df divergence
  anchor passes (CI-green).
- `xtask migrate-c-tests --algo {sha3,drbg} --check` drift gate passes.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` tally ‚Üí 1046 emitted / 3815 total C cases
  across 13 algorithms.
- Workspace `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets`
  clean.

### Files Modified

|------|--------|-------------|
| `xtask/src/sha3.rs` | Added | SHA-3/SHAKE hash + XOF KAT emitter. |
| `xtask/src/drbg.rs` | Added | DRBG NIST-vector KAT emitter (Hash/HMAC/CTR-no-df). |
| `xtask/src/main.rs` | Modified | `sha3` + `drbg` dispatch arms + module decls + help string. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/ctr_drbg.rs` | Modified | CTR-AES-256-df NIST-vector divergence anchor. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sha3.rs` | Added | 46 generated SHA-3/SHAKE KATs. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_drbg.rs` | Added | 5 generated DRBG NIST-vector KATs. |
| `docs/c-test-na-list.md` | Modified | SHA-3 + DRBG tally rows + DRBG gap rows + CTR-df divergence note. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 373 + Testing summary T1‚ÄďT139. |

### Build Status (Post T139)

13 of the crypto algorithm families now have a generated C‚ÜíRust KAT
suite. SHA-3 interops cleanly; DRBG Hash/HMAC/CTR-no-df interop cleanly,
with the CTR-AES-256-df `block_cipher_df` divergence pinned by a CI
anchor for a future fix.

## Phase I131 ‚ÄĒ CTR-DRBG block_cipher_df BCC Fix (SP 800-90A ¬ß10.3.3) (2026-05-27)

### Summary

Fixed a NIST SP 800-90A non-compliance in CTR-DRBG's `Block_Cipher_df`
derivation function, surfaced by the T139 DRBG KAT migration. CTR-DRBG
instantiated/reseeded *with* the df produced seeds that diverged from the
openHiTLS C / NIST vectors, even though Hash-DRBG, HMAC-DRBG and
CTR-DRBG-no-df all matched ‚ÄĒ which isolated the bug to `block_cipher_df`.

### Root cause (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/ctr_drbg.rs`)

`Block_Cipher_df` derives its key+X material with BCC (a CBC-MAC over
`IV ‚ÄĖ S`). Per SP 800-90A ¬ß10.3.3, BCC starts the chaining value at
`0^outlen`, so the counter `IV` is processed as the **first data block**:
`chaining = Block_Encrypt(0 XOR IV) = E(IV)`, and only then are the blocks
of `S` folded in (`chaining = E(chaining XOR S_i)`).

The Rust code seeded the chain *with* the IV (`let mut chaining = iv;`)
and immediately XOR-folded the first `S` block, i.e. it computed
`E(IV XOR S_0)` where the standard wants `E(E(IV) XOR S_0)`. The
standalone `E(IV)` step was missing, so every df-derived seed was wrong.

### Fix

One line ‚ÄĒ encrypt the IV block before chaining `S`:

```rust
let mut chaining = iv;
df_cipher.encrypt_block(&mut chaining)?; // chaining = E(0 XOR IV) = E(IV)
for chunk in s.chunks(BLOCK_LEN) { /* chaining = E(chaining XOR chunk) */ }
```

Confirmed against the openHiTLS C reference `DRBG_CtrBCCInit` /
`DRBG_CtrBCCUpdateBlock` (commented "BCC is CBC-MAC: CBC encryption +
IV(0)"). Only CTR-DRBG **with df** is affected; CTR-no-df, Hash-DRBG and
HMAC-DRBG never call `block_cipher_df`.

### Verification

- The T139 CTR-df divergence anchor flipped (now `assert_eq!`): renamed
  `test_ctr_drbg_aes256_df_nist_vector`, the C `BSL_CID_RAND_AES256_CTR_DF`
  vector now reproduces byte-for-byte.
- Migrated the now-passing vector via the xtask `drbg` emitter
  (`CtrDrbg::with_df`): `migrated_drbg.rs` 5 ‚Üí **6** tests; DRBG
  unsupported 13 ‚Üí 12; `c-test-na-list.md` tally ‚Üí 1047 emitted.
- No regression: hitls-crypto **40 drbg lib** PASS (incl. the pre-existing
  `test_block_cipher_df` and `test_ctr_drbg_with_df`) + **6 migrated
  DRBG**; xtask `--check` drift gate passes; workspace `fmt` + `clippy -D
  warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/ctr_drbg.rs` | Modified | BCC IV-first fix in `block_cipher_df`; T139 anchor ‚Üí positive KAT. |
| `xtask/src/drbg.rs` | Modified | Emit `BSL_CID_RAND_AES256_CTR_DF` via `CtrDrbg::with_df` (`Drbg::CtrDf`). |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_drbg.rs` | Modified | Regenerated ‚ÄĒ +1 CTR-AES-256-df KAT (6 total). |
| `docs/c-test-na-list.md` | Modified | DRBG tally 5‚Üí6 / unsupported 13‚Üí12; divergence note ‚Üí resolved-by-I131. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 374 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI131. |

### Build Status (Post I131)

CTR-DRBG is now SP 800-90A-compliant for both df and no-df instantiate;
all migrated DRBG NIST vectors (6) pass. A security-relevant primitive
fix found by the C‚ÜíRust KAT migration discipline (cf. the SLH-DSA
FIPS-205 fix in I129).

## Phase T140 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust ECC KAT Migration: ECDSA-verify + ECDH (Phase A continued) (2026-05-27)

### Summary

Extended the `xtask migrate-c-tests` generator to the ECC families,
taking the mechanically-migrated crypto algorithm count from 13 (T139)
to **14**. Two deterministic families across NIST P-192/224/256/384/521,
Brainpool P-256/384/512r1 and the SM2 prime curve. Both interop with
openHiTLS C with **zero divergence** ‚ÄĒ 44/44 first run.

### ECDSA verify (`migrated_ecc.rs`, 17 tests)

`SDV_CRYPTO_ECDSA_SIGN_VERIFY_FUNC_TC001`
(`eccId : mdId : prvKey : msg : signR : signS : rand : pubKeyX : pubKeyY
: pointFormat : isProvider`). The C signs with an injected nonce to match
the published `(R,S)`, then verifies ‚ÄĒ the sign side is not reproducible
without a nonce hook (same reproducibility limit as DSA/SM2). The
**verify** side is migrated: build the public key from the row's
`(pubKeyX, pubKeyY)` as uncompressed `0x04 ‚ÄĖ X ‚ÄĖ Y`, DER-encode `(R,S)`
as `SEQUENCE { INTEGER r, INTEGER s }`, and check
`EcdsaKeyPair::from_public_key(curve, pk).verify(MD(msg), sig)`. `MD` is
the row's `mdId` (SHA-1/224/256/384/512).

### ECDH key exchange (`migrated_ecc.rs`, 27 tests)

`SDV_CRYPTO_ECDH_EXCH_FUNC_TC001`
(`eccId : prvKey : pubKeyX : pubKeyY : pointFormat : shareKey :
isProvider`). Deterministic ‚ÄĒ local private key √ó peer public point ‚Üí
shared secret:
`EcdhKeyPair::from_private_key(curve, prv).compute_shared_secret(0x04 ‚ÄĖ X
‚ÄĖ Y) == shareKey`.

### Not migrated (API-surface)

ECDSA sign-side (`SIGN_VERIFY_FUNC_TC002` + the sign halves), ECC key-gen
/ key-pair & private-key checks, ECC point mul/add property tests, and
all `_API_` / ctx-CRUD rows. No new Rust-API gaps (the verify/ECDH
constructors already existed); the ECDSA sign-side is the usual
injected-nonce reproducibility limit, documented in `c-test-na-list.md`.

### Verification

- `migrated_ecc` **44/0** (17 ECDSA-verify + 27 ECDH).
- `xtask migrate-c-tests --algo ecc --check` drift gate passes.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` tally ‚Üí 1091 emitted / 4462 total C cases
  across 14 algorithms.
- Workspace `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets`
  clean.

## Phase I134 ‚ÄĒ Deterministic-Nonce Sign Hook (`kat-nonce`) + ECDSA Sign KAT (2026-05-27)

### Summary

Unblocks the C‚ÜíRust **sign-side** KAT reproducibility limit. The C sign
KATs pin the per-signature nonce `k` via a stubbed RNG so the published
`(R, S)` is reproducible; the Rust port's public `sign` draws `k`
randomly (as it must in production), so a vector's exact `(R, S)` was not
reproducible ‚ÄĒ every sign-side family (ECDSA / DSA / SM2 / ML-DSA) was
therefore routed to API-surface. This adds a guarded test-only nonce
injection point and migrates the ECDSA sign side as the pilot.

### The hook (`crates/hitls-crypto`)

- New **non-default** cargo feature `kat-nonce`, documented TEST/KAT-ONLY
  and **not pulled in by any other feature** (so it cannot reach a
  production build).
- `EcdsaKeyPair::sign_with_nonce(digest, k)` ‚ÄĒ `#[doc(hidden)]`,
  `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")]`, `#[deprecated]`. Validates `1 ‚Č§ k < n`,
  then signs with the caller's `k`. Doc comment spells out the footgun (a
  chosen/reused ECDSA nonce leaks the private key). The `#[deprecated]`
  danger sentinel means any caller that is not a sanctioned KAT (the
  generated sign tests opt in with `#[allow(deprecated)]`) hits a
  build-breaking deprecation warning under the workspace `-D warnings`, so
  enabling `kat-nonce` downstream cannot silently surface a key-leaking
  entry point (addresses the pre-merge AI-review HIGH on the `pub` symbol).
- Refactor: the body of `sign`'s random-`k` retry loop is extracted into a
  private `sign_with_k(e, k) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>>` (`Ok(None)` ‚áí this
  `k` gives `kG==‚ąě` / `r==0` / `s==0` ‚áí the loop retries). Both `sign`
  (random `k`) and `sign_with_nonce` (fixed `k`) call it, so production
  `sign` is byte-identical to before.

### ECDSA sign KAT (`xtask` + `migrated_ecc.rs`)

The `ecc` emitter now emits a **second** test per
`ECDSA_SIGN_VERIFY_FUNC_TC001` row ‚ÄĒ `sign_with_nonce(MD(msg),
randVector) == DER(signR, signS)` ‚ÄĒ each per-test `#[cfg(feature =
"kat-nonce")]`. `migrated_ecc.rs` goes **44 ‚Üí 61** tests under
`--all-features`: 17 ECDSA deterministic-sign KATs (NIST
P-192/224/256/384/521 + Brainpool P-256/384/512r1 + SM2 prime), all
byte-exact against openHiTLS C on the first run. Without `kat-nonce` the
sign tests are cfg'd out (44).

### Verification

- Probe confirmed `sign_with_nonce` reproduces the FIPS-186-4 P-256/SHA-256
  vector byte-for-byte before wiring the emitter.
- `migrated_ecc` **61/0** (`--all-features`) and **44/0** (no `kat-nonce`);
  hitls-crypto ecdsa lib **21/0** (production `sign` unchanged).
- CI's main job runs `cargo nextest run --workspace --all-features`, which
  enables `kat-nonce` ‚Üí the sign KATs run; narrow feature combos and the
  cargo-careful steps cfg the file out (it needs ecdsa+ecdh+sha1+sha2).
- xtask `--check` drift gate passes; `docs/c-test-na-list.md` tally ‚Üí 1108
  emitted; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-crypto/Cargo.toml` | Modified | new non-default `kat-nonce` feature. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/ecdsa/mod.rs` | Modified | `sign_with_k` extraction + `sign_with_nonce` (kat-nonce). |
| `xtask/src/ecc.rs` | Modified | emit a deterministic-sign test per ECDSA verify row. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_ecc.rs` | Modified | +17 ECDSA sign KATs (kat-nonce-gated). |
| `docs/c-test-na-list.md` | Modified | ECC emitted 44‚Üí61 + the `kat-nonce` hook section. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 378 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI134. |

### Build Status (Post I134)

The deterministic-nonce hook pattern is established and proven on ECDSA
(17 sign KATs byte-exact vs C). DSA / SM2 / ML-DSA sign sides can adopt
the same `kat-nonce`-gated `sign_with_nonce` to unblock their sign KATs.

## Phase I136 ‚ÄĒ kat-nonce Sign Hook Extended to DSA + SM2 (2026-05-27)

### Summary

Extends the `kat-nonce` deterministic-sign hook (introduced for ECDSA in
I134) to **DSA** and **SM2**, unblocking two more sign-side KAT families.
The C sign KATs pin the nonce; the Rust public `sign` draws it randomly
(correct for production), so the sign sides were API-surface. The same
guarded test-only injection point now reproduces them byte-for-byte.

### DSA (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/dsa/mod.rs` + `xtask/src/dsa.rs`)

- `DsaKeyPair::sign_with_nonce(digest, k)` ‚ÄĒ `#[doc(hidden)]` +
  `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")]` + `#[deprecated]` danger sentinel;
  validates `1 ‚Č§ k < q`. The `sign` retry-loop body is extracted into a
  private `sign_with_k(e, k) -> Result<Option<Vec<u8>>>` shared by `sign`
  (random k) and `sign_with_nonce` (fixed k) ‚Üí production `sign` unchanged.
- The `dsa` emitter emits a deterministic-sign test per `SIGN_VERIFY_*`
  row (`sign_with_nonce(MD(Msg), K) == DER(R, S)`). `migrated_dsa.rs`
  **600 ‚Üí 1200** tests (+600 NIST FIPS 186-4 sign KATs).

### SM2 (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm2/mod.rs` + `xtask/src/sm2.rs`)

- `Sm2KeyPair::sign_with_id_nonce(user_id, message, k)` ‚ÄĒ same triple
  guard. Factored `sign_with_id` into `sign_digest` (`e = SM3(Z_A ‚ÄĖ M)`) +
  a private `sign_with_k(e, k)` (GB/T 32918.2 ¬ß6.1) shared with the
  nonce path.
- The `sm2` emitter gains a `SignPos` kind for `SM2_SIGN_FUNC_TC001/TC002`
  (`TC002` prepends a `prvKeyTmp` like the verify TC002), emitting
  `sign_with_id_nonce(userId, msg, k) == sign`. `migrated_sm2.rs`
  **12 ‚Üí 14** tests (+2 GB/T 32918.5 sign KATs).

### ML-DSA ‚ÄĒ deferred

ML-DSA `SIGNDATA_TC001` injects a 32-byte `seed` as the FIPS-204 hedging
`rnd` (`ŌĀ' = H(K ‚ÄĖ rnd ‚ÄĖ őľ)`), but the Rust `mldsa_sign` is deterministic
(`ŌĀ' = H(K ‚ÄĖ őľ)`, no rnd slot ‚ÄĒ which may itself differ from FIPS-204's
deterministic `H(K ‚ÄĖ 0^32 ‚ÄĖ őľ)`). That needs its own investigation +
possibly a `sign-with-rnd` hook, so it is left for a follow-up.

### Verification

- migrated_dsa **1200/0**, migrated_sm2 **14/0** (`--all-features`,
  `-D warnings`); without `kat-nonce` the sign tests cfg out (600 / 12).
- hitls-crypto dsa lib **15/0** + sm2 lib **15/0** (production `sign`
  byte-identical).
- xtask `--check` drift gate passes for both; `docs/c-test-na-list.md`
  tally ‚Üí 1710 emitted; `fmt` + `clippy -D warnings --all-features
  --all-targets` clean.

### Files Modified

| File | Status | Description |
|------|--------|-------------|
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/dsa/mod.rs` | Modified | `sign_with_k` + `sign_with_nonce` (kat-nonce). |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm2/mod.rs` | Modified | `sign_digest` + `sign_with_k` + `sign_with_id_nonce` (kat-nonce). |
| `xtask/src/dsa.rs` | Modified | emit a deterministic-sign test per DSA row. |
| `xtask/src/sm2.rs` | Modified | `SignPos` kind + `emit_sign`. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_dsa.rs` | Modified | +600 DSA sign KATs. |
| `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm2.rs` | Modified | +2 SM2 sign KATs. |
| `docs/c-test-na-list.md` | Modified | DSA 600‚Üí1200, SM2 12‚Üí14 + hook note. |
| `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` | Modified | This entry + Phase Index row 380 + Implementation summary I1‚ÄďI136. |
