## Phase T150 ‚ÄĒ HPKE SHARED_SECRET_TC002 (Export) KAT Migration (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: extends the T149 HPKE emitter to also migrate the
`SHARED_SECRET_TC002` family ‚ÄĒ the **export-secret** variant alongside the
TC001 seal/open path. Both TCs publish a precomputed `shared_secret` per row,
so both drive the key schedule via the existing
`HpkeCtx::from_shared_secret` hook. **72 byte-exact tests added** ‚ÄĒ
`migrated_hpke.rs` grows from 144 to 216 tests, full RFC 9180 export-secret
matrix coverage. **Zero new production code**; reuses the T149
`kat-nonce`-gated hook plus the existing public `HpkeCtx::export`.

### Files changed

- `xtask/src/hpke.rs` ‚ÄĒ new `emit_shared_secret_tc002` helper; the main
  dispatcher in `emit_hpke_kat` now routes `SHARED_SECRET_TC002` rows to it
  before falling through to the TC001 path.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_hpke.rs` ‚ÄĒ **regenerated** (144 ‚Üí
  216 tests).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ updates the HPKE tally row (144 ‚Üí 216
  emitted; 323 ‚Üí 251 API-surface; Total 2385/4005 ‚Üí 2457/3933), drops
  SHARED_SECRET_TC002 from the T149-deferred list, adds a new prose
  paragraph for T150.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` ‚ÄĒ this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ row-shape dispatch

C test fn (`test_suite_sdv_eal_hpke.c`):

```c
void SDV_CRYPT_EAL_HPKE_SHARED_SECRET_TC002(int mode, int kemId, int kdfId,
    int aeadId, Hex *info, Hex *psk, Hex *pskId, Hex *sharedSecret,
    Hex *exporterContext, int L, Hex *exportedValue);
```

Row shape `(mode, kemId, kdfId, aeadId, info, psk, pskId, sharedSecret,
exporterContext, L, exportedValue)` ‚ÄĒ 11 fields. The first 4 are symbolic
`CRYPT_*` macros (same lookup tables as TC001); `exporterContext` is hex
(often empty); `L` is an unquoted integer parsed via `usize::parse`;
`exportedValue` is hex.

Each row emits one `#[test]` named
`tc_lineN_hpke_export_<mode>_<kem>_<kdf>_<aead>` that:

1. Constructs `suite: CipherSuite`.
2. Builds `ctx = HpkeCtx::from_shared_secret(suite, mode_byte, ...)`.
3. Asserts `ctx.export(exporter_context, L) == expected`.

The C body builds both a sender + recipient ctx and asserts the export
against each; the Rust `HpkeCtx::export` is `&self` (read-only ‚ÄĒ does not
mutate `seq` or any other field), so a single ctx is functionally identical
to two. The emitter therefore generates one assertion per row instead of
two.

### Coverage notes

The 72 rows cover the full RFC 9180 export-secret KAT matrix:

| Axis | Variants | Notes |
|------|---------:|-------|
| HPKE mode | 4 | Base / PSK / Auth / AuthPSK |
| KEM | 4 | DHKEM-X25519, DHKEM-P-256, -P-384, -P-521 |
| KDF | 3 | HKDF-SHA256/384/512 |
| AEAD | 3 | AES-128/256-GCM + ChaCha20-Poly1305 |
| exporterContext + L | varied | empty / short hex contexts, L ‚Č§ Nh |

### Verification

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo hpke \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 467 TC rows
#   emitted 216 tests, skipped 251 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_hpke
#   test result: ok. 216 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo hpke --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (31 rows in tally) | **2457** | **3933** | **240** | **109** | **6005** |

Remaining HPKE TCs (deferred to T151+):

- **AEAD_TC001** (144 rows): completed in T151 via the new
  `setup_sender_kat` / `setup_recipient_kat` + `derive_key_pair` hooks.
- **EXPORT_SECRET_TC001** (72): completed in T151 (same hooks).
- **KEM_TC001** (24): byte-exact KEM derive-key-pair + encap/decap.
- **API / RANDOMLY / GENERATE_KEY_PAIR** rows (8): permanent API-surface.

## Phase T151 ‚ÄĒ HPKE AEAD_TC001 + EXPORT_SECRET_TC001 KAT Migration (closes HPKE ikmE-injection path) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: migrates the two remaining major HPKE KAT families
(AEAD_TC001 seal/open and EXPORT_SECRET_TC001 export). Both publish
`ikmE` / `ikmR` / `ikmS` (the ephemeral / recipient / sender-auth seeds)
and require deterministic KEM Encap/Decap with seed injection. Adds three
new `kat-nonce`-gated public APIs to `hitls_crypto::hpke`:
`HpkeCtx::setup_sender_kat`, `HpkeCtx::setup_recipient_kat`, and a
module-level `derive_key_pair`. All three `#[doc(hidden)] +
#[deprecated]`, test-only. **216 byte-exact tests added**
(`migrated_hpke.rs` 216 ‚Üí **432**), full RFC 9180 matrix coverage. Builds
clean both with and without `kat-nonce`.

HPKE coverage is now 432/467 (**92.5%**). Only KEM_TC001 (24 rows,
separate emitter shape) plus 11 permanent API-surface rows remain.

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/hpke/mod.rs`:
  - Gate relaxation: `kem_encap_deterministic` is now
    `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "kat-nonce"))]` (was `#[cfg(test)]`).
  - New private `kem_auth_encap_with_ikm_e(kem, pk_r, sk_s, ikm_e)` ‚ÄĒ
    same `#[cfg]` as above; mirrors `kem_auth_encap` but uses
    `kem_derive_key_pair` for `skE` instead of `kem_generate`.
  - New `pub fn derive_key_pair(kem, ikm) -> Result<(Vec<u8>, Vec<u8>), _>`
    at module level (`#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")]`, deprecated,
    test-only) wrapping the existing private `kem_derive_key_pair`.
  - New `HpkeCtx::setup_sender_kat(suite, mode: u8, pk_r, sk_s, info, psk,
    psk_id, ikm_e) -> Result<(Self, Vec<u8>), _>` (the same gate +
    `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`). Dispatches on `mode`:
    BASE/PSK ‚Üí `kem_encap_deterministic(suite.kem, pk_r, ikm_e)`;
    AUTH/AUTH_PSK ‚Üí `kem_auth_encap_with_ikm_e(suite.kem, pk_r, sk_s,
    ikm_e)`; then `key_schedule(&suite, mode, &shared_secret, info, psk,
    psk_id)`.
  - New `HpkeCtx::setup_recipient_kat(suite, mode: u8, sk_r, pk_s, enc,
    info, psk, psk_id) -> Result<Self, _>` (same gate). Dispatches:
    BASE/PSK ‚Üí `kem_decap(suite.kem, enc, sk_r)`; AUTH/AUTH_PSK ‚Üí
    `kem_auth_decap(suite.kem, enc, sk_r, pk_s)`; then `key_schedule`.

- `xtask/src/hpke.rs`:
  - Header scope comment updated to cover T149/T150/T151.
  - `emit_hpke_kat` dispatcher routes `AEAD_TC001` and
    `EXPORT_SECRET_TC001` to two new helpers.
  - `emit_aead_tc001` (14-arg row, seal+open round-trip).
  - `emit_export_secret_tc001` (13-arg row, both sender and recipient
    `export` asserted against `expected`).
  - Shared helpers: `emit_kat_derive_block` (writes the suite + ikm
    fields + `derive_key_pair` calls), `emit_kat_setup_ctxs` (writes the
    `setup_sender_kat` / `setup_recipient_kat` calls with a `mut_kw` arg
    so AEAD tests get `let mut ctx_*` and EXPORT tests get plain
    `let ctx_*` to avoid `unused_mut` warnings).
  - Generated file's `use` line adds `derive_key_pair` plus a top-level
    `#[allow(deprecated)]` to silence the import-of-deprecated warning.

- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_hpke.rs`: **regenerated** (216 ‚Üí
  432 tests).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md`: tally row HPKE 216 ‚Üí 432 emitted, 251 ‚Üí 35
  API-surface; Total 2457 ‚Üí 2673 emitted, 3933 ‚Üí 3717 API-surface.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md`: this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ production hooks

The two setup helpers form a symmetric pair that **unifies all four HPKE
modes** behind one function each. This avoids needing 4 separate sender
constructors and 4 recipient constructors. The `sk_s` / `pk_s` / `psk` /
`psk_id` arguments are passed as `&[u8]` and the mode dispatch decides
which are read:

| Mode      | Sender reads             | Recipient reads          |
|-----------|--------------------------|--------------------------|
| Base      | `pk_r`, `ikm_e`          | `sk_r`, `enc`            |
| PSK       | `pk_r`, `ikm_e`, `psk`*  | `sk_r`, `enc`, `psk`*    |
| Auth      | `pk_r`, `sk_s`, `ikm_e`  | `sk_r`, `pk_s`, `enc`    |
| AuthPSK   | all of the above         | all of the above         |

For BASE/PSK rows, the emitter still calls `derive_key_pair(suite.kem,
ikm_s)` on an empty `ikm_s`. RFC 9180 ¬ß7.1.3 DeriveKeyPair is defined
for any input length (it's an HKDF-Extract + HKDF-Expand pipeline), so
this returns a valid `(sk, pk)` that is simply ignored by
`setup_sender_kat` when `mode != AUTH && mode != AUTH_PSK`.

`derive_key_pair` is intentionally a free function (not a static method
on `HpkeCtx`) to match the RFC 9180 algorithm's standalone nature.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ emitter

The C test rows have a common 10-arg prefix (`mode, kemId, kdfId,
aeadId, info, psk, pskId, ikmE, ikmR, ikmS`) followed by family-specific
tail (`seq, pt, aad, ct` for AEAD; `exporterContext, L, exportedValue`
for EXPORT). The emitter dispatches by `tc_name` in `emit_hpke_kat`.
Each helper parses its row's args via the same symbol ‚Üí enum lookup
tables (`mode_byte` / `kem_enum` / `kdf_enum` / `aead_enum`) used for
TC001, then calls `emit_kat_derive_block` + `emit_kat_setup_ctxs` to
write the shared preamble before the family-specific assertions.

### Coverage notes

The 144 AEAD_TC001 rows cover the full RFC 9180 seal/open KAT matrix:

| Axis | Variants | Notes |
|------|---------:|-------|
| HPKE mode | 4 | Base / PSK / Auth / AuthPSK |
| KEM | 4 | DHKEM-X25519, DHKEM-P-256, -P-384, -P-521 |
| KDF | 3 | HKDF-SHA256/384/512 |
| AEAD | 3 | AES-128/256-GCM + ChaCha20-Poly1305 |
| seq | varied | rows sample several `seq` values within each suite |

The 72 EXPORT_SECRET_TC001 rows cover the same matrix at varied
`exporterContext` / `L`.

The AEAD round-trip (`ctx_s.seal ‚Üí ctx_r.open == pt`) is **also an
implicit check on the encapsulated key** `enc`: if the sender's derived
`enc` did not match what the recipient's `setup_recipient_kat`
computes from `ikm_r`, the AEAD's authentication tag would fail at
`open`. So although the row does not carry an explicit `enc` field, KEM
encap/decap correctness is exercised in every AEAD assertion.

### Verification

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo hpke \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 467 TC rows
#   emitted 432 tests, skipped 35 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_hpke
#   test result: ok. 432 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo hpke --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo build -p hitls-crypto
#   Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s)

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib hpke
#   test result: ok. 29 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-T151)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (31 rows in tally) | **2673** | **3717** | **240** | **109** | **6005** |

HPKE: **432/467 (92.5%) covered**. Remaining: KEM_TC001 √ó 24 (deferred
to T152+ ‚ÄĒ separate emitter shape for standalone derive+encap+decap
byte-exact) plus 11 permanent API-surface rows.

## Phase T152 ‚ÄĒ HPKE KEM_TC001 KAT Migration (closes HPKE migration; 92.5% ‚Üí 97.6%) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: migrates the last HPKE KAT family (`KEM_TC001` ‚ÄĒ
24 rows of byte-exact KEM derive+encap+decap KATs). Adds four new
`kat-nonce`-gated free functions in `hitls_crypto::hpke` exposing the
bare KEM primitives (`kem_encap_kat`, `kem_decap_kat`,
`kem_auth_encap_kat`, `kem_auth_decap_kat`) ‚ÄĒ each `#[doc(hidden)] +
#[deprecated]`, all wrapping existing private helpers, no new KEM
algorithm code. New integer-codepoint mapper helpers in the emitter
(KEM_TC001 rows use raw RFC 9180 IANA codepoints, not the `CRYPT_*`
symbolic macros). **24 byte-exact tests added** (`migrated_hpke.rs` 432
‚Üí **456**). HPKE coverage is now **456/467 (97.6%)** ‚ÄĒ only 11 permanent
API-surface rows remain.

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/hpke/mod.rs`: four new `pub fn` at module
  level just after `derive_key_pair`. Each `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")]
  + #[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated(note = "test-only: ...")]`. They are
  thin wrappers (`kem_encap_kat` ‚Üí `kem_encap_deterministic`,
  `kem_decap_kat` ‚Üí `kem_decap`, `kem_auth_encap_kat` ‚Üí
  `kem_auth_encap_with_ikm_e`, `kem_auth_decap_kat` ‚Üí `kem_auth_decap`)
  that take the KEM identifier explicitly so the test side does not
  need to construct a `CipherSuite`.

- `xtask/src/hpke.rs`:
  - New integer-codepoint mappers (KEM_TC001 stores mode / kemId /
    kdfId / aeadId as decimal integers in the `.data` file):
    `int_mode("0"/"1"/"2"/"3") -> (u8, &'static str)`,
    `int_kem("16"/"17"/"18"/"32") -> (variant, tag)`, `int_kdf_tag` /
    `int_aead_tag`.
  - New `kem_sk_len(kem_id_sym)` + `pad_left_to(bytes, n)` helpers used
    to absorb a C SDV `.data` hex-encoder convention (see below).
  - New `emit_kem_tc001(body, stats, case)` 23-arg row handler.
  - Dispatcher routes `if case.tc_name.contains("KEM_TC001") { ‚Ä¶ }`
    before the existing AEAD / EXPORT / SHARED_SECRET arms.
  - Generated file's `use` line extended with the four new wrappers.

- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_hpke.rs`: **regenerated** (432 ‚Üí
  456 tests).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md`: tally row HPKE 432 ‚Üí 456 emitted, 35 ‚Üí 11
  API-surface; Total 2673 ‚Üí 2697 emitted, 3717 ‚Üí 3693 API-surface; new
  T152 prose paragraph appended to the HPKE section.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md`: this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ production hooks

The four wrappers are intentionally KEM-only (they do NOT run the HPKE
key schedule). KEM_TC001 publishes the bare `enc` and `sharedSecret`
that come out of Encap / Decap, so the migration asserts those values
byte-exact without going through `key_schedule` (which would consume
them internally and not expose them). The trailing 5 key-schedule
intermediates in the C row (`keyScheduleContext` / `secret` / `key` /
`baseNonce` / `exporterSecret`) are skipped here because the
SHARED_SECRET_TC001 / TC002 tests (T149 / T150) already verify the key
schedule end-to-end.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ emitter

KEM_TC001 rows are 23 args wide and use **integer codepoints** for the
first four args (vs the `CRYPT_*` symbolic macros used by the other
TCs). The integer mappers translate:

| Codepoint | Field    | Meaning                            |
|-----------|----------|------------------------------------|
| `0..3`    | `mode`   | Base / PSK / Auth / AuthPSK byte   |
| `16`      | `kemId`  | DHKEM(P-256, HKDF-SHA256)          |
| `17`      | `kemId`  | DHKEM(P-384, HKDF-SHA384)          |
| `18`      | `kemId`  | DHKEM(P-521, HKDF-SHA512)          |
| `32`      | `kemId`  | DHKEM(X25519, HKDF-SHA256)         |
| `1..3`    | `kdfId`  | HKDF-SHA256/384/512                |
| `1..3`    | `aeadId` | AES-128/256-GCM / ChaCha20-Poly... |

Per row the emitted test:

1. Builds `kem` from `int_kem`.
2. `(sk_e, pk_e) = derive_key_pair(kem, ikm_e); assert == (skEm, pkEm)`.
3. `(sk_r, pk_r) = derive_key_pair(kem, ikm_r); assert == (skRm, pkRm)`.
4. For AUTH / AUTH_PSK: `(sk_s, pk_s) = derive_key_pair(kem, ikm_s);
   assert == (skSm, pkSm)`.
5. Sender-side: `(shared_secret_s, enc) = kem_(auth_)encap_kat(...);
   assert enc == enc_row && shared_secret_s == sharedSecret_row`.
6. Recipient-side: `shared_secret_r = kem_(auth_)decap_kat(...);
   assert == sharedSecret_row`.

### Data convention fix ‚ÄĒ NIST scalar leading-zero stripping

A first pass surfaced 3 failures on rows where the row's `skSm` is one
byte shorter than the curve byte length:

```
tc_line53_hpke_kem_auth_p521_sha512_aes256gcm      P-521 sk_s 65 vs 66 bytes
tc_line65_hpke_kem_auth_psk_p256_sha512_aes128gcm  P-256 sk_s 31 vs 32 bytes
tc_line71_hpke_kem_auth_psk_p521_sha512_aes256gcm  P-521 sk_s 65 vs 66 bytes
```

The Rust `derive_key_pair` returns the full curve-byte-length scalar
(`ceil(curve_order_bits / 8)`). When the NIST scalar happens to start
with `0x00`, the C SDV `.data` file's hex encoder writes one fewer
byte (`"8feea043‚Ä¶"` instead of `"008feea043‚Ä¶"`). The Rust value is
correct ‚ÄĒ this is a C-side encoder choice. The emitter fix:
`kem_sk_len(kem_id_sym)` (`32` for P-256 and X25519, `48` for P-384,
`66` for P-521) plus `pad_left_to(bytes, n)`. The parsed `sk{E,R,S}m`
hex byte slices are left-padded with `0x00` to the curve length before
being formatted into the test source. This is a no-op when the C row
already wrote the full-length scalar, and it exactly recovers the lost
leading byte when it didn't. X25519 secrets are always 32 bytes (no
leading-zero semantics ‚ÄĒ they are not mod-N scalars), so the pad is
also a no-op there.

### Coverage notes

The 24 rows cover the four HPKE modes √ó the three curves and four
suites that appear in RFC 9180's KEM_TC001 vectors:

| KEM (kemId)                | Modes covered          |
|----------------------------|------------------------|
| DHKEM(X25519, SHA-256) (32)| Base / PSK / Auth / AuthPSK |
| DHKEM(P-256, SHA-256) (16) | Base / PSK / Auth / AuthPSK |
| DHKEM(P-521, SHA-512) (18) | Base / PSK / Auth / AuthPSK |

Note: P-384 (kemId 17) is not exercised by KEM_TC001 (the C SDV file
includes P-384 only in the SHARED_SECRET / AEAD / EXPORT TCs, which
are covered by T149 / T150 / T151).

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo hpke \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 467 TC rows
#   emitted 456 tests, skipped 11 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_hpke
#   test result: ok. 456 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo hpke --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo build -p hitls-crypto
#   Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s)

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib hpke
#   test result: ok. 29 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-T152)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (31 rows in tally) | **2697** | **3693** | **240** | **109** | **6005** |

HPKE: **456/467 (97.6%) covered**. Remaining 11 rows: abnormal /
randomly / generate-key-pair API rows (permanent API-surface ‚ÄĒ EAL ctx
CRUD only, no Rust counterpart).

## Phase T153 ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA VERIFY_KAT_TC001 KAT Migration (first SLH-DSA migration) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: migrates the SLH-DSA verify-side KATs from
`test_suite_sdv_eal_slh_dsa1.data` against `hitls_crypto::slh_dsa`. **168
byte-exact tests, all passing first run**, covering all 12 FIPS 205
parameter sets (SHA-2 / SHAKE √ó {128, 192, 256} √ó {f, s}) ‚ÄĒ 24 positive
(CRYPT_SUCCESS) + 144 negative (HYPERTREE_VERIFY_FAIL 96 +
INVALID_SIG_LEN 48). **Zero new production code** ‚ÄĒ `from_public_key`
and `verify` already exist; the FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.2 pure-mode message prefix
`M' = 0x00 || OCTET_TO_INT(|ctx|, 1) || ctx || msg` is constructed in
each generated test (mirroring the existing `assert_verify_kat` helper
in the lib tests).

### Files changed

- `xtask/src/slhdsa.rs` ‚ÄĒ **new file**, `emit_slhdsa_kat` + `alg_enum`
  (12-variant `CRYPT_SLH_DSA_*` ‚Üí `SlhDsaParamId` mapper).
- `xtask/src/main.rs` ‚ÄĒ `mod slhdsa;` + `"slhdsa"` dispatch arm pointing
  at **both** SLH-DSA `.data` files so total accounting is accurate (the
  sign / genkey / api rows in `slh_dsa.data` route to API-surface here,
  pending T154+).
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_slhdsa.rs` ‚ÄĒ **generated** (168
  tests, gated `#![cfg(feature = "slh-dsa")]`).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ added the SLH-DSA tally row
  (168/181/0/0/349), bumped Total to 2865/3874/240/109/6354, added a
  prose paragraph at the end of the HPKE section.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` ‚ÄĒ this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ row-shape dispatch

C test fn (`test_suite_sdv_eal_slh_dsa1.c`):

```c
void SDV_CRYPTO_SLH_DSA_VERIFY_KAT_TC001(int id, Hex *key, Hex *addrand,
    Hex *msg, Hex *context, Hex *sig, int result);
```

- `id` ‚ÄĒ `CRYPT_SLH_DSA_SHA2_128S` etc. (12 variants).
- `key` ‚ÄĒ full secret key `seed(N) || prf(N) || pk.seed(N) || pk.root(N)`.
  Public key is `key[2N..4N]` (`N = 16 / 24 / 32` for 128/192/256
  security categories).
- `addrand` ‚ÄĒ sign-side hedging randomness (FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.1
  `opt_rand`); irrelevant for verify, ignored by the emitter.
- `msg` / `context` ‚ÄĒ raw message and FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2 pure-mode context.
- `sig` ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA signature.
- `result` ‚ÄĒ `CRYPT_SUCCESS` (24) or one of two negative result codes:
  - `CRYPT_SLHDSA_ERR_INVALID_SIG_LEN` (48) ‚Üí `sig.len()` ‚Č† the
    parameter set's `sig_bytes`. Rust returns `Ok(false)` from the
    length-check branch.
  - `CRYPT_SLHDSA_ERR_HYPERTREE_VERIFY_FAIL` (96) ‚Üí signature content
    rejected by the hypertree verify.

The negative rows assert `!matches!(result, Ok(true))` so the test
accepts either `Ok(false)` or `Err(_)` ‚ÄĒ matching Rust's actual return
behaviour.

### Coverage notes

| Axis | Variants | Notes |
|------|---------:|-------|
| Parameter set | 12 | SHA-2/SHAKE √ó {128, 192, 256} √ó {f, s} |
| Outcome | 2 | CRYPT_SUCCESS / negative |
| Negative subtype | 2 | INVALID_SIG_LEN / HYPERTREE_VERIFY_FAIL |
| Rows per param | 14 | 2 success + 4 INVALID_SIG_LEN + 8 HYPERTREE_FAIL |

The 2 CRYPT_SUCCESS rows per param exercise both empty and non-empty
context ‚ÄĒ the M' prefix wrap is tested in both forms.

### Deferred to T154+

- **SIGN_KAT_TC001** (62) ‚ÄĒ needs a `kat-nonce`-gated
  `sign_with_addrand(msg, addrand)` (deterministic when `addrand == ""`,
  hedged otherwise).
- **GENKEY_KAT_TC001** (88) ‚ÄĒ needs `keygen_with_seed(...)`.
- **VERIFY_PREHASHED_KAT_TC001** (1) ‚ÄĒ FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.3 pre-hash verify
  entry point.
- API / lifecycle rows ‚ÄĒ permanent API-surface.

### Verification

```bash

    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 349 TC rows
#   emitted 168 tests, skipped 181 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_slhdsa
#   test result: ok. 168 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo slhdsa --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib slh_dsa
#   test result: ok. 64 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-T153)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (32 rows in tally) | **2865** | **3874** | **240** | **109** | **6354** |

SLH-DSA: **168/349 (48%) covered**. Remaining 181 = 177 sign/genkey/api
rows (deferred to T154+) + 1 prehash row + 3 misc API rows.

## Phase T154 ‚ÄĒ SLH-DSA SIGN_KAT_TC001 + GENKEY_KAT_TC001 KAT Migration (SLH-DSA 48% ‚Üí 91%) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: extends the T153 SLH-DSA emitter to migrate the
two byte-exact KAT families (SIGN_KAT_TC001 + GENKEY_KAT_TC001) deferred
in T153. **149 byte-exact tests added** (`migrated_slhdsa.rs` 168 ‚Üí
**317**, +88 genkey + 61 sign ‚ÄĒ 62 SIGN rows minus 1 provider-flag
duplicate). All passing first run. Three new `kat-nonce`-gated public
APIs on `SlhDsaKeyPair`; builds clean both with and without `kat-nonce`.
SLH-DSA coverage is now **317/349 (90.8%)**.

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/slh_dsa/mod.rs`: three new
  `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")] + #[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated]`
  `pub fn` on `SlhDsaKeyPair`:
  - `from_private_key(param_id, full_key: &[u8])` ‚ÄĒ `full_key` is the
    4N-byte secret key `SK.seed || SK.prf || PK.seed || PK.root`. Builds
    `Self` directly (skips randomised keygen + the XMSS-top-tree-root
    computation; trusts the row's pre-computed `PK.root`).
  - `from_seeds(param_id, sk_seed, sk_prf, pk_seed)` ‚ÄĒ mirrors
    `generate()`'s body but with caller-supplied seeds (the only
    randomness `generate()` uses); computes `PK.root` via
    `hypertree::xmss_compute_root` at layer `d-1` from `sk_seed` +
    `pk_seed`, assembles `private_key` + `public_key`.
  - `sign_with_addrand(message, addrand)` ‚ÄĒ wraps the existing private
    `sign_internal`. `addrand.is_empty()` ‚áí `opt_rand = PK.seed`
    (`private_key[2N..3N]`), matching FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.1's deterministic
    mode and the C `CRYPT_CTRL_SET_DETERMINISTIC_FLAG`; otherwise
    `opt_rand = addrand` (hedged mode with fixed hedge for KAT
    reproducibility).

- `xtask/src/slhdsa.rs`:
  - `emit_sign_kat` (7-arg row): `(isProvider, algId, key, addrand,
    msg, context, sig)`. Drops `isProvider == 1` as API-surface
    (provider-framework duplicate). Wraps `M' = 0x00 || ctx_len || ctx
    || msg`, then `assert_eq!(kp.sign_with_addrand(&M', addrand),
    sig)`.
  - `emit_genkey_kat` (3-arg row): `(algId, key_3n,
    expected_pk_2n)`. Splits `key` into three N-byte slices `(sk_seed,
    sk_prf, pk_seed)`, then
    `assert_eq!(SlhDsaKeyPair::from_seeds(...).public_key(),
    expected_pk)`.
  - Dispatcher routes `SIGN_KAT_TC001` and `GENKEY_KAT_TC001` to the
    new helpers before falling through to the T153 verify path.

- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_slhdsa.rs`: **regenerated** (168 ‚Üí
  317 tests; file size grew from 33MB ‚Üí 46MB because SLH-DSA-256-F
  signatures are ~50KB each and SIGN_KAT publishes them inline).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md`: SLH-DSA row 168‚Üí317 emitted, 181‚Üí32
  API-surface; Total 2865‚Üí3014 emitted, 3874‚Üí3725 API-surface; T153
  paragraph extended with a T154 paragraph.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md`: this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ production hooks

`from_private_key` is the simplest of the three: the C SIGN_KAT row
already publishes the full 4N-byte secret key with a pre-computed
`PK.root`. We trust that value and assemble `SlhDsaKeyPair` directly.
The signature integration check (`sign_with_addrand` byte-exact against
the row's `sig`) is implicitly verifying that the row's `PK.root` is
consistent with `sk_seed` + `pk_seed`, because any divergence would flow
through `make_hasher(pk_seed, pk_root)` into `prf_msg` / `h_msg` and
produce a different signature.

`from_seeds` cannot just call `from_private_key(...)` with an assembled
4N key because the GENKEY_KAT row only publishes the 3N seeds ‚ÄĒ the
whole point of the KAT is to assert the *computed* `PK.root`. So
`from_seeds` recomputes `PK.root` exactly the way `generate()` does
(top-layer XMSS tree root via `hypertree::xmss_compute_root`), then
assembles the structure. The public_key after construction is the
assertion target.

`sign_with_addrand` is a 5-line wrapper. The deterministic-mode dispatch
(empty `addrand` ‚áí `opt_rand = PK.seed`) is the SLH-DSA analogue of the
ML-DSA `rnd = 0` deterministic mode added in I137 ‚ÄĒ and similarly safe:
SLH-DSA `opt_rand` is hedging-only randomness (FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.1),
reusing it across signatures of *different* messages does not leak the
secret key.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ emitter

The C SIGN_KAT row shape adds `isProvider` as the first column. With the
workspace-wide convention (drop the `isProvider == 1` duplicate rows
that exercise the EAL provider framework), the 62 SIGN rows become 61
byte-exact tests. The `isProvider == 0` row and the `isProvider == 1`
row carry byte-identical KAT data; we keep one.

The 88 GENKEY rows have no `isProvider` column (the C GENKEY function
does not take it). They are 1:1 with the emitted tests.

### Coverage notes

The 88 GENKEY rows cover all 12 parameter sets (uneven distribution ‚ÄĒ
some have 2 rows, some have 10). The 61 SIGN rows likewise cover all 12
parameter sets (4 of them ‚ÄĒ SHA2-192F / SHAKE-128F / SHAKE-192F /
SHA2-128F ‚ÄĒ have 14 / 14 / 14 / 3 rows; the rest have 2-3 each). Both
empty-`addrand` (deterministic) and non-empty-`addrand` (hedged) modes
are exercised in SIGN, in roughly equal proportion (32 empty + 30
non-empty across the 62 raw rows).

### Verification

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo slhdsa \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 349 TC rows
#   emitted 317 tests, skipped 32 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_slhdsa
#   test result: ok. 317 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; finished in 3.31s

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo slhdsa --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo build -p hitls-crypto                       # no kat-nonce
#   Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s)

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib slh_dsa
#   test result: ok. 64 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-T154)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (32 rows in tally) | **3014** | **3725** | **240** | **109** | **6354** |

SLH-DSA: **317/349 (90.8%) covered**. Remaining 32 rows:
- 1 `VERIFY_PREHASHED_KAT_TC001` (FIPS 205 ¬ß10.2.3 pre-hash; needs a
  Rust pre-hash verify entry point).
- 1 `SIGN_KAT_TC001` `isProvider == 1` row (permanent provider-flag
  duplicate).
- 21 `CHECK_KEYPAIR_TC001` / `CHECK_KEYPAIR_TC002` /
  `CHECK_PRVKEY_TC001` (EAL key-validity check API ‚ÄĒ no Rust
  counterpart).
- 9 `API_*` / `GENKEY_TC001` (non-KAT) / `GETSET_KEY_*` / `NEW` /
  `CTRL` rows (permanent API-surface ‚ÄĒ EAL ctx CRUD only).

## Phase T155 ‚ÄĒ XMSS / XMSS-MT VERIFY_KAT_TC001 KAT Migration (first XMSS migration) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: migrates the XMSS / XMSS-MT verify-side KATs from
`test_suite_sdv_eal_xmss.data` against `hitls_crypto::xmss`. **42
byte-exact tests, all passing first run**, covering all 13 parameter
sets the C SDV exercises: 6 single-tree `XmssKeyPair` variants + 7
multi-tree `XmssMtKeyPair` variants. 14 positive (CRYPT_SUCCESS) + 14
MERKLETREE_ROOT_MISMATCH + 14 INVALID_SIG_LEN. **Two new default-feature
public APIs** added to mirror the existing SLH-DSA / ML-DSA
`from_public_key` constructors.

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/xmss/mod.rs`:
  - New `pub fn XmssKeyPair::from_public_key(param_id, public_key)` ‚ÄĒ
    takes the raw 2N public key `PK.seed || PK.root` (no OID prefix),
    prepends the parameter set's OID from `params::oid`, and reorders
    to the internal `OID(4) || PK.root(N) || PK.seed(N)` storage
    layout. Returns a verify-only `XmssKeyPair` (`private_key:
    Vec::new()`, `leaf_idx: 0`, `max_signatures: 0`).
  - New `pub fn XmssMtKeyPair::from_public_key(param_id, public_key)`
    ‚ÄĒ same shape, using `params::mt_oid` and `get_mt_params`.

- `xtask/src/xmss.rs` ‚ÄĒ **new file**, `emit_xmss_kat` + two
  `(C symbol ‚Üí Rust variant, short tag)` lookup tables (`xmss_enum`
  6-entry + `xmssmt_enum` 7-entry). Dispatches on the algId prefix
  (`CRYPT_XMSS_*` ‚Üí single-tree path; `CRYPT_XMSSMT_*` ‚Üí multi-tree
  path). Each row emits one `tc_lineN_xmss_verify_<algo>_<ok|rej>`
  test that builds `kp = <KeyPair>::from_public_key(param, pk)` and
  asserts `matches!(kp.verify(msg, sig), Ok(true))` for CRYPT_SUCCESS
  or `!matches!(..., Ok(true))` for negative rows.

- `xtask/src/main.rs` ‚ÄĒ `mod xmss;` (alphabetic) + `"xmss"` dispatch
  arm; appended `xmss` to the supported-algos error string.

- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_xmss.rs` ‚ÄĒ **generated** (42
  tests, gated `#![cfg(feature = "xmss")]`).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ added the XMSS tally row (42/46/0/0/88);
  Total 3014 ‚Üí 3056 emitted, 3725 ‚Üí 3771 API-surface; appended a T155
  prose paragraph after the SLH-DSA section.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` ‚ÄĒ this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ from_public_key

The Rust `XmssKeyPair` stores `public_key` as `OID(4) || PK.root(N) ||
PK.seed(N)` (matches the RFC 8391 ¬ß4.1.3 wire encoding); the C SDV row
publishes the raw `PK.seed(N) || PK.root(N)` without the OID and in the
reverse order. The constructor:

1. Validates `public_key.len() == 2 * n`.
2. Splits `(pk_seed, pk_root) = (&public_key[..n], &public_key[n..2n])`.
3. Builds `[OID_be(4) | pk_root(n) | pk_seed(n)]` and stores it.

`from_public_key` is *not* `kat-nonce`-gated ‚ÄĒ it's a natural
verify-side entry point that any consumer of XMSS public keys (X.509 /
CMS / TLS) will need. The SLH-DSA / ML-DSA modules have the same
pattern.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ emitter

The C algId symbol mapping has one quirk: `CRYPT_XMSSMT_SHAKE_20_2_512`
denotes the SHAKE256 multi-tree variant (the SHAKE128 family stops at
256-bit hash output per RFC 8391 ¬ß5.2 ‚ÄĒ there is no
`SHAKE128_20_2_512` parameter set). The `xmssmt_enum` lookup maps it
to `XmssMtParamId::Shake256_20_2_512`. A comment in the emitter source
documents this.

The 14 INVALID_SIG_LEN rows test the length-check fast path; the 14
MERKLETREE_ROOT_MISMATCH rows test the inner Merkle root comparison
(via `subtle::ConstantTimeEq::ct_eq` against the public root). Both
are negative KATs; the assertion accepts any `!Ok(true)`.

### Coverage notes

| Family | Variants | Notes |
|--------|---------:|-------|
| Single-tree XMSS | 6 | SHA2_{10_256, 10_512, 10_192} + SHAKE128_10_256 + SHAKE256_{10_256, 10_192} |
| Multi-tree XMSS-MT | 7 | SHA2_{20_2_256, 20_2_512, 20_2_192} + SHAKE128_20_2_256 + SHAKE256_{20_2_512, 20_2_256, 20_2_192} |

All 13 parameter sets get 3 rows each (1 success + 1
INVALID_SIG_LEN + 1 MERKLETREE_ROOT_MISMATCH) = 42 byte-exact tests.

### Deferred to T156+

- **SIGN_KAT_TC001** (28 rows) ‚ÄĒ needs a `kat-nonce`-gated
  `XmssKeyPair::from_private_key(param, full_key_4n, leaf_idx)` (the
  C row includes an `index` column that pins the stateful counter)
  and likely a `sign_at_index` (or some way to set `leaf_idx`
  externally ‚ÄĒ currently `sign` reads it from `&mut self` and
  increments).
- **GENKEY_KAT_TC001** (14 rows) ‚ÄĒ needs `from_seeds(param, sk_seed,
  sk_prf, pk_seed)` (same pattern as the SLH-DSA T154 hook).
- **API / GETSET / GENKEY (non-KAT)** (4 rows) ‚ÄĒ permanent API-surface.

### Verification

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo xmss \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 88 TC rows
#   emitted 42 tests, skipped 46 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_xmss
#   test result: ok. 42 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo xmss --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib xmss
#   test result: ok. 62 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-T155)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (33 rows in tally) | **3056** | **3771** | **240** | **109** | **6442** |

XMSS: **42/88 (48%) covered**. Remaining 46 = 28 sign + 14 genkey + 4
API rows.

## Phase T156 ‚ÄĒ XMSS SIGN_KAT + GENKEY_KAT Migration Attempted + Blocked (Reference-Interop Divergence Diagnosis) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation attempt ‚ÄĒ extends the T155 XMSS emitter with
SIGN_KAT (28) + GENKEY_KAT (14) emitters and the four `kat-nonce`-gated
production hooks needed (`from_private_key` + `from_seeds` on both
`XmssKeyPair` and `XmssMtKeyPair`). All hooks compile clean under both
default and `kat-nonce` configs. **But trial-running the 42 emitted
tests produced 28 byte-exact failures: 14 GENKEY rows + 14
SIGN-success rows**. The 14 SIGN-rej rows (KEY_EXPIRED at
`index == 1 << h`) work fine ‚ÄĒ they test state-checking, not tree
contents.

Diagnostic finding: Rust's `xmss::tree::compute_root` (called by
`generate()` for keygen, and indirectly via `xmss_sign`) produces a
**different `PK.root` from C / RFC 8391** given the same
`(SK.seed, SK.prf, PK.seed)` seeds. Same flavor as the **FrodoKEM
I145** bug ‚ÄĒ internally self-consistent (Rust round-trip works, T155
VERIFY_KAT 42/42 passes), but the **full-tree-build path** is not
byte-compatible with the C reference.

**Production + emitter changes were reverted.** Only the na-list
Structural Gap entry shipped, documenting the divergence + the unblock
path. Same scope-cut decision as **T146** (FrodoKEM/McEliece decaps
diagnosis before I145 fix).

### Why VERIFY_KAT passes but SIGN/GENKEY fail

This is the most informative part of the diagnosis. T155 (VERIFY) and
T156 (SIGN/GENKEY) exercise two different code paths:

| Path | Code | Used by |
|------|------|---------|
| Leaf-from-signature climb | `xmss_root_from_sig` (`wots_pk_from_sig` ‚Üí climb auth path with `h.h(adrs, sibling, node)`) | VERIFY only |
| Full-tree build | `compute_root` (`wots_pk_gen` √ó 2^h leaves ‚Üí `l_tree` compress ‚Üí parent hash up the tree) | GENKEY + SIGN |

VERIFY_KAT passes ‚áí the verify-path leaf-recovery + auth-path-climb is
byte-compatible with C. GENKEY/SIGN failures ‚áí the full-tree-build
path differs. Since the two paths *should* produce the same root for
the same public key, **one of `wots_pk_gen` / `l_tree` /
parent-hash-ADRS in `compute_root`** is off relative to RFC 8391
¬ß4.1.4‚Äď6 / openHiTLS C `XmssTree_ComputeNode`.

The Rust impl is round-trip-correct because both Rust's keygen and
Rust's sign use the same (divergent) tree shape; the resulting
signatures match Rust's pk_root via Rust's auth-path climb. The
mismatch only surfaces against the C reference.

### Trial-run details (verifying the diagnosis, not landed)

The prototype emitted:

- **GENKEY_KAT** (14 rows): row shape `(algId, key_3n,
  expected_root_2n)`. Each row split `key` into three N-byte seeds
  `(sk_seed, sk_prf, pk_seed)` per the C `RandInjection` order, called
  `from_seeds(...)`, then asserted `kp.public_key()[4..4+n] ==
  expected_root[n..2n]` (the `PK.root` half). All 14 rows failed
  byte-exact on the `PK.root` assertion. The `PK.seed` half asserts
  successfully (it's just stored as-is). Confirms divergence is in
  the *computed* root.

- **SIGN_KAT** (14 success + 14 rej rows): row shape `(algId, index,
  key, msg, sig, result)`. SUCCESS rows (index 0, fresh key) failed
  the byte-exact signature comparison; REJ rows (index = 1 << h =
  exhausted) passed (Rust correctly returned
  `Err(XMSS signatures exhausted)`). Confirms the sign path shares
  the divergent tree shape ‚ÄĒ every SUCCESS sign also produces a
  different signature than C.

Both failure modes are consistent with a single root cause in the
full-tree-build path.

### Unblock path

A focused **Implementation phase** (estimate: I-class, akin to I145)
would:

1. Cross-instrument Rust `wots_pk_gen(h, i, adrs, p)` vs C `WotsPkGen`
   for `i == 0` with a fixed `sk_seed` + `pk_seed` ‚ÄĒ compare the
   per-WOTS-chain pub key bytes. If divergent, the bug is in
   `wots_pk_gen` (chain hashing or its ADRS sequence).
2. If WOTS leaves match: instrument `l_tree(...)` vs C
   `LTreeCompress` ‚ÄĒ compare the n-byte compressed leaf. If divergent,
   the bug is in L-tree compression (which RFC 8391 ¬ß4.1.5 specifies
   in detail ‚ÄĒ tree_height / tree_index encoding, KEY/MASK address
   sub-types).
3. If both match: the bug is in the parent-hash loop in `compute_root`
   ‚ÄĒ `tree_adrs.set_tree_height(level)` and
   `set_tree_index(j as u32)` ordering vs C's `XmssTree_ComputeNode`
   recursion.

Once the divergence is fixed (likely a small ADRS-encoding or
off-by-one issue), the T156 prototype hooks + emitters can be
re-landed and the 42 deferred tests will pass byte-exact against C.

### Files changed

- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ adds an "XMSS reference-interop on
  keygen / sign" Structural Gap entry pointing at this phase entry
  for diagnosis details.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` ‚ÄĒ this phase entry.

### Verification

```bash
# Sanity check that T155 baseline still passes after reverting the prototype:
cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_xmss
#   test result: ok. 42 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored
cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib xmss
#   test result: ok. 62 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored
```

### Migration tally (post-T156)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (33 rows in tally) | **3056** | **3771** | **240** | **109** | **6442** |

Unchanged from T155. The 42 XMSS sign+genkey rows that would have been
migrated remain in the deferred bucket pending the unblock work above.

## Phase I146 ‚ÄĒ XMSS PRF_KEYGEN + PRF_msg RFC 8702 Conformance Fix (resolves T156 divergence) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Resolves the **T156-diagnosed XMSS reference-interop divergence** ‚ÄĒ
7th bug found via the migration discipline, cf. SLH-DSA/I129,
CTR-DRBG-df/I131, ASN.1/I133, ML-DSA/I137, CBC-MAC/I144, FrodoKEM/I145.

Two related bugs in `crates/hitls-crypto/src/xmss/hash.rs`:

1. **`prf_keygen` used the RFC 8391 ¬ß5.1 `PRF` construction** instead
   of the **RFC 8702 / NIST SP 800-208 ¬ß6.4 `PRF_KEYGEN`** construction:
   - Wrong domain-separation byte (`3` = `PADDING_PRF` instead of
     `4` = `PADDING_PRF_KEYGEN`)
   - Missing `PK.seed` in the hash input (`PRF_KEYGEN` binds both
     seeds to defeat multi-target attacks; `PRF` binds only `SK.seed`)
2. **`prf_msg` included a spurious `msg` parameter** in the PRF input
   (the C `PrfmsgGeneric` is `H(toByte(3, plen) || SK.prf ||
   toByte(idx, 32))` with no message dependency; the buggy Rust
   version XOR'd `msg` at the end, making `R` message-dependent and
   producing signatures that wouldn't byte-match C even after the
   PRF_KEYGEN fix).

### Why the divergence survived round-trip + T155 VERIFY KAT

The verify path never calls `prf_keygen` ‚ÄĒ `wots_pk_from_sig` recovers
each WOTS+ chain's terminal value from the signature itself by
chaining `msgw[i]..w-1` steps; the secret-key elements are never
re-derived from `SK.seed` during verify. So `generate ‚Üí sign ‚Üí verify`
self-tests succeed (Rust's keygen and sign use the same wrong
`prf_keygen`; Rust's verify uses the right-by-accident `wots_pk_from_sig`
+ `chain`, which give a leaf that the auth-path climb resolves to
Rust's own `PK.root`). And the T155 VERIFY KAT succeeds because
verifying a C signature against a C public key likewise depends only
on `wots_pk_from_sig` + `chain` + `h_msg` + parent-hash, none of which
touch `prf_keygen`.

The bug only surfaces when Rust *generates* a key from a known seed
and we compare against C's `PK.root` (GENKEY KAT) ‚ÄĒ or signs and we
compare against C's signature (SIGN_KAT). The T156 attempt to migrate
these surfaced the divergence.

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/xmss/hash.rs`:
  - `prf_keygen` body rewritten to `H(toByte(4, padding_len) ||
    SK.seed || PK.seed || ADRS)` ‚ÄĒ no longer delegates to `prf`.
  - `prf_msg` body trimmed to drop the `msg` parameter from the hash
    input; the `msg: &[u8]` arg is now `_msg` (kept on the signature
    so call sites don't need to thread an extra `()`). A docstring
    explains the construction and the previous bug for future
    readers.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/xmss/mod.rs`:
  - New permanent KAT lib test `test_xmss_compute_root_kat_sha2_10_256`
    that pins the first `SDV_CRYPTO_XMSS_GENKEY_KAT_TC001`
    SHA2_10_256 row's expected `PK.root` against `compute_root` from
    the row's fixed seeds. Guards against any future regression of
    this fix.

No emitter / dispatch / migrated-test changes ‚ÄĒ those are deferred to
the follow-up T157 phase (re-land the T156-prototype hooks and emit
the 42 deferred SIGN+GENKEY tests).

### Diagnosis path (T156 ‚Üí I146)

T156 documented the divergence and listed a 3-step unblock plan:

1. Cross-instrument `wots_pk_gen` vs C `WotsPkGen` for leaf 0.
2. If WOTS leaves match ‚Üí check `l_tree`.
3. If both match ‚Üí check parent-hash ADRS sequencing.

The actual root-cause investigation took a shortcut: comparing Rust
`prf_keygen` against C `XPrfGeneric` in
`/Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/crypto/xmss/src/xmss_hash.c` line
58-71 surfaced both bugs immediately ‚ÄĒ the C source binds `SK.seed`
**and** `PK.seed` with domain-separation byte `4`, while Rust bound
only `SK.seed` with byte `3`. A diagnostic `#[ignore]` probe test
confirmed the fix produces the expected `PK.root` byte-exact before
the fix was promoted to a permanent KAT.

### Verification

```bash
# New regression KAT ‚ÄĒ pins the fix.
cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib xmss_compute_root_kat
#   test result: ok. 1 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

# Existing xmss lib suite (was 62/0 before this fix).
cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib xmss
#   test result: ok. 63 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored

# T155 VERIFY KAT ‚ÄĒ unchanged behavior, still passes.
cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_xmss
#   test result: ok. 42 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored

cargo run -p xtask --quiet -- migrate-c-tests --algo xmss --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes (drift gate)
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Production impact

The externally-visible byte encoding of XMSS / XMSS-MT public keys
(`PK.root`) and signatures (`R` + WOTS+ chains) now matches the
RFC 8702 / NIST SP 800-208 reference. Previously a Rust-generated
XMSS key or signature was **not** byte-compatible with any standard
implementation; this is the same class of pre-fix state as FrodoKEM
before I145.

The 42 T156-deferred SIGN_KAT + GENKEY_KAT rows are now unblockable.
Re-landing the T156-prototype `kat-nonce`-gated hooks
(`XmssKeyPair::from_private_key` / `from_seeds` /
`sign_with_leaf_idx` + the XmssMt counterparts) + the emitter
SIGN/GENKEY paths is the T157 follow-up.

### Bug count

This is the **7th** real bug found via the C‚ÜíRust migration
discipline. Running tally:

| # | Phase | Bug | Hidden by |
|---|-------|-----|-----------|
| 1 | I129 | SLH-DSA verify | Self round-trip masking |
| 2 | I131 | CTR-DRBG-df BCC chain IV | Self round-trip + only no-df DRBG had KATs |
| 3 | I133 | ASN.1 charset parsing | Tests only exercised ASCII |
| 4 | I137 | ML-DSA `rnd` omitted from `ŌĀ'` | Self round-trip masking |
| 5 | I144 | CBC-MAC double-encryption | Self-fulfilling unit-test expected values |
| 6 | I145 | FrodoKEM pack/unpack endianness | Self round-trip masking |
| **7** | **I146** | **XMSS `prf_keygen` wrong PRF construction** | **Verify path doesn't call `prf_keygen` ‚ÄĒ visible only against external reference vectors** |

---

## Phase T157 ‚ÄĒ XMSS / XMSS-MT SIGN_KAT + GENKEY_KAT Migration (XMSS 48% ‚Üí 95.5%; re-lands T156 prototype post-I146) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: re-lands the T156-prototype `kat-nonce`-gated
production hooks + the SIGN_KAT / GENKEY_KAT emitter additions now that
the **I146 PRF_KEYGEN fix** has made Rust XMSS byte-compatible with the
RFC 8702 reference. **+42 byte-exact tests** added (`migrated_xmss.rs`
42 ‚Üí **84**); the 14 GENKEY rows + 14 SIGN-success rows that all failed
in T156 now pass first run, and the 14 SIGN-rej KEY_EXPIRED rows
(which already worked in T156 because they only exercise state-check)
also pass. XMSS coverage is now **84/88 (95.5%)** ‚ÄĒ only 4 permanent
API-surface rows remain.

This is the mirror of the **T146 ‚Üí I145 ‚Üí T149/T150/T151/T152**
diagnose ‚Üí fix ‚Üí migrate cycle for FrodoKEM/HPKE, and similarly mirrors
**SLH-DSA T154** (verify ‚Üí sign+genkey hook additions).

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/xmss/mod.rs`: 4 new
  `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")] + #[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated]`
  `pub fn` (2 per keypair type):
  - `XmssKeyPair::from_private_key(param_id, full_key: &[u8],
    leaf_idx: u64)` ‚ÄĒ `full_key` is the 4N-byte secret key
    `SK.seed || SK.prf || PK.seed || PK.root`. Builds `Self` directly
    with the row's pre-computed `PK.root` (skips
    `compute_root`) and sets `leaf_idx` from the row's `index` column.
  - `XmssKeyPair::from_seeds(param_id, sk_seed, sk_prf, pk_seed)` ‚ÄĒ
    mirrors `generate()`'s body with caller-supplied seeds; computes
    `PK.root` via the **I146-fixed** `compute_root` from `sk_seed` +
    `pk_seed`. Used by GENKEY_KAT to assert the row's expected
    `PK.root`.
  - `XmssMtKeyPair::from_private_key` / `from_seeds` ‚ÄĒ multi-tree
    counterparts using `get_mt_params` / `params::mt_oid` and the
    layer-d-1 ADRS setup from `XmssMtKeyPair::generate()`.

- `xtask/src/xmss.rs`:
  - `emit_sign_kat` (6-arg row `(algId, index, key, msg, sig, result)`):
    parses the index from the second arg as `u64`; dispatches on the
    algId prefix to either `XmssKeyPair` or `XmssMtKeyPair`; emits one
    `tc_lineN_xmss_sign_<algo>_<ok|rej>` test. For `CRYPT_SUCCESS`
    rows: `kp.sign(msg).expect(...) == expected_sig`. For
    `CRYPT_XMSS_ERR_KEY_EXPIRED` rows: `kp.sign(msg).is_err()` (Rust's
    `sign` returns `Err(InvalidArg("XMSS signatures exhausted"))` when
    `leaf_idx >= max_signatures`).
  - `emit_genkey_kat` (3-arg row `(algId, key_3n, expected_root_2n)`):
    splits `key` into three N-byte slices `(sk_seed, sk_prf,
    pk_seed)` per the C `RandInjection` order; builds `kp =
    <KP>::from_seeds(...)`; asserts both halves of the public key ‚ÄĒ
    `kp.public_key()[4..4 + n] == expected_root[n..2n]` (`PK.root`)
    and `kp.public_key()[4 + n..4 + 2n] == expected_root[..n]`
    (`PK.seed`).
  - New `xmss_n(sym)` / `xmssmt_n(sym)` lookups that return the
    security parameter `n` for the algId ‚ÄĒ needed by the GENKEY
    emitter to slice the 3N seed and 2N expected-root fields without
    importing the params module.
  - Dispatcher routes `SIGN_KAT_TC001` + `GENKEY_KAT_TC001` to the
    new emitters before falling through to the T155 verify path.
  - Generated file's cfg gate widened to
    `all(feature = "xmss", feature = "kat-nonce")` ‚ÄĒ same pattern as
    SLH-DSA / HPKE / RSA test files.

- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_xmss.rs` ‚ÄĒ **regenerated**
  (42 ‚Üí 84 tests; file size 32KB ‚Üí 2.1MB ‚ÄĒ XMSS sigs are ~2-5KB each
  vs SLH-DSA's ~50KB, so the file stays modest).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ tally row XMSS 42 ‚Üí 84 emitted, 46 ‚Üí 4
  API-surface; Total 3056 ‚Üí 3098 emitted, 3771 ‚Üí 3729 API-surface;
  appended T157 paragraph to the XMSS section.
- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` ‚ÄĒ this phase entry.

### Verification

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo xmss \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 88 TC rows
#   emitted 84 tests, skipped 4 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_xmss
#   test result: ok. 84 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; finished in 14.91s

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo xmss --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo build -p hitls-crypto                       # no kat-nonce
#   Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s)

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib xmss
#   test result: ok. 63 passed; 0 failed; 1 ignored
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Coverage notes

The 14 GENKEY rows cover all 13 parameter sets (one per single-tree
algo + one per multi-tree algo, except SHAKE_10_256 which has 2 rows
‚ÄĒ this matches the imbalance in the C `.data` file).

The 28 SIGN rows split evenly across ok / rej: each parameter set has
1 SIGN-ok row at `index=0` (fresh key) and 1 SIGN-rej row at
`index = 1 << h` (exhausted) ‚ÄĒ except SHAKE_10_256 / SHA2_192F /
SHAKE_192F which have additional rows. The `index = 1 << h` values are
**1024** for single-tree (h=10) and **1048576** for multi-tree
(total_h=20) ‚ÄĒ these test that `Self::sign` correctly rejects when the
caller-supplied state has reached `max_signatures`.

### Migration tally (post-T157)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (33 rows in tally) | **3098** | **3729** | **240** | **109** | **6442** |

XMSS: **84/88 (95.5%) covered**. Remaining 4 rows are permanent
API-surface:
- `API_NEW_TC001` (ctx-CRUD smoke)
- `GENKEY_TC001` (non-KAT keygen lifecycle)
- `GETSET_KEY_TC001` / `GETSET_KEY_TC002` (key import/export)

All exercise EAL ctx CRUD only and have no Rust counterpart.

## Phase T158 ‚ÄĒ SM9 (GB/T 38635) Round-Trip KAT Migration (first SM9 family) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

Phase A continuation: opens the SM9 identity-based PKC family. The
C SDV files (`test_suite_sdv_eal_sm9_sign.data` / `_crypt.data` /
`_exchange.data`) are entirely **API-test format** ‚ÄĒ no byte-exact
KAT vectors ‚ÄĒ but the `*_API_TC001/TC002/TC003` and
`CHECK_*_FUNC_TC001` families publish a fixed master secret + user
ID + message per row and drive round-trip / cross-identity /
structural assertions from them. **7 round-trip tests migrated**,
all passing first run.

This phase mirrors the pattern from RSA encrypt (round-trip only)
and the broader migration-discipline principle of "if the C vectors
aren't byte-exact, the migration is the round-trip assertion rather
than a byte-compare". The 7 tests still provide real interop
coverage: they confirm Rust SM9 can self-roundtrip with a fixed
master secret + that derived user keys are functional + that
cross-identity decrypts / verifies fail as expected.

### Files changed

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm9/mod.rs`: one new
  `#[cfg(feature = "kat-nonce")] + #[doc(hidden)] + #[deprecated]`
  `pub fn`:
  - `Sm9MasterKey::from_master_secret(key_type, master_secret: &[u8])`
    ‚ÄĒ `master_secret` is the 32-byte SM9 master scalar `ks`.
    Bypasses `master_keygen()`'s random scalar generation and
    recomputes `master_public` via `ks * P2` (Sign) / `ks * P1`
    (Encrypt) ‚ÄĒ same derivation path used by `generate()`
    post-randomness.

- `xtask/src/sm9.rs` ‚ÄĒ **new file**, `emit_sm9_kat` + 5 per-family
  emitters (`emit_sign_api` / `emit_crypt_api_self` /
  `emit_crypt_api_cross` / `emit_check_keypair` /
  `emit_check_prvkey`). Header-row detection: rows where
  `args.first().as_hex().is_none()` (all-symbol arg list, no quoted
  hex) route to API-surface so they are counted but not migrated.

- `xtask/src/main.rs` ‚ÄĒ `mod sm9;` + `"sm9"` dispatch arm pointing
  at the three `.data` files; appended `sm9` to the supported-algos
  error string.

- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm9.rs` ‚ÄĒ **generated** (7
  tests, gated
  `#![cfg(all(feature = "sm9", feature = "kat-nonce"))]`).

- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ adds the SM9 tally row (7/31/0/0/38),
  bumps Total to 3105/3760/240/109/6480, adds the SM9 KEYEX gap to
  the Structural Gaps table, adds a T158 prose paragraph after the
  T157 XMSS section.

- `DEV_LOG.md` / `PROMPT_LOG.md` ‚ÄĒ this phase entry.

### Implementation details ‚ÄĒ round-trip families

| C TC                            | Row shape                              | Migration                                                                                                                                                |
|---------------------------------|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `SIGN_API_TC001` / `TC002`      | `(masterKey, userId, message)`         | `sign + verify` round-trip; negative wrong-message verify must fail.                                                                                     |
| `CRYPT_API_TC001` / `TC002`     | `(masterKey, userId, plaintext)`       | `encrypt(uid, pt) ‚Üí decrypt(ct) == pt` (same-user round-trip).                                                                                          |
| `CRYPT_API_TC003`               | `(masterKey, uidA, uidB, plaintext)`   | `encrypt(uidB) ‚Üí user_b.decrypt(ct) == pt`; negative: `user_a.decrypt(ct).is_err()` (cross-identity decrypt must fail).                                  |
| `CHECK_KEYPAIR_FUNC_TC001`      | `(masterKey, userId1, userId2)`        | Extract two user keys from the same master; sign+verify both; negative: verify `sig1` against `userId2` (cross-identity) must fail.                      |
| `CHECK_PRVKEY_FUNC_TC001`       | `(masterKey, userId)`                  | Extract one user key; sign+verify a sample message ‚ÄĒ validates that the derived private key is functional.                                               |

### Structural Gap ‚ÄĒ SM9 key exchange

The 2 `KEYEX_API_TC001 / TC002` rows in `sm9_exchange.data` exercise
the SM9 identity-based key-exchange protocol (GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4). The
Rust `Sm9MasterKey` has no such API ‚ÄĒ `extract_user_key` / `sign` /
`verify` / `encrypt` / `decrypt` are the only operations. Added to
the na-list Structural Gaps table; unblock = add an SM9
key-exchange API per GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4 (will surface 2 more tests).

### Verification

```bash
cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo sm9 \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   parsed 38 TC rows
#   emitted 7 tests, skipped 31 API-surface, 0 unknown, 0 unsupported-alg

cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --test migrated_sm9
#   test result: ok. 7 passed; 0 failed; 0 ignored; finished in 5.74s

cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo sm9 --check \
    --c-root /Users/dongqiu/Dev/code/openhitls/testcode/sdv/testcase
#   passes

cargo build -p hitls-crypto                       # no kat-nonce
#   Finished `dev` profile [unoptimized + debuginfo] target(s)
```

`cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
--all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-T158)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3105** | **3760** | **240** | **109** | **6480** |

SM9: **7/38 (18.4%) covered**. The low coverage ratio reflects the
API-test nature of the C SDV (15 header rows + 14 ctx-CRUD rows are
permanent API-surface). The 7 emitted tests are the **substantive**
SM9 coverage ‚ÄĒ they exercise sign+verify, encrypt+decrypt (same-user
and cross-user), and user-key derivation+validity end-to-end.

## Phase I147 ‚ÄĒ AES-KW PAD (RFC 5649) Implementation (resolves T148 Structural Gap) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

`hitls_crypto::modes::wrap` previously only implemented the **RFC 3394
NOPAD** key-wrap (`key_wrap` / `key_unwrap` ‚ÄĒ block-aligned input only).
T148 surfaced this as a Structural Gap: 8 C-SDV `*_WRAP_PAD` rows could
not be migrated because the Rust port had no entry point for **RFC 5649
Key Wrap with Padding**, which allows arbitrary byte-length input via a
self-describing AIV.

I147 ships the two public APIs and resolves the gap end-to-end:

- `pub fn key_wrap_pad(kek: &[u8], plaintext: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError>`
- `pub fn key_unwrap_pad(kek: &[u8], wrapped: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError>`

Both APIs are **default-feature** (no `kat-nonce` gating; this is
production crypto, not a test hook).

### Algorithm

Per RFC 5649 ¬ß3:

- **AIV** = `0xA65959A6 ‚ÄĖ MLI` where `MLI = plaintext.len() as u32`
  (big-endian).
- The plaintext is zero-padded to the next 8-byte boundary; call the
  block count `n = ceil(len / 8)`.
- **Wrap:**
  - `n == 1` (1..=8 byte plaintext): single AES-ECB block,
    `ciphertext = AES_Encrypt(K, AIV ‚ÄĖ padded_8B)` (RFC 5649 ¬ß4.1).
  - `n ‚Č• 2`: identical to RFC 3394's 6-round wrap, but with the AIV as
    the initial `A` value (delegated to the new shared
    `wrap_inner(cipher, iv, plaintext_blocks)` helper).
- **Unwrap:**
  - `len == 16`: single-block AES-ECB decrypt ‚Üí `(AIV, padded_8B)`.
  - `len ‚Č• 24`: 6-round reverse via the new shared
    `unwrap_inner(cipher, wrapped) -> (iv, plaintext_blocks)` helper.
  - Validate **AIV prefix** (constant-time compare against
    `0xA65959A6`), **MLI bounds** (`8*(n-1) < MLI ‚Č§ 8*n`), and **zero
    padding** (constant-time `OR`-accumulator over
    `padded[MLI..8*n]`). Any failure ‚Üí `CryptoError::AeadTagVerifyFail`.

The shared `wrap_inner` / `unwrap_inner` helpers replace inlined
6-round loops that previously lived inside `key_wrap` / `key_unwrap`.
The NOPAD path is a pure refactor ‚ÄĒ it now calls `wrap_inner` with
`DEFAULT_IV` (`0xA6A6A6A6A6A6A6A6`) and verifies the same constant on
unwrap. **No semantic change** to the existing NOPAD API.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/wrap.rs` ‚ÄĒ module doc updated;
  `RFC5649_AIV_PREFIX` constant; `wrap_inner` / `unwrap_inner`
  extracted; `key_wrap` / `key_unwrap` re-wired to delegate;
  `key_wrap_pad` / `key_unwrap_pad` added; 5 new inline `#[test]`
  cases (2 RFC 5649 ¬ß6 / openHiTLS C SDV positive vectors + 3
  negatives).
- `xtask/src/aead.rs` ‚ÄĒ `emit_aes_kw_kat` recognises `_PAD` algids and
  emits `key_wrap_pad` / `key_unwrap_pad` calls (was: drop as
  `skipped_unsupported_alg`).
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_aes_kw.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated
  (16 ‚Üí 24 tests; 0 unsupported).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ AES-KW PAD Structural Gap row struck
  through, points at I147; AES-KW tally `16/18/0/8/42` ‚Üí `24/18/0/0/42`;
  Total `3105/3760/240/109/6480` ‚Üí `3113/3760/240/101/6480`.

### Verification

- `wrap.rs` lib tests: 12/0 (was 7/0; +5 new).
- `migrated_aes_kw` tests: 24/0 (was 16/0; +8 PAD rows).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo aes-kw --check`: drift gate
  clean.
- Default-feature build: `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo build -p
  hitls-crypto` clean (PAD APIs unconditional).
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy --workspace
  --all-features --all-targets` clean.

### Migration tally (post-I147)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3113** | **3760** | **240** | **101** | **6480** |

AES-KW: **24/42 (57.1%) covered** (up from 16/42 = 38.1%). The
remaining 18 are permanent API-surface (TC002 lifecycle + NOT_ALIGN /
ADDR_NOT_ALIGN families).

## Phase I148 ‚ÄĒ DRBG Variants Expansion (resolves 4 T139-deferred Structural Gaps) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

T139 migrated the openHiTLS C SDV DRBG vectors but could only emit 6
of 18 distinct algIds ‚ÄĒ `HashDrbgType` was SHA-256/384/512 only,
`HmacDrbg` was hardcoded to SHA-256, `CtrDrbg` was AES-256 only, and
`Sm4CtrDrbg` lacked the with-df constructor. Twelve rows were
recorded as four na-list Structural Gaps:

| Gap | Rows |
|-----|-----:|
| Hash-DRBG SHA-1 / SHA-224 / SM3 | 3 |
| HMAC-DRBG SHA-1 / 224 / 384 / 512 | 4 |
| CTR-DRBG AES-128 / 192 (¬Īdf) | 4 |
| SM4-CTR-DRBG-df | 1 |

I148 closes all four gaps end-to-end. All production additions are
**additive and default-feature** ‚ÄĒ no breaking ABI change to the
existing constructors that other crates call.

### Production additions

**`HashDrbgType`** (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/hash_drbg.rs`)
gains three new variants:

- `Sha1` (seedlen=55, output=20)
- `Sha224` (seedlen=55, output=28)
- `Sm3` (seedlen=55, output=32) ‚ÄĒ mirrors SHA-256's seedlen per
  the openHiTLS C reference `DRBG_NewHashCtxBase` switch and
  GM/T 0105-2021.

SP 800-90A Table 2 confirms 55 / 55 / 55 / 111 / 111 seed lengths
for SHA-1 / SHA-224 / SHA-256 / SHA-384 / SHA-512.

**`HmacDrbgType`** (new public enum) +
**`HmacDrbg::with(ty, seed)`** +
**`HmacDrbg::from_system_entropy_with(ty, seed_len)`** generalise
HMAC-DRBG over the digest. Internal storage (`k` / `v`) migrates
from `[u8; 32]` to `Vec<u8>` sized by `ty.output_size()`. A new
internal `hmac(ty, key, data, out)` helper dispatches the digest
factory based on the type. `HmacDrbg::new(seed)` and
`from_system_entropy(seed_len)` retained as SHA-256 thin wrappers,
so all 8 existing callers (`fips::kat`, benches, `migrated_drbg`
old emitter output, interop `concurrency.rs` tests) keep working
without source-level changes.

**`CtrDrbgType`** (new public enum) +
**`CtrDrbg::with(ty, seed)`** +
**`CtrDrbg::with_df_typed(ty, entropy, nonce, pers)`** +
**`CtrDrbg::from_system_entropy_with(ty)`** generalise CTR-DRBG
over the AES key length. Storage stays as `[u8; MAX_KEY_LEN=32]`
with the active length tracked via `ty.key_len()` ‚ÄĒ avoids a
`Vec` allocation on the hot encrypt-loop path. `block_cipher_df`
takes a new `key_len` parameter so the canonical seed K =
`0x00,0x01,‚Ä¶,0x1F` truncated to `key_len` matches the C reference
`dfKey[32]` table truncated via `ctx->keyLen`. `CtrDrbg::new` /
`with_df` / `from_system_entropy` retained as AES-256 wrappers.

**`Sm4CtrDrbg::with_df(entropy, nonce, personalization)`** mirrors
the AES-CTR-DRBG `with_df` path, with a new SM4-CBC-MAC
`block_cipher_df` helper (canonical K = `0x00,0x01,‚Ä¶,0x0F`).

**`Cargo.toml`** ‚ÄĒ the `drbg` feature gains `sha1` + `sm3` deps so
the new `HashDrbgType::Sha1` / `Sm3` constructors compile
out-of-the-box for anyone enabling `drbg`. The `hmac_drbg`
generalisation does not add new feature deps (Hmac was already
generic; SHA-1/SM3/etc. just need their hash modules visible,
which `drbg` now ensures).

### Emitter additions

`xtask/src/drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ the `Drbg` enum gains six variants
(`Hash(ty)` / `Hmac(ty)` / `CtrNoDf{ty, seed_len}` / `CtrDf(ty)` /
`Sm4CtrNoDf` / `Sm4CtrDf`). `migratable()` adds 12 new algId rows.
The generate-call emission has one special case: SM4-CTR-DRBG
chunks the 32-byte request into two 16-byte calls. This mirrors
openHiTLS C `DRBG_MAX_REQUEST_SM4 = 16` (GM/T 0105-2021 quirk; AES
uses 65536) ‚ÄĒ the C SDV invokes the EAL-chunked
`CRYPT_EAL_RandbytesEx(output, 32)`, which `DRBG_GenerateBytes`
splits into multiple spec'd single-generate calls of size
`maxRequest`, each with its own post-Update step. Without this
chunking the second 16 bytes of the SM4-DF KAT mismatch (different
post-Update mutates K and V between blocks).

### Verification

- `drbg` lib tests: 53/0 (was 51/0; +1 HMAC variant smoke test, +2
  CTR variant smoke tests, +1 SM4 with-df smoke test = +4 new
  internal tests in fact).
- `migrated_drbg.rs`: 6 ‚Üí **18 byte-exact tests** (+12). All
  pass first run, including the previously-failing SM4-CTR-DF row
  once the 16-byte chunking was wired into the emitter.
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo drbg --check`: clean.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: green across all
  test binaries (sha2/sha3/aes/sm4/sm3/hash/hmac/hkdf/pbkdf2/etc.).
- `cargo test -p hitls-integration-tests --test concurrency`: 10/0
  ‚ÄĒ `HmacDrbg::from_system_entropy` retained the SHA-256 default
  ABI.
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/hash_drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ `HashDrbgType` +
  `Sha1` / `Sha224` / `Sm3` variants.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/hmac_drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ rewritten:
  `HmacDrbgType` enum + `HmacDrbg::with` / `from_system_entropy_with`;
  `k`/`v` ‚Üí `Vec<u8>`; internal `hmac(ty, ...)` helper.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/ctr_drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ rewritten:
  `CtrDrbgType` enum + `CtrDrbg::with` / `with_df_typed` /
  `from_system_entropy_with`; storage tracks active key length;
  `block_cipher_df(input, output_len, key_len)`.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/sm4_ctr_drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ added
  `Sm4CtrDrbg::with_df` + SM4 `block_cipher_df`.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/drbg/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ re-export the three new
  type enums.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/Cargo.toml` ‚ÄĒ `drbg = ["hmac", "sha1",
  "sha2", "sm3", "aes"]`.
- `xtask/src/drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ emitter rewrite (+12 algId rows; SM4
  chunking).
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_drbg.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ 4 DRBG gap rows struck through;
  tally bump.

### Migration tally (post-I148)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3125** | **3760** | **240** | **89** | **6480** |

DRBG: **18/290 (6.2%) covered**. The remaining 272 are permanent
API-surface (init/reseed/generate/deinit lifecycle exercise rows).
The 18 emitted are the **substantive** DRBG coverage ‚ÄĒ they exercise
every standardised DRBG variant byte-exactly against NIST + GM/T
reference vectors.

## Phase I149 ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 PRF SHA-512 (resolves T144-deferred Structural Gap) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

T144 migrated the TLS 1.2 PRF KAT family but had to drop the
2 `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA512` rows because `hitls_tls::crypt::HashAlgId`
only carried `Sha256` / `Sha384` / `Sha1` (+ `Sm3` under
`tlcp`/`sm_tls13`). The gap was recorded as a na-list entry. I149
adds the `Sha512` variant so the two rows can be migrated byte-exact.

### Production additions (all default-feature, additive)

- `HashAlgId::Sha512` ‚ÄĒ new variant on the lightweight TLS PRF /
  HKDF / transcript hash identifier enum.
- `DigestVariant::Sha512(Sha512)` ‚ÄĒ matching arm in the stack-
  allocated digest dispatch enum, with new arms in
  `DigestVariant::new`, `output_size_for`, and the `Digest` impl
  (`output_size`, `block_size`, `update`, `finish`, `reset`).
- `hitls_tls::crypt::mod` adds `Sha512` to its
  `use hitls_crypto::sha2::{...}` import.

**Notably: no production *call site* changes.** TLS 1.2 itself
never negotiates a SHA-512 PRF ‚ÄĒ no cipher suite in RFC 5246 / RFC
5288 advertises it (HMAC-SHA-256 / HMAC-SHA-384 cover the entire
TLS 1.2 PRF surface). The new variant is unreachable from
`CipherSuiteParams::from_suite` or any handshake state-machine
code; it exists purely so the C SDV `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA512` rows
can be expressed against the same `prf(HashAlgId::...)` entry
point as the SHA-256/SHA-384 rows.

### Emitter

`xtask/src/kdf.rs::mac_to_hashalgid` adds a `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA512
‚Üí "Sha512"` row. The module doc is reworded ‚ÄĒ the SHA-512 variant
is no longer "no HashAlgId" but "migration-only".

### Verification

- `hitls-tls --all-features` lib tests: **1556/0** ‚ÄĒ unchanged
  (the new variant is unreachable from any production call site,
  so no behaviour regression).
- `migrated_kdf_tls12.rs`: 4 ‚Üí **6 byte-exact tests** (+2 SHA-512
  PRF vectors). All pass first run.
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo kdf-tls12 --check`:
  drift gate clean.
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-tls/src/crypt/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `HashAlgId` + `DigestVariant`
  + the 5 `Digest`-trait arms.
- `xtask/src/kdf.rs` ‚ÄĒ `mac_to_hashalgid` SHA-512 row + doc
  reword.
- `crates/hitls-tls/tests/migrated_kdf_tls12.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ TLS 1.2 PRF SHA-512 gap row struck
  through; TLS1.2-PRF tally 4/16/0/2/22 ‚Üí 6/16/0/0/22;
  Total bump.

### Migration tally (post-I149)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3127** | **3760** | **240** | **87** | **6480** |

## Phase I150 ‚ÄĒ HMAC SHA-3 + CMAC SM4 (resolves HMAC/CMAC SHA-3 Structural Gap) (2026-05-31)

### Summary

The T6-era HMAC/CMAC migration left 8 rows on the table because the
Rust port had two distinct gaps in the MAC layer:

- `crate::provider::Digest` was not implemented for any SHA-3 variant,
  so `Hmac::mac(factory, key, msg)` could not be instantiated with a
  SHA-3 hasher (4 HMAC SHA-3 KAT rows).
- `cmac::Cmac` was hardcoded to AES via `AesKey`, so there was no
  Rust counterpart for `CRYPT_MAC_CMAC_SM4` (4 CMAC SM4 KAT rows).

I150 closes both gaps end-to-end with three additive, default-feature
production changes.

### Production additions

**1. SHA-3 ‚Üí `Digest`** (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/sha3/mod.rs`).
A new `impl_sha3_digest!($ty, $out_size, $block_size)` macro
generates the trait impl for each fixed-output variant:

| Variant   | output | block / rate |
|-----------|-------:|-------------:|
| SHA3-224  |     28 |          144 |
| SHA3-256  |     32 |          136 |
| SHA3-384  |     48 |          104 |
| SHA3-512  |     64 |           72 |

The block size reported by `Digest::block_size()` is the **Keccak
rate in bytes** per FIPS 202 ¬ß6.1, which is also the HMAC
inner-pad / outer-pad block length. The XOF variants
(`Shake128` / `Shake256`) intentionally **don't** implement `Digest`
‚ÄĒ their output length is caller-chosen, and `Digest::output_size()`
expects a fixed number.

**2. `CmacSm4`** (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/cmac/mod.rs`).
The `Cmac` struct was refactored into a private
`define_cmac!($name, $cipher)` macro that generates both:

- `Cmac` (over `AesKey`) ‚ÄĒ the existing public API, unchanged
  signature, all 9 existing AES lib tests + RFC 4493 vectors
  still pass byte-identically.
- `CmacSm4` (over `Sm4Key`, `#[cfg(feature = "sm4")]`) ‚ÄĒ new
  feature-gated companion.

Both 128-bit block ciphers, so RFC 4493 / NIST SP 800-38B applies
verbatim: same Rb = `0x87`, same K1/K2 = `dbl(E_K(0^n))` derivation,
same 10*-pad rule for the partial last block. Only the cipher
primitive plugged in differs. The shared body keeps the two
contexts byte-identical and avoids the maintenance cost of a
copy-paste second implementation.

**3. `MAX_BLOCK_SIZE` widened** (`crates/hitls-crypto/src/hmac/mod.rs`).
HMAC stores the inner/outer-pad-XOR-key in a stack `[u8; MAX_BLOCK_SIZE]`
buffer. The constant was 128 (sized for SHA-512). SHA3-224's rate
is **144**, so a 128-byte buffer panicked on `copy_from_slice` when
HMAC-SHA3-224 ran (root cause for the initial 2 failing tests
during T6 emission verification). I150 widens to 144 ‚ÄĒ the widest
block among all wired digests. Other rates fit:

- SHA-512 / SHA-384: 128
- SHA3-256: 136
- SHA3-384: 104
- SHA3-512: 72
- SHA-256 / SHA-224 / SM3 / SHA-1 / MD5: 64

### xtask emitter updates

`xtask/src/mac.rs`:

- `alg_to_rust` learns `CRYPT_MAC_HMAC_SHA3_{224,256,384,512} ‚Üí
  ("Sha3_{N}", "sha3")`. The 4 previously-unsupported HMAC rows
  now route through the new `Digest` impls.
- `feature_for` / `alg_lower` gain SHA-3 entries; the file-header
  writer adds a `use hitls_crypto::sha3::{Sha3_*}` import block.
- `cmac_alg_label` is renamed to `cmac_alg` and returns
  `(label, CmacCipher)`. `CmacCipher::Sm4` triggers an additional
  `use hitls_crypto::cmac::CmacSm4` import + widens the file-level
  cfg gate to include `feature = "sm4"`.
- Both generated test files keep their existing layout ‚ÄĒ no
  `#![cfg(...)]` change for files without any new variants.

### Verification

- `sha3` lib tests: unchanged (Digest impls are additive).
- `hmac` lib tests: 31/0 unchanged.
- `cmac` lib tests: 9/0 unchanged (AES path RFC 4493 + NIST SP 800-38B
  D.3 vectors still byte-exact after the macro refactor).
- `migrated_hmac.rs`: 43/0 ‚Üí **47/0** (+4 SHA-3 KAT ‚ÄĒ SHA3-224 /
  SHA3-256 / SHA3-384 / SHA3-512, NIST CAVP).
- `migrated_cmac.rs`: 12/0 ‚Üí **16/0** (+4 SM4 KAT ‚ÄĒ
  `CRYPT_MAC_CMAC_SM4` FUN_TC004 row).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo hmac --check` /
  `--algo cmac --check`: clean drift.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: green across all
  test binaries (sha3 / hmac / cmac / aes / sm4 / kdf / drbg / etc.).
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sha3/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `impl_sha3_digest!`
  macro + 4 `impl Digest for Sha3_N`.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/cmac/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Cmac` refactored to
  `define_cmac!` macro + new `CmacSm4`.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/hmac/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `MAX_BLOCK_SIZE`
  128 ‚Üí 144 + doc rationale.
- `xtask/src/mac.rs` ‚ÄĒ SHA-3 alg rows + SM4 cipher routing +
  per-feature header writes.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_hmac.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_cmac.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.

  through; HMAC + CMAC tallies bumped; Total bump.
