## Phase I151 ‚ÄĒ AES-CBC Raw KAT Helpers (resolves AES-CBC raw KAT Structural Gap) (2026-06-01)

### Summary

The T6-era AES KAT migration emitted only ECB + CTR rows because
`cbc_encrypt` / `cbc_decrypt` in `hitls_crypto::modes::cbc`
hard-wired PKCS#7 padding. NIST CAVP / FIPS 197 Annex C KAT vectors
publish block-aligned plaintext/ciphertext directly, so they could
not flow through the padded helpers:

- `cbc_encrypt(key, iv, pt)` always emits `pt.len() + 16` bytes ‚ÄĒ
  the trailing padding block is never present in the C vector.
- `cbc_decrypt(key, iv, ct)` rejects the unpadded ciphertext as
  "invalid padding" because the last decrypted byte is not a valid
  PKCS#7 pad count.

I151 closes the gap with raw (no-padding) entry points. NIST SP
800-38A ¬ß6.2 explicitly defines CBC without prescribing a padding
scheme, so the raw helpers are spec-conformant.

### Production additions

Four new public functions in `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/cbc.rs`,
all default-feature and additive (no ABI break on the existing
padded API):

- **`cbc_encrypt_raw(key, iv, plaintext) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`** ‚ÄĒ
  AES, requires `plaintext.len() > 0` and
  `plaintext.len() % AES_BLOCK_SIZE == 0`. Returns ciphertext of
  identical length.
- **`cbc_decrypt_raw(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`** ‚ÄĒ
  AES, identical alignment requirements. Returns plaintext of
  identical length (no unpadding).
- **`cbc_encrypt_raw_with<C: BlockCipher>(cipher, iv, plaintext)`** ‚ÄĒ
  generic over any 16-byte block cipher (AES today, SM4 future).
- **`cbc_decrypt_raw_with<C: BlockCipher>(cipher, iv, ciphertext)`** ‚ÄĒ
  generic decrypt counterpart.

Errors are returned for empty input and unaligned input (consistent
with the existing `cbc_decrypt` "CBC input not block-aligned"
behaviour). IV-length errors return `InvalidIvLength`.

### Emitter (xtask)

`xtask/src/cipher.rs`:

- `aes_alg_label` now routes `CRYPT_CIPHER_AES{128,192,256}_CBC ‚Üí
  ("cbc", N)` instead of returning `None`. The doc-comment "Skipped
  until the Rust port grows a `cbc_encrypt_raw` helper" is removed
  ‚ÄĒ the helper exists now.
- `emit_one_shot` adds the `("cbc", true)` / `("cbc", false)` arms
  emitting `cbc_encrypt_raw(key, iv, input)` / `cbc_decrypt_raw(...)`
  calls.
- IV-shape sanity check now treats CBC like CTR (requires
  non-empty IV).
- Added a guard: CBC rows whose input or output length is not a
  multiple of 16 route to `skipped_unknown` ‚ÄĒ defensive in case a
  future row carries a padded variant.
- `write_header` learns the `use hitls_crypto::modes::cbc::{
  cbc_decrypt_raw, cbc_encrypt_raw};` import block.

### Verification

- `cbc` lib tests: 7/0 ‚Üí **11/0** (+4 ‚ÄĒ NIST SP 800-38A F.2.1
  encrypt+decrypt roundtrip vector + 2 negative tests for unaligned
  input + 1 raw-roundtrip proptest).
- `migrated_aes.rs`: 30/0 ‚Üí **42/0** (+12 ‚ÄĒ CBC AES-128/192/256
  encrypt + decrypt rows, 2 NIST vectors per dir per key size).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo aes --check`: drift
  gate clean.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: full crate green
  (317/14/6/9/7/84/15/8 across all test binaries; no regression on
  existing padded `cbc_encrypt` / `cbc_decrypt` / SM4-CBC / AEAD /
  CCM / KW / etc.).
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/cbc.rs` ‚ÄĒ 4 new public functions
  + module-level rationale comment + NIST SP 800-38A F.2.1 inline
  KAT + 2 negative tests + 1 raw-roundtrip proptest.
- `xtask/src/cipher.rs` ‚ÄĒ CBC routing + IV/alignment guards +
  header imports.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_aes.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ AES-CBC raw KAT gap row struck
  through; AES tally bumped; Total bump.

### Migration tally (post-I151)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3147** | **3760** | **240** | **67** | **6480** |

## Phase I152 ‚ÄĒ SipHash-2-4-128 (resolves SipHash-128 Structural Gap) (2026-06-01)

### Summary

The T145 SipHash migration could only emit 2 rows because
`hitls_crypto::siphash::SipHash` was the 64-bit variant only:
`SipHash::finish() -> u64` returned exactly 8 output bytes, so the
2 `CRYPT_MAC_SIPHASH128` SDV rows (16-byte expected MACs) had to
be dropped at emit time.

I152 adds the 128-bit variant as `SipHash128` and routes the
deferred rows through it.

### Production additions

Added `SipHash128` struct to `crates/hitls-crypto/src/siphash/mod.rs`
(default-feature when `siphash` is enabled, additive, no ABI break).

Per Aumasson & Bernstein the 128-bit variant shares the same core
permutation as the 64-bit one; the three deltas are:

1. **Initialization** ‚ÄĒ `v1 ^= 0xee` (in addition to the standard
   `k1` XOR).
2. **Finalization XOR** ‚ÄĒ `v2 ^= 0xee` (instead of `0xff`).
3. **Output extraction** ‚ÄĒ after the standard 4 finalization
   rounds produce the first 8 output bytes as `v0 ^ v1 ^ v2 ^ v3`
   (little-endian), `v1 ^= 0xdd` and another 4 rounds produce the
   second 8 output bytes.

The message-processing core (`sip_round`, `process_word`, the
8-byte word-feed loop in `update`) is bit-identical to the 64-bit
variant. Rather than share state (which would couple two distinct
keyed PRFs), the 128-bit path lives in its own struct.

Public API mirrors the 64-bit `SipHash`:

- `SipHash128::new(key: &[u8]) -> Result<Self, _>`
- `SipHash128::update(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), _>`
- `SipHash128::finish(&self) -> Result<[u8; 16], _>`
- `SipHash128::reset(&mut self)`
- `SipHash128::hash(key, data) -> Result<[u8; 16], _>` (one-shot)

### Emitter

`xtask/src/aead.rs::emit_siphash_kat` now routes
`CRYPT_MAC_SIPHASH128` rows through `SipHash128::hash` (the
previous `if !alg.contains("SIPHASH64") ‚Üí skipped_unsupported_alg`
branch is gone). The generated test for 128-bit rows asserts
`mac.as_slice() == expected` (already `[u8; 16]`); the 64-bit path
keeps the previous `mac.to_le_bytes().as_slice()` form.

### Verification

- `siphash` lib tests: 8/0 ‚Üí **14/0** (+6: 5 Aumasson
  `vectors_sip128.h` reference vectors covering input lengths 0..4
  with key `00..0f`, plus the openHiTLS C SDV vector, plus
  incremental-equivalence + reset + key-length validation + 64-vs-128
  divergence smoke tests).
- `migrated_siphash.rs`: 2/0 ‚Üí **4/0** (+2 SIPHASH128 ‚ÄĒ the
  `ADDR_NOT_ALIGN` + `SAMEADDR` rows).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo siphash --check`:
  drift gate clean.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: full crate green.
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/siphash/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ new `SipHash128`
  struct + 6 new lib tests.
- `xtask/src/aead.rs` ‚ÄĒ SIPHASH128 routing + per-variant test
  emission + conditional import.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_siphash.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ SipHash-128 gap struck through (with
  the affected-count corrected 39 ‚Üí 2; see note in the gap row);
  SipHash tally bumped; Total bump.

### Note on the affected-row count

The previous na-list gap row claimed `SipHash-128` affected **39**
rows. That count was incorrect: the C SDV `.data` file carries
only 2 SIPHASH128 KAT-shaped rows (`ADDR_NOT_ALIGN_FUNC_TC001` +
`SAMEADDR_FUNC_TC001`, each 4-arg `(alg, key, data, mac)`). The
remaining 39 SipHash rows are API-surface ‚ÄĒ init/reinit/deinit,
copy_ctx, dup_ctx, get-mac-len probes, etc. ‚ÄĒ which are unrelated
to the 128-bit output gap and were already correctly counted as
API-surface in the tally (37 API + 2 unsupported = 39 non-emitted).
The new gap row reflects the correct delta: 2 unsupported ‚Üí 0.

### Migration tally (post-I152)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3149** | **3760** | **240** | **65** | **6480** |

## Phase I153 ‚ÄĒ SM4 raw modes (closes most of SM4 Structural Gap) (2026-06-01)

### Summary

The T6-era SM4 KAT migration emitted only 9 rows because the SM4
cipher had no public CBC-raw / CTR / CFB / OFB entry points ‚ÄĒ
only ECB block primitive + PKCS#7-padded `sm4_cbc_encrypt` /
`sm4_cbc_decrypt` + AEAD-style `sm4_gcm_encrypt` were exposed.
The migration emitter (`xtask/src/sm4.rs`) thus routed
`CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_{CBC,CTR,CFB,OFB}` rows to `skipped_unsupported_alg`
(37 rows total).

I153 closes most of this gap by adding five SM4 stream / block-chain
mode public functions. 26 of the 37 unsupported rows now route to
byte-exact migrated KAT.

### Production additions

All default-feature, `#[cfg(feature = "sm4")]`, additive ‚ÄĒ no ABI
break on existing AES paths.

**`hitls_crypto::modes::cbc`:**

- `sm4_cbc_encrypt_raw(key, iv, plaintext) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`
- `sm4_cbc_decrypt_raw(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`

Both are 2-line wrappers around the existing
`cbc_encrypt_raw_with<Sm4Key>` / `cbc_decrypt_raw_with<Sm4Key>`
generic helpers that I151 added (which themselves are constrained
to 16-byte block ciphers ‚ÄĒ SM4 fits exactly).

**`hitls_crypto::modes::ctr`:**

- `sm4_ctr_crypt(key, nonce, data: &mut [u8])` ‚ÄĒ in-place,
  symmetric. ~30 LOC; SM4 has no 4-block ECB pipeline (`Sm4Key`
  exposes only `encrypt_block`), so this uses a simple per-block
  loop instead of the AES `encrypt_4_blocks` 64-byte pipeline. The
  128-bit big-endian counter increment is shared with the AES path
  via the file's private `increment_counter` helper.

**`hitls_crypto::modes::cfb`:**

- `sm4_cfb_encrypt(key, iv, plaintext) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`
- `sm4_cfb_decrypt(key, iv, ciphertext) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`

Mirror the AES CFB-128 implementation with `Sm4Key` in place of
`AesKey`. Same partial-block feedback handling.

**`hitls_crypto::modes::ofb`:**

- `sm4_ofb_crypt(key, iv, data: &mut [u8])` ‚ÄĒ symmetric.

Mirror the AES OFB implementation with `Sm4Key`.

The 128-bit block + GM/T 0002-2012 SM4 round function plug in
unchanged into NIST SP 800-38A ¬ß6.2‚Äď6.5 mode constructions. The
math is identical to AES ‚ÄĒ only the cipher primitive changes.

### Emitter (xtask)

`xtask/src/sm4.rs`:

- New `UsedModes` struct (gcm / cbc / ctr / cfb / ofb) replaces
  the single `used_gcm: bool` flag. `write_header` emits a
  `use` import per active mode.
- `emit_kat` learns 4 new `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_*` arms (CBC / CTR /
  CFB / OFB) and dispatches to new `emit_cbc` / `emit_ctr` /
  `emit_cfb` / `emit_ofb` writers.
- The CBC arm enforces block-aligned input at emission time;
  unaligned rows route to `skipped_unknown` (none exist in the
  current `.data`, but the guard is defensive).
- The module doc-comment is rewritten to enumerate the now-supported
  modes and the still-skipped HCTR / XTS / GCM-decrypt families.

### Verification

- `modes::cbc` / `modes::ctr` / `modes::cfb` / `modes::ofb` lib
  tests: green; +1 new openHiTLS SDV SM4-CTR KAT pinned in
  `ctr.rs::tests` as a regression anchor.
- All `modes::` lib tests combined: **109/0**.
- `migrated_sm4.rs`: 9/0 ‚Üí **35/0** (+26):
  - 19 CBC rows (encrypt + decrypt directions; AES-128/192/256 doesn't apply ‚ÄĒ SM4 is fixed at 128-bit key)
  - 4 CTR rows
  - 2 CFB rows
  - 1 OFB row
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo sm4 --check`: drift
  gate clean.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: full crate green
  (no regression on existing PKCS#7 `sm4_cbc_encrypt` /
  `sm4_cbc_decrypt` / AEAD `sm4_gcm_encrypt` paths).
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/cbc.rs` ‚ÄĒ `sm4_cbc_encrypt_raw`
  / `sm4_cbc_decrypt_raw` wrappers.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/ctr.rs` ‚ÄĒ `sm4_ctr_crypt` +
  inline openHiTLS SDV KAT.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/cfb.rs` ‚ÄĒ `sm4_cfb_encrypt` /
  `sm4_cfb_decrypt`.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/ofb.rs` ‚ÄĒ `sm4_ofb_crypt`.
- `xtask/src/sm4.rs` ‚ÄĒ `UsedModes` struct + CBC/CTR/CFB/OFB
  routing + per-mode emitter functions + header imports.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm4.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ split the wide SM4 gap row: the
  closed portion (CBC/CTR/CFB/OFB, 26 rows) struck through;
  new narrower `SM4 HCTR / XTS / GCM-decrypt` gap row records
  the residual 11 rows; SM4 tally bumped.

### Remaining SM4 gap (11 rows)

- **HCTR** (3 rows) ‚ÄĒ no public SM4 entry point in
  `hitls_crypto::modes::hctr` (the existing impl is AES-only).
- **XTS** (4 rows) ‚ÄĒ same situation; XTS additionally needs a
  separate tweak key.
- **GCM-decrypt** (4 rows) ‚ÄĒ the C SDV `.data` cipher field
  carries only ciphertext without the 16-byte auth tag, so
  `sm4_gcm_decrypt` cannot authenticate. Migration would require
  the C SDV to publish the tag.

### Migration tally (post-I153)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3175** | **3760** | **240** | **39** | **6480** |

## Phase I154 ‚ÄĒ SM2 key exchange (closes SM2 Structural Gap) (2026-06-03)

### Summary

`Sm2KeyPair` had no key-exchange method, so the SM2 key-agreement
families in the C SDV (`EXCHANGE_FUNC_TC001` byte-exact KAT + the
related workflow / validity TCs, 17 rows total) routed to
`skipped_unsupported_alg`. I154 implements **GB/T 32918.3-2016 ¬ß6.1**
SM2 key agreement as `Sm2KeyPair::exchange_with_nonce` and wires it
through the migration emitter.

### Production additions

**`Sm2KeyPair::exchange_with_nonce(my_r, peer_pubkey, peer_r_point,
my_id, peer_id, is_initiator, key_len) -> Result<Vec<u8>, _>`** ‚ÄĒ
default-feature, additive, no ABI break.

Algorithm (matching GB/T 32918.3-2016 ¬ß6.1 numbering for party A;
party B differs only in the `is_initiator` flag's effect on the
`Z_A || Z_B` ordering):

1. Parse the ephemeral private nonce `r ‚ąą [1, n-1]`.
2. Compute `R_self = r * G`.
3. Parse peer's static public key `P_peer` and ephemeral point
   `R_peer = r_peer * G` (both uncompressed 65 bytes).
4. Compute the truncated coordinates `xŐĄ = 2^w + (x mod 2^w)` for both
   `R_self` and `R_peer`, with `w = ‚Ćąlog2(n)/2‚ĆČ - 1 = 127` for the
   SM2P256V1 256-bit order.
5. `t = (d_self + xŐĄ_self * r) mod n`.
6. `V = h * t * (P_peer + xŐĄ_peer * R_peer)`. SM2's cofactor `h = 1`,
   so this is `t * (P_peer + xŐĄ_peer * R_peer)`.
7. If `V` is the point at infinity, return `Sm2DecryptFail` (the
   spec's step-7 validity check).
8. `key = KDF(V.x || V.y || Z_A || Z_B, key_len)` where `Z_A` is the
   **initiator**'s identity hash and `Z_B` is the **responder**'s,
   regardless of which side is computing. The `is_initiator` flag
   places `compute_za(my_id, self.public_key, group)` vs
   `compute_za(peer_id, p_peer, group)` in the correct slot.

### Helper additions

- **`x_bar(x, w) -> Result<BigNum, _>`** ‚ÄĒ returns `2^w + (x mod 2^w)`
  via `set_bit(w)` to build `pow2w = 2^w` then `mod_reduce` for the
  truncation. Cleaner than bit-masking + bitwise-or, and uses the
  existing BigNum API.
- **`sm2_exchange_kdf(vx, vy, z_a, z_b, klen)`** ‚ÄĒ parallel to the
  existing `sm2_kdf(x, y, klen)` used by encrypt/decrypt; the
  exchange-specific variant prepends both Z hashes ahead of the SM3
  counter, matching the ¬ß6.1 KDF input
  `V.x || V.y || Z_A || Z_B`.

### Emitter (xtask)

`xtask/src/sm2.rs`:

- New `Kind::ExchangePos` variant; the older `Kind::Unsupported` is
  removed (no remaining SM2 family is unsupported after this).
- `classify` now routes `SM2_EXCHANGE_FUNC_TC001 ‚Üí ExchangePos`; the
  remaining EXCHANGE workflow / validity rows (`TC002/003/004` +
  `EXCHANGE_CHECK_TC001/002`) route to `ApiSurface`.
- New `emit_exchange(out, case, stats)` writer parses the row
  `prvKey : pubKey : r : R : shareKey : userId1 : userId2 : server :
  provider`, maps `server == 1 ‚Üí is_initiator = true` and
  `server == 0 ‚Üí is_initiator = false`, and emits a byte-exact KAT
  assertion against `shareKey`.
- Existing `skip_if_provider_dup` carries the row's `isProvider`
  duplicate elimination through unchanged.

### Verification

- `sm2` lib tests: 63/0 ‚Üí **69/0** (+6 ‚ÄĒ `test_sm2_exchange_gb_32918_5_vector1`
  + `test_sm2_exchange_roundtrip` + `test_sm2_exchange_public_only_fails`
  + 3 supporting setups).
- The Rust implementation hits the **GB/T 32918.5-2017 ¬ßA.1 vector 1**
  (`shareKey = 6c89347354de2484c60b4ab1fde4c6e5`) byte-exact on
  **first run** ‚ÄĒ no debugging required.
- `test_sm2_exchange_roundtrip` generates two random keypairs + two
  random ephemeral nonces and asserts both sides derive the same
  32-byte shared secret.
- `test_sm2_exchange_public_only_fails` asserts a public-only
  `Sm2KeyPair` (no `d`) returns `EccInvalidPrivateKey`.
- `migrated_sm2.rs`: 14/0 ‚Üí **16/0** (+2 byte-exact: row 1 with
  `server=1` + row 2 with `server=0`, both vectors deriving the same
  `shareKey`).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo sm2 --check`: drift gate
  clean.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: full crate green (no
  regression on existing SM2 sign/verify/encrypt/decrypt; the new
  `sm2_exchange_kdf` is a distinct function from `sm2_kdf`).
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean. (Initial
  `(n.bit_len() + 1) / 2` clippy-warned for `manual_div_ceil`; fixed
  to `n.bit_len().div_ceil(2)`.)

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm2/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ `exchange_with_nonce` public
  method + private `x_bar` helper + private `sm2_exchange_kdf` helper
  + 3 new lib tests (KAT, roundtrip, public-only-fails).
- `xtask/src/sm2.rs` ‚ÄĒ `Kind::ExchangePos` arm + `emit_exchange`
  writer + classify routing for EXCHANGE_FUNC_TC001.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm2.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ `SM2 key exchange` gap row struck
  through; SM2 tally bumped; Total bump.

### Note on `is_initiator` mapping

The C SDV `server` flag in `EXCHANGE_FUNC_TC001` corresponds to the
GB/T 32918.3 initiator-vs-responder role. `server == 1` means "this
side is party A (initiator)" ‚ÄĒ confirmed by byte-exact match against
the GB/T 32918.5-2017 ¬ßA.1 vector 1 expected output with
`is_initiator = true`. The `server == 0` row in the same TC swaps
the prvKey/pubKey pair (so that side has the other party's
long-term keys) and yields the same `shareKey` because both parties
in an SM2 key agreement derive the same secret.

### Migration tally (post-I154)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3177** | **3760** | **240** | **22** | **6480** |

---

## Phase I155 ‚ÄĒ CRL parser strictness (closes CRL parser leniency Structural Gap) (2026-06-03)

### Summary

The Rust X.509 CRL parser was lenient in three places that the
openHiTLS C reference parser was strict about: UTCTime /
GeneralizedTime validation, nextUpdate error propagation, and
signature-algorithm OID recognition. As a result, 9 negative
`CRL_PARSE_FILE_FUNC_TC003/006/007/008` rows (where the C SDV
expected the parse to fail) could not be migrated ‚ÄĒ the
`emit_crl_parse_res` writer routed them to
`skipped_unsupported_alg` because an `is_err()` assertion would
have been false.

I155 tightens the parser along all three dimensions and switches
the emitter to assert `is_err()` for negative rows.

### Production tightening

**1. UTCTime / GeneralizedTime strictness**
(`crates/hitls-utils/src/asn1/decoder.rs`)

Before:
* `parse_utc_time` stripped a trailing `'Z'` if present (else
  treated the entire string as the time), then required `len ‚Č• 12`
  and parsed the first 12 chars.
* `parse_generalized_time` did the same with `len ‚Č• 14`.

After (matching RFC 5280 ¬ß4.1.2.5):
* `parse_utc_time` requires **exactly** `YYMMDDHHMMSSZ` ‚ÄĒ 13 ASCII
  chars ending in `Z`. Rejects:
  - timezone offsets (`...260826073347+0800` from
    `change_time_z.crl`);
  - trailing junk (`2608260733ZZZ` from `change_time_no_ss.crl`);
  - any other length.
* `parse_generalized_time` requires **exactly**
  `YYYYMMDDHHMMSSZ` ‚ÄĒ 15 ASCII chars ending in `Z`. Rejects
  fractional seconds (`20551213093359.123Z` from
  `change_time_float_ss.crl`).

**2. CRL `nextUpdate` no longer lenient**
(`crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/crl.rs`)

`CertificateRevocationList::from_der` removed a `.ok()` on the
nextUpdate `read_time()` call that silently masked malformed time
tags. Now propagates the error. This rejects the `no_next_time.crl`
fixture (which encodes a UTCTime tag with length 0 in the
nextUpdate slot).

**3. Signature-algorithm OID gate**
(`crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/crl.rs`)

New `pub(crate) fn is_known_signature_algorithm(oid: &Oid) -> bool`
enumerates the same set already used by `verify_signature_with_oid`:
sha1/256/384/512-with-RSA, ECDSA-with-SHA256/384/512, Ed25519,
Ed448, SM2-with-SM3, RSASSA-PSS.

`from_der` now validates **both** the TBS-inner and outer signature
algorithm OIDs against this gate at parse time. This rejects:
* `change_invalid_cid_1.crl` (TBS-inner OID
  `1.2.840.113549.1.1.16` = sha512-256WithRSAEncryption, unknown to
  this crate);
* `change_invalid_cid_2.crl` (outer OID `1.2.840.113549.1.1.15` =
  sha512-224WithRSAEncryption, unknown).

### Emitter (xtask)

`xtask/src/x509.rs::emit_crl_parse_res`:

- Dropped the `res != HITLS_PKI_SUCCESS ‚Üí skipped_unsupported_alg`
  early-return.
- Positive rows emit `assert!(CertificateRevocationList::from_pem(pem)
  .is_ok())` (unchanged).
- Negative rows emit `assert!(... .is_err())` with a `_fail`
  function-name suffix.

### Test updates

- `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/crl.rs::test_parse_crl_no_next_update`
  ‚ÄĒ this test was asserting the leniency-buggy behaviour
  (`next_update.is_none()`). Renamed to
  `test_parse_crl_no_next_update_is_rejected` and updated to assert
  `is_err()`. The fixture is the same
  `demoCA_rsa2048_v2_no_next_time.crl` from the C SDV ‚ÄĒ same source,
  same fixture, now matching the C reference expectation.

### Verification

- Probe: 9 deferred negative fixtures, before I155 = 6/9 ACCEPTED +
  3/9 REJECTED; after I155 = **0/9 ACCEPTED + 9/9 REJECTED**.
- `hitls-pki` lib tests: 457/0 ‚Üí **458/0** (the renamed test was
  +1).
- `hitls-pki` `migrated_x509_parse`: 1064/0 ‚Üí **1073/0** (+9 = the
  exact 9 negative CRL rows that now emit).
- `hitls-tls` lib tests: 1114/0 unchanged.
- `hitls-utils` lib tests: 80/0 unchanged.
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo x509-parse --check`:
  drift gate clean.
- `cargo test --workspace --all-features`: green.
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-utils/src/asn1/decoder.rs` ‚ÄĒ
  `parse_utc_time` / `parse_generalized_time` strict.
- `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/crl.rs` ‚ÄĒ
  `is_known_signature_algorithm` helper + inner/outer sig-alg
  validation + nextUpdate `.ok()` removal + renamed
  `test_parse_crl_no_next_update_is_rejected`.
- `xtask/src/x509.rs` ‚ÄĒ `emit_crl_parse_res` positive/negative
  branching.
- `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_x509_parse.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ `CRL parser leniency` gap struck
  through; Total bump.

|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|

## Phase I156 ‚ÄĒ SM4 HCTR + XTS wrappers (partial close of SM4 mode gap) (2026-06-03)

### Summary

The T6-era SM4 KAT migration left 11 unsupported rows after I153
closed the CBC/CTR/CFB/OFB family: 2 HCTR + 6 XTS + 3 GCM-decrypt.
I156 closes 2 of the 11 by adding SM4 XTS / HCTR public wrappers
and routing the block-aligned XTS rows through them. The remaining
9 split across two well-understood categories: a
**reference-interop divergence** (the Rust XTS-ciphertext-stealing
path + Rust HCTR `uhash` are internally self-consistent but not yet
proven byte-compatible with the openHiTLS C reference) and a **C
SDV data limitation** (the GCM-decrypt rows lack the auth tag).

### Production additions

Four new default-feature public functions in `hitls_crypto::modes`,
all `#[cfg(feature = "sm4")]`:

- `sm4_hctr_encrypt(key, hash_key, tweak, plaintext)` /
  `sm4_hctr_decrypt(key, hash_key, tweak, ciphertext)` ‚ÄĒ
  HCTR with SM4 as the underlying block cipher.
- `sm4_xts_encrypt(key1, key2, tweak, plaintext)` /
  `sm4_xts_decrypt(key1, key2, tweak, ciphertext)` ‚ÄĒ
  XTS with SM4 (IEEE 1619 ¬ß5).

Implementation pattern (matching I153's SM4 mode work):

- **HCTR refactor.** `hctr.rs` extracts the algorithm body into
  private `hctr_encrypt_inner<C: BlockCipher>` /
  `hctr_decrypt_inner<C>`. The existing public `hctr_encrypt` /
  `hctr_decrypt` become 2-line `AesKey::new` + dispatch wrappers.
  The `apply_ctr` helper is also generic-ified over `BlockCipher`.
- **XTS refactor.** `xts.rs` extracts the algorithm body into
  `xts_encrypt_inner<C: BlockCipher>` / `xts_decrypt_inner<C>`. The
  existing public `xts_encrypt` / `xts_decrypt` become 2-line
  `AesKey::new` + dispatch wrappers.
- **SM4 wrappers** call `Sm4Key::new` and dispatch to the same
  generic inners.

Net source delta: pure refactor ‚ÄĒ algorithm bodies move but do not
change.

### Probe outcome ‚ÄĒ reference-interop divergence

Migrating all 6 XTS + 2 HCTR rows produced **4 failures** on first
run:

- `tc_line680_sm4_hctr_encrypt` ‚ÄĒ HCTR, 16-byte input
- `tc_line682_sm4_hctr_decrypt`
- `tc_line172_sm4_xts_encrypt` ‚ÄĒ XTS, 56-byte input (ciphertext stealing)
- `tc_line211_sm4_xts_decrypt`

The 2 block-aligned XTS rows (`tc_line193`, `tc_line232`) **pass
byte-exact**.

Diagnosis: the Rust XTS ciphertext-stealing path + Rust HCTR
`uhash` are internally self-consistent (encrypt/decrypt round-trip
cleanly under proptest; IEEE P1619 vector 1 passes for AES-XTS
aligned input) but were never validated byte-exactly against the
openHiTLS C `EAL_SM4` reference. Same diagnostic flavor as the
pre-I145 FrodoKEM gap (Rust `pack` / `unpack` bit-endianness
diverged from C ‚ÄĒ internally fine, not C-compatible).

### Emitter scoping

`xtask/src/sm4.rs::emit_kat`:

- `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_XTS` rows route through the new `emit_xts`
  writer **only** when `input.len() == 16` (the 2 block-aligned
  rows that match byte-exact). Other XTS rows route to
  `skipped_unsupported_alg` with a clear comment pointing at the
  ciphertext-stealing divergence.
- All `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_HCTR` rows route to
  `skipped_unsupported_alg` with the same divergence comment.
- New `UsedModes::xts` flag + `write_header` adds the
  `use hitls_crypto::modes::xts::{sm4_xts_decrypt, sm4_xts_encrypt};`
  import when needed.
- The earlier provisional `emit_hctr` writer (added then removed
  during this phase) is not present in the final diff.

### Verification

- `modes::` lib tests: **109/0** unchanged ‚ÄĒ the AES paths are
  preserved by the refactor; IEEE P1619 vector 1 (the only
  byte-exact XTS KAT in the lib) still passes.
- `migrated_sm4.rs`: 35/0 ‚Üí **37/0** (+2 byte-exact SM4-XTS).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo sm4 --check`: clean.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features --lib`: **1505/0**
  (3 `#[ignore]`d, unchanged).
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy
  --workspace --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/hctr.rs` ‚ÄĒ generic inner helpers
  + `sm4_hctr_encrypt` / `sm4_hctr_decrypt` wrappers + `apply_ctr`
  generic.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/xts.rs` ‚ÄĒ generic inner helpers
  + `sm4_xts_encrypt` / `sm4_xts_decrypt` wrappers.
- `xtask/src/sm4.rs` ‚ÄĒ `UsedModes::xts` + `emit_xts` writer +
  routing + module doc.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm4.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated.
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ narrowed `SM4 HCTR / XTS / GCM-decrypt`
  row (11 ‚Üí 9) with explicit reference-interop-divergence +
  data-limitation breakdown.

### Note on kept-but-unmigrated APIs

The `sm4_hctr_*` functions + the long-input `sm4_xts_*` cases are
still legitimate public APIs. They execute the spec'd HCTR / XTS
algorithms and round-trip correctly; they simply don't match the
openHiTLS C reference's byte-for-byte output. Pinning the
divergence is a follow-up (instrument `xts_*_inner` ciphertext-
stealing + `hctr::uhash` against the C SDV ‚ÄĒ same playbook as
I145 FrodoKEM `pack`/`unpack` debugging).

### Migration tally (post-I156)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3188** | **3760** | **240** | **11** | **6480** |

## Phase I157 ‚ÄĒ SM4-XTS GM-convention fix + HCTR data-unverified diagnostic (closes SM4-XTS Structural Gap) (2026-06-03)

### Summary

I156 left two SM4 reference-interop divergences open: SM4-XTS
ciphertext-stealing rows + all SM4-HCTR rows failed byte-exact
against the openHiTLS C SDV. I157 closes the SM4-XTS half and
documents the SM4-HCTR half as a **C SDV data limitation**, not a
Rust bug.

### Finding 1 ‚ÄĒ SM4-XTS uses the GM-convention őĪ-multiplication

The Rust XTS implementation hard-coded the **IEEE 1619 / AES**
őĪ-multiplication for the tweak update ‚ÄĒ left-shift + reduction byte
`0x87` (MSB-out). The openHiTLS C XTS code branches on the underlying
algorithm and uses a different convention for SM4 (`modes_xts.c`
lines 174-178):

- AES ‚Üí `GF128Mul` (lines 95-108): left-shift + `0x87` reduction
  (IEEE 1619, MSB-out).
- SM4 ‚Üí `GF128Mul_GM` (lines 126-139): **right-shift + `0xE1`
  reduction** (GM/T 0002-2012, LSB-out ‚ÄĒ the GHASH/GM polynomial
  representation).

Block-aligned single-block rows never trigger the tweak multiply, so
the bug was masked for those ‚ÄĒ that is exactly why I156's 2 migrated
rows (line 174, line 213 ‚ÄĒ both 16-byte VECTOR2) coincidentally
passed byte-exact while the 56-byte VECTOR1 rows (line 172 / line 211
ciphertext-stealing path) failed.

### Fix

`crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/xts.rs`:

- New private `gf_mul_alpha_gm` (right-shift + `0xE1` reduction)
  alongside the existing `gf_mul_alpha` (AES IEEE 1619). Both operate
  on `&mut [u8; AES_BLOCK_SIZE]`.
- `xts_encrypt_inner<C: BlockCipher>` and `xts_decrypt_inner<C>`
  gain a `gf_mul: fn(&mut [u8; AES_BLOCK_SIZE])` parameter ‚ÄĒ the
  őĪ-multiplication is now caller-selected, not hard-coded.
- AES `xts_encrypt` / `xts_decrypt` continue to pass `gf_mul_alpha`
  (unchanged behaviour for AES; IEEE P1619 vector 1 still matches).
- SM4 `sm4_xts_encrypt` / `sm4_xts_decrypt` now pass
  `gf_mul_alpha_gm` instead, matching the C `GF128Mul_GM` path.

### Finding 2 ‚ÄĒ SM4-HCTR C SDV cipher field is unverified

While probing the HCTR divergence I read `crypto/modes/src/modes_hctr.c`
in the C reference and found that `MODES_HCTR_Update` (lines 291-317)
is a **no-op buffer stage**: it copies the input into the context's
internal buffer and writes **nothing** to `out`. The SM4-HCTR SDV
test in turn calls `MODES_HCTR_Update` and then asserts
`memcmp(out, cipherText, len) == 0` with **`len == 0`** (because the
API hasn't produced output at the Update step), which is a trivially
true assertion. The `cipherText` field in the `.data` rows is
therefore never actually compared to anything ‚ÄĒ the published values
are **unverified**.

The Rust `hctr_encrypt` / `hctr_decrypt` are internally self-consistent
(`gf128_mul`, `uhash` byte-exact against side-by-side C reads;
proptest round-trip clean), so the byte divergence vs the C `.data`
is *not* a Rust bug ‚ÄĒ there is simply no trustworthy C KAT to migrate
against. Migration is therefore deferred until an independent
third-party HCTR KAT (e.g. a GM/T 0002 erratum or `MODES_HCTR_Final`
output-augmented vectors) appears.

### Emitter widening

`xtask/src/sm4.rs::emit_kat`:

- `CRYPT_CIPHER_SM4_XTS` arm: the `input.len() == 16` restriction is
  dropped in favour of `input.len() >= 16`. All rows of legal XTS
  size now route through `emit_xts`. The previous "ciphertext-stealing
  diverges" comment is replaced with an I157-anchored one explaining
  the GM-convention fix.
- Module-level doc-comment updated to reflect the full SM4-XTS
  coverage and the HCTR data-unverified finding.

### Verification

- `modes::xts` lib tests: **10/0** unchanged (the AES IEEE 1619
  vector + the AES ciphertext-stealing tests still pass ‚ÄĒ the
  caller-selected `gf_mul` parameter is transparent for AES).
- `migrated_sm4.rs`: **37/0 ‚Üí 39/0** (+2 byte-exact SM4-XTS
  ciphertext-stealing rows: `tc_line172_sm4_xts_encrypt`,
  `tc_line211_sm4_xts_decrypt`).
- xtask `cargo xtask migrate-c-tests --algo sm4 --check`: clean ‚ÄĒ
  emitted 37 ‚Üí 39, unsupported 9 ‚Üí 7.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: **all suites green,
  0 regressions**.
- `cargo fmt --all` + `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p
  hitls-crypto -p xtask --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/modes/xts.rs` ‚ÄĒ `gf_mul_alpha_gm` added;
  `*_inner` signatures gain `gf_mul` parameter; SM4 wrappers pass
  GM-convention.
- `xtask/src/sm4.rs` ‚ÄĒ XTS emitter widened to `len >= 16`; module
  doc updated.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm4.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated (37 ‚Üí 39).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ SM4-XTS row struck through (closed in
  I157); remaining gap narrowed to SM4-HCTR (unverified C SDV data)
  + SM4-GCM-decrypt (C SDV omits tag). Count 9 ‚Üí 7.

### Migration tally (post-I157)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3190** | **3760** | **240** | **9** | **6480** |

## Phase I158 ‚ÄĒ SM9 key exchange (GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4 / GM/T 0044.3-2016) (closes T158-recorded SM9 Structural Gap)

### Summary

Adds the openHiTLS C SM9 key-exchange algorithm (`crypto/sm9/src/sm9.c::SM9_Alg_KeyEx_*` + `sm9_exch.c` + the `CRYPT_SM9_ComputeShareKey` EAL wrapper) as a new pure-Rust sub-module on the encryption-system SM9 master key, plus a one-shot helper that mirrors the openHiTLS C EAL `ComputeShareKey` behaviour used by the T158-recorded SDV row.

GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4 is a pairing-based two-party identity-based key agreement built on the SM9 encryption master key. Both parties receive their `dX` from the same KGC; the master public `Ppub_enc` is on G1 and user keys are on G2 (mirroring SM9 encryption, not signature). The protocol has three phases:

- **Init**   ‚ÄĒ each party draws a private nonce `rX`, computes `Q_peer = H1(ID_peer ‚ÄĖ HID_enc) ¬∑ P1 + Ppub_enc` (an identity-bound point on G1), and publishes `RX = [rX] ¬∑ Q_peer` on G1.
- **Confirm** ‚ÄĒ each party computes the three pairing values `g1, g2, g3 ‚ąą F_{p^12}` from its own nonce + the peer's `R`, derives `SK = KDF(g1 ‚ÄĖ SM3(g2 ‚ÄĖ g3 ‚ÄĖ IDA ‚ÄĖ IDB ‚ÄĖ RA ‚ÄĖ RB), klen)`, and a side-tag `SA = SM3(0x82 ‚ÄĖ g1 ‚ÄĖ inner_hash)` for the initiator / `SB = SM3(0x83 ‚ÄĖ ‚Ä¶)` for the responder.
- **Verify** ‚ÄĒ each party recomputes the peer's side-tag from its own view of `(g1, inner_hash)` and constant-time-compares it to the received tag.

The pairing identity `e(Ppub_enc, P2) = e(QX, dX) = e(P1, P2)^ks` makes A's and B's `(g1, g2, g3)` tuples identical, so `SK_A == SK_B` and both side-tags cross-verify.

### Implementation

- **New `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm9/key_exchange.rs`** (added to `sm9/mod.rs`'s module list).
  - Private 3-phase primitives operating on raw bytes:
    - `key_exchange_init_a(peer_id_b, r_a_bytes_32, master_pub_enc) -> (BigNum, [u8; 64])`
    - `key_exchange_init_b(peer_id_a, r_b_bytes_32, master_pub_enc) -> (BigNum, [u8; 64])`
    - `key_exchange_confirm_a(id_a, id_b, r_a, RA, RB, dA, mpk, klen) -> (Vec<u8>, [u8; 32])`
    - `key_exchange_confirm_b(id_a, id_b, r_b, RA, RB, dB, mpk, klen) -> (Vec<u8>, [u8; 32])`
    - `verify_side_tag(g1_bytes, inner_hash, expected_tag, peer_s) -> bool` (constant-time, via `subtle::ConstantTimeEq`)
  - Shared `confirm_finalise(g1, g2, g3, id_a, id_b, RA, RB, klen, self_tag)` extracts the inner hash + KDF + side-tag wrap-up so the two `confirm_*` roles stay byte-identical and can't drift.
  - One-shot `compute_share_key(self_user_id, self_user_key, peer_user_id, peer_user_key, master_pub_enc, klen) -> Result<Vec<u8>>`:
    - Decides initiator by lex-comparing IDs (`self < peer ‚áí self is initiator`), rejects self-exchange and `klen == 0`.
    - Derives deterministic nonces `rA = SM3(id_init ‚ÄĖ id_resp ‚ÄĖ 'A')` and `rB = SM3(id_init ‚ÄĖ id_resp ‚ÄĖ 'B')` (both reduced mod `n` inside `init_compute_r`).
    - Runs `init_a` + `init_b`, then either `confirm_a` (self == initiator) or `confirm_b` (self == responder), returning the local `SK`.
    - This shape exactly mirrors the C EAL `CRYPT_SM9_ComputeShareKey(self_ctx, peer_ctx, out, &out_len)` so the T158 SDV row's `SK_A == SK_B` assertion can be replayed in Rust by calling `compute_share_key` from each side and comparing.
  - Local `fp12_to_bytes` helper (12 √ó 32-byte BE Fp coordinates = 384 bytes) ‚ÄĒ kept module-local to avoid promoting `alg.rs::fp12_to_bytes` to crate-visible API.
- **New `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key(&self, self_user_key, peer_user_key, klen)` public method** in `sm9/mod.rs`:
  - Enforces `key_type == Encrypt` for both the master and the two user keys (returns `InvalidArg("")` otherwise; SM9 key exchange is unrelated to the signing system).
  - Delegates to `key_exchange::compute_share_key` with the user keys' `user_id` / `private_key` byte vectors + `self.master_public`.
  - This is the entry point the follow-up T-phase will route the 2 SDV `KEYEX_API_TC*` rows through. The lower-level `key_exchange_init_*` / `key_exchange_confirm_*` primitives are not currently re-exported because the SDV doesn't need them ‚ÄĒ a real two-party network agreement would re-expose them in a follow-up.

### Verification

- 6 new lib tests in `key_exchange::tests`:
  - `test_sm9_key_exchange_roundtrip_default_klen` ‚ÄĒ `klen = 32`, asserts `SK_A == SK_B` and length.
  - `test_sm9_key_exchange_long_klen_crosses_sm3_boundary` ‚ÄĒ `klen = 63`, exercises `rcnt=1` full SM3 block + `rbit=31` tail (mirrors the SDV's `SK_Long` 62-byte case shape).
  - `test_sm9_key_exchange_short_klen` ‚ÄĒ `klen = 15`, tail-only path (`SK_Short` case).
  - `test_sm9_key_exchange_self_rejected` ‚ÄĒ same ID on both sides must return `Err`.
  - `test_sm9_key_exchange_zero_klen_rejected` ‚ÄĒ `klen = 0` must return `Err`.
  - `test_sm9_key_exchange_three_phase_with_side_tags` ‚ÄĒ drives `key_exchange_init_a/b` + `key_exchange_confirm_a/b` directly with fixed nonces, asserts `SK_A == SK_B`, re-derives `(g1_bytes, inner_hash)` from A's perspective to feed `verify_side_tag` against both `S_A` and `S_B`, and that a tampered tag is rejected.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --features sm9 key_exchange`: **6/0**.
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: **4483/0** (was 4477; +6 new key-exchange tests; no regression).
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`: clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto --all-features --all-targets`: clean (initial pass needed `doc_overindented_list_items` indent fix in the module doc bullet list + `needless_question_mark` removed from `derive_nonce`'s tail; second pass also touched a single `doc_lazy_continuation` warning in a test docstring).

### Files modified

- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm9/key_exchange.rs` ‚ÄĒ new file (~400 lines including tests).
- `crates/hitls-crypto/src/sm9/mod.rs` ‚ÄĒ added the `key_exchange` sub-module to the module list + new public `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key` method.

### Migration tally (post-I158)

| Category | Emitted | API-surface | Unknown | Unsupported | Total |
|----------|--------:|------------:|--------:|------------:|------:|
| All algos (34 rows in tally) | **3190** | **3760** | **240** | **9** | **6480** |

(Production change only ‚ÄĒ no migration tally delta yet. The 2 SDV `KEYEX_API_TC*` rows that this work unblocks will land via a follow-up T-phase that emits them through `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key`.)

## Phase T159 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust SM9 KEYEX_API_TC001 migration through `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key` (closes T158-recorded SM9 key-exchange Structural Gap end-to-end)

### Summary

Follow-up to I158: now that `Sm9MasterKey::compute_share_key` is the canonical Rust entry point for GB/T 38635 ¬ß4.4 key agreement, route the C SDV `KEYEX_API_TC001` rows through it via the xtask emitter. This closes the SM9 key-exchange Structural Gap that was opened during T158 (when the 2 `KEYEX_API_TC*` rows were noted as having no Rust counterpart).

### Implementation

- **`xtask/src/sm9.rs`**:
  - New `emit_keyex_api(body, stats, case)` writer. Unpacks the 3-arg row `(masterKey: 32B hex, userIdA: hex, userIdB: hex)`, then emits a test function that:
    - Calls `Sm9MasterKey::from_master_secret(Sm9KeyType::Encrypt, master_key)` (the T158-added `kat-nonce`-only constructor ‚ÄĒ same hook the rest of the SM9 SDV migrations already use).
    - Derives both encryption user keys via `master.extract_user_key(...)`.
    - Loops over the SDV-mandated klen variants `[32, 63, 15]` ‚ÄĒ default (one full SM3 block), `SK_Long` (crosses an SM3 block boundary: `rcnt=1` + `rbit=31`), and `SK_Short` (tail-only path, `rcnt=0` + `rbit=15`) ‚ÄĒ and asserts `sk_a == sk_b` byte-exact for each, with both `(self_user_key, peer_user_key)` orderings exercised so the lex-comparison branch of `compute_share_key` is hit from both sides.
  - Dispatch arm `name.contains("KEYEX_API_TC001")` added to `emit_sm9_kat`.
  - The catch-all `else` branch now explicitly documents that `KEYEX_API_TC002` stays API-surface: it drills NULL-pointer rejection on the C EAL `ComputeShareKey` wrapper, which Rust's borrow checker forbids at compile time ‚ÄĒ there's no runtime check to migrate.
  - Module-level doc-comment extended with a `KEYEX_API_TC001` bullet pointing at the T159 emitter and the I158 API.
- **`crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm9.rs`** ‚ÄĒ regenerated via `cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo sm9`. New `#[test] fn tc_line4_sm9_keyex_roundtrip()` appended.

### Verification

- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --features sm9,kat-nonce --test migrated_sm9`: **8/0** (was 7/0; +1 = the new KEYEX roundtrip).
- `cargo test -p hitls-crypto --all-features`: **4484/0** (was 4483/0; +1 new; no regression).
- `cargo run -p xtask -- migrate-c-tests --algo sm9 --check`: clean ‚ÄĒ emitted 7 ‚Üí 8, API-surface 31 ‚Üí 30, unsupported 0 ‚Üí 0; generated file up-to-date.
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`: clean.
- `RUSTFLAGS="-D warnings" cargo clippy -p hitls-crypto -p xtask --all-features --all-targets`: clean.

### Files modified

- `xtask/src/sm9.rs` ‚ÄĒ new `emit_keyex_api` writer + dispatch arm + doc-comment update.
- `crates/hitls-crypto/tests/migrated_sm9.rs` ‚ÄĒ regenerated (7 ‚Üí 8 tests).
- `docs/c-test-na-list.md` ‚ÄĒ SM9 row updated 7/31/0/0/38 ‚Üí 8/30/0/0/38; Total updated 3188/3760/240/11/6480 ‚Üí 3189/3759/240/11/6480; the SM9 key-exchange Structural Gap row at the bottom struck through with "Resolved in I158 + T159" pointer; the SM9 section's API-surface count breakdown updated to reflect TC001 ‚Üí emitted / TC002 ‚Üí API-surface.

## Phase I159 ‚ÄĒ RSA PSS SHA-224 śĒĮśĆĀÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ RSA-PSS SHA-224 ÁľļŚ§ĪŚŹėšĹďÁöĄ Structural GapÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

RSA-PSS ÁöĄ Rust ŚģěÁéįś≠§ŚČćšĽÖśĒĮśĆĀ SHA-256 / SHA-384 / SHA-512 šłČś°£ŚďąŚłĆÔľõ`RsaHashAlg` śěöšłĺťáĆś≤°śúČ `Sha224` ŚŹėšĹďÔľĆŚĮľŤáī 4 śĚ° C SDV Ť°ĆÔľą2 śĚ° `RSA_VERIFY_PSS_FUNC_TC003` + 2 śĚ° `RSA_SIGN_PSS_FUNC_TC003`ÔľČŚú®ŤŅĀÁßĽšĺßŤźĹŚąį unsupported„ÄāI159 śääÁľļŚ§ĪÁöĄ SHA-224 šłÄśĚ°ÁļŅŤ°•ťĹźÔľöśěöšłĺŤŅĹŚä† `Sha224` ŚŹėšĹďÔľĆśČÄśúČ 4 Ś§ĄÁ©∑ŚįĹ matchÔľąmgf1 h_len Ť°® + mgf1 śĮŹŤĹģŚďąŚłĆśīĺŚŹĎ + pss h_len Ť°® + pss hash_with ŚďąŚłĆśīĺŚŹĎ + oaep l_hash śīĺŚŹĎÔľČŤ°•ŚÖ®ŚźéÔľĆŚĮĻŚļĒÁöĄŚÖ¨ŚľÄ API `RsaPrivateKey::sign_pss(digest, alg)` / `RsaPublicKey::verify_pss(digest, sig, alg)` Ś§©ÁĄ∂śé•ŚŹó SHA-224„Äā

### ŚģěÁéį

- **`crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/mod.rs`**Ôľö
  - `pub enum RsaHashAlg` ŤŅĹŚä† `Sha224` variantÔľĆÁīßŤ∑üŚú® `Sha1` šĻčŚźéÔľąśĆČśĎėŤ¶ĀťēŅŚļ¶ťÄíŚĘěÁöĄśóĘśúČť°ļŚļŹÔľČ„Äā
  - `mgf1_with_hash`ÔľąMGF1 - RFC 8017 ¬ßB.2ÔľČÔľö
    - `h_len` Ť°®ŤŅĹŚä† `RsaHashAlg::Sha224 => 28`„Äā
    - śĮŹŤĹģŚďąŚłĆśīĺŚŹĎŤŅĹŚä† `Sha224 => { let mut hasher = Sha224::new(); hasher.update(seed)?; hasher.update(&c)?; t.extend_from_slice(&hasher.finish()?); }`ÔľĆŚĹĘÁä∂Ť∑ü `Sha256/384/512` ŚģĆŚÖ®ŚĮĻťĹź„Äā
    - import ŚąóŤ°®ŤŅĹŚä† `Sha224`„Äā
- **`crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/pss.rs`**Ôľö
  - `pub(crate) const fn h_len` Ť°®ŤŅĹŚä† `RsaHashAlg::Sha224 => 28`„Äā
  - `fn hash_with` ŤŅĹŚä† `Sha224` ŚąÜśĒĮÔľĆŚ§ćÁĒ® `crate::sha2::Sha224`„Äā
  - ś®°ŚĚóť°∂ÁęĮÁöĄÁģóś≥ēśĒĮśĆĀŤ°®ś≥®ťáäŚźĆś≠•śõīśĖįÔľą"SHA-256/384/512" ‚Üí "SHA-224/256/384/512"ÔľĆŚĻ∂ŚÜôśėé I159 ŚÖ≥ťó≠ÁöĄ 4 Ť°ĆÔľČ„Äā
- **`crates/hitls-crypto/src/rsa/oaep.rs`**Ôľö
  - `fn l_hash` ŤŅĹŚä† `Sha224` ŚąÜśĒĮ„ÄāŤŅôśĚ° PSS ÁĒ®šłćŚąįÔľĆšĹÜ OAEP šłé PSS ŚÖĪÁĒ®šłÄšł™ `RsaHashAlg`ÔľõšłćŤ°•ŤŅôšł™ŚąÜśĒĮšľöŤģ©šĽ•ŚźéšĽĽšĹē"OAEP-SHA-224"ŤįÉÁĒ®Ť∑ĮŚĺĄÁľĖŤĮĎŚ§ĪŤī• ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ÁéįŚú®šłÄŚĻ∂Ť°•ťĹźťĀŅŚÖćšĽäŚźéÁēôŚĚĎ„Äā
- **śĖįŚĘě lib śĶčŤĮē**Ôľą`rsa/mod.rs::tests::test_rsa_pss_sign_verify_sha224`ÔľČÔľö
  - 2048-bit RSA ŚĮÜťí•ÔľąŚźĆśóĘśúČ PSS ŚÖ®ŚďąŚłĆśĶčŤĮēÁöĄŚįļŚĮłÔľõemLen ‚Č• hLen + sLen + 2 = 28 + 28 + 2 = 58 Ś≠óŤäāÔľĆ2048-bit ś®°śēį 256 Ś≠óŤäāŤŅúŤ∂Ö ‚Üí šĽĽšĹē salt ťēŅŚļ¶ťÉĹťÄöŚĺóŤŅá emLen Áļ¶śĚüÔľČ„Äā
  - Ť∑ĮŚĺĄÔľöś∂ąśĀĮ ‚Üí `Sha224::digest` ‚Üí 28-byte śĎėŤ¶Ā ‚Üí `sign_pss(d224, Sha224)` ‚Üí `verify_pss(d224, sig, Sha224) == Ok(true)`„Äā
  - ŚŹćšĺč 1Ôľö`verify_pss(d256, sig, Sha224)` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ SHA-256 śĎėŤ¶ĀÔľą32BÔľČŚú® SHA-224 ś®°ŚľŹšłčŤĘę EMSA-PSS ŚČćÁĹģťēŅŚļ¶ś£Äśü•śčíśéČšłļ `Err(_)`ÔľąšłćśėĮ `Ok(false)`ÔľĆŚõ†šłļśėĮÁ≠ĺŚźćś†ľŚľŹŚŹāśēįś£Äśü•šłćśėĮť™ĆŤĮĀŤĮ≠šĻČÁĽďśěúÔľČ„Äā
  - ŚŹćšĺč 2ÔľöÁĮ°śĒĻ `d224[0] ^= 0x01` Śźé `verify_pss ‚Üí Ok(false)`ÔľąÁ≠ĺŚźćÁĽďśěĄ OK šĹÜŚÜÖŚģĻšłćŚĆĻťÖćÔľĆŤĶįť™ĆŤĮĀŤĮ≠šĻČŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÔľĆšłćŤĶįÁ≠ĺŚźćś†ľŚľŹśčíÁĽĚŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÔľČ„Äā

### śé•ŚŹ£ŚÖľŚģĻśÄßŤĮīśėé

`pub enum RsaHashAlg` ś≤°śúČ `#[non_exhaustive]` ś†áś≥®ÔľĆśĖįŚĘě variant šł•ś†ľśĚ•ŤĮīŚĮĻ *ŚÖ¨ŚľÄšłčśłł* śėĮ minor breakingÔľąšĽĽšĹē `match alg { Sha1 | Sha256 | Sha384 | Sha512 => ... }` ŤÄĆśó† `_` wildcard ÁöĄšłčśłłšľöÁľĖŤĮĎŚ§ĪŤī•ÔľČ„ÄāšĽďŚÜÖŤá™ś£ÄÔľöworkspace śČÄśúČ `match RsaHashAlg` Ś∑≤Śú®śú¨ťė∂śģĶŚźĆś≠•Ť°• `Sha224`ÔľõŚ§ĖťÉ®šłčśłłÁõģŚČćś≤°śúČÔľĆśČÄšĽ•ŚģěťôÖśó†ŚĹĪŚďć„ÄāŚźéÁĽ≠ŚŹĮŤÄÉŤôĎŚú® RsaHashAlg šłäŚä† `#[non_exhaustive]` śėéÁ°ģ"śú™śĚ•ŤŅėšľöŚä†ŚŹėšĹď"Áļ¶ŚģöÔľĆśääŤŅôÁßćśĖįŚĘěŚŹėšĹďśįłŤŅúŚĹíŚąį minor ŤÄĆťĚě break„Äā

## Phase T160 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust RSA PSS-SHA-224 KAT ŤŅĀÁßĽÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ RSA-PSS-SHA-224 Structural Gap ÁęĮŚąįÁęĮÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

śČŅśé• I159 śää `RsaHashAlg::Sha224` ťÄöŚąį `verify_pss` / `sign_pss_with_salt` šĻčŚźéÔľĆśää xtask emitter ÁöĄ SHA-224 śčíÁĽĚŚąÜśĒĮśčÜśéČÔľĆŤģ© 4 Ť°Ć C SDV `RSA_{VERIFY,SIGN}_PSS_FUNC_TC001` ÁöĄ SHA-224 Ť°ĆšĽé `unsupported` ŤĶįŚąį `emitted`ÔľĆśĆČŚ∑≤ÁĽŹśąźÁÜüÁöĄ SHA-256/384/512 byte-exact KAT śĶĀÁ®čŤźĹŚúį„ÄāśēīŚ•ó RSA Áģóś≥ēŤŅĀÁßĽÁĽďśěĄśÄßťó≠ÁéĮ ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ śČÄśúČ deterministic Śģ∂śóŹÔľąPKCS#1 v1.5 + OAEP + PSSÔľČŤ∑® SHA-1/224/256/384/512 ŚÖ®ťÉ®Ť¶ÜÁõĖ„Äā

### ŚģěÁéį

- **`xtask/src/rsa.rs::md_to_pss_alg`**ÔľöŚú®Ś∑≤śúČÁöĄ SHA-256/384/512 ŚĆĻťÖćŚČćťĚĘŤŅĹŚä† `"CRYPT_MD_SHA224" => Some("Sha224")`„ÄāŤŅôśėĮśú¨ťė∂śģĶŚĒĮšłÄÁöĄ"šŅ°ŚŹ∑"śĒĻŚä® ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚáĹśēįŤŅĒŚõěšĽé `None` śĒĻśąź `Some("Sha224")` ŚźéÔľĆŤįÉÁĒ®ŚģÉÁöĄ `emit_verify_pss` / `emit_sign_pss` šł§šł™ writer śóĘśúČÁöĄ `let Some(alg) = ... else { stats.skipped_unsupported_alg += 1; return; };` ŚģąŚćęŚ§©ÁĄ∂ťÄŹšľ†śĖįŚÄľÔľĆśó†ťúÄŤŅõšłÄś≠•śĒĻŚÜô„Äā
- **ŚáĹśēįť°∂ťÉ® doc-comment**ÔľöšĽé"SHA-256/384/512 only ‚ÄĒ MGF1 has no SHA-1 and there is no SHA-224 variant"śĒĻšłļ"SHA-224/256/384/512 ‚ÄĒ I159 added the SHA-224 variant; MGF1 has no SHA-1 so SHA-1 PSS rows still fall to `unsupported`"„ÄāśääŚéÜŚŹ≤ŚŹôŤŅį"ÁéįŚģěťáĆšłćśĒĮśĆĀ SHA-224"śćĘśąź"I159 šĻčŚźéśĒĮśĆĀ"ÁöĄŚźĆśó∂ÔľĆśääšĽćÁĄ∂ unsupported ÁöĄÁúüś≠£ŚéüŚõ†ÔľąSHA-1 PSSÔľČŚÜôśėéÁôĹ„Äā
- **`emit_sign_pss` doc-comment**Ôľö"PSS in the Rust API supports SHA-256/384/512 only, so SHA-1 / SHA-224 rows are `unsupported`" ‚Üí "PSS in the Rust API supports SHA-224/256/384/512 (I159 added SHA-224); SHA-1 PSS rows still fall to `unsupported` because MGF1 SHA-1 is not wired"„Äā

## Phase I160 ‚ÄĒ McEliece sk Ś≠óŤäāŚłÉŚĪÄŚĮĻťĹźŚąį C ŚŹāŤÄÉÔľąŤß£ŚÜ≥ reference-interop ŚąÜś≠ßÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

C SDV `MCELIECE_ENCAPS_DECAPS_FUNC_TC001` ÁöĄ 1 Ť°ĆŚŹāŤÄÉŚźĎťáŹŚú® Rust šłÄÁõīŤ∑Ď `decode` śä•ťĒô„Äāna-list ŚĀáŤģĺśėĮ Benes śéßŚą∂šĹćÁľĖÁ†ĀÁöĄŚ∑ģŚľā ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚģěťôÖť™ĆŤĮĀŚźé**ś†ĻŚõ†śą™ÁĄ∂šłćŚźĆ**ÔľöśėĮ Rust `serialize_private_key` / `deserialize_private_key` ÁöĄ sk Ś≠óŤäāśĶĀŚíĆ C ŚŹāŤÄÉšł•ťáćšłćšłÄŤáīÔľĆRust‚ÜĒRust self round-trip Śõ†šłļŚĮĻÁßįŚÜôŤĮĽŚĮĻÁßįťĒôŤÄĆšłÄÁõīÁĽŅÔľĆšļéśėĮ bug šłÄÁõīś≤°śöīťú≤„ÄāI160 śääšł§šł™ codec ŚĮĻťĹźŚąį C ŚŹāŤÄÉÔľĆšĹŅ Rust decaps Śú® C ŚŹāŤÄÉ KAT šłä byte-exact ťÄöŤŅá„Äā

### ŤĮäśĖ≠Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ

ŚĮĻśĮĒ C `mceliece.c::McelieceExportPrvKey`ÔľąŚÜô skÔľČšłé Rust `mod.rs::serialize_private_key`ÔľĆťÄźŤäāś†łŚĮĻÔľö

| śģĶ | C ŚŹāŤÄÉ | Rust pre-I160 | Ś∑ģŚľā |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. `delta` | 32 B | 32 B | ‚úÖ |
| 2. `c` (pivot mask) | 8 B LE | 8 B LE | ‚úÖ |
| 3. `g` Á≥Ľśēį | **t** u16-LEÔľąŤß£śěźśó∂ `g[t] = 1` ťöźŚľŹŤ°• leadingÔľČ | **t+1** u16-LEÔľąŚźę leadingÔľČ | ‚ĚĆ Ś§öŚÜô 1 ť°Ļ |
| 4. `alpha`Ôľąfield orderingÔľČ | **šłćŚ≠ė**ÔľąŤß£Á†Āśó∂šĽé controlbits ÁĽŹ Benes ŤŅėŚéüÔľČ | **ťĘĚŚ§Ė 2¬∑Q = 16384 B** | ‚ĚĆ Ś§öŚ°ěśēīśģĶ |
| 5. section ť°ļŚļŹ | `controlbits` ‚Üí `s` | `s` ‚Üí `controlbits` | ‚ĚĆ ť°ļŚļŹŚŹć |

Ś≠óŤäāťáŹŚĮĻŤī¶ÔľąMcEliece6688128ÔľĆm=13ÔľĆt=128ÔľĆn_bytes=836ÔľČÔľö

- C ŚŹāŤÄÉ skÔľö32 + 8 + 2¬∑128 + 12800 + 836 = **13932 B**Ôľąšłé `privateKeyBytes` ŚÖÉśēįśćģšł•ś†ľÁõłÁ≠ČÔľČ
- Rust pre-I160 skÔľö32 + 8 + 2¬∑129 + 16384 + 836 + 12800 = **30318 B**ÔľąśĮĒś†áÁöĄ 13932 Ś§ö 16386 BÔľČ

Goppa Ś§öť°ĻŚľŹŚ§©ÁĄ∂ monicÔľąleading šłÄŚģöśėĮ 1ÔľČÔľĆśČÄšĽ• C šłćšłä wire šĻüśó†šŅ°śĀĮśćüŚ§ĪÔľõalpha ÁĒĪ controlbits + Benes ŤŅėŚéüśČÄšĽ•šĻüšłćťúÄŤ¶ĀŚ≠ė„ÄāRust ťĒôŚłÉŚĪÄšļßÁČ©šłä wire śó∂śääŤŅôšł§śģĶťĘĚŚ§ĖÁľĖšļÜŤŅõŚéĽ„Äā

### šŅģŚ§ć

`crates/hitls-crypto/src/mceliece/mod.rs`Ôľö

```rust
fn serialize_private_key(kp, p) {
    // delta(32) || c(8) || g[0..t] (2t LE) || controlbits || s(n_bytes)
    Vec::with_capacity(p.private_key_bytes)
      .extend(delta)
      .extend(c.to_le_bytes())
      .extend(for i in 0..p.t { kp.sk_g.coeffs[i].to_le_bytes() })  // šłćŚÜô g[t]
      .extend(kp.sk_controlbits)                                     // ť°ļŚļŹŚČćÁßĽ
      .extend(kp.sk_s)                                                // ť°ļŚļŹŚźéÁßĽ
}

fn deserialize_private_key(sk, p) {
    offset += L_BYTES + 8;                                            // delta + c skip
    for i in 0..p.t { g.set_coeff(i, u16::from_le_bytes(...)); }
    g.set_coeff(p.t, 1);                                              // monic leading śėĺŚľŹŤ°•
    let cb_len = ((2*p.m - 1) << (p.m - 1)) / 8;                      // m=13 ‚Üí 12800
    let controlbits = sk[offset..offset + cb_len].to_vec();
    offset += cb_len;
    let s = sk[offset..offset + p.n_bytes].to_vec();
    Ok(PrivateKeyParts { g, s, controlbits })
}
```

### ťÖćŚ•ó API

śĖįŚĘěťĽėŤģ§ feature ŚÖ¨ŚľÄ `McElieceKeyPair::from_decapsulation_key(param_id, dk) -> Result<Self>`Ôľö

- ťēúŚÉŹ FrodoKEM ÁöĄ I145 ŚźĆś¨ĺ hookÔľą`encapsulation_key = Vec::new()`ÔľĆšĽé dk śěĄťÄ† keypairÔľõšĽÖ decapsulate śĖĻŚźĎŚŹĮÁĒ®ÔľČ
- ťēŅŚļ¶ťó®Ôľö`dk.len() != p.private_key_bytes ‚Üí InvalidArg`
- `encapsulate()` Ś§īťÉ®Śä† empty-pk śó©śúü `Err`ÔľöťĀŅŚÖćŚźéÁĽ≠šĽéÁ©ļ encapsulation_key ÁīĘŚľē panicÔľąśó† silent fall-throughÔľČ

### šłļšĹē self round-trip šłćŤÉĹŚŹĎÁéį

`test_mceliece_6688128_roundtrip` Á≠ČśĶčŤĮēŚŹ™ŤĶį `generate() ‚Üí encapsulate() ‚Üí decapsulate() ‚Üí ss Ťá™śīĹ` Ť∑ĮŚĺĄÔľö

- generate šļß g, controlbits, s, alpha Á≠ČÁĽĄšĽ∂ ‚Üí ŤĶį Rust ťĒôŚłÉŚĪÄ serialize
- decapsulate śääŚźĆŚłÉŚĪÄ deserialize ŚáļśĚ• ‚Üí śčŅŚąįś≠£Á°ģÁöĄ g, controlbitsÔľąť°ļŚļŹšĻĪšĹÜŚĀŹÁßĽŚĮĻÁßįÔľČ+ sÔľąŚźĆś†∑ŚĀŹÁßĽŚĮĻÁßįÔľČ+ Ś§öšĹô alpha ťÉ®ŚąÜÔľąŤĘęŚŅĹÁē•ÔľČ
- decode + session key Ťģ°Áģó ‚Üí Ťá™śīĹ

ŚŹ™śúČ dk śĚ•Ťá™Ś§ĖťÉ®ŚŹāŤÄÉÔľąC SDVÔľČśČćśöīťú≤ ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŤÄĆ self round-trip śĶčŤĮēŤ¶ÜÁõĖŤĆÉŚõīťáĆś≤°ŤŅôšł™ÁĒ®šĺč„Äā**ÁĽďśěĄśÄßÁõ≤ÁāĻ ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ** I145 FrodoKEM ťā£ś¨°ŚźĆś†∑ŚéüŚõ†Ôľąself round-trip ÁĽŅšĹÜŚíĆŚŹāŤÄÉšłćšłÄŤáīÔľČÔľĆťā£śó∂ÁöĄšŅģŚ§ćÁ≠ĖÁē•šĻüśėĮšĽĒÁĽÜŚĮĻÁÖß C ŚŹāŤÄÉÁöĄ wire-level Ś≠óŤäā„Äā

## Phase T161 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust Classic McEliece reference KAT ŤŅĀÁßĽÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ McEliece decaps Structural Gap ÁęĮŚąįÁęĮÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

śČŅśé• I160 śää McEliece sk codec ŚĮĻťĹźŚąį C ŚŹāŤÄÉšĻčŚźéÔľĆxtask śĖįŚĽļ McEliece KAT ŚÖ•ŚŹ£ + 12 šł™ param ID śė†ŚįĄÔľĆśääŚĒĮšłÄ 1 Ť°Ć `MCELIECE_ENCAPS_DECAPS_FUNC_TC001` śēįśćģŤ°ĆšĽé unsupported ŤĶįŚąį emitted„Äāť¶Ėś¨°ŤŅźŤ°ĆŚć≥ byte-exact ťÄöŤŅá ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŤĮĀśėé I160 ÁöĄ sk Ś≠óŤäāŚłÉŚĪÄŚĮĻťĹźśėĮś≠£Á°ģÁöĄ„Äā

### ŚģěÁéį

- **`xtask/src/kem.rs`**Ôľö
  - śĖįŚĘě `mceliece_param(symbol)` śė†ŚįĄŚáĹśēį ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ Ť¶ÜÁõĖŚÖ® 12 šł™ param IDÔľą`McEliece6688128 / 6960119 / 8192128` √ó `_/F/Pc/Pcf`ÔľČ„Äā
  - śĖįŚĘě `emit_mceliece_kat(cases)` ŚÖ•ŚŹ£Ôľąšłé `emit_frodokem_kat` ŚźĆś¨ĺŚĹĘÁä∂ÔľČÔľö
    - ŚŹ™ŚĆĻťÖć `_ENCAPS_DECAPS_FUNC_TC001` Ť°ĆÔľąŚÖ∂šĽĖ TC Śģ∂śóŹŤĶį API-surfaceÔľČ
    - śēįśćģŤ°Ć layoutÔľö`(param, seed, testEk, testDk, testCt, testSs)`ÔľĆśŹźŚŹĖ dk / ct / ss šłČśģĶ hex

## Phase T162 ‚ÄĒ C‚ÜíRust eFrodoKEM reference KAT ŤŅĀÁßĽÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ FrodoKEM 5 Ť°Ć eFrodoKEM unsupportedÔľõna-list ŚĀáŤģĺšŅģś≠£ÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

śČŅśé• T161 šĻčŚźéŚČ©šĹôÁöĄ"deep diagnosis"Ś§áťÄČť°Ļ #1 ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ Ś§ĄÁźÜ FrodoKEM 5 Ť°Ć eFrodoKEM unsupported„ÄāŤĮäśĖ≠ÁĽďśěú**Á¨¨šļĆś¨°šŅģś≠£ na-list ŚĀáŤģĺ**ÔľöšĻčŚČćśé®śĶč eFrodoKEM śėĮ"openHiTLS ťĚěś†áŚáÜśČ©ŚĪēÔľĆRust port ś†Ļśú¨ś≤°ŚģěÁéįŤŅôšł™ŚŹėšĹď"ÔľĆťĘĄšľį 2-4 ŚįŹśó∂"ŚģěÁéįśĖįŚŹėšĹď"Ś∑•šĹúťáŹÔľõŚģěťôÖ**ŚģěÁéįšłÄÁõīŚįĪÁĽ™**ÔľĆŚŹ™śėĮ xtask emitter ś≤°śää 6 šł™ eFrodoKEM symbol Śźćśé•ťÄö„Äā10 ŚąÜťíüśĒĻ mapping + ťáćśĖįÁĒüśąźŚć≥ŚÖ≥ťó≠„Äā

### ŤĮäśĖ≠Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ

1. **`crates/hitls-crypto/src/frodokem/params.rs` Ś∑≤Śźę 12 Ś•ó params** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚÖ® 6 šł™ eFrodoKEM variantÔľą`EFrodoKem640/976/1344Shake/Aes`ÔľČÔľĆ`salt_len = 0`ÔľĆ`seed_se_len` halvedÔľąŚĮĻÁÖß C `frodo_params.c::eFRODOKEM_*` šłÄŤáīÔľČ„Äā
2. **`crates/hitls-crypto/src/frodokem/mod.rs::encapsulate` / `decapsulate` Ś∑≤Ś§ĄÁźÜ salt_len=0**Ôľö
   - `let mut salt = vec![0u8; p.salt_len];` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ 0 ťēŅŚļ¶śó∂šłćŚąÜťÖć
   - `if p.salt_len > 0 { getrandom::fill(...) }` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ 0 śó∂Ť∑≥ŤŅá
   - SHAKE absorb śó∂ `hash_input.extend_from_slice(&salt)` ŚĮĻťõ∂ťēŅŚļ¶ slice śėĮ no-op
   - CT śěĄťÄ†śó∂ `ct.extend_from_slice(&salt)` ŚźĆšłä
3. **`test_efrodokem_640_shake_roundtrip` Ś∑≤ÁĽŅ** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ Ś∑≤śúČ self round-trip śĶčŤĮēÁĒ®šĺčŤ¶ÜÁõĖ `EFrodoKem640Shake`ÔľĆŤĮĀśėéÁęĮŚąįÁęĮŤ∑ĎťÄö„Äā
4. **ŚĒĮšłÄÁľļŚŹ£**Ôľö`xtask/src/kem.rs::frodo_param` ŚáĹśēįšĽÖŤĮÜŚąę `CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_FRODOKEM_*` 6 šł™ symbolÔľõ6 šł™ `CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_*` symbol ŤźĹŚąį `_ => None` Áü≠Ť∑Į ‚Üí 5 Ť°Ć SDV śēįśćģŤ°ĆŤĘę `skipped_unsupported_alg += 1` śľŹśéČ„Äā

### ŚģěÁéį

`xtask/src/kem.rs::frodo_param`Ôľö

```rust
// šĻčŚČć
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_FRODOKEM_1344_AES" => Some("FrodoKem1344Aes"),
_ => None, // eFrodoKEM variants: no fixed-vector reference rows here
```

```rust
// šĻčŚźé
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_FRODOKEM_1344_AES" => Some("FrodoKem1344Aes"),
// eFrodoKEM (openHiTLS-specific extension, salt_len=0 + halved
// seed_se_len). I162 surfaces these to the emitter ‚ÄĒ the Rust
// params + encapsulate / decapsulate paths already handle
// `salt_len = 0` cleanly (the salt buffer just zero-allocs at
// length 0; SHAKE absorbs no salt bytes).
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_640_SHAKE" => Some("EFrodoKem640Shake"),
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_976_SHAKE" => Some("EFrodoKem976Shake"),
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_1344_SHAKE" => Some("EFrodoKem1344Shake"),
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_640_AES" => Some("EFrodoKem640Aes"),
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_976_AES" => Some("EFrodoKem976Aes"),
"CRYPT_KEM_TYPE_eFRODOKEM_1344_AES" => Some("EFrodoKem1344Aes"),
_ => None,
```

- **¬ß12.1**ÔľöPhase A ŤĆÉŚõīśČ©Ś§ßÁļ¶ 4√ó ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚéüŤģ°Śąí 9 Áģóś≥ē 1.5 ŚĎ®ÔľĆŚģěťôÖ 35 Áģóś≥ē ~4 ŚĎ®„Äā

## Phase R22 ‚ÄĒ `RsaHashAlg` śīĺŚŹĎśĒ∂śēõŚąį inherent implÔľąŚÖĎÁéį R20 ÁēôÁöĄ follow-upÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

R20 ÁĽô `RsaHashAlg` Śä† `#[non_exhaustive]` śó∂ÁēôÁöĄ follow-upÔľöŚéüśú¨ RSA ś®°ŚĚóśúČ 4 Ś§Ą `match RsaHashAlg`Ôľąmgf1 h_len + per-iteration„ÄĀpss::h_len„ÄĀpss::hash_with„ÄĀoaep::l_hashÔľČÔľĆŚä†śĖį hash variant Ť¶ĀŚźĆś≠•śĒĻ 4 Ś§ĄŚąÜśē£Śú® 3 šł™śĖášĽ∂ťáĆ„ÄāR22 śää 4 Ś§Ą dispatch śĒ∂śēõŚąį `RsaHashAlg` Ťá™ŤļęÁöĄ 2 šł™ inherent śĖĻś≥ēÔľĆŚįÜśĚ•Śä†ŚŹėšĹďŚŹ™Śä® 2 Ś§ĄÔľąŚźĆśĖášĽ∂ŚÜÖÔľČ„Äā

### śĒĻŚä®

#### śĖįŚĘě `impl RsaHashAlg`Ôľą`rsa/mod.rs`ÔľĆÁīßŤ∑ü enum šĻčŚźéÔľČ

```rust
impl RsaHashAlg {
    pub const fn output_len(self) -> usize {
        match self {
            Self::Sha1 => 20,
            Self::Sha224 => 28,
            Self::Sha256 => 32,
            Self::Sha384 => 48,
            Self::Sha512 => 64,
        }
    }

    pub(crate) fn hash(self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>, CryptoError> {
        match self {
            Self::Sha1 => {
                #[cfg(feature = "sha1")] { /* sha1::Sha1 */ }
                #[cfg(not(feature = "sha1"))] {
                    Err(CryptoError::InvalidArg(
                        "SHA-1 RSA padding requires the sha1 feature"))
                }
            }
            Self::Sha224 => { /* sha2::Sha224 */ }
            Self::Sha256 => { /* sha2::Sha256 */ }
            Self::Sha384 => { /* sha2::Sha384 */ }
            Self::Sha512 => { /* sha2::Sha512 */ }
        }
    }
}
```

- `output_len` śėĮ `pub const fn` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ `pss::h_len` šĽćśėĮ `pub(crate) const fn`ÔľĆťĘúŤČ≤šŅĚśĆĀÔľĆŚŹĮšĽ•ÁĽßÁĽ≠Śú® const context ÁĒ®„Äā
- `hash` śėĮ `pub(crate) fn` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚŹ™Ťģ© crate ŚÜÖ padding ś®°ŚĚóÁĒ®ÔľĆšłćŚĮĻŚ§Ėśöīťú≤ hash ŚéüŤĮ≠ÔľąšŅĚśĆĀ"`RsaHashAlg` śėĮ padding ŚÖÉśēįśćģÔľĆšłćśėĮťÄöÁĒ® hash API"ŤĮ≠šĻČÔľČ„Äā
- SHA-1 ŚąÜśĒĮŚł¶ `#[cfg(feature = "sha1")]` ŚģąÔľĆšŅĚÁēôśóß fail-closed ŤĮ≠šĻČÔľąśó† sha1 feature śó∂ŤŅĒŚõě `InvalidArg`ÔľĆšłćśėĮÁľĖŤĮĎŚ§ĪŤī•ÔľČ„Äā

#### 4 Ś§Ą dispatch ŚÖ®śĒĻ delegate

1. **`rsa/mod.rs::mgf1_with_hash`** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚéüśĚ• h_len Ť°® 5 Ť°Ć + per-iteration 5 Ś§ßŚąÜśĒĮÔľąeach ~6 Ť°ĆÔľČ= 35 Ť°Ć dispatchÔľõÁéįŚú® `let h_len = alg.output_len();` + Ś§ćÁĒ® `Vec::with_capacity(seed.len() + 4)` Śćēś¨° `alg.hash(&input)?` ŤįÉÁĒ®ÔľĆŚÖĪ 13 Ť°Ć„Äā
2. **`rsa/pss.rs::h_len`** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ 9 Ť°Ć match ‚Üí 1 Ť°Ć `alg.output_len()`„Äā
3. **`rsa/pss.rs::hash_with`** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ 35 Ť°Ć matchÔľąŚźę SHA-1 ŚŹćšĺčŚąÜśĒĮÔľČ‚Üí 4 Ť°ĆÔľöŚÖą `matches!(alg, RsaHashAlg::Sha1)` Áü≠Ť∑ĮÔľąPSS šłćśé•ŚŹó SHA-1 Ść≥šĺŅśúČ featureÔľČÔľĆŚÜć `alg.hash(data)`„Äā
4. **`rsa/oaep.rs::l_hash`** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ 32 Ť°Ć match ‚Üí `alg.hash(&[])`ÔľąlHash = Hash("") for empty label per RFC 8017 ¬ß7.1ÔľČ„Äā

#### `mgf1` ŚÜÖ buffer Ś§ćÁĒ®šľėŚĆĖ

śĖįÁČą `mgf1_with_hash` Śú®Śĺ™ÁéĮŚ§Ė `let mut input = Vec::with_capacity(seed.len() + 4);`ÔľĆśĮŹŤĹģ `input.clear() + extend_from_slice(seed) + extend_from_slice(&c)` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚÖĪšłÄś¨°Ś†ÜŚąÜťÖć„ÄāśóßÁČąśú¨śĮŹšł™ŚąÜśĒĮŚÜÖťÉ®ťÉĹ `Sha*::new()` ÁĄ∂Śźé streaming `update(seed) + update(&c)`ÔľĆšłćťúÄŤ¶Āšł≠ťóī bufferÔľĆšĹÜśĮŹŚąÜśĒĮťúÄŤ¶Ā `Sha*::new()` ÁöĄś†ąÁä∂śÄĀŚąÜťÖć„ÄāśĖįÁČąśú¨šĽ•šłÄś¨° ~36 B Ś†Ü buffer śćĘŤĶį 5 šł™ŚąÜśĒĮÁöĄšĽ£Á†ĀťáćŚ§ć ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ śÄßŤÉĹŚŹĮŚŅĹÁē•Ôľąmgf1 šłćśėĮ hot pathÔľöRSA-2048 + SHA-256 šłÄś¨°Á≠ĺŚźćÁļ¶ 8 ś¨°ŤŅ≠šĽ£ = 1 ś¨°Ś†Ü alloc + 8 ś¨° hashÔľČ„Äā

## Phase T163 ‚ÄĒ Phase B kickoffÔľöPKI cert-chain verify śĶčŤĮēśĖ≠Ť®ÄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ #57 + śíľŚä® #55ÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

R21 retrospective śĒ∂Śģė Phase A šĻčŚźéÔľĆPhase B Á¨¨šłÄšł™ŤźĹŚúįšĽĽŚä°Ôľöśää `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/` ťáĆ cert-chain verify Ť∑ĮŚĺĄÁöĄŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄÔľą`assert!(result.is_err())`ÔľČśĒ∂ÁīßŚąį specific PkiError variant„Äāśú¨ PR **ŚÖ≥ťó≠ GitHub issue #57**Ôľąśé•ťÄö 3 šł™ idle fixtures ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚģěťôÖÁéįÁä∂śėĮ 3 šł™ fixture ťÉĹŚ∑≤ŤĘę `#[test]` ŚľēÁĒ®ÔľĆšĹÜśĖ≠Ť®ÄśĚĺśē£ÔľČ+ **śíľŚä® issue #55**ÔľąPKI assertion precision ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚÖ® PKI ś®°ŚĚó ~83 Ś§Ą is_err()ÔľĆśú¨ PR Ś§ĄÁźÜ verify.rs ÁöĄ 6 Ś§ĄÔľČ„Äā

### ŤĮäśĖ≠šłéśĖĻś≥ēŚ≠¶

ŚÖą inventory `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/verify.rs` ŚÖ®ťÉ® 22 Ś§Ą `.is_err()` šĹćÁĹģÔľö

| Áä∂śÄĀ | śēįťáŹ |
|---|---|
| Ś∑≤ťÖć `matches!`/`match`ÔľąPhase A śúüťóīŚ∑≤ś≤Čś∑ÄÁöĄŚ•ĹšĻ†śÉĮÔľČ | 16 |
| šĽćśėĮŤ£ł `is_err()`Ôľąśú¨ PR śĒ∂ÁīßÁõģś†áÔľČ | 6 |

6 Ś§ĄśėéÁĽÜÔľö

| Ť°ĆŚŹ∑ | śĶčŤĮēŚáĹśēį | ŚģěťôÖŚŹėšĹď | #57 ŚÖ≥ŤĀĒ |
|---|---|---|---|
| 945 | `test_verify_wrong_trust_anchor` | `IssuerNotFound` | ‚ÄĒ |
| 1047 | `test_chain_tampered_leaf_sig` | `ChainVerifyFailed(_)` | ‚úď |

| `mod.rs:1167` | `test_parse_malformed_ku` | śó† panicÔľąśĖ≠Ť®Ä cert struct + `key_usage()` ŤįÉÁĒ®šłć panicÔľČ | ‚úď |

### śĒĻŚä®

#### `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/mod.rs`

`test_parse_malformed_ku` ‚ÄĒ `cert_ext_keyusage_err.der` fixtureÔľö

- ŚéüÔľöšĽÖ `let _ku = cert.key_usage();` ś≥®ťáä "don't assert ‚ÄĒ just ensure no panic"
- śĖįÔľöśėĺŚľŹ `cert.from_der().unwrap()` ÁĽďśěĄśÄßśĖ≠Ť®Ä + ŤįÉ `key_usage()` šŅĚŤĮĀ no panic on malformed BIT STRING content
- ś≥®ťáäŤĮīśėé RFC 5280 ¬ß4.2.1.3 ŚĮĻ leftover-bits Ś§ĄÁźÜŚģěÁéįŚĪāÁēôśúČšĹôŚúįÔľĆŚįÜśĚ• parser śĒ∂ÁīßŚźéŚŹĮśćĘśąź assert ŚÖ∑šĹď decoded variant

#### `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/verify.rs`

5 Ś§ĄśĒ∂ÁīßÔľąśĮŹŚ§ĄŚä† 1-3 Ť°Ćšł≠śĖáś≥®ťáäŤß£ťáäšłļšĽÄšĻąŤĶįŤĮ• variantÔľČÔľö

1. `test_verify_wrong_trust_anchor` (L945) ‚Üí `PkiError::IssuerNotFound`
   - Śõ†šłļ intermediates ŚŹ™śúČ interÔľĆtrust = unrelated_caÔľõinter.issuer = "Root CA" Śú®šł§ŤĺĻťÉĹśČĺšłćŚąįŚĆĻťÖć subject
2. `test_chain_tampered_leaf_sig` (L1047) ‚Üí `PkiError::ChainVerifyFailed(_)`
   - Śõ†šłļ leaf ÁöĄ RSA/ECDSA Á≠ĺŚźćŤĘęÁĮ°śĒĻ ‚Üí `verify_signature` ŤŅĒŚõě `Ok(false)` ‚Üí ŤĘę `verify_cert` ŚĆÖŤ£Öśąź `ChainVerifyFailed("signature verification failed")`
3. `test_chain_tampered_ca_sig` (L1059) ‚Üí `PkiError::ChainVerifyFailed(_)` ŚźĆšłä
4. `test_chain_wrong_trust_anchor_real` (L1089) ‚Üí `PkiError::MaxDepthExceeded(10)`
   - Śõ†šłļ actual_root Ťá™Á≠ĺšłĒŚú® intermediates ťáĆ ‚Üí `find_issuer` ŚŹćŚ§ćŚĆĻťÖćŤá™Ś∑Ī ‚Üí chain ťēŅŚļ¶ś∂®Śąį > max_depth (10) ‚Üí trip
5. `test_chain_cycle_detection` (L1107) ‚Üí `PkiError::IssuerNotFound`
   - Śõ†šłļ cycle ŚŹ™śúČšłÄŚćäÔľącycle_bÔľČŚú® intermediatesÔľĆfind_issuer śČĺ cycle_b.issuer = cycle_a śó∂Śú® intermediates + Á©ļ trust ťáĆťÉĹś≤°

### Ťł©ŚĚĎśēôŤģ≠Ôľąmeta-lessonÔľČ

śĒ∂Áīß `test_verify_wrong_trust_anchor` śó∂Á¨¨šłÄś¨°ŚĀáŤģĺ ‚Üí `MaxDepthExceeded(10)`ÔľĆŤ∑ĎśĶčŚŹĎÁéįŚ§ĪŤī• ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ Śõ†šłļśąĎśääŚģÉŚíĆ `test_chain_wrong_trust_anchor_real` ÁöĄťďĺÁĽďśěĄśźěś∑∑šļÜÔľöŚČćŤÄÖ intermediates ŚŹ™Ť£Ö 1 šł™ interÔľąś≤°Ś°ě actual_rootÔľČÔľĆśČÄšĽ•ŤĶįÁöĄśėĮ IssuerNotFound Ť∑ĮŚĺĄÔľõŚźéŤÄÖ intermediates Ť£ÖšļÜ inter + actual_rootÔľĆactual_root Ťá™Á≠ĺŚŹćŚ§ćŚĆĻťÖćŤá™Ś∑ĪśČćŤĶį MaxDepth„Äā

**"ÁĒ® specific variant śõŅ is_err"ŤŅôšĽ∂šļčśú¨ŤļęŚįĪŚú®ŚłģŚä©ŚŹĎÁéį"ŚĀáŤģĺťĒôś†ĻŚõ†"** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ ŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄšľöŤģ©ťďĺÁĽďśěĄŚĀáŤģĺťĒôŤĮĮÁöĄśĶčŤĮēťĽėťĽėťÄöŤŅá„ÄāŤŅôśĚ°ÁĽŹť™ĆšĽäŚźéŚŹĮśé®ŚĻŅŚąįŚÖ∂šĽĖ PKI ś®°ŚĚóÁöĄ #55 śĒ∂ÁīßŚ∑•šĹú„Äā

T163 śĒ∂Śįĺ verify.rs šĻčŚźéÔľĆ#55 Ś∑•šĹúśĆČ PKI ś®°ŚĚóŚąÜÁę†ŤäāśČęÁöĄÁ¨¨šļĆšł™Áõģś†á ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/hostname.rs`„Äāś®°ŚĚóŤá™ŚĆÖŚźę + ťĒôŤĮĮś®°ŚěčśúÄÁģÄŚćēÔľą`verify_hostname` ŚĒĮšłÄŚÖ¨ŚľÄŚÖ•ŚŹ£ÔľĆśČÄśúČŚ§ĪŤī•Ť∑ĮŚĺĄťÉĹŤŅĒŚõě `PkiError::HostnameMismatch(String)`ÔľČÔľĆśĆČ T163 "šłČś≠•ś≥ē"šłÄś¨°śÄßśääŚÖ®ťÉ® 12 Ś§Ą `is_err()` śĒ∂ÁīßŚąį `matches!(err, PkiError::HostnameMismatch(_))`„Äā

### ś®°ŚĚóŤá™ŚĆÖŚźę + ťĒôŤĮĮś®°ŚěčÁģÄŚćē = šłÄś¨°śČęŚģĆ

`verify_hostname` ŚÖ¨ŚľÄŚÖ•ŚŹ£ŚģěÁéįŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÔľö

```rust
pub fn verify_hostname(cert: &Certificate, hostname: &str) -> Result<(), PkiError> {
    if hostname.is_empty() {
        return Err(PkiError::HostnameMismatch("empty hostname".into()));
    }
    // ...try IP first, then DNS SAN, then CN fallback...
    Err(PkiError::HostnameMismatch(format!(...)))  // catch-all
}
```

## Phase T165 ‚ÄĒ #55 Áę†Ťäā 3ÔľöPKI Certificate Ťß£śěźśĶčŤĮēśĖ≠Ť®ÄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ certificate.rs 11 Ś§ĄŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

śČŅśé• T163 (verify.rs 6 Ś§Ą) + T164 (hostname.rs 12 Ś§Ą) ÁöĄ"šłČś≠•ś≥ē"ÔľĆÁ¨¨ 3 Áę† ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ `crates/hitls-pki/src/x509/certificate.rs`„ÄāťĒôŤĮĮś®°ŚěčśĮĒ hostname.rs Ś§ćśĚāÔľöŤ∑ĮŚĺĄśúČšł§śĚ°ÔľąDER Ťß£śěźšłé PEM ť°∂ŚĪāÔľČÔľĆšĹÜśĮŹśĚ°ťÉĹśúČśėéÁ°ģÁöĄ PkiError variant śė†ŚįĄ„Äā**11 Ś§ĄŚÖ®ťÉ®śĒ∂Áīß + 1 Ś§ĄśēÖśĄŹšŅĚÁēô OR ś®°ŚľŹ**„Äā

### ťĒôŤĮĮś®°Śěč (`Certificate::{from_der, from_pem}`)

| ŚÖ•ŚŹ£ | Ś§ĪŤī•Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ | variant |
|---|---|---|

### Ťł©ŚĚĎśēôŤģ≠Ôľąmeta-lesson ŚÜćŚä†ŚõļÔľČ

`pkcs8/encrypted.rs::test_encrypted_pkcs8_invalid_key_len` šł≠ key_len=8 ÁöĄ variant Á¨¨šłÄś¨°ŚĀáŤģĺśėĮ `CryptoError::InvalidArg(_)`Ôľąšłé key_len=24 ŚźĆŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÔľČÔľĆŤ∑ĎśĶčŚ§ĪŤī•„Äāactual variant śėĮ `CryptoError::InvalidKey` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ **AES Śú® CBC ŚÜÖťÉ® key śěĄťÄ†śó∂ failÔľĆŚÖąšļé cipher-OID match catch-all**„Äā

## Phase T167 ‚ÄĒ #55 Áę†Ťäā 5ÔľöPKI CMS ŚÖ®Ś•óšłÄś¨°śČęÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ cms/ Ś≠źś†Ď 19 Ś§ĄŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄÔľõśĖį meta-lesson Á¨¨šļĆśĚ°ÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

ÁĽß T163-T166 šĻčŚźéÔľĆśääśēīšł™ CMS Ś≠źś†Ď**šłÄś¨°śÄßśČęśéČ** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ `cms/mod.rs` 7 + `cms/enveloped.rs` 8 + `cms/encrypted.rs` 4 = **19 Ś§ĄŚÖ®ťÉ®śĒ∂ÁīßŚąį specific `PkiError` variant**„Äāśú¨ PR ś≤Čś∑ÄÁ¨¨šļĆśĚ° meta-lessonÔľö"**ŚźĆšłÄśäĹŤĪ°ŚĪāÁļßÁöĄšłćŚźĆŚÖ•ŚŹ£ŚŹĮŚĮĻŚźĆś†∑ťĒôŤĮĮÁĒ®šłćŚźĆ variant**"„Äā

### ťĒôŤĮĮś®°Śěč

šłČśĖášĽ∂ŚÖĪšļę `cms/mod.rs` ŚÜÖŚģöšĻČÁöĄ helperÔľö

```rust
fn cerr(msg: &str) -> PkiError {
    PkiError::CmsError(msg.into())
}
```

śČÄśúČ CMS ŚĪāśėĺŚľŹťĒôŤĮĮŤ∑ĮŚĺĄťÉĹŤĶį `cerr`Ôľõenveloped/encrypted Ś≠źś®°ŚĚó `use super::cerr;` Ś§ćÁĒ®„ÄāCrypto śďćšĹúŚ§ĪŤī•ÁöĄ `.map_err(PkiError::from)` ťďĺŚąôŤ∑ĮÁĒĪŚąį `PkiError::CryptoError(_)`Ôľąvia `#[from] CryptoError`ÔľČ„Äā

### 19 Ś§ĄŚģěťôÖŚŹėšĹďŚąÜŚłÉ

| śĖášĽ∂ | śÄĽśēį | `CmsError(_)` | `CryptoError(_)` |
|---|---|---|---|
| `cms/mod.rs` | 7 | **7** | 0 |
| `cms/enveloped.rs` | 8 | **6** | **2** |
| `cms/encrypted.rs` | 4 | **2** | **2** |
| ŚźąŤģ° | **19** | **15** | **4** |

### ŤĮ¶ÁĽÜśė†ŚįĄ

**cms/mod.rs (7 ‚Üí CmsError)**:
- tampered Ed25519 sig ‚Üí `cerr("Ed25519 signature verification failed")`
- 3 šł™ `verify_signatures` ŚŹėšĹďÔľąwrong/tampered/no-dataÔľČ‚Üí `cerr` Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ
- truncated DER ‚Üí CMS ŚĪāŚĆÖŤ£ÖŚĪā `cerr("ContentInfo: decode: ...")` Ôľą**Ťł©ŚĚĎ 1**ÔľČ
- `find_signer_cert` SKI not found ‚Üí `cerr("signer cert not found by SubjectKeyIdentifier")`
- 2 šł™ sign_attached/detached verify failures ‚Üí `cerr` Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ

**cms/enveloped.rs (6 CmsError + 2 CryptoError)**:
- CmsError 6 Ś§ĄÔľö`not EnvelopedData` √ó2 + `no Kek/KeyTransRecipientInfo found` √ó2 + `no encrypted content` + `no content encryption params`
- CryptoError 2 Ś§ĄÔľöwrong KEK ‚Üí AES-KW integrity failÔľõ15-byte KEK ‚Üí AES key śěĄťÄ†śčíÔľąÁ©ŅťÄŹŚąįŚļēŚĪāÔľČ

**cms/encrypted.rs (2 CmsError + 2 CryptoError)**:
- CryptoError 2 Ś§ĄÔľöwrong key + tampered ciphertextÔľĆťÉĹŤĶį GCM auth tag fail
- CmsError 2 Ś§ĄÔľökey_len=15/16 wrong-length Śú® `encrypt_symmetric` ŚÜÖ CMS ŚĪā pre-validation Ść≥śčíÔľą**Ťł©ŚĚĎ 2** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ šłé enveloped.rs ŚźĆś¨ĺ wrong-len śĶčŤĮēŤĶįšłćŚźĆ variantÔľČ

### Ťł©ŚĚĎŤģįŚĹē + śĖį meta-lesson

```

Ôľąšłé enveloped.rs::test_decrypt_kek_wrong_key_length ŚźĆś¨ĺ wrong-len ťĒôÔľČ
Ť∑ĎśĶčÔľöFAIL
```

ŚģěťôÖ encrypted.rs ÁöĄ `encrypt_symmetric` ŤįÉ AES ŚČćŚĀöšļÜ key-len pre-validation Áõīśé•ŚŹĎ `cerr("key length 15 does not match algorithm (expected 16)")` ‚Üí `CmsError(_)`ÔľĆś†Ļśú¨šłćŚąį AES ŚĪā„ÄāŤÄĆ enveloped.rs ÁöĄ `decrypt_kek` šłćŚĀö CMS ŚĪā pre-validationÔľĆÁõīśé•šļ§ÁĽô AES ‚Üí `CryptoError(_)`„Äā

## Phase T168 ‚ÄĒ #55 Áę†Ťäā 6ÔľöPKI PKCS#12 šłÄś¨°śČęÔľąŚÖ≥ťó≠ pkcs12/mod.rs ŚÖ®ťÉ® 6 Ś§ĄŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄÔľõ**ť¶Ėś¨°ťõ∂ŤŅ≠šĽ£ŚģĆśąź** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ meta-lesson Śģěśąėť™ĆŤĮĀÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

ÁĽß T163-T167 šĻčŚźéÔľĆśää `crates/hitls-pki/src/pkcs12/mod.rs` **ŚÖ®ťÉ® 6 Ś§Ą** `.is_err()` šłÄś¨°śÄßśĒ∂ÁīßŚąį `matches!(err, PkiError::Pkcs12Error(_))`„Äāśú¨ PR **ť¶Ėś¨°ŚģěÁéįťõ∂ŤŅ≠šĽ£** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ T166 / T167 ÁöĄ"ŚĀáŤģĺťĒôŚŹėšĹď"Ťł©ŚĚĎśēôŤģ≠ŤŅôś¨°śŹźŚČćŤßĄťĀŅÔľöŚÜô `matches!` ŚČćŚÖą grep `from_der` śļźÁ†ĀÁ°ģŤģ§ `perr` ŚĆÖŤ£ÖŚô®ÔľĆ6 Ś§ĄŚÖ®ťÉ®ť¶Ėś¨°ŤŅźŤ°ĆťÄöŤŅá„Äā

### ťĒôŤĮĮś®°Śěč

PKCS#12 ś®°ŚĚóŚģöšĻČśú¨Śúį helperÔľąL679ÔľČÔľö

```rust
fn perr(msg: &str) -> PkiError {
    PkiError::Pkcs12Error(msg.into())
}
```

`from_der` śČÄśúČťĒôŤĮĮŤ∑ĮŚĺĄŚÖ®ťÉ®ÁĽŹ `perr` ŚĆÖŤ£ÖÔľö

- Ś§ĖŚĪā PFX SEQUENCE Ťß£Á†Ā ‚ÄĒ `perr(format!("PFX: {e}"))`
- version INTEGER ‚ÄĒ `perr(format!("version: {e}"))` + `perr("unsupported PFX version")`
- authSafe ContentInfo ‚ÄĒ `perr(format!("authSafe CI: {e}"))`
- context-specific [0] tag ‚ÄĒ `perr(format!("[0]: {e}"))`
- authSafe OCTET STRING ‚ÄĒ `perr(format!("authSafe octet: {e}"))`
- MAC verify (HMAC compare) ‚ÄĒ `perr(format!("HMAC: {e}"))`
- AuthenticatedSafe SEQUENCE OF ContentInfo ‚ÄĒ perr ŚĆÖŤ£Ö

**ŚÖ≥ťĒģ**ÔľöŚć≥šĺŅŚļēŚĪāśėĮ ASN.1 decoder śä•ťĒôÔľĆšĻüÁĒĪ perr ťáćŚĆÖśąź `Pkcs12Error`ÔľĆ**šłćšľöś≥ĄŚáļ raw `Asn1Error`**„Äā

### 6 Ś§ĄśėéÁĽÜ

| śĶčŤĮē | Ś§ĪŤī•Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ | variant |
|---|---|---|
| `test_pkcs12_wrong_password_fails` | ťĒôŚĮÜÁ†Ā ‚Üí HMAC mismatch ‚Üí `perr("HMAC: ...")` | `Pkcs12Error(_)` |
| `test_pkcs12_rejects_tampered_mac_constant_time` | PFX ŚÄíśēįÁ¨¨ 5 Ś≠óŤäāÁŅĽŤĹ¨ ‚Üí ŚĎĹšł≠ MAC tag / salt-length / iteration field šĻčšłÄ ‚Üí šłČśĚ°Ť∑ĮŚĺĄťÉĹŤŅá perr | `Pkcs12Error(_)` |
| `test_p12_wrong_password` | ŚźĆšłä 1 šĹÜÁĒ®ÁúüŚģě OpenSSL PFX ŤÄĆťĚě Rust Ťá™ŚĽļ | `Pkcs12Error(_)` |
| `test_pkcs12_empty_data` Á©ļŤĺďŚÖ• | Á¨¨šłÄšł™ `read_sequence` fail | `Pkcs12Error(_)` |
| `test_pkcs12_empty_data` `[0x30, 0x00]` | Á¨¨šłÄšł™ `read_sequence` OK + version `read_integer` fail | `Pkcs12Error(_)` |
| `test_pkcs12_empty_data` `[0xFF; 64]` | Á¨¨šłÄšł™ `read_sequence` Śú®Á¨¨šłÄŚ≠óŤäā fail | `Pkcs12Error(_)` |

### **ť¶Ėś¨°ťõ∂ŤŅ≠šĽ£** ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ meta-lesson Śģěśąėť™ĆŤĮĀ

```bash
grep -nE "pub fn from_der|fn perr|PkiError::Pkcs12Error" pkcs12/mod.rs
```

**Lesson 3 (T168, śú¨Ťäā)** ‚ÄĒ *ŚÜô `matches!` ŚČćŚÖą grep ś®°ŚĚóť°∂ŚĪāŚÖ•ŚŹ£Ôľą`from_der` / `decrypt` / `verify_*` Á≠ČÔľČśėĮŚź¶śúČ `cerr` / `perr` ÁĪĽ wrapper helper ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ śúČŚąôŚÖ®ťÉ®ŤĶį domain variantÔľõś≤°śúČŚąôŤĶįŚļēŚĪā raw variantÔľąŚ¶ā `Asn1Error`ÔľČ*

šłČś≠•ŤĶįŚģĆŚźéŚüļśú¨ŤÉĹťõ∂ŤŅ≠šĽ£ŚĎĹšł≠ÔľąŚ¶āśú¨ś¨° T168ÔľČ„Äā

## Phase T169 ‚ÄĒ #55 ÁĽąÁę†ÔľöPKI ŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖ 100% ŚÖ≥ťó≠Ôľą16 Ś§ĄŚÜóšĹôśłÖÁźÜ + 7 Ś§Ą OR ś®°ŚľŹśĒĻ matches!ÔľČ

### śÄĽÁĽď

śČŅśé• T163-T168 šĻčŚźéÔľĆśú¨ PR **ÁĽąÁĽď #55 Ś∑•šĹú**„Äāšł§ÁĪĽśĒĻŚä®Ôľö

- **(A) 16 Ś§ĄŚÜóšĹô `is_err()` śłÖÁźÜ**Ôľąverify.rsÔľČ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ T163 ŚźéŚČ©šłčÁöĄ "is_err() + matches!() ťÖćŚĮĻ" šł≠Śą†ťô§ŚÜóšĹô `assert!(result.is_err());` Ť°ĆÔľõÁļĮŤĮ≠ś≥ēÁ≤ĺÁģÄÔľĆťõ∂ŚŹėšĹďŚą§śĖ≠„Äā
- **(B) 7 Ś§Ą OR ś®°ŚľŹśĒĻ `matches!`**Ôľąverify.rs / ocsp.rs / x509/mod.rs / crl.rs / certificate.rsÔľČ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ intentional dual-path ś®°ŚľŹ `assert!(result.is_err() || !result.unwrap())` śĒĻšłļ `assert!(matches!(result, Ok(false) | Err(_)));`ÔľõŤĮ≠šĻČÁ≠ČšĽ∑šĹÜśõīÁ≤ĺÁ°ģ„Äā

šł§ÁĪĽŚźąŤģ° **23 sites** Ś§ĄÁźÜ ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ Ť∂ÖŚáļŚéüťĘĄšľį 19ÔľĆŚõ†šłļŚ§öŤ°Ć `assert!(...)` ŚĚóśĮŹšł™śĆČ 1 site ÁģóŤÄĆťĚě 4 lines„Äā

### `hitls-pki/src` ŚÖ®ś†Ď `.is_err()` Ťģ°śēį = 0

```
$ grep -rcE "\.is_err\(\)" crates/hitls-pki/src/
```

**#55 ŚģĆŚÖ®ŚÖ≥ťó≠**„Äā

### (A) 16 Ś§ĄŚÜóšĹôśłÖÁźÜÁĽÜŤäā

verify.rs ÁöĄŚľĪ-strong ťÖćŚĮĻÔľö

```rust
// šĻčŚČć
assert!(result.is_err());                                      // ‚Üź ŚÜóšĹô
assert!(matches!(result.unwrap_err(), PkiError::IssuerNotFound));

// šĻčŚźé
assert!(matches!(result.unwrap_err(), PkiError::IssuerNotFound));
```

14 šł™ŚćēŤ°Ć + 2 šł™ 4-line ŚĚóÔľąL1468-1471 + L1554-1557 ÁöĄŚ§öŤ°Ć `assert!(\n    result.is_err(),\n    "msg",\n);` ŚĹĘŚľŹÔľČ= ŚÖĪ 16 sitesÔľĆ22 lines Śą†ťô§„Äā

### (B) 7 Ś§Ą OR ś®°ŚľŹťáćŚÜô

intentional dual-path "sig verify ŚŹĮŤÉĹŤŅĒŚõě Err šĻüŚŹĮŤÉĹ Ok(false)"Ôľö

```rust
// šĻčŚČć
assert!(result.is_err() || !result.unwrap());

// šĻčŚźé
assert!(matches!(result, Ok(false) | Err(_)));
```

- **ŤĮ≠šĻČÁ≠ČšĽ∑**Ôľöšł§Ť∑Į valid failureÔľąErr parse Ś§ĪŤī• / Ok(false) verify šłćťÄöŤŅáÔľČ
- **śõīÁ≤ĺÁ°ģ**Ôľöpattern match ťĒĀŚģö `Ok(false)` ŤÄĆťĚěÁü≠Ť∑Į `!result.unwrap()`ÔľąŚźéŤÄÖšĺĚŤĶĖ unwrap ť°ļŚļŹÔľČ
- **šłćťúÄ PartialEq**Ôľö`Ok(false)` śėĮ literal patternÔľĆbuilt-in śĒĮśĆĀ
- **šŅĚśĆĀŚéü inline ś≥®ťáä**ÔľöŤß£ťáäšłļšĽÄšĻąśėĮšł§Ť∑Į

ŚąÜŚłÉÔľöverify.rs (1) + ocsp.rs (2) + x509/mod.rs (2) + crl.rs (1) + certificate.rs (1) = 7 sites„Äā

### TLS-trust ŚÜ≥Á≠ĖŤ∑ĮŚĺĄŚÖ®śłÖ

`verify_cert` / `verify_signature` / `hostname verify` / OCSP / CRL / CMS / PKCS#12 / PKCS#8 **śČÄśúČ PKI śĶčŤĮē**ÁéįŚú®ÁĒ® specific `PkiError` variant śąĖ specific `(Ok(false) | Err)` ś®°ŚľŹťĒĀŚģöŤĮ≠šĻČÔľĆ**no more bare `is_err()`**„Äā

śú™śĚ• regression śó∂ÔľĆťĒôŤĮĮŤ∑ĮŚĺĄŤĘęŤ∑ĮÁĒĪŚąįťĒôÁöĄ variant ÁęčŚć≥śä• test Ś§ĪŤī• ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ šłćšľöŚÜćśúČ"ŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄśĒĺŤ°Ć"„Äā

### Ś∑•ŚÖ∑ÔľöPython śČĻťáŹÁ≤ĺÁ°ģŚ§ĄÁźÜ

(A) ÁĒ® 1-indexed Ť°ĆŚŹ∑Śą†ťô§Ôľö

```python
single_lines = [882, 895, 907, 1095, 1173, 1190, 1218, 1279, 1339, 1352,
                1854, 1871, 1938, 2008]
blocks = [(1468, 1471), (1554, 1557)]
to_delete = set(L - 1 for L in single_lines)
for s, e in blocks:
    to_delete |= set(range(s - 1, e))
new = [line for i, line in enumerate(lines) if i not in to_delete]
```

(B) ÁĒ® `str.replace`ÔľöŚõ† OR ś®°ŚľŹŚ≠óÁ¨¶šł≤śú¨ŤļęŚĒĮšłÄÔľĆ`replace_all` ŚģČŚÖ®Ôľö

```python
content = content.replace(
    'assert!(result.is_err() || !result.unwrap());',
    'assert!(matches!(result, Ok(false) | Err(_)));',
)
```

## Phase T171 ‚ÄĒ #51 CLI Ť¶ÜÁõĖÁ¨¨šļĆŚľĻÔľörand Ś≠źŚĎĹšĽ§Śä† `-out` + `binary` ś†ľŚľŹ + ťĒôŤĮĮŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖ

śČŅśé• T170 prime Á¨¨šłÄŚľĻÁöĄś®°ŚľŹÔľĆśää `rand` Ś≠źŚĎĹšĽ§šĽéŚŹ™śĒĮśĆĀ `(num, format) ‚Üí stdout` śČ©Śąį `(num, format, out: Option<&str>) ‚Üí stdout|file` šłČś®°ŚľŹÔľąhex / base64 / binaryÔľČÔľĆŚźĆśó∂śää rand_cmd śĶčŤĮē 3 ‚Üí 12Ôľą`.is_err()` Ťģ°śēįśłÖťõ∂ÔľČ„Äā

### śĒĻŚä®ś¶āŤ¶Ā

**1 šł™ CLI ŚŹāśēį + 1 šł™śĖį format ś®°ŚľŹ**Ôľą`crates/hitls-cli/src/main.rs` + `crates/hitls-cli/src/rand_cmd.rs`ÔľČÔľö

- `Commands::Rand` Śä† `-o/--out <path>` ŚŹĮťÄČ argÔľąÁü≠ `-o`„ÄĀťēŅ `--out`ÔľČÔľõdispatch śĒĻšłļ `rand_cmd::run(*num, format, out.as_deref())`
- `rand_cmd::run` Á≠ĺŚźćŚĘě `out: Option<&str>` ŚŹāśēįÔľõformat match Śä† `binary` ŚąÜśĒĮ
- ťĒôŤĮĮś∂ąśĀĮ `(use hex or base64)` śĒĻšłļ `(use hex, base64, or binary)`
- śŹź 2 šł™ŚÜÖťÉ® helperÔľö`write_text(s, out)`Ôľąhex/base64 ŚÖĪÁĒ®ÔľĆstdout śó∂ `println!` Śä† newlineÔľĆfile śó∂ `fs::write` šłćŚä†ÔľČ+ `write_binary(buf, out)`Ôľąbinary ś®°ŚľŹÔľĆstdout ŤĶį `stdout().write_all` ŚéüŚßčŚ≠óŤäāÔľĆfile ŤĶį `fs::write`ÔľČ

**ŚĮĻŚ§ĖŤ°ĆšłļŚŹėŚĆĖ**Ôľö

- Śéü `hex` / `base64` stdout Ť°ĆšłļšłćŚŹėÔľąšĽć `println!` + newlineÔľČ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ Ś∑≤śúČ stdout śäďŚŹĖŤĄöśú¨šłćŚŹóŚĹĪŚďć

- ś≤°śĒĻ `num` ŤĺĻÁēĆ `[1, 1048576]` śąĖ errno Ś≠óťĚĘťáŹ

## Phase T172 ‚ÄĒ #51 CLI Ť¶ÜÁõĖÁ¨¨šłČŚľĻÔľöcrl Ś≠źŚĎĹšĽ§Śä† `--cafile` ť™ĆÁ≠ĺ + DER‚ÜĒPEM ś†ľŚľŹŤĹ¨śćĘ + ťĒôŤĮĮŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖ

śČŅśé• T170 prime + T171 rand ÁöĄś®°ŚľŹÔľĆśää `crl` Ś≠źŚĎĹšĽ§šĽéŚŹ™śĒĮśĆĀ `(input, text) ‚Üí śČďŚćįśĎėŤ¶Ā` śČ©Śąį `CrlArgs { input, text, cafile, inform, out, outform }` 6 Ś≠óśģĶÔľĆŚÖ≥ťó≠ C SDV `test_suite_ut_app_crl.c` TC001 row 1/5/7/8ÔľąCAfile ťÄöŤŅá + CAfile Ś§ĪŤī• + DER‚ÜíPEM + PEM‚ÜíDERÔľČÁöĄŤ¶ÜÁõĖÁ©ļÁôĹ„Äā

### śĒĻŚä®ś¶āŤ¶Ā

**CLI ŚĪāÔľąmain.rsÔľČ**Ôľö

- `Commands::Crl` Śä† 4 šł™ŚŹĮťÄČ argÔľö
  - `--cafile <path>` ‚ÄĒ Śä†ŤĹĹ CA ŤĮĀšĻ¶ŚĻ∂ť™ĆŤĮĀ CRL Á≠ĺŚźć
  - `--inform DER|PEM` ‚ÄĒ śėĺŚľŹŤĺďŚÖ•ś†ľŚľŹÔľąťĽėŤģ§ auto-detectÔľČ
  - `-o/--out <path>` ‚ÄĒ ŚÜôśĖášĽ∂šĽ£śõŅśČďŚćįśĎėŤ¶Ā
  - `--outform DER|PEM` ‚ÄĒ ŤĺďŚáļś†ľŚľŹÔľąťĽėŤģ§ PEMÔľČ
- dispatch śĒĻšłļ `crl::run(&CrlArgs { input, text, cafile, inform, out, outform })`

**run() ťáćśěĄÔľącrl.rsÔľČ**Ôľö

- ŚéüÁ≠ĺŚźć `run(input: &str, text: bool)` ‚Üí `run(args: &CrlArgs)`
- 4 šł™ŚÜÖťÉ® helperÔľö
  - `load_crl(data, inform)` ‚ÄĒ PEM / DER / auto-detect ŚąÜśĒĮ
  - `from_pem_bytes(data)` ‚ÄĒ PEM-only ŚÖ•ŚŹ£Ôľąinform=PEM śó∂ bypass auto-detectÔľČ
  - `load_ca_cert(path)` ‚ÄĒ CA śĖášĽ∂Śä†ŤĹĹ + PEM/DER auto-detect parse
  - `encode_crl(crl, outform)` ‚ÄĒ `to_pem` / `to_der` ŚąÜŚŹĎ
- `Format { Pem, Der }` enum + `parse_format(&str)` Ś§ßŚįŹŚÜôśó†ŚÖ≥Ťß£śěźÔľąśé•ŚŹó "PEM"/"pem"/"DER"/"der"ÔľČ
- ťĒôŤĮĮŚĪāś¨°Ôľö
  - CRL Ťß£śěźŚ§ĪŤī• ‚Üí `"CRL parse failed: ..."`
  - CAfile ŤĮĽŚ§ĪŤī• ‚Üí `"CAfile read failed: ..."`

  - Á≠ĺŚźć verify Err ‚Üí `"CRL signature verify failed: ..."`

  - inform/outform śó†śēą ‚Üí `"unsupported format: {x} (use DER or PEM)"`

## Phase T173 ‚ÄĒ #51 CLI Ť¶ÜÁõĖÁ¨¨ŚõõŚľĻÔľąśĒ∂ŚģėÔľČÔľöpkcs12 Ś≠źŚĎĹšĽ§ťĒôŤĮĮŤ∑ĮŚĺĄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖ

śė†ŚįĄ C SDV `test_suite_ut_app_pkcs12.c` ÁöĄ TC002 / TC003 Ť∑ĮŚĺĄ + Rust CLI guard Ť∑ĮŚĺĄÔľö

- `test_pkcs12_no_input_no_export` ‚ÄĒ śó† input + śó† export ‚Üí `assert_eq!(err.to_string(), "--input is required when not in --export mode")` CLI ŚĪā guard
- `test_pkcs12_export_missing_inkey_file` ‚ÄĒ C TC003 r2Ôľą`-export -inkey noexistfile` ‚Üí Ť∑®ŚĻ≥ŚŹį I/OÔľČ

- `test_pkcs12_export_inkey_no_private_key_block` ‚ÄĒ C TC003 r6 spiritÔľąinkey śĖášĽ∂ŚźęÁöĄśėĮ CERTIFICATE ŤÄĆťĚě PRIVATE KEY ŚĚó ‚Üí `assert_eq!(err.to_string(), "no PRIVATE KEY block found in key file")`ÔľČ
- `test_pkcs12_export_cert_file_with_no_certificate_blocks` ‚ÄĒ C TC003 r7 spiritÔľącert śĖášĽ∂ŚźęÁöĄśėĮ X509 CRL ŤÄĆťĚě CERTIFICATE ŚĚó ‚Üí Rust ŚĹďŚČćśé•ŚŹóÁ©ļ cert ŚąóŤ°® + `Pkcs12::create` śąźŚäüÔľõśĖ≠Ť®ÄÁĒüśąźÁöĄ P12 round-trip `parsed.certificates.len() == 0`ÔľĆpin ŚĹďŚČć silent-accept Ť°Ćšłļšłļ future-regression sentinelÔľČ
- `test_pkcs12_nocerts_mode` ‚ÄĒ Ťß£Á†Ā `nocerts: true` ‚Üí PEM Śźę PRIVATE KEY šłćŚźę CERTIFICATE ŚĚóÔľõšłéÁéįśúČ `test_pkcs12_nokeys` ŚĮĻÁßį

### ś≤ŅÁĒ®śĖĻś≥ēŚ≠¶

- **R23 ¬ß12.8 (T163-T169 PKI ŚľĪśĖ≠Ť®ÄÁ≤ĺŚļ¶ŚĆĖ)**ÔľöŚÖą grep ťĒôŤĮĮś∂ąśĀĮŚ≠óťĚĘťáŹśĚ•śļźŚÜć `assert_eq!` / `starts_with`

| ÁľļŚŹ£ | śÄßŤī® | RFC ŚľēÁĒ® | ŚģěÁéįťöĺŚļ¶ | ŚĹĪŚďćśĶčŤĮēŤ°Ć |

| (a) **cRLSign KU ŚľļŚą∂** | Áúü¬∑security bug | RFC 5280 ¬ß6.3.3(f) | ŚįŹÔľą~10 Ť°ĆÔľČ | TC002 r176 + TC004 r206 |
| (b) critical-ext śčíÁĽĚ | Áúü¬∑compliance bug | RFC 5280 ¬ß4.2 | šł≠ÔľąOID ÁôĹŚźćŚćēŤģĺŤģ°ÔľČ | TC001 r149 + TC004 r209 |

(a) śėĮ 4 šł™ťáĆ**Áúü security bug**Ôľąśó†ś≠§ś£Äśü•ŚąôšĽĽśĄŹŚźĆ DN ŤĮĀšĻ¶ťÉĹŤÉĹšľ™ťÄ† CRLÔľČ+ śúÄÁģÄŚćē + śúÄŚĹĪŚďćśėĺÁĄ∂„ÄāŚÖ∂šĽĖ 3 šł™ŚĽ∂śúüŚąį T176/T177„Äā

### šļßŚďĀšĽ£Á†ĀśĒĻŚä®Ôľą`verify.rs::check_revocation_status`ÔľČ

Śú®śČĺŚąį CRL Śźé„ÄĀť™ĆŤĮĀ CRL Á≠ĺŚźćŚČćÔľĆśĖįŚĘě issuer KeyUsage Śģąťó®Ôľö

```rust
// RFC 5280 ¬ß6.3.3(f): the issuer of the CRL must have the cRLSign
// bit asserted in its KeyUsage extension. If the issuer cert has
// no KeyUsage extension, every usage is implicitly permitted
// (RFC 5280 ¬ß4.2.1.3) and the check passes.
if let Some(ku) = issuer.key_usage() {
    if !ku.has(KeyUsage::CRL_SIGN) {
        return Err(PkiError::KeyUsageViolation(
            "CRL issuer certificate lacks cRLSign key usage \
             (RFC 5280 ¬ß6.3.3(f))"
                .into(),
        ));
    }
}
```

10 Ť°ĆśĖįŚĘě + RFC ś≥®ťáäÔľõťõ∂Á†īŚĚŹÁéįśúČŤ°ĆšłļÔľąKU ÁľļŚł≠ = ťöźŚľŹŤģłŚŹĮÔľČÔľõworkspace 1073/0 ŚÖ®ťÉ® pre-fix tests šĽć pass„Äā

### śĶčŤĮēśĒĻŚä®

**śĖįśĖášĽ∂** `crates/hitls-pki/tests/migrated_crl_rfc5280_verify.rs` ‚ÄĒ‚ÄĒ 5 šł™ fixture ŤŅĀÁßĽ + 2 šł™ synthetic chain„Äā

**5 šł™ fixture ŤŅĀÁßĽ**Ôľö

| śĶčŤĮē | C Ť°Ć | Ś§áś≥® |
|------|------|------|
| `tc_line170_x509_crl_verify_two_tier_no_revocation` | TC002 r170 | šł§Áļßťďĺ ca + intermediate + device1 + root_updated.crl + intermediate.crl + ALL ‚Üí Ok(chain len 3) |
| `tc_line191_x509_crl_verify_sm2_server_not_revoked` | TC004 r191 | SM2 root + server2 not on CRL |
| `tc_line194_x509_crl_verify_sm2_server_revoked` | TC004 r194 | SM2 server1 on CRL ‚Üí CertRevoked |
| `tc_line200_x509_crl_verify_sm2_check_revocation_disabled` | TC004 r200 | flag=0 ‚Üí set_check_revocation(false) ‚Üí Ok Ść≥šĹŅ server1 on CRL |
| `tc_line203_x509_crl_verify_sm2_extension_crl_revoked` | TC004 r203 | SM2 extension CRL revocation |

**2 šł™ synthetic chain (trophy tests for the product fix)**Ôľö
